job.title: Academy Education Officer - AWFC
job.location: The Hive London, London, HA8 6AG, GB
job.html_description:
The Role

We are looking for an Academy Education Officer to join us at AWFC and lead, design, and quality assure, the Academy Education Programme across the PGA and club funded Academy. In this vital role, you will ensuring all players receive personalised, bespoke academic support. You'll be working closely with the Player Care Lead and in collaboration with players, parents/guardians and education providers.

You will be entrusted to act as the PGA’s strategic lead and primary point of contact for all education providers and partners to ensuring clear communication pathways and a robust progress monitoring system.

 

Who we are

We are one of the most famous clubs in world football, with a rich heritage and history of success – read more about our history, here.

Beyond that, we are passionate about our local community and, behind the scenes, we have a wide variety of opportunities and career paths for all. We have a very defined purpose: to act for a winning team, culture and community. We achieve this by ensuring we are courageous in the pursuit of progress, we champion our community and each other, and that we do the right thing (even when no one is looking).

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

We are committed to ensuring that our environments for children, young people and adults at risk remain safe and inclusive. We therefore require successful candidates to complete an enhanced DBS check and other essential background checks prior to starting their employment.

Your day-to-day

  • Oversee our education strategy - you will lead the education provision for the AWFC Academy PGA and club funded Academy, including the effective delivery of the Diploma in Sporting Excellence (DiSE), and you'll drive the development of a comprehensive long-term education strategy including continual monitoring and review

  • Provide academic support - you'll ensure players receive appropriate guidance and assistance to meet their educational commitments

  • Create individual learner plans - you will develop and maintain individual learner plans for all players within the PGA, setting clear targets and reviewing progress with relevant stakeholders

  • Build and maintain effective relationships with external stakeholders - you'll establish and uphold effective working relationships with schools, colleges, and NGB education staff to support players in balancing academic and football responsibilities

  • Monitor student progress - you will monitor, record, and review student progress, academic performance, and behaviour on a regular basis, and you will identify and implement early intervention strategies to support students where additional input is required

  • Partner with our Safeguarding team - you'll collaborate with colleagues in our Safeguarding team to contribute to the delivery of the safeguarding strategy for the PGA and club-funded Academy, ensuring compliance with child protection policies and prioritising player welfare at all times

  • Be on hand to offer valuable guidance - you will conduct regular check-ins with players and their parents/guardians to provide support, updates and advice

  • Champion a forward-thinking approach - you'll support players transitioning out of the programme by assisting with future planning, including further education, employment opportunities, or scholarship pathways

What we are looking for

  • Background in education - you come with a history of working with and mentoring young people in an education setting, and you have a good understanding of school systems, secondary and further education qualifications and courses

  • Credible expertise - you're educated to degree level and you have Qualified Teacher Status (QTS) or Qualified Teacher Learning and Skills (QTLS) status

  • Knowledge of the Women's game - you know the standard of players required at AWFC and you have an understanding of the overall Women's Talent Pathway in England

  • Safeguarding experience - you are well aware of child protection issues, and you hold current certificates in FA Safeguarding Children and Introduction to First Aid in Football (IFAiF)

  • Natural team player - you're easily able to work as part of a multidisciplinary team and you've got the ability to motivate others

  • Best-in-class communicator - you are a dynamic, confident communicator who can build exceptional rapport with players and parents/guardians, and you adapt your style and approach as and when required

  • Organised and adaptable approach - you possess excellent organisational and time management skills

  • Enthusiastic approach - you are a motivated and driven individual with commitment and passion for our game



    Position of Trust - As someone in a position of trust role involving regular, direct contact with children or vulnerable adults you will be expected to uphold high standards of conduct to prevent abuse, including sexual, physical, or emotional harm. This role requires you to keep updated with both Club and FA Safeguarding training and education



Why choose us

At Arsenal, we want everyone to feel a sense of trust and belonging, so we are proud of both our club values and also what we offer to our employees. As one of our Gunners, you will receive:

  • An exciting reward and recognition scheme

  • Generous holiday allowance which increases with your length of service

  • Great internal learning and development programmes

  • A flexible hybrid working model

  • Priority access to apply for match tickets

  • A competitive health and wellbeing benefits package

  • A leading Employee Assistance Programme

  • Great discounts with some of our Partners

  • An opportunity to play a vital role in delivering our ED&I strategy by joining one of our Employee Networks

Discover why ‘Our Gunners’ are proud to be part of our team - view their stories here.

Arsenal for Everyone

Arsenal for Everyone is our commitment to promoting and embracing equality, diversity and inclusion, so that everyone connected to the club feels like they belong to the same Arsenal family. We believe that diversity of background, skills and experience drives our success on and off the pitch. 


Disability Confident Leader

We are a Disability Confident Leader. We actively welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities and long-term health conditions. If you need disability-related adjustments to the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a ‘disabled person’ according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme - please indicate this in your application form below. The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment decisions.


Application Closing Date - Friday 17th July 2026

Please note: we reserve the right to close the position early if application volumes are particularly high. We encourage you to get your application in sooner rather than later.

Updates on the status of your application will be sent via email, please routinely check your spam and junk folders, as occasionally emails sent via the Teamtailor platform will find themselves there.

Good luck!

job.title: Kitchen Assistant and Cashier (Casual Role)
job.html_description:
The Role

We are looking for a Kitchen Assistant and Cashier to join our Kitchen team at our training ground in Hale End on a casual basis. We are looking for someone with a passion for food, who has a positive attitude and thrives in a team environment.

You will be responsible for providing friendly and efficient front-of-house service, including serving food buffet style, processing card payments, and supporting staff and players.

This role involves managing accurate records of staff food spend, ensuring smooth service and upholding high standards of hygiene and professionalism at all times.

Please note: this role will involve working flexible hours, including weekday evenings and weekends. 

 

Who we are

We are one of the most famous clubs in world football, with a rich heritage and history of success – read more about our history, here.

Beyond that, we are passionate about our local community and, behind the scenes, we have a wide variety of opportunities and career paths for all. We have a very defined purpose: to act for a winning team, culture and community. We achieve this by ensuring we are courageous in the pursuit of progress, we champion our community and each other, and that we do the right thing (even when no one is looking).

Discover why ‘Our Gunners’ are proud to be part of our team - view their stories here.

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

We are committed to ensuring that our environments for children, young people and adults at risk remain safe and inclusive. We therefore require successful candidates to complete a DBS check and other essential background checks prior to starting their employment. Depending on the responsibilities of the role, a DBS will be either a basic, standard, or enhanced check.

Your day-to-day

  • Deliver excellent customer service - you will serve food and drink in a buffet style setup, providing excellent customer service to staff, players and visitors

  • Oversee orders and payments - you'll operate the till and process card payments accurately, tracking staff food spend and maintain clear records

  • Lead by example - you will communicate clearly and professionally with all customers, upholding club policies at all times, especially health and safety, food hygiene and safety procedures and safeguarding

  • Ensure consistent stock levels - you'll support the team in restocking front-of-house areas and uphold cleanliness throughout service

  • Maintain safety and compliance - you will ensure the safety of others is considered and confidentiality is maintained at all times when work is being undertaken, ensuring the compliance of nutrition and sanitation regulations and all industry hygiene and safety standards

  • Support the Front-of-House and Kitchen team - you'll show flexibility and a positive, can do attitude to help out in other areas where required

 

What we are looking for

  • Proven experience in a similar kitchen environment - you have experience in a customer-facing or food service role

  • Excellent communicator - you are a confident, clear communicator with a positive team player attitude

  • Expertise in processing payments - you are confident in handling transactions and using a POS system

  • True team player - you can effectively work in a team as well as well as independently and you're willing to support and help with your teams needs when required

  • Adaptable and organised - you thrive under pressure and you’re able to effectively adapt to change and prioritise workloads accordingly

  • Flexible and dedicated - you have availability to work weekdays and / or weekends and early mornings



Arsenal for Everyone

Arsenal for Everyone is our commitment to promoting and embracing equality, diversity and inclusion, so that everyone connected to the club feels like they belong to the same Arsenal family. We believe that diversity of background, skills and experience drives our success on and off the pitch. 


Disability Confident Leader

We are a Disability Confident Leader. We actively welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities and long-term health conditions. If you need disability-related adjustments to the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a ‘disabled person’ according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme - please indicate this in your application form below. The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment decisions.

Application Closing Date - Thursday 23rd July 2026

Please note: we reserve the right to close the position early if application volumes are particularly high. We encourage you to get your application in sooner rather than later.

Updates on the status of your application will be sent via email, please routinely check your spam and junk folders, as occasionally emails sent via the Teamtailor platform will find themselves there.

Good luck!

job.title: Grounds Person - Hale End
job.html_description:
The Role

We are looking for a Grounds Person to join our team and support the maintenance and general upkeep of our world class playing surfaces and amenity landscapes at our Hale End Academy.

 

Who we are

We are one of the most famous clubs in world football, with a rich heritage and history of success – read more about our history, here.

Beyond that, we are passionate about our local community and, behind the scenes, we have a wide variety of opportunities and career paths for all. We have a very defined purpose: to act for a winning team, culture and community. We achieve this by ensuring we are courageous in the pursuit of progress, we champion our community and each other, and that we do the right thing (even when no one is looking).

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

We are committed to ensuring that our environments for children, young people and adults at risk remain safe and inclusive. We therefore require successful candidates to complete a DBS check and other essential background checks prior to starting their employment. Depending on the responsibilities of the role, a DBS will be either a basic, standard, or enhanced check.

Your day-to-day

  • Uphold our world class playing surfaces and landscapes - you'll be entrusted with the maintenance of our playing surfaces and amenity landscapes

  • Carry out daily activities to as part of a wider team - you’ll be using our handheld and ride on cutting equipment, strimming around trees, operating our sprinklers, hand digging borders, spreading composts, incorporating into soil, sweeping up hard standing areas, pruning shrubs, disposing of leaves, cuttings and debris, and hand weeding sensitive areas where chemical control is not permitted

  • Efficiently use specialist equipment - you will carry out tractor and trailer work and use a range of machinery in renovation of grass areas such as turf cutters, stone buriers, scarifiers and seeders

  • Keep a keen-eye on the finer details - you'll assist in the renovation of the grounds and play a key role in marking out pitches, applying fertiliser and watering plants

  • Support wider team with important manual tasks - you will help out with deliveries and undertake manual handling of bags of sand, fertiliser and seed ranging from 20-25kg

  • Prioritise best-in-class standards - you'll proactively clean equipment stores and washdown areas and play a vital role in ensuring we're working in an impeccable and spotless environment

What we are looking for

  • Qualified expert - you hold NVQ level 2 or above in green keeping and groundsmanship or equivalent

  • Experience with a range of key equipment - you've used a wide range of turf equipment such as cylinder mowers, ride on mowers, scarification equipment and a wide range of renovation equipment as well as hedge cutters, strimmers and all equipment associated with amenity landscapes

  • Background in all aspects of grounds keeping - you've not only applied your specialist skills to playing surfaces but also to wider amenity landscapes as well

  • Proud team player - you're able to collaborate courageously with the wider team but you also demonstrate the capabilities to work using your own initiative

  • Safety conscious individual - you have a good understanding of the latest health and safety legislation related to the proper use of equipment and machinery

  • Excellent attention to detail and communication skills - you take a meticulous approach to your work and pride yourself on your ability to build strong relationships with colleagues

  • Clean UK driving licence - you'll be able to transport yourself to our club's various different locations


Why choose us

At Arsenal, we want everyone to feel a sense of trust and belonging, so we are proud of both our club values and also what we offer to our employees. As one of our Gunners, you will receive:

  • An exciting reward and recognition scheme

  • Generous holiday allowance which increases with your length of service

  • Great internal learning and development programmes

  • A flexible hybrid working model

  • Priority access to apply for match tickets

  • A competitive health and wellbeing benefits package

  • A leading Employee Assistance Programme

  • Great discounts with some of our Partners

  • An opportunity to play a vital role in delivering our ED&I strategy by joining one of our Employee Networks

Discover why ‘Our Gunners’ are proud to be part of our team - view their stories here.

Arsenal for Everyone

Arsenal for Everyone is our commitment to promoting and embracing equality, diversity and inclusion, so that everyone connected to the club feels like they belong to the same Arsenal family. We believe that diversity of background, skills and experience drives our success on and off the pitch. 


Disability Confident Leader

We are a Disability Confident Leader. We actively welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities and long-term health conditions. If you need disability-related adjustments to the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a ‘disabled person’ according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme - please indicate this in your application form below. The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment decisions.


Application Closing Date - Wednesday 29th July 2026

Please note: we reserve the right to close the position early if application volumes are particularly high. We encourage you to get your application in sooner rather than later.

Updates on the status of your application will be sent via email, please routinely check your spam and junk folders, as occasionally emails sent via the Teamtailor platform will find themselves there.

Good luck!

Updates on the status of your application will be sent via email, please routinely check your spam and junk folders, as occasionally emails sent via the Teamtailor platform will find themselves there.
Good luck!

job.title: Video & Production Lead
job.location: Arsenal Football Club, London, N5 1BU, GB
job.html_description:
The Role

We are looking for a Video & Production Lead to oversee the creation of a distinctive, best-in-class video and audio content offering at our club. Bringing together creative leadership, strategic direction, and operational excellence to deliver impactful output at scale.

Sitting at the heart of the Content & Media department, this role will be pivotal in shaping a bold, creatively led, culturally resonant vision.

Partnering closely with teams across our club, the Video & Production Lead will play a critical role in unlocking value, ensuring content supports key priorities while strengthening our club's brand, relevance, and emotional connection with global audiences.

 

Who we are

We are one of the most famous clubs in world football, with a rich heritage and history of success – read more about our history, here.

Beyond that, we are passionate about our local community and, behind the scenes, we have a wide variety of opportunities and career paths for all. We have a very defined purpose: to act for a winning team, culture and community. We achieve this by ensuring we are courageous in the pursuit of progress, we champion our community and each other, and that we do the right thing (even when no one is looking).

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

We are committed to ensuring that our environments for children, young people and adults at risk remain safe and inclusive. We therefore require successful candidates to complete a DBS check and other essential background checks prior to starting their employment. Depending on the responsibilities of the role, a DBS will be either a basic, standard, or enhanced check.

Your day-to-day

  • Own our video strategy - you'll define and lead our video strategy across the full range of content output, translating wider content strategy into clear video priorities, using audience insight, data and platform behaviour

  • Lead a dedicated team - you will inspire your team to produce world-class content that defines our club's brand, strengthens emotional connections with our supporters, and advances our strategic ambitions

  • Operate as a senior leader in the department - you'll play a key role in the Content & Media department's senior management team to shape and elevate the range and standard of content, working in collaboration with other units

  • Uphold our high standards - you will build and champion a culture of creative excellence, collaboration and high performance, empowering your team to deliver exceptional work and realise their full potential

  • Collaborate with colleagues across our club - you'll serve as the primary point of contact for key stakeholders, managing relationships and prioritising requests to align with business objectives and departmental capacity

  • Oversee team operations - you will manage an integrated planning and resource model, ensuring projects are delivered on time, on budget, and resources are allocated effectively across the team

 

What we are looking for

  • Extensive experience within a sports, entertainment or media-focused environment - you're a visionary with a history of working as a leader within a sport-adjacent organisation, entertainment brand, or culturally relevant digital media environment, and you come with a track record of delivering market-leading, innovative video and audio output across large-scale productions, live programming, branded content and coverage of major events

  • Trusted and inspiring leader - you're proud of your ability to motivate, coach and develop a talented team, delivering results as a collective, but also to lead and empower individuals to succeed and develop

  • Pride in your craft - you have exceptional creative judgement, with a proven ability to set the direction for storytelling and define best-in-class content, and you're easily able to develop ideas that feel unique, distinctive, engaging and culturally relevant

  • Data-driven approach - you leverage audience data, editorial insight and platform performance to shape content decisions, using performance insight to improve content formats and challenge assumptions

  • Excellent stakeholder management skills - you thrive when you're managing senior stakeholders, competing priorities and resource decisions in a fast-moving organisation

  • Confident communicator - you have a clear, calm and transparent communication style, and you're able to build and maintain impactful relationships with a diverse range of colleagues and external stakeholders, tailoring your approach as and when required

  • Organised and adaptable mindset - you're equipped with exceptional planning, resource management, workflow and prioritisation skills

  • Working knowledge of platforms and systems - you are well versed in working with a range of production hardware, systems, emerging technology and AI

  • Fluency with our brand - you've got a high level of cultural awareness, with an understanding of our club's brand and role beyond the pitch

Why choose us

At Arsenal, we want everyone to feel a sense of trust and belonging, so we are proud of both our club values and also what we offer to our employees. As one of our Gunners, you will receive:

  • An exciting reward and recognition scheme

  • Generous holiday allowance which increases with your length of service

  • Great internal learning and development programmes

  • A flexible hybrid working model

  • Priority access to apply for match tickets

  • A competitive health and wellbeing benefits package

  • A leading Employee Assistance Programme

  • Great discounts with some of our Partners

  • An opportunity to play a vital role in delivering our ED&I strategy by joining one of our Employee Networks

Discover why ‘Our Gunners’ are proud to be part of our team - view their stories here.

Arsenal for Everyone

Arsenal for Everyone is our commitment to promoting and embracing equality, diversity and inclusion, so that everyone connected to the club feels like they belong to the same Arsenal family. We believe that diversity of background, skills and experience drives our success on and off the pitch. 


Disability Confident Leader

We are a Disability Confident Leader. We actively welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities and long-term health conditions. If you need disability-related adjustments to the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a ‘disabled person’ according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme - please indicate this in your application form below. The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment decisions.


Application Closing Date - Friday 24th July 2026

Please note: we reserve the right to close the position early if application volumes are particularly high. We encourage you to get your application in sooner rather than later.

Updates on the status of your application will be sent via email, please routinely check your spam and junk folders, as occasionally emails sent via the Teamtailor platform will find themselves there.

Good luck!
job.plain_text_description:
The Role
We are looking for aVideo & Production Leadto oversee the creation of a distinctive, best-in-class video and audio content offering at our club. Bringing together creative leadership, strategic direction, and operational excellence to deliver impactful output at scale.
Sitting at the heart of the Content & Media department, this role will be pivotal in shaping a bold, creatively led, culturally resonant vision.
Partnering closely with teams across our club, the Video & Production Lead will play a critical role in unlocking value, ensuring content supports key priorities while strengthening our club's brand, relevance, and emotional connection with global audiences.
Who we are
We are one of the most famous clubs in world football, with a rich heritage and history of success – read more about our history, here.
Beyond that, we are passionate about our local community and, behind the scenes, we have a wide variety of opportunities and career paths for all. We have a very defined purpose: to act for a winning team, culture and community. We achieve this by ensuring we are courageous in the pursuit of progress, we champion our community and each other, and that we do the right thing (even when no one is looking).
Our Commitment to Safeguarding
We are committed to ensuring that our environments for children, young people and adults at risk remain safe and inclusive. We therefore require successful candidates to complete a DBS check and other essential background checks prior to starting their employment. Depending on the responsibilities of the role, a DBS will be either a basic, standard, or enhanced check.
Your day-to-day
- Own our video strategy- you'll define and lead our video strategy across the full range of content output, translating wider content strategy into clear video priorities, using audience insight, data and platform behaviour - Lead a dedicated team - you will inspire your team to produce world-class content that defines our club's brand, strengthens emotional connections with our supporters, and advances our strategic ambitions - Operate as a senior leader in the department- you'll play a key role in the Content & Media department's senior management team to shape and elevate the range and standard of content, working in collaboration with other units - Uphold our high standards- you will build and champion a culture of creative excellence, collaboration and high performance, empowering your team to deliver exceptional work and realise their full potential - Collaborate with colleagues across our club- you'll serve as the primary point of contact for key stakeholders, managing relationships and prioritising requests to align with business objectives and departmental capacity - Oversee team operations- you will manage an integrated planning and resource model, ensuring projects are delivered on time, on budget, and resources are allocated effectively across the team
What we are looking for
- Extensive experience within a sports, entertainment or media-focused environment- you're a visionary with a history of working as a leader within a sport-adjacent organisation, entertainment brand, or culturally relevant digital media environment, and you come with a track record of delivering market-leading, innovative video and audio output across large-scale productions, live programming, branded content and coverage of major events - Trusted and inspiring leader- you're proud of your ability to motivate, coach and develop a talented team, delivering results as a collective, but also to lead and empower individuals to succeed and develop - Pride in your craft- you have exceptional creative judgement, with a proven ability to set the direction for storytelling and define best-in-class content, and you're easily able to develop ideas that feel unique, distinctive, engaging and culturally relevant - Data-driven approach- you leverage audience data, editorial insight and platform performance to shape content decisions, using performance insight to improve content formats and challenge assumptions - Excellent stakeholder management skills - you thrive when you're managing senior stakeholders, competing priorities and resource decisions in a fast-moving organisation - Confident communicator- you have a clear, calm and transparent communication style, and you're able to build and maintain impactful relationships with a diverse range of colleagues and external stakeholders, tailoring your approach as and when required - Organised and adaptable mindset- you're equipped with exceptional planning, resource management, workflow and prioritisation skills - Working knowledge of platforms and systems- you are well versed in working with a range of production hardware, systems, emerging technology and AI - Fluency with our brand- you've got a high level of cultural awareness, with an understanding of our club's brand and role beyond the pitch
Why choose us
At Arsenal, we want everyone to feel a sense of trust and belonging, so we are proud of both our club values and also what we offer to our employees. As one of our Gunners, you will receive:
- An exciting reward and recognition scheme - Generous holiday allowance which increases with your length of service - Great internal learning and development programmes - A flexible hybrid working model - Priority access to apply for match tickets - A competitive health and wellbeing benefits package - A leading Employee Assistance Programme - Great discounts with some of our Partners - An opportunity to play a vital role in delivering our ED&I strategy by joining one of our Employee Networks
Discover why ‘Our Gunners’ are proud to be part of our team -view their stories here.
Arsenal for Everyone
Arsenal for Everyone is our commitment to promoting and embracing equality, diversity and inclusion, so that everyone connected to the club feels like they belong to the same Arsenal family. We believe that diversity of background, skills and experience drives our success on and off the pitch.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a ‘disabled person’ according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme - please indicate this in your application form below. The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment decisions.
Application Closing Date - Friday 24th July 2026
Please note: we reserve the right to close the position early if application volumes are particularly high. We encourage you to get your application in sooner rather than later.

job.title: Steward Supervisor (Casual)
job.html_description:
Location: Vitality Stadium, and other venues as directed by AFC Bournemouth 
Hours: Varied 

AFC Bournemouth has an exciting opportunity to lead and supervise a team of stewards to ensure a safe, secure, and welcoming environment for all people present at the ground. The Supervisor is responsible for implementing the management's safety policies within their designated area and acting as a critical link in the chain of command between the stewards and the Control Room. 

This role requires a satisfactory enhanced DBS check due to responsibilities involving children and adults at risk. 
 
This role is seasonal and based on a casual worker agreement. 
 
Shifts are normally 6 hours on a match day, as an example report time for a 15:00 kick off will be 11:00 with the finish time being between 17:45 and 18:00
  • Deliver cascade safety briefings to their assigned section of stewards, ensuring all staff understand their specific roles, the ground layout, and contingency plans. 
  • Monitor and reinforce the Stewards’ Code of Conduct, ensuring staff are polite, courteous and respectful  
  • Monitor crowd movements and densities within viewing areas to prevent overcrowding and identify potential hazards like trip hazards or fire risks. 
  • Oversee the free flow of spectators during ingress and egress, ensuring that gangways, vomitories, and exit routes remain unobstructed at all times. 
  • Serve as the first point of call for incidents or issues in their area, coordinating early action and reporting to the Control Room via radio. 
  • Implement contingency plans for various scenarios, such as fire alarms, medical incidents, or crowd disorder. 
  • Coordinate the emergency evacuation of all people in their area, including specific procedures for disabled spectators and children. 
  • Conduct pre-event inspections of structures, installations, and components (e.g., seats, barriers, and exit gates) within their area to ensure they are safe and fit for purpose. 
  • Ensure that any incidents, accidents, or ejections are accurately recorded on standard forms before finishing duty. 
  • Actively promote equality and inclusion, ensuring the needs of disabled spectators and those with protected characteristics are met. 
The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.
  • Must hold a Level 3 spectator safety qualification. 
  • Excellent customer service and communication skills, including a calm approach to difficult situations. 
  •  A strong desire to work as part of a team to deliver a safe and enjoyable matchday. 
  • An understanding and ability to communicate verbal and written instructions in English; ability to deliver clear instructions under pressure. 
The Club’s values are at the core of its staff behaviour and how they approach everything they do; therefore, we are looking for someone is honest, inclusive, kind, hardworking, loyal, and respectful. 

The successful candidate will be required perform a reasonable level of physical activity (standing for prolonged periods etc), along with having the maturity, reliability, and interpersonal skills needed to carry out duties effectively and responsibly.

job.title: Academy Football Administrator
job.location: AFC Bournemouth Performance Centre, Wimborne, Wimborne, Dorset
job.html_description:
Co-ordinate the administrative and logistical aspects of all incoming players.

Academy Values: 
  • Players First: holding individual player development as the main intention behind decisions made as a coach and wider professional.  
  • Togetherness: working in a prosocial manner as part of a wider team – willing to contribute beyond own interests. 
  • Integrity: carrying out academy and club values in an open and honest manner. 
  • Accountability: willing to take responsibility for actions in a manner which allows learning, growth and reflection. 
  • Be the main point of contact for trialists supporting them as and when needed, both pre and during their trial period.
  • Ensure all U9-Development Squad trialist paperwork is completed and players are registered appropriately.
  • Manage travel and accommodation for trialists, liaising with the Player Care department, when necessary (U16-Development Squad).
  • Manage trialist schedules (U16-Development Squad) as well as keeping track and scheduling pre/mid/end-of-trial meetings (U9-U16).
  • Organise or liaise with the relevant staff to ensure trialists have the appropriate kit ready for their trial period.
  • Lead on the Academy player registration process and ensure all required paperwork is complete in a timely manner.
  •  Obtain International Clearance where necessary and liaise with Football Administration department to ensure knowledge of current protocols for
    players of different registration status.
  • Organise the player signing process and handover to Player Care & Operations.
  • Manage requests through the Kitman Labs platform for AFCB scouts attending fixtures.
  • Manage travel and accommodation for Recruitment staff, as and when necessary.
  • Support the Operations department with managing weekly transport schedules.
Other Duties
  • As a member of staff to ensure in all matters, you follow and actively promote the club’s mental health policies and practices to ensure an open, non-judgmental and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club. 
  • As a member of staff to ensure that you to adhere to the club’s safeguarding policies and procedures and are committed to our safeguarding values, creating a safe and positive environment for all those who interact with the club. 
  • Obligation to adhere to the club’s health and safety policy and procedures. 
  • Responsibility to comply with the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974, the Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005, and all related Company policies, taking reasonable care for their own safety and that of others, reporting hazards or incidents, following safe systems of work, and co-operating with all training and emergency procedures.
  • As a member of staff to ensure in all matters you follow and actively promote the club’s equality policies and practices to ensure a welcoming, safe and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club. 
The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.
  • Degree in Sport Field or Business Management field (Desirable) 
  • Experience of working in sports (Desirable) 
  • Full driving license or ability to travel between venues 
  • Free onsite parking.
  • Season ticket and allocation of complimentary/purchased tickets (subject to availability).
  • Subsidised lunches and complimentary healthy snacks throughout the day.
  • Discounts at the club Superstore.
  • Access to Health Cash Plan with Simplyhealth.
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Contribution towards eye tests and glasses.
  • Discounts and benefits from partners and local businesses.
  • Club pension & Life Assurance Scheme.
  • Paid parental leave (bank of five days per year).
  • Club events or other social events throughout the year run by our club social team.
  • Paid volunteer opportunities (2 days per year).
  • Paid day’s leave on your birthday.

job.title: Concourse Bar Staff (Casual)
job.html_description:
We are recruiting staff to serve food, confectionery, hot /cold drinks, etc.
 The shifts are approx. 4 - 6 hours home match day only (usually Saturday ko. 3.00pm.).
Staff will be expected to work at a fast pace as we only have a smalltime frame to serve the supporters.

The stadium is very busy on a match day but there is a good atmosphere within the team and you get to know the supporters.

Experience is a bonus, but not essential as full training given onsite.
  • Duties include using tills, recording information, replenishing stock, ensuring your work area is clean and tidy.
  •  You will also be expected to handle food this can include an element of cooking.
  •  You will be expected to work as part of a team as there are 22 concourse bars and kiosk around the stadium.
  • Customer care is very important to us, so you will be expected to be polite and helpful to all individual customers..
  • As a casual worker you must adhere to the club’s safeguarding policies and procedures and are committed to our safeguarding values, creating a safe and positive environment for all those who interact with the club. 
  • Obligation to adhere to the club’s health and safety policy and procedures. 
  • Actively promote the club’s equality policies and practices to ensure a welcoming, safe and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club. 
The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.

job.title: Bar and Waiting Staff (Casual)
job.html_description:
We are recruiting for:
  • Waiting Staff
  • Food Runners
  • Glass Collectors
  • Matchday Restaurant Supervisors (previous experience in a similar role essential)
  • Matchday Bar Supervisors (previous experience in a similar role essential)

We are looking for friendly, enthusiastic, reliable and self-motivated individuals with a hard-working positive approach, a polite manner and excellent attention to detail to work on matchdays and optional non matchday midweek shifts.
 
Previous experience within a quality, fast-paced catering environment is preferable as staff members are required to display initiative and calmness under pressure. A strong work ethic and a can-do attitude are also prerequisites.
 
As a guide, weekend matchday shifts will be 10am through to 8pm and midweek matchday shifts will be 4pm to midnight.
  • Serving guests Food and Beverage requirements in our Hospitality suites.
  • Processing transaction using Epos till systems.
  • Recording and monitoring temperature controls in correct manner 
  • Replenish and re stock bars where required.
  • Keep bar areas clean and tidy throughout the day.
  • Always deliver high standard of customer service with the aim to exceed customer expectations.
  • As a casual worker you must adhere to the club’s safeguarding policies and procedures and are committed to our safeguarding values, creating a safe and positive environment for all those who interact with the club. 
  • Obligation to adhere to the club’s health and safety policy and procedures. 
  • Actively promote the club’s equality policies and practices to ensure a welcoming, safe and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club. 
  • Responsibility to comply with the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974, the Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005, and all related Company policies, taking reasonable care for their own safety and that of others, reporting hazards or incidents, following safe systems of work, and co-operating with all training and emergency procedures.

The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.
  • Previous experience in a fast-paced hospitality environment but not essential as full training will be given.
  • Ability to work well under pressure. 
  • A good understanding of customer service and willingness to go the extra mile to ensure customer satisfaction.
  • Be self-motivated with a proven track record of working well within a team environment. 
  • An understanding of basic food hygiene and health and safety practises.

job.title: Superstore Assistant (Casual)
job.html_description:
AFC Bournemouth is a family and community club. We strive to deliver a professional, friendly and welcoming approach in all areas of the club. We pride ourselves on hard work, passion and togetherness. 

The retail team have an exciting opportunity for multiple roles to be filled within its retail and superstore team on matchday.  You will help our customer’s/supporters find the right products to enhance their enjoyment of supporting AFC Bournemouth through the provision of exceptional customer service.

  • To personally provide excellent standards of customer service by: 
  • Displaying good listening skills to identify customer needs and meeting those needs with the right product advice. 
  • Provide relevant and comprehensive key product information to customers. 
  • Demonstrating appropriate body language - friendly, open, helpful and approachable. 
  • Confidently engaging with customers and closing sales. 
  • Looking for link selling and till add on opportunities which will increase spend per head. 
  • Understanding the importance of product presentation and general store/ retail area standards.  
  • Confident in solving customer problems quickly and effectively. 
  • Maintaining good working relationships with ‘internal stakeholders’ such as; Commercial team, Ticketing team, Accounts team, Operations Director, Chief Executive and Chairman. 
  • Providing our customers with the right products and experience so that every customer leaves inspired to enjoy supporting AFC Bournemouth. 
  • Replenish stock and maintain high standards of merchandising by ensuring that products are attractively displayed, correctly barcoded and priced with relevant Point of Sale (POS) applied. 
  • Conduct till point operations in line with guidelines. 
  • Conduct shirt printing where applicable (following training). 
  • Understanding and operation of the Touch Office EPOS system. 
  • Conduct Stock Checks as requested. 
  • Maintain vigilance in store and awareness of security issues including for example, fraudulent bank notes, credit cards, theft and suspicious behaviour; escalate security concerns to management as and when required.  
  • Demonstrating commitment to the team, supporting and helping other colleagues with tasks. 
  • Displaying a genuine and positive attitude to help team and Superstore achieve its objectives. 
  • Getting behind Retail initiatives. 
  • Carrying out additional duties as when required. 
  • Commitment to undertaking identified training requirements and putting into place learnings. 
  • Ability and willingness to acquire job related skills and knowledge through self-development and by learning from others. 
  • As a casual worker you must adhere to the club’s safeguarding policies and procedures and are committed to our safeguarding values, creating a safe and positive environment for all those who interact with the club.  
  • Obligation to adhere to the club’s health and safety policy and procedures.  
  • Actively promote the club’s equality policies and practices to ensure a welcoming, safe and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club.  
The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.
  • Enjoy helping others 
  • Comfortable working in a fast paced and busy environment  
  • Experience in customer service or sales 
  • Experience in retail (not essential) 
  • Good verbal and written communication 
  • Punctual and effective time keeping
job.plain_text_description:
AFC Bournemouth is a family and community club. We strive to deliver a professional, friendly and welcoming approach in all areas of the club. We pride ourselves on hard work, passion and togetherness.
The retail team have an exciting opportunity for multiple roles to be filled within its retail and superstore team on matchday. You will help our customer’s/supporters find the right products to enhance their enjoyment of supporting AFC Bournemouth through the provision of exceptional customer service.
- To personally provide excellent standards of customer service by: - Displaying good listening skills to identify customer needs and meeting those needs with the right product advice. - Provide relevant and comprehensive key product information to customers. - Demonstrating appropriate body language - friendly, open, helpful and approachable. - Confidently engaging with customers and closing sales. - Looking for link selling and till add on opportunities which will increase spend per head. - Understanding the importance of product presentation and general store/ retail area standards. - Confident in solving customer problems quickly and effectively. - Maintaining good working relationships with ‘internal stakeholders’ such as; Commercial team, Ticketing team, Accounts team, Operations Director, Chief Executive and Chairman. - Providing our customers with the right products and experience so that every customer leaves inspired to enjoy supporting AFC Bournemouth. - Replenish stock and maintain high standards of merchandising by ensuring that products are attractively displayed, correctly barcoded and priced with relevant Point of Sale (POS) applied. - Conduct till point operations in line with guidelines. - Conduct shirt printing where applicable (following training). - Understanding and operation of the Touch Office EPOS system. - Conduct Stock Checks as requested. - Maintain vigilance in store and awareness of security issues including for example, fraudulent bank notes, credit cards, theft and suspicious behaviour; escalate security concerns to management as and when required. - Demonstrating commitment to the team, supporting and helping other colleagues with tasks. - Displaying a genuine and positive attitude to help team and Superstore achieve its objectives. - Getting behind Retail initiatives. - Carrying out additional duties as when required. - Commitment to undertaking identified training requirements and putting into place learnings. - Ability and willingness to acquire job related skills and knowledge through self-development and by learning from others. - As a casual worker you must adhere to the club’s safeguarding policies and procedures and are committed to our safeguarding values, creating a safe and positive environment for all those who interact with the club. - Obligation to adhere to the club’s health and safety policy and procedures. - Actively promote the club’s equality policies and practices to ensure a welcoming, safe and inclusive environment for everyone engaged with the club.
The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and additional tasks may be assigned to the employee from time to time; or the scope of the job may change as necessitated by business demands.
- Enjoy helping others - Comfortable working in a fast paced and busy environment - Experience in customer service or sales - Experience in retail (not essential) - Good verbal and written communication - Punctual and effective time keeping

job.title: Intern Ticket Operations & Services
job.html_description:
Position:                             Ticket Operations & Services Intern
Department:                     Ticket Operations
Status:                                 Intern (Non-Exempt)
Duration:                            Temporary - Runs through the end of the 2026 Charlotte FC season, to include potential playoffs.
 
About Charlotte FC
Charlotte Football Club is the Major League Soccer Club in the Carolinas. The Club’s mission is to be a unifying force that is bigger than one city and encourage a sense of belonging through a progressive, collective, and ambitious approach that guides efforts on and off the pitch. CLTFC is committed to driving growth through the sport and community outreach. Charlotte FC is owned by David Tepper and is a part of Tepper Sports & Entertainment, which also includes Bank of America Stadium and the Carolina Panthers. For more information, visit CharlotteFootballClub.com.
 
Position Summary
This position will support the Ticket Operations and Ticket Services departments with day-to-day tasks, including administrative duties such as filing, answering phones, interacting with other departments, and overall contributing to the organization’s function.  The position will comprehensively overview all Ticket Operations/Services areas.
 
Primary Responsibilities: 
Ticket Operations Responsibilities:
  • Gain familiarity and utilize the Ticketmaster system including Archtics, TM1 and Account Manager
  • Assist in the processing and establishment of season, group, and single match accounts. This is done through new sales, transfers, exchanges, and upgrades of tickets.
  • Provide support in the areas of ticket resolution, customer service, and walk-up sales on Match Days in the box office for Charlotte FC and Crown Legacy FC. 
 
Ticket & Premium Services Responsibilities:
  • Responsible for assisting fans with inbound inquiries by phone, email, and other communication platforms.
  • Assist with current season ticket member accounts by being the liaison between the club and the fan.
  • Execute initiatives outside of the office on both game days and at non-gameday events.
  • Assist Premium Services team in execution of match day duties, Suite and Suite Club member events, concierge staff duties and specialized member amenity programs.
  • Assist in various logistics required for stadium events, including but not limited to:
  • General Setup (ex - roster cards, suite inspections)
  • Prepare Platinum Access Lobby staff folders.
  • Suite Visits
  • Identifying any event maintenance needs
 
Sales Responsibilities:
  • Reaching out to group contacts to sell Theme Nights and Group Initiatives with Charlotte FC and Crown Legacy FC.
  • Assisting the Groups sales team on Group Initiatives on both gamedays and events held outside of gameday for Charlotte FC and Crown Legacy FC.
  • Attending all sales and service training sessions.
  • Assist in Charlotte FC Sales events for Season Tickets, Group Tickets, and Service events. 
 
Additional Responsibilities:
  • Administrative duties include filing, answering phones, and providing overall support to the Ticketing Department.
  • Manual labor is sometimes required (in-suite/ gift or supply delivery, event implementation, etc.)
  • Assist with all aspects of Tepper Sports and Entertainment including Bank of America Stadium events, Crown Legacy FC and Carolina Panthers games as needed. 
 
Minimum Qualifications:
  • A college degree or actively pursuing a college degree.
  • Must be flexible to work evenings and/or weekends and all Charlotte Football Club. and Stadium events, including events held on weekends, nights, and holidays.
  • Must pass Pre-Employment Screenings.
 
Essential Functions:
  • Strength in teamwork, professionalism, and time management.
  • Must possess excellent oral, written, organizational and proofreading skills.
  • Positive and mature attitude, team player, self-motivated and responsible.
  • Detail Oriented. Must be able to multi-task without frustration.
 
Physical Requirements
  • Standing for extended periods of time
  • Sitting and working at a desk on a computer
  • Walking throughout the stadium, which may include stairs.
  • Lift at least 30 pounds
 
Work Environment:
This job responsibility description is a broad overview of the position. This does not limit expected contributions to the organization. The Charlotte Football Club organization is based upon a teamwork type atmosphere.
 
We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Pro Pathway Coach
job.html_description:
Position:                                                      Pro Pathway Coach
Department:                                               Academy
Reporting Relationship:                           Technical Director
Status:                                                          Full-Time (Exempt)

About Charlotte FC
Charlotte Football Club is the Major League Soccer Club in the Carolinas. The Club’s mission is to be a unifying force that is bigger than one city and encourage a sense of belonging through a progressive, collective, and ambitious approach that guides efforts on and off the pitch. CLTFC is committed to driving growth through sport and community outreach. Charlotte FC is owned by David Tepper and is a part of Tepper Sports & Entertainment, which also includes Bank of America Stadium and the Carolina Panthers.

Charlotte FC Academy Overview
The Charlotte FC Academy vision is to provide a high-performance environment for the most talented, high-potential young soccer players from across the Carolinas and beyond. Our mission is to inspire, develop, and support elite-level players on their pathway to success in Major League Soccer (MLS) and at the highest levels of the game. With a holistic, long-term approach, we strive to help young men reach their full potential both on and off the field.

Position Summary
The Pro Pathway Coach is part of a rotational coaching pool serving the Charlotte FC Academy’s U14–U18 MLS NEXT teams. Rather than being assigned to a fixed head or assistant role on a single team, coaches in this pool step into head or assistant coaching responsibilities across age groups based on organizational need and individual merit. This structure is designed to develop well-rounded coaches, maximize staff expertise across the full Academy, and create the best possible environment for player development at every level.
 
In any given role, the Pro Pathway Coach will contribute to day-to-day coaching, individual player development, and age-appropriate periodization with the overarching goal of preparing players for success at the MLS NEXT Pro and MLS levels. This position reports to the Technical Director and works collaboratively with the full Academy coaching and support staff.
 
Primary Responsibilities
Duties and responsibilities include, but are not limited to, the following. Specific responsibilities may vary based on team assignment and whether serving in a head or assistant capacity.
 
Coaching & Player Development
  • Lead or assist with the design, organization, and execution of daily training sessions in alignment with the Charlotte FC Academy curriculum and playing identity.
  • Create and maintain weekly, monthly, and seasonal periodization plans appropriate to the assigned age group.
  • Develop and monitor Individual Development Plans (IDPs) for players, with consistent formal and informal reviews conducted at the direction of the Club.
  • Provide video analysis for both team and individual players; present findings to players and staff.
  • Conduct advanced scouting and match preparation, contributing to both pre-match and post-match review processes.
 
Team & Staff Collaboration
  • Actively participate in daily and weekly planning meetings; share feedback and provide support for staff and players.
  • Attend all matches and travel with Academy teams as required, including home and away fixtures.
  • Occasional scouting responsibilities as directed.
  • Collaborate across the coaching pool to ensure consistent application of the Academy’s player development philosophy.
 
Other
  • Other duties as assigned.
 
Minimum Qualifications
  • USSF “A” or “B” coaching license (or foreign equivalent).
  • At least 3 years of coaching experience; experience within a professional youth development environment preferred.
  • Knowledge of the MLS Player Development Pathway.
  • Excellent skills in Microsoft Office, including Excel and PowerPoint.
  • Video editing and presentation capabilities.
  • Valid U.S. Driver’s License; ability to drive a team vehicle during travel as required.
  • Availability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays.
  • Willingness and ability to travel to away trips and events.
  • Must pass pre-employment screening.
  • Must be eligible to work in the United States.
 
Preferred Qualifications
  • Previous playing and/or coaching experience at the college or professional level.
  • Experience in a high-level, age-appropriate development environment.
  • Bilingual.
 
Skills for Success
  • Strong work ethic and attention to detail.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Works well within a collaborative, team-driven, and goal-based environment.
  • Works well under pressure while maintaining a positive attitude.
  • Reliable, dependable, and demonstrably a team player.
  • Must maintain complete confidentiality of privileged and/or sensitive information.
  • Must, at all times, display the character and values of the Tepper Sports & Entertainment brand.
  • Ability to prioritize workload and manage time and tasks effectively.
  • Active listener; detail-oriented and critical thinker.
  • Adaptable, open-minded, driven, and curious.
  • Demonstrates professionalism, punctuality, and ethical conduct at all times.
  • Ability to work with diverse groups and individuals.
 
Work Environment & Physical Requirements
This position operates primarily on athletic fields in variable outdoor conditions, including inclement weather. Regular travel to away matches and events is required.
  • Must be able to traverse various athletic grounds and facilities.
  • Ability to remain standing or walking for extended periods (up to 8 hours).
  • Ability to remain seated at a desk or computer for up to 8 hours.
  • Must be able to lift and carry up to 30 lbs.
  • Ability to climb stairs.
  • Must be able to work in non-traditional settings and non-traditional hours, including nights, weekends, and holidays.
 
We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

Work Environment & Physical Requirements

- Must be able to traverse various athletic grounds and facilities. - Ability to remain standing or walking for extended periods (up to 8 hours). - Ability to remain seated at a desk or computer for up to 8 hours. - Must be able to lift and carry up to 30 lbs. - Ability to climb stairs. - Must be able to work in non-traditional settings and non-traditional hours, including nights, weekends, and holidays.

job.title: Coordinator - Ticket Operations
job.html_description:
Position:                                    Ticket Operations Coordinator
Department:                             Ticket Operations
Reporting Relationship:        Reports to the Ticket Operations Manager
Status:                                         Full-Time (Exempt)
 
About Charlotte FC
Charlotte Football Club is the Major League Soccer Club in the Carolinas. The Club’s mission is to be a unifying force that is bigger than one city and encourage a sense of belonging through a progressive, collective, and ambitious approach that guides efforts on and off the pitch. CLTFC is committed to driving growth through the sport and community outreach. Charlotte FC is owned by David Tepper and is a part of Tepper Sports & Entertainment, which also includes Bank of America Stadium and the Carolina Panthers. For more information, visit CharlotteFootballClub.com.

Summary
The Ticket Operations Coordinator position is primarily responsible for assisting in the management of the Charlotte FC ticketing system and assisting with the oversight of the box office on a daily and event day basis.  The ideal candidate has proven experience in ticket operations and a strong desire to provide the very best customer service.
 
Primary Responsibilities
  • Assist in creating manifests, events, plans and price codes for all Bank of America Stadium events.
  • Provides support to the Ticket Sales Department by maintaining accurate seat inventory, accurate pricing information and ensuring that the completion of new season ticket sales is done in a timely manner.
  • Ensures that all seasons, individual event, and group tickets are paid in full and fulfilled accurately and in a timely manner.
  • Serves as lead in the recruitment of all Ticket Operations game day staff.
  • Assist with all functions of the Ticket Office including, but not limited to, ticket sales, phone calls, written correspondence and programs, etc.
  • Serve as liaison for special event promoters for the Ticket Operations Department.
  • Become familiar with the Ticketmaster system including customer service to season ticket accounts, as well as selling single game tickets.
  • Administrative duties including filing, answering phones, and providing support to the Ticketing department.
  • Other duties as assigned by the Director and/or Manager of Ticket Operations.
 
Minimum Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree
  • At least 2 years of ticket operations experience
  • Experience in building events, plans and price codes on Ticketmaster
  • Must be flexible to work evenings and/or weekends and all Charlotte FC and Stadium events, including events held on weekends, nights, and holidays
  • Must pass pre-employment screens
 
Preferred Qualifications
  • Preferred experience with Archtics, TM Host ticketing systems, TM Painter, as well as the TMone online portal.
 
Essential Functions
  • Strength in teamwork, professionalism, and time management
  • Must possess excellent oral, written, organizational and proofreading skills
  • Must, at all times, display the character and values of the Tepper Sports and Entertainment brand
  • Must be detail-oriented and must be able to multi-task while managing time successfully
  • Works well under pressure and flexible when necessary
  • Must be able to handle confidential, privileged, and/or sensitive information with carefully and with sensitivity
  • Ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams to achieve desired outcome
  • Self-starter with a hands-on approach, good instincts for prioritization and the ability to work under a high-pressure, fast-paced environment
 
Physical Requirements
  • Standing for extended periods of time
  • Sitting and working at desk on a computer
  • Walking throughout the stadium which may include stairs
  • Lift at least 30 pounds
 
Work Environment
  • This job operates mostly in an office environment and outside in a stadium facility at times which may include inclement weather conditions.

job.title: 2026 Soccer in the Community Coach
job.html_description:
The Chicago Fire Football Club (Chicago Fire FC) is an American professional soccer club that competes in Major League Soccer (MLS), the top U.S. domestic league. Founded on October 8, 1997, on the 126th anniversary of the Great Chicago Fire, the Club began play in 1998 as one of the League’s first expansion franchises. The Fire have won six major domestic titles, including the 1998 MLS Cup; the 1998, 2000, 2003, and 2006 Lamar Hunt U.S. Open Cups; as well as the 2003 MLS Supporters’ Shield.

The Club recently announced plans to build a $750M+ privately financed downtown stadium in Chicago in 2028. The stadium will anchor Chicago’s newest neighborhood, the 78, a 62-acre development and entertainment district located along the Chicago River just south of Roosevelt Road. For more information on our bold plans, visit DearChicago.com.

Additionally, the Club recently opened the state-of-the-art $100M Endeavor Health Performance Center in March 2025 that has set the standard for North American training facilities. With tremendous growth and expansion, the Club is laying the foundation for an audacious and ambitious vision that will transform the brand and the company in the coming years.

At Chicago Fire FC, we’re on a mission to entertain, serve, and unite Chicago by delivering excellence on and off the pitch to ensure a world-class experience. As a club we let our values guide everything we do: we uphold uncompromising integrity, show respect for all at all times, never settle for less, constantly innovate, and hold ourselves relentlessly accountable.

Position Overview:
Chicago Fire Football Club is seeking dedicated Seasonal Soccer Coaches to join our team in summer 2026! Seasonal Soccer Coaches will work on our summer programs to grow participation and love for our sport throughout Chicago and the surrounding suburbs. Programming will include weekly soccer camp and clinic instruction to players aged between 3-16 years. As this is the first interaction with CFFC for a lot of participants, our Seasonal Soccer Coaches will need to be passionate and enthusiastic about child and soccer development as well as growing the club’s next generation of fans.

Job Responsibilities:
  • Lead the on-field delivery of age specific summer camp programs in Chicago and the surrounding suburbs
  • Deliver age-appropriate soccer development activities/sessions following the curriculum provided
  • Provide a best-in-class camp experience to all participants and their families
  • Develop strong relationships with community partners
  • Drive participation and club affinity through exceptional program delivery and customer service
  • Attend and participate in all mandated coach education training and workshops
  • Attend Camp Celebration event’s at Soldier Field
  • Completion of other duties as assigned

Required Qualifications:
  • Minimum of USSF Grassroot License (or equivalent) required
  • Minimum of 3 years coaching (or playing experience) required
  • Candidate must have a strong work ethic and a desire to work in soccer
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills
  • Willingness to learn in a fast-paced environment
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including but not limited to evenings and weekends
  • Age 18 or over
  • Valid Drivers license and access to your own vehicle.
  • Ability to work non-traditional hours, in non-traditional settings, including all home games (or other work events) that fall on nights, weekends, and/or holidays

Compensation:
  • This role offers a weekly rate of $250-$300 for per week (1/2 day)
  • This role offers a weekly rate of $550-$600 for per week (full day)
  • This role is classified as an independent contractor

Please note:
  • Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. Our diversity initiatives start with the recruitment and selection process; therefore, we’d like to welcome all diverse candidates to apply to opportunities within our Club.
  • CFFC is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and maintaining a workplace for employees and applicants for employment that is free from discrimination. The Company does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, citizenship, age, veteran or military status, disability, sexual orientation, sexual orientation or expression, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law. This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training.
  • If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the People and Culture Department at peopleandculture@chicagofirefc.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.

MLS Privacy Policy: · By clicking apply, you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy

job.title: Accounting Manager
job.html_description:
The Chicago Fire Football Club (Chicago Fire FC) is an American professional soccer club that competes in Major League Soccer (MLS), the top U.S. domestic league. Founded on October 8, 1997, on the 126th anniversary of the Great Chicago Fire, the Club began play in 1998 as one of the League’s first expansion franchises. The Fire have won six major domestic titles, including the 1998 MLS Cup; the 1998, 2000, 2003, and 2006 Lamar Hunt U.S. Open Cups; as well as the 2003 MLS Supporters’ Shield.

The Club recently announced plans to build a $750M+ privately financed downtown stadium in Chicago in 2028. The stadium will anchor Chicago’s newest neighborhood, the 78, a 62-acre development and entertainment district located along the Chicago River just south of Roosevelt Road. For more information on our bold plans, visit DearChicago.com.

Additionally, the Club recently opened the state-of-the-art $100M Endeavor Health Performance Center in March 2025 that has set the standard for North American training facilities. With tremendous growth and expansion, the Club is laying the foundation for an audacious and ambitious vision that will transform the brand and the company in the coming years.

At Chicago Fire FC, we’re on a mission to entertain, serve, and unite Chicago by delivering excellence on and off the pitch to ensure a world-class experience. As a club we let our values guide everything we do: we uphold uncompromising integrity, show respect for all at all times, never settle for less, constantly innovate, and hold ourselves relentlessly accountable.

Position Overview:
The Accounting Manager will be a key member of the Finance and Accounting team and be responsible for the execution of the accounting functions and operations for Chicago Fire Football Holdings, LLC, its various subsidiaries and related not-for-profit entities. They will take the lead in review and close procedures for monthly and annual closes, oversee the accounts payable and accounts receivable processes and be heavily involved in annual audits and tax preparation.

This role will report directly to the Controller and be responsible for managing a Staff Accountant. The right candidate is personable, diligent, and effective at finding solutions and is an outstanding communicator.
 
Job Responsibilities:
  • Lead the month end close process by efficiently assigning and reviewing work and providing hands on preparation/review of the financial statement consolidation
  • Responsible for the scheduling and supervision of day-to-day duties of a staff accountant, as well as assessing and conducting annual performance reviews, quarterly performance check-ins and providing coaching and mentoring to accounting staff as needed
  • Ensures proper revenue and expense recognition
  • Responsible for the review and approval of adjusting and general journal entries before posting to the general and subsidiary ledger accounts
  • Responsible for the analysis of balance sheet accounts and related account reconciliations
  • Work with accounting staff and department stakeholders to manage open accruals
  • Maintain and monitor policies and procedures for accounting and finance functions to ensure accuracy, consistency, and compliance with applicable standards and regulations
  • Evaluate, identify, and improve areas of potential exposure and internal control weakness
  • Proficient in accounting and finance research; providing updates and recommendations on necessary treatment and reporting changes as rules and regulations change
  • Support the annual audit process and assist with tax preparation activities, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and timely completion
  • This role requires being on-site 4-5 days per week
  • Other duties as assigned

Qualifications:
  • Bachelor’s degree in accounting and/or finance or relevant experience 
  • 4+ years of progressive accounting experience
  • Prior managerial or staff oversight experience
  • Strong Microsoft Office skills, specific to Excel and Outlook
  • Very strong written and oral communication skills, with the ability to establish and maintain interdepartmental relationships
  • High level of initiative with ability to prioritize, multitask, and manage projects
  •  Experience with financial statement consolidations and not-for-profit accounting is preferred, but not required
  • NetSuite or similar cloud-based ERP software experience is a plus
  •  CPA license a plus

Compensation: 
  • This role offers a salary range of $80,000-$95,000
  • The final salary is determined by carefully considering the applicant's background, experience, and qualifications. Base pay may vary significantly based on geographic location, job-specific knowledge, skills, and overall experience. This role is classified as Full-time, exempt
  • This role is bonus eligible based on club wide goals 
  • Additional benefits & perks: https://www.chicagofirefc.com/careers 

Please note:
  • Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. Our diversity initiatives start with the recruitment and selection process; therefore, we’d like to welcome all diverse candidates to apply to opportunities within our Club. 
  • CFFC is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and maintaining a workplace for employees and applicants for employment that is free from discrimination.   The Company does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, citizenship, age, veteran or military status, disability, sexual orientation, sexual orientation or expression, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law. This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training.   
    • If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the Human Resources Department at hr@chicagofirefc.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.    

 MLS Privacy Policy: 
  • By clicking apply, you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.
