job.title: Financial Analyst
job.html_description:
There has never been a more exciting time to join Chicago Fire FC.
 
As one of Major League Soccer's original clubs, Chicago Fire FC is in the midst of an exciting era fueled by tremendous investment, ambition, and growth. With a new $100M state-of-the-art Endeavor Health Performance Center now open, new offices in Chicago's iconic Wrigley Building, and plans underway for a privately-funded $750M+ soccer-specific stadium set to open in 2028 in the heart of Chicago, the Club is building the future of soccer in one of the world's great sports cities.

Chicago Fire FC is becoming a cultural force in Chicago by connecting communities, shaping experiences, and creating new ways for fans to engage with the game and the city. The Club's future home, McDonald’s Park, will anchor Chicago's newest neighborhood and serve as a catalyst of growth for the next chapter of professional sports and entertainment in the city.

Joining Chicago Fire FC today means joining an organization on the rise. Our growth creates once-in-a-lifetime opportunities for ambitious professionals to generate meaningful impact and help shape the future of our Club. Whether on the pitch working match day or in the front office, every team member plays a role in building something incredibly special. 

At Chicago Fire FC, our mission is to entertain, serve, and unite Chicago through world-class experiences and a relentless pursuit of excellence. We are guided by our core values: Integrity, Respect, Innovation, Accountability, and a commitment to Never Settle.

If you're looking for a place where your work will matter, your ideas will be heard, and your career can grow alongside one of the most ambitious projects in professional sports, we'd love to hear from you.
 
Position Overview: 
Chicago Fire FC is seeking a high-performing Financial Analyst to join the Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) team. This role is designed for an analytically strong early-career professional who can contribute quickly and grow into a Senior Financial Analyst role within a short timeframe based on performance. Reporting to the FP&A leadership team, this individual will play a key role in financial reporting, forecasting, cash flow analysis, and financial modeling, helping ensure the accuracy and integrity of the Club’s financial planning processes. The Analyst will partner closely with cross-functional stakeholders to analyze performance, support budgeting efforts, and deliver actionable insights that drive business decisions. This position offers strong exposure to senior leadership and is ideal for someone looking to accelerate their career in FP&A within a dynamic, fast-paced professional sports environment.
  
Job Responsibilities:
  • Review financial results and explain key drivers of change using analysis and basic modeling
  • Work with departments to build budgets and forecasts, helping shape assumptions and identify risks and opportunities
  • Prepare and present monthly financial reporting packages for senior leadership, highlighting key trends and business insights
  • Own and enhance operating cash flow forecasting and analysis, ensuring visibility into the organization’s financial position
  • Build and maintain financial models to support business planning and strategic initiatives
  • Analyze key performance indicators (KPIs) and financial trends to provide data-driven recommendations
  • Support ongoing process improvements to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of financial reporting and forecasting
  • Assist with ad hoc analysis and special projects as needed
 
Qualifications:
Required
  • Bachelor’s degree in finance, accounting, or a related field
  • 2–3 years of experience in FP&A, financial analysis, accounting, or a related role
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including financial modeling and data analysis
  • Strong understanding of financial statements and accounting principles
  • Proven ability to analyze data and translate findings into clear, actionable insights
  • Organizational and time management skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities
  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to partner with non-finance stakeholders
  • Demonstrated ability to work independently and take ownership of key deliverables
Preferred
  • Experience in sports & entertainment, live events, or hospitality industries
  • Experience supporting forecasting and budgeting cycles
  • Familiarity with ERP systems (NetSuite a plus)
  • Experience with data visualization tools

Compensation:
  • This role offers a salary range of 68,000.00 - 75,000.00
  • Final salary is determined by carefully considering the applicant's background, experience, and qualifications. Base pay may vary significantly based on geographic location, job-specific knowledge, skills, and overall experience. This role is classified as full-time, exempt
  • This role is bonus eligible based on Club-wide goals
  • Additional benefits & perks: https://www.chicagofirefc.com/careers
 
 Please note:
  • Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. Our diversity initiatives start with the recruitment and selection process; therefore, we’d like to welcome all diverse candidates to apply to opportunities within our Club. 
  • CFFC is committed to providing equal employment opportunities and maintaining a workplace for employees and applicants for employment that is free from discrimination.   The Company does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, citizenship, age, veteran or military status, disability, sexual orientation, sexual orientation or expression, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law. This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training.  
    •  If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the People and Culture Department at peopleandculture@chicagofirefc.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.    

 MLS Privacy Policy: 
  • By clicking apply, you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.

job.title: Academy Equipment Manager
job.html_description:
There has never been a more exciting time to join Chicago Fire FC. 

As one of Major League Soccer's original clubs, Chicago Fire FC is in the midst of an exciting era fueled by tremendous investment, ambition, and growth. With a new $100M state-of-the-art Endeavor Health Performance Center now open, new offices in Chicago's iconic Wrigley Building, and plans underway for a privately-funded $750M+ soccer-specific stadium set to open in 2028 in the heart of Chicago, the Club is building the future of soccer in one of the world's great sports cities. 
 
Chicago Fire FC is becoming a cultural force in Chicago by connecting communities, shaping experiences, and creating new ways for fans to engage with the game and the city. The Club's future home, McDonald’s Park, will anchor Chicago's newest neighborhood and serve as a catalyst of growth for the next chapter of professional sports and entertainment in the city. 
 
Joining Chicago Fire FC today means joining an organization on the rise. Our growth creates once-in-a-lifetime opportunities for ambitious professionals to generate meaningful impact and help shape the future of our Club. Whether on the pitch working match day or in the front office, every team member plays a role in building something incredibly special.  
 
At Chicago Fire FC, our mission is to entertain, serve, and unite Chicago through world-class experiences and a relentless pursuit of excellence. We are guided by our core values, Communication, Get Better, Joyful Purpose, Own the Outcome, Selfless, and We Compete.
 
If you're looking for a place where your work will matter, your ideas will be heard, and your career can grow alongside one of the most ambitious projects in professional sports, we'd love to hear from you. 
 
POSITION OVERVIEW: 
The Chicago Fire Football Clubs is seeking a dedicated Academy Equipment Manager to oversee the management and maintenance of all soccer-related gear and supplies for all Academy teams. This role is crucial for ensuring that our athletes have access to safe and high-quality equipment, enhancing their training and competition experience. This role will directly report to the First Team Equipment Manager. 
 
JOB RESPONSIBILITIES: 
  • Manage and oversee all Academy equipment inventory, including uniforms, training equipment, apparel, and match-day gear. 
  • Ensure all equipment is properly maintained, organized, and readily available for all training sessions, matches, and other Academy activities. 
  • Coordinate the distribution, collection, and replacement of equipment for players and coaching staff, ensuring accurate inventory records and accountability. 
  • Assist in the setup, breakdown, transportation, and storage of equipment for training sessions, home and away matches, tournaments, and special events. 
  • Maintain detailed records of equipment usage, repairs, and maintenance schedules. 
  • Partner with coaches, team administration, and Academy leadership to evaluate equipment needs, recommend purchases, and support budget planning.  
  • Implement and enforce handling, storage, and safety protocols to ensure compliance with organizational standards and best practices. 
  • Travel with Academy teams as required to provide operational and equipment support during matches and other team events. 
  • Perform other duties, special projects, and ad hoc assignments as directed to support Academy operations, player development initiatives, and organizational objectives. 
 
REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS 
  • Minimum of 1 – 2 years of previous experience in equipment management, team operations, or athletic support, preferably in a soccer or sports environment. 
  • Strong organizational, inventory management, and time-management skills with strong attention to detail. 
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work collaboratively in a team environment. 
  • Ability to adapt in a fast-paced environment. 
  • Must be able to move and/or lift up to 50 pounds. 
  • Ability to stand and walk extended periods of time. 
  • Ability to work non-traditional hours in non-traditional settings, including all home games, traveling for away games, or other work events that fall on nights, weekends, and/or holidays. 
 
PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS 
  • Bachelor’s degree in sports management or related field. 
  • Passion for soccer and a commitment to supporting the development of young athletes. 
  • Experience in major league sports or collegiate sporting environment. 

COMPENSATION: 
  • This role offers a salary range of $55,000.00 - $60,000.00 
  • Final salary is determined by carefully considering the applicant's background, experience, and qualifications. Base pay may vary significantly based on geographic location, job-specific knowledge, skills, and overall experience. This role is classified as Full-time, exempt 
  • This role is bonus eligible based on Club-wide goals 
  • Additional benefits & perks: https://www.chicagofirefc.com/careers 
 
PLEASE NOTE: 
  • Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. Our diversity initiatives start with the recruitment and selection process; therefore, we’d like to welcome all diverse candidates to apply to opportunities within our Club.  
  • CFFC is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and maintaining a workplace for employees and applicants for employment that is free from discrimination. The Company does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, citizenship, age, veteran or military status, disability, sexual orientation, sexual orientation or expression, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law. This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training.   
  • If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the People and Culture Department at peopleandculture@chicagofirefc.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.  
MLS Privacy Policy:  
  • By clicking apply, you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.

job.title: Account Executive, Premium Hospitality | New Stadium (McDonald's Park)
job.html_description:
There has never been a more exciting time to join Chicago Fire FC.

As one of Major League Soccer's original clubs, Chicago Fire FC is in the midst of an exciting era fueled by tremendous investment, ambition, and growth. With a new $100M state-of-the-art Endeavor Health Performance Center now open, new offices in Chicago's iconic Wrigley Building, and plans underway for a privately-funded $750M+ soccer-specific stadium set to open in 2028 in the heart of Chicago, the Club is building the future of soccer in one of the world's great sports cities.

Chicago Fire FC is becoming a cultural force in Chicago by connecting communities, shaping experiences, and creating new ways for fans to engage with the game and the city. The Club's future home, McDonald’s Park, will anchor Chicago's newest neighborhood and serve as a catalyst of growth for the next chapter of professional sports and entertainment in the city.

Joining Chicago Fire FC today means joining an organization on the rise. Our growth creates once-in-a-lifetime opportunities for ambitious professionals to generate meaningful impact and help shape the future of our Club. Whether on the pitch working match day or in the front office, every team member plays a role in building something incredibly special. 

At Chicago Fire FC, our mission is to entertain, serve, and unite Chicago through world-class experiences and a relentless pursuit of excellence. We are guided by our core values: Integrity, Respect, Innovation, Accountability, and a commitment to Never Settle.

If you're looking for a place where your work will matter, your ideas will be heard, and your career can grow alongside one of the most ambitious projects in professional sports, we'd love to hear from you.

Position Overview:
Chicago Fire Football Club is seeking an Account Executive to play a key role in driving premium revenue for our new stadium, opening in 2028. This individual will be responsible for generating significant annual and contractual revenue through the sale of premium hospitality products to Chicagoland businesses, C-suite executives, and high-net-worth individuals. 
 
The role will execute a strategic outbound new business campaign across the region while also managing and growing relationships with existing clients. Supported by best-in-class training, leadership, technology, and sales tools, this position will leverage our world-class Experience Center and premium product portfolio to exceed sales targets and help shape the future of Chicago Fire FC. 

Job Responsibilities: 
Reporting to the new stadium premium leadership team, responsibilities and essential job functions include but are not limited to: 
  • Generate significant premium revenue by successfully prospecting, pitching, and closing comprehensive five, six, and seven figure agreements to current clients, C-Level executives and/or key decision makers of corporations, and high net worth individuals. 
  • Sell a full menu of premium hospitality products including suites, chairman’s club, loge boxes, field seats, and club seats via outbound calls, emails, face-to-face appointments, experience center presentations, and stadium tours.  
  • Execute and lead dynamic sales presentations, face-to-face appointments, and sales events at the Dear Chicago Experience Center.  
  • Represent Chicago Fire FC at home matches, local networking, community, and corporate events to generate awareness, excitement, and sales leads throughout Chicagoland. 
  • Work closely with the Chicago Fire FC membership and premium service team to ensure clients receive superior service and complete fulfillment of every contract detail. 
  • Consistently and effectively manage a robust sales pipeline while providing updates and forecasts to management. 
  • Utilize CRM system to consistently manage accounts, pipelines, and touchpoint activity while following sales processes diligently. 
  • Exceed daily, weekly, and annual individual sales goals and meeting targets. 
  • Clearly and effectively communicate personally and professionally within all levels of a company, both internally and externally. 
  • Completion of other duties as assigned with a team-player mindset.  
Required Qualifications:  
  • Minimum of two (2) years in sales experience; prior experience selling five, six, and seven figure premium hospitality deals in sports & entertainment is strongly preferred. 
  • History of top B2B revenue production for a sport and/or entertainment organization. 
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills, both written and oral.  
  • Exceptional organization, time management, and pipeline management skills. 
  • Skilled in setting and running sales meetings, with ability to effectively pitch and present premium products.  
  • Deep knowledge of the corporate sector and demonstrated ability to build meaningful relationships with key stakeholders in the market. 
  • Due to the nature of this role, the candidate must be willing to work non-traditional hours, weekends, and holidays. 
 
Preferred Qualifications:  
  • Bachelor's degree in business, Sports Management, or a related field. 
  • Proven track record of consistently generating premium ticketing revenue, ideally with multi-year lease products within sports and hospitality. 
  • Experience with corporate sales in the Chicagoland area. 
 
Compensation: 
  • This role offers a salary range of $55,000 - $70,000 
  • Final salary is determined by carefully considering the applicant's background, experience, and qualifications. Base pay may vary significantly based on geographic location, job-specific knowledge, skills, and overall experience. This role is classified as Full-time, exempt.
  • This role is commission and bonus eligible based on individual and club-wide goals. 
  • Additional benefits and perks: https://www.chicagofirefc.com/careers. 
 
Please note: 
  • Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. Our diversity initiatives start with the recruitment and selection process; therefore, we’d like to welcome all diverse candidates to apply to opportunities within our Club.  
  • CFFC is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and maintaining a workplace for employees and applicants for employment that is free from discrimination. The Company does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, citizenship, age, veteran or military status, disability, sexual orientation, sexual orientation or expression, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law. This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training.   
  • If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the People and Culture Department at peopleandculture@chicagofirefc.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.  
 
MLS Privacy Policy:  
  • By clicking apply, you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.

job.title: Pre-Academy Manager
job.html_description:
There has never been a more exciting time to join Chicago Fire FC. As one of Major League Soccer's original clubs, Chicago Fire FC is in the midst of an exciting era fueled by tremendous investment, ambition, and growth. With a new $100M state-of-the-art Endeavor Health Performance Center now open, new offices in Chicago's iconic Wrigley Building, and plans underway for a privately-funded $750M+ soccer-specific stadium set to open in 2028 in the heart of Chicago, the Club is building the future of soccer in one of the world's great sports cities. 

Chicago Fire FC is becoming a cultural force in Chicago by connecting communities, shaping experiences, and creating new ways for fans to engage with the game and the city. The Club's future home, McDonald’s Park, will anchor Chicago's newest neighborhood and serve as a catalyst of growth for the next chapter of professional sports and entertainment in the city. 
 
Joining Chicago Fire FC today means joining an organization on the rise. Our growth creates once-in-a-lifetime opportunities for ambitious professionals to generate meaningful impact and help shape the future of our Club. Whether on the pitch working match day or in the front office, every team member plays a role in building something incredibly special.  
 
At Chicago Fire FC, our mission is to entertain, serve, and unite Chicago through world-class experiences and a relentless pursuit of excellence. We are guided by our core values, Communication, Get Better, Joyful Purpose, Own the Outcome, Selfless, and We Compete.
 
If you're looking for a place where your work will matter, your ideas will be heard, and your career can grow alongside one of the most ambitious projects in professional sports, we'd love to hear from you. 
  
Position Overview: 
Chicago Fire FC is looking for an outstanding Pre-Academy Manager to build and lead a high-performance program for players. This is a rare opportunity to shape something from the ground up: to help define how the club identifies, develops, and prepares the next generation of Academy talent.
 
At its core, this is a coach-leader role. The person we are looking for is as comfortable on the training pitch as they are designing systems and leading people. They will bring a strong on-field presence, a sharp eye for talent, and the vision and drive to build a program that becomes a cornerstone of the club's long-term player development strategy.
 
Working closely with the Academy Director and Head of Recruitment, the Pre-Academy Manager will establish a best-with-best training environment, build a trusted pre-academy scouting network across the Chicagoland area, and create the processes and culture that give every player the best possible chance of progressing into the U13 Academy.
 
Success in this role is defined by the quality, readiness, and number of players who successfully make that transition.
 
Job Responsibilities:

Program Leadership & Strategy
  • Design, build, and lead Chicago Fire FC's Pre-Academy program.
  • Develop and implement a training curriculum that reflects the club's game model.
  • Establish a clear, well-understood player development pathway leading to U13 Academy intake.
  • Set and maintain high, consistent standards across all Pre-Academy environments and activities.
  • Continuously review and evolve the program, drawing on best practices in elite youth development.

Coaching & Player Development (Coach-Leader Role)
  • Maintain a strong, visible on-field coaching presence across training sessions.
  • Plan, deliver, and evaluate high-quality sessions that bring the club's playing philosophy to life.
  • Create and sustain a genuinely competitive, best-with-best environment that raises the standard for every player.
  • Implement and monitor Individual Development Plans (IDPs) that give each player a meaningful, personalised pathway.
  • Lead structured player reviews and deliver honest, constructive feedback to players and their families.
  • Champion a holistic, player-centered approach that keeps long-term development at the heart of every decision.

 Talent Identification & Scouting 
  • Own and lead the Pre-Academy talent identification strategy, working closely with the Academy Head of Recruitment to shape recruitment priorities.
  • Build, manage, and develop a network of local scouts and contacts, maintaining clear standards around:
  • Consistent player identification criteria
  • Regular, structured reporting and communication
  • Alignment with the club's recruitment priorities and game model
  • Be a visible presence in the Chicagoland soccer landscape, actively attending games, training sessions, and events to find the best young talent.
  • Develop and maintain a comprehensive database of high-potential players within the club's catchment area.
  • Hold scouts accountable for the quality, consistency, and impact of their identification and recommendations.
  • Organise and lead talent ID events, festivals, and recruitment initiatives that raise the club's profile and attract top players.
  • Define clear, consistent processes for player entry, progression, and exit that give families confidence and give the Academy a reliable pipeline.
  • Work closely with U13 Academy colleagues to ensure scouting outcomes directly inform intake decisions.

Staff Management & Development
  • Recruit, lead, and manage Pre-Academy coaching staff with clarity and purpose.
  • Set clear expectations around roles, responsibilities, and performance, and hold people to them.
  • Design and deliver an ongoing coach development and CPD program that raises the quality of coaching across the program.
  • Collaborate closely with the Head of Methodology to ensure consistency and coherence across all Academy age groups.
  • Build a coaching culture that is high-performing, accountable, and genuinely collaborative.

Relationships & Community Engagement
  • Invest in building strong, lasting relationships with clubs, schools, community organisations, and key figures across the Chicagoland soccer landscape.
  • Be a genuine ambassador for Chicago Fire FC, positioning the club as the destination of choice for the best young talent in the region.
  • Support and educate parents, helping them understand the Academy pathway, what to expect, and how they can best support their child's development.

Operations & Collaboration
  • Keep accurate, up-to-date records across player development, scouting, and recruitment.
  • Attend and contribute actively to Academy meetings, fixtures, and events.
  • Work collaboratively with the wider multidisciplinary team across performance, medical, analytics, and player care.
  • Ensure full alignment with MLS NEXT standards and club operational procedures.
 
Safeguarding & Player Care
  • Uphold the highest standards of player welfare, safeguarding, and well-being without exception.
  • Ensure full compliance with all club, MLS, and U.S. Soccer safeguarding policies.
  • Create and protect an environment where every player feels safe, included, and valued.
 
Required Qualifications: 
  • Minimum of 3 years of experience in youth player development or a similar role. 
  • US Soccer A license (UEFA A license equivalent) or advanced youth award.
  • Strong proven experience in talent identification and scouting leadership.
  • Must display a strong work ethic and desire to build a career in professional sports.
  • Excellent understanding of long-term athlete development principles.
  • Experience working within an MLS Academy, MLS NEXT environment, or equivalent academy setting.
  • Ability to adapt in a fast-paced environment.
  • Travel and ability to work weekends/flexible hours required.
  • Ability to work non-traditional hours, in non-traditional settings, including all home games (or other work events) that fall on nights, weekends, and/or holidays.

Preferred Qualifications:
  • An established network within the Chicagoland youth soccer landscape.
  • Experience building or leading a program or department from inception.
  • Experience designing and delivering coach education programs.
  • Familiarity with player performance tracking and data systems.

Compensation:
  • This role offers a salary range of $75,000.00 - $80,000.00.
  • Final salary is determined by carefully considering the applicant's background, experience, and qualifications. Base pay may vary significantly based on geographic location, job-specific knowledge, skills, and overall experience. This role is classified as Full-time, exempt.
  • This role is bonus eligible based on club-wide goals.
  • Additional benefits & perks: https://www.chicagofirefc.com/careers.
 
Please note:
Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. Our diversity initiatives start with the recruitment and selection process; therefore, we’d like to welcome all diverse candidates to apply to opportunities within our Club. 

CFFC is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and maintaining a workplace for employees and applicants for employment that is free from discrimination.   The Company does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, citizenship, age, veteran or military status, disability, sexual orientation, sexual orientation or expression, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law. This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training.  

If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the People and Culture Department at Peopleandculture@chicagofirefc.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.    

MLS Privacy Policy: 
  • By clicking apply, you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.

job.title: Director of CFII & Academy Team Operations
job.html_description:
There has never been a more exciting time to join Chicago Fire FC.

As one of Major League Soccer's original clubs, Chicago Fire FC is in the midst of an exciting era fueled by tremendous investment, ambition, and growth. With a new $100M state-of-the-art Endeavor Health Performance Center now open, new offices in Chicago's iconic Wrigley Building, and plans underway for a privately-funded $750M+ soccer-specific stadium set to open in 2028 in the heart of Chicago, the Club is building the future of soccer in one of the world's great sports cities.

Chicago Fire FC is becoming a cultural force in Chicago by connecting communities, shaping experiences, and creating new ways for fans to engage with the game and the city. The Club's future home, McDonald’s Park, will anchor Chicago's newest neighborhood and serve as a catalyst of growth for the next chapter of professional sports and entertainment in the city.

Joining Chicago Fire FC today means joining an organization on the rise. Our growth creates once-in-a-lifetime opportunities for ambitious professionals to generate meaningful impact and help shape the future of our Club. Whether on the pitch working match day or in the front office, every team member plays a role in building something incredibly special. 

If you're looking for a place where your work will matter, your ideas will be heard, and your career can grow alongside one of the most ambitious projects in professional sports, we'd love to hear from you.

POSITION OVERVIEW:
The Director of Fire II & Academy Team Operations is responsible for the daily team operations supporting the club's MLS NEXT Pro (MLSNP) Fire II team and Academy teams. This role ensures that players, coaches, and staff across the player pathway have everything they need to train, compete, and develop in a world-class environment. They should be able to proactively address operational issues before they arise and build scalable systems / processes across several age groups to best support Fire II & the Academy’s player development priorities.

JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:
  • Contribute to the overall vision, mission, and values of Chicago Fire FC.
  • Oversee all logistics for Fire II (MLS NEXT Pro) & Academy team operations, including (1) daily operations at the training facility, (2) home games, and (3) away games (incl. air & ground travel, hotels, meals, per diems, and itinerary management for preseason, regular season, and domestic and international tournaments).
  • Serves as the connector between Fire II & Academy departments to ensure smooth operations and that everyone has the support they need to be successful: technical staff, player wellness staff (medical, physical, nutrition, mindset), facilities staff, first team staff, etc.
  • Directly manage the Fire II & Academy team administrators, providing daily oversight, supporting their performance, and investing in their development.
  • Manage Fire II operational budget and support Academy Director in management of Academy operational budget, tracking against forecast and giving regular updates to General Manager.
  • Ensure that the Academy & Fire II team operations department’s standards, templates, and processes are in alignment with the First Team and introduce new ideas to continue to elevate and streamline while working closely with General Manager.
  • Manage external operational relationships: bus companies, airport contacts, hotels, tournament organizers, etc.
  • Support Academy Director with execution of family communication logistics
  • Support Academy Director with execution of any homestay or player assistance needs (e.g. transportation assistance)
  • Collaborate with Head of Player Wellbeing & Academy Director to ensure execution of safeguarding processes surrounding minors (e.g. SafeSport, travel policies for minors, international trip checklists)
  • Serve as primary point of contact with MLS NEXT Pro league office on operational, scheduling, and compliance matters
  • Ensure full compliance and registration with MLS NEXT, US Soccer, and any state / local regulatory needs
  • Maintain the highest levels of confidentiality and professionalism at all times
 
REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS
  • Minimum of 5 years of experience in team operations, ideally within professional MLS NEXT Pro, MLS Academy, or a comparable pro / youth pathway environment.
  • Demonstrated experience managing complex team travel logistics (domestic and, ideally, international).
  • Proven budget management experience.
  • Prior experience directly managing staff, including setting priorities, conducting performance reviews, and developing direct reports.
  • Excellent organizational, project management, and vendor negotiation skills.
  • Experience working within MLS, MLS NEXT Pro, or MLS NEXT, with familiarity with league operational and compliance standards.
  • Experience managing multiple age-group programs simultaneously.
  • Experience with team management software/platforms (e.g., Teamworks).
  • Ability to work nights, weekends, and holidays aligned with team training and competition schedules.
  • Conversational Spanish language fluency preferred.

COMPENSATION:
  • This role offers a salary of up to $70,000.00.
  • Final salary is determined by carefully considering the applicant's background, experience, and qualifications. Base pay may vary significantly based on geographic location, job-specific knowledge, skills, and overall experience. This role is classified as Full-time, exempt.
  • This role is bonus eligible based on Club-wide goals.
  • Additional benefits & perks: https://www.chicagofirefc.com/careers.
 

PLEASE NOTE:
Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. Our diversity initiatives start with the recruitment and selection process; therefore, we’d like to welcome all diverse candidates to apply to opportunities within our Club. 

CFFC is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and maintaining a workplace for employees and applicants for employment that is free from discrimination.   The Company does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, citizenship, age, veteran or military status, disability, sexual orientation, sexual orientation or expression, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law. This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training.  

If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the People and Culture Department at peopleandculture@chicagofirefc.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.    

MLS Privacy Policy: 
  • By clicking apply, you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.

job.title: Maintenance Associate, Chicago Fire Pitch
job.html_description:
There has never been a more exciting time to join Chicago Fire FC.

As one of Major League Soccer's original clubs, Chicago Fire FC is in the midst of an exciting era fueled by tremendous investment, ambition, and growth. With a new $100M state-of-the-art Endeavor Health Performance Center now open, new offices in Chicago's iconic Wrigley Building, and plans underway for a privately-funded $750M+ soccer-specific stadium set to open in 2028 in the heart of Chicago, the Club is building the future of soccer in one of the world's great sports cities.

Chicago Fire FC is becoming a cultural force in Chicago by connecting communities, shaping experiences, and creating new ways for fans to engage with the game and the city. The Club's future home, McDonald’s Park, will anchor Chicago's newest neighborhood and serve as a catalyst of growth for the next chapter of professional sports and entertainment in the city.

Joining Chicago Fire FC today means joining an organization on the rise. Our growth creates once-in-a-lifetime opportunities for ambitious professionals to generate meaningful impact and help shape the future of our Club. Whether on the pitch working match day or in the front office, every team member plays a role in building something incredibly special. 

At Chicago Fire FC, our mission is to entertain, serve, and unite Chicago through world-class experiences and a relentless pursuit of excellence. We are guided by our core values: Integrity, Respect, Innovation, Accountability, and a commitment to Never Settle.

If you're looking for a place where your work will matter, your ideas will be heard, and your career can grow alongside one of the most ambitious projects in professional sports, we'd love to hear from you.
 
Position Overview:
Chicago Fire Football Club is seeking a Maintenance Associate for Chicago Fire Pitch, the Club’s premier indoor/outdoor community soccer facility. Located on the north side of Chicago, Fire Pitch welcomes 300,000+ visitors each year, providing year-round playing opportunities for youth and adult players of all skill levels.

We aim to provide the best possible experience for our guests, and we are excited to welcome a Maintenance Associate to our team. With oversight and support from the Facility Manager, the focus for this position will be placed on delivering a safe, clean, friendly, soccer-first atmosphere on a daily basis.

 
Job Responsibilities:
The Maintenance Associate will work with the Facility Manager to execute the facility’s maintenance and operations plan. Specific duties include, but are not limited to:
  •  Oversee trash removal around the facility, maintaining a litter-free environment on the playing fields, in the parking lots, and all public-facing areas.
  •  Supplement work of vendors to ensure all aspects of the facility meet our high standard for cleanliness and safety.
  •  Perform general equipment maintenance, ensuring equipment is kept in good working order, reporting all significant mechanical issues to the Operations Manager.
  • Refill supplies throughout the facility as needed, including the restrooms, locker rooms, front desk, and office areas. Coordinate re-order needs with Facility Manager.
  •  Ensure closets, shed, off-site storage, and dumpster enclosure are kept clean and organized.
  • Assist snow removal process around seasonal dome, supporting Operations Manager and external snow removal team to ensure snow removal standards are met.
  • Perform all aspects of field maintenance, inclusive of grooming, line painting, and turf repairs.
  •  Participate in semi-annual dome conversion process, transitioning facility from outdoor-to-indoor in November, and from indoor-to-outdoor in April.
  •  Support all event operations efforts, including assistance with set-up, tear-down, and clean-up.
  •  Fulfill weekly Manager on Duty responsibilities, ensuring the facility runs in a smooth, professional, and safe manner during programming hours. MOD responsibilities require an ability to engage and interact with visitors throughout the facility.
  • Serve as Club ambassador, delivering passion for soccer and the Chicago Fire Football Club on a daily basis.
  • Other duties as assigned.

Qualifications:
  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience.
  • 2+ years’ experience conducting maintenance and operation-oriented tasks preferred, previous experience working as a janitor/handyman a plus.
  •  Basic mechanical knowledge and enthusiasm for learning new skills.
  • Strong attention to detail is a must.
  •  Proactive self-starter, with a demonstrated history of problem solving.
  • No job is too big or too small – unafraid to get hands dirty, able to wear multiple hats, and at times work outside the job description.
  • Comfortable using power tools and machinery.
  •  Good communication skills and ability to quickly build relationships.
  •  Proven ability to interact and work within a team setting.
  • Well-organized with an ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines.
  • Punctuality, reliability, and flexibility are important in this fast-paced work environment.
  •  Comfortable in customer-facing situations, providing friendly customer service to both children and adults.
  • Desire to create an inclusive and cooperative work environment.
  • Passion for the Chicago Fire Football Club, MLS, and soccer as a whole.

Requirements
  • Must be able to work flexible hours - nights, weekends, and holidays are required.
  • This job consists of manual labor. Must have physical ability to lift 50 pounds and spend long hours on feet. Frequent bending, sitting, standing, exposure to elements (cold/heat), climbing ladders, walking, and carrying items.
  • Must have driver’s license. Access to vehicle strongly preferred.
  •  Forklift Certification or willingness to gain certification within specified time.

Compensation:
  • This role offers an hourly rate range of $20/hour 
  •  This role is classified as Full-time, exempt
  • This role is bonus eligible based on individual and club-wide goals
  • Additional benefits & perks: https://www.chicagofirefc.com/careers

Please note:
  • Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. Our diversity initiatives start with the recruitment and selection process; therefore, we’d like to welcome all diverse candidates to apply to opportunities within our Club.  
  • CFFC is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and maintaining a workplace for employees and applicants for employment that is free from discrimination. The Company does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, citizenship, age, veteran or military status, disability, sexual orientation, sexual orientation or expression, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law. This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training.   
  • If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the People and Culture Department at peopleandculture@chicagofirefc.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.

There has never been a more exciting time to join Chicago Fire FC.
As one of Major League Soccer's original clubs, Chicago Fire FC is in the midst of an exciting era fueled by tremendous investment, ambition, and growth. With a new $100M state-of-the-art Endeavor Health Performance Center now open, new offices in Chicago's iconic Wrigley Building, and plans underway for a privately-funded $750M+ soccer-specific stadium set to open in 2028 in the heart of Chicago, the Club is building the future of soccer in one of the world's great sports cities.
Chicago Fire FC is becoming a cultural force in Chicago by connecting communities, shaping experiences, and creating new ways for fans to engage with the game and the city. The Club's future home, McDonald’s Park, will anchor Chicago's newest neighborhood and serve as a catalyst of growth for the next chapter of professional sports and entertainment in the city.
Joining Chicago Fire FC today means joining an organization on the rise. Our growth creates once-in-a-lifetime opportunities for ambitious professionals to generate meaningful impact and help shape the future of our Club. Whether on the pitch working match day or in the front office, every team member plays a role in building something incredibly special.
At Chicago Fire FC, our mission is to entertain, serve, and unite Chicago through world-class experiences and a relentless pursuit of excellence. We are guided by our core values: Integrity, Respect, Innovation, Accountability, and a commitment to Never Settle.
If you're looking for a place where your work will matter, your ideas will be heard, and your career can grow alongside one of the most ambitious projects in professional sports, we'd love to hear from you.
Position Overview:
Chicago Fire Football Club is seeking a Maintenance Associate for Chicago Fire Pitch, the Club’s premier indoor/outdoor community soccer facility. Located on the north side of Chicago, Fire Pitch welcomes 300,000+ visitors each year, providing year-round playing opportunities for youth and adult players of all skill levels.
We aim to provide the best possible experience for our guests, and we are excited to welcome a Maintenance Associate to our team. With oversight and support from the Facility Manager, the focus for this position will be placed on delivering a safe, clean, friendly, soccer-first atmosphere on a daily basis.
Job Responsibilities:The Maintenance Associate will work with the Facility Manager to execute the facility’s maintenance and operations plan. Specific duties include, but are not limited to:
- Oversee trash removal around the facility, maintaining a litter-free environment on the playing fields, in the parking lots, and all public-facing areas. - Supplement work of vendors to ensure all aspects of the facility meet our high standard for cleanliness and safety. - Perform general equipment maintenance, ensuring equipment is kept in good working order, reporting all significant mechanical issues to the Operations Manager. - Refill supplies throughout the facility as needed, including the restrooms, locker rooms, front desk, and office areas. Coordinate re-order needs with Facility Manager. - Ensure closets, shed, off-site storage, and dumpster enclosure are kept clean and organized. - Assist snow removal process around seasonal dome, supporting Operations Manager and external snow removal team to ensure snow removal standards are met. - Perform all aspects of field maintenance, inclusive of grooming, line painting, and turf repairs. - Participate in semi-annual dome conversion process, transitioning facility from outdoor-to-indoor in November, and from indoor-to-outdoor in April. - Support all event operations efforts, including assistance with set-up, tear-down, and clean-up. - Fulfill weekly Manager on Duty responsibilities, ensuring the facility runs in a smooth, professional, and safe manner during programming hours. MOD responsibilities require an ability to engage and interact with visitors throughout the facility. - Serve as Club ambassador, delivering passion for soccer and the Chicago Fire Football Club on a daily basis. - Other duties as assigned.
Qualifications:
- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience. - 2+ years’ experience conducting maintenance and operation-oriented tasks preferred, previous experience working as a janitor/handyman a plus. - Basic mechanical knowledge and enthusiasm for learning new skills. - Strong attention to detail is a must. - Proactive self-starter, with a demonstrated history of problem solving. - No job is too big or too small – unafraid to get hands dirty, able to wear multiple hats, and at times work outside the job description. - Comfortable using power tools and machinery. - Good communication skills and ability to quickly build relationships. - Proven ability to interact and work within a team setting. - Well-organized with an ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines. - Punctuality, reliability, and flexibility are important in this fast-paced work environment. - Comfortable in customer-facing situations, providing friendly customer service to both children and adults. - Desire to create an inclusive and cooperative work environment. - Passion for the Chicago Fire Football Club, MLS, and soccer as a whole.
Requirements
- Must be able to work flexible hours - nights, weekends, and holidays are required. - This job consists of manual labor. Must have physical ability to lift 50 pounds and spend long hours on feet. Frequent bending, sitting, standing, exposure to elements (cold/heat), climbing ladders, walking, and carrying items. - Must have driver’s license. Access to vehicle strongly preferred. - Forklift Certification or willingness to gain certification within specified time.
Compensation:
- This role offers an hourly rate range of $20/hour - This role is classified as Full-time, exempt - This role is bonus eligible based on individual and club-wide goals - Additional benefits & perks: https://www.chicagofirefc.com/careers

- Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. job.title: Director, Partnership Strategy
job.html_description:
There has never been a more exciting time to join Chicago Fire FC.

As one of Major League Soccer's original clubs, Chicago Fire FC is in the midst of an exciting era fueled by tremendous investment, ambition, and growth. With a new $100M state-of-the-art Endeavor Health Performance Center now open, new offices in Chicago's iconic Wrigley Building, and plans underway for a privately-funded $750M+ soccer-specific stadium set to open in 2028 in the heart of Chicago, the Club is building the future of soccer in one of the world's great sports cities.

Chicago Fire FC is becoming a cultural force in Chicago by connecting communities, shaping experiences, and creating new ways for fans to engage with the game and the city. The Club's future home, McDonald’s Park, will anchor Chicago's newest neighborhood and serve as a catalyst of growth for the next chapter of professional sports and entertainment in the city.

Joining Chicago Fire FC today means joining an organization on the rise. Our growth creates once-in-a-lifetime opportunities for ambitious professionals to generate meaningful impact and help shape the future of our Club. Whether on the pitch working match day or in the front office, every team member plays a role in building something incredibly special. 

At Chicago Fire FC, our mission is to entertain, serve, and unite Chicago through world-class experiences and a relentless pursuit of excellence. We are guided by our core values: Integrity, Respect, Innovation, Accountability, and a commitment to Never Settle.

If you're looking for a place where your work will matter, your ideas will be heard, and your career can grow alongside one of the most ambitious projects in professional sports, we'd love to hear from you.

Position Overview:
Chicago Fire FC is seeking an innovative, strategic, and data-driven Director of Corporate Partnership Strategy to lead the Club's partnership strategy function and help shape the future of commercial partnerships as we prepare for the opening of McDonald's Park.

Reporting to the Vice President of Partnership Marketing & Strategy, this leadership role will oversee the development of integrated partnership strategies that drive new business revenue, support partner renewals and upsells, and position Chicago Fire FC as an industry leader in sports partnerships.

The Director will lead a team of two while collaborating across the organization to develop compelling partnership narratives, create innovative partnership assets, build best-in-class sales materials, and leverage research and analytics to tell impactful commercial stories. This role will play a critical part in the commercialization of McDonald's Park by helping define new inventory, developing scalable partnership platforms, and creating innovative opportunities that maximize long-term revenue growth.

Job Responsibilities: 
Partnership Strategy & Commercial Development
  • Lead the Club's partnership strategy function, developing innovative sponsorship solutions that drive new business, renewals, and long-term revenue growth.
  • Drive the commercialization of McDonald's Park by developing new partnership assets, entitlement opportunities, and scalable sponsorship platforms.
  • Oversee the creation of compelling sales presentations, partnership proposals, and strategic storytelling that effectively communicate the value of Chicago Fire FC partnerships.
  • Partner with senior leadership to identify, package, and position premium partnership opportunities that maximize commercial value.

Asset Strategy & Insights
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to create innovative, sellable partnership assets while balancing fan experience and business objectives.
  • Leverage market research, fan insights, and partnership analytics to inform partnership strategy and strengthen sales efforts.
  • Oversee partnership inventory management, valuation recommendations, rate cards, and performance reporting to support revenue growth.

Leadership
  • Lead, mentor, and develop the Partnership Strategy team while establishing processes and best practices that drive collaboration, innovation, and operational excellence.
  • Serve as a strategic thought partner to the Corporate Partnerships leadership team, helping shape the Club's long-term commercial vision.

Required Qualifications:  
  • Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Sports Management, or a related field.
  • 7+ years of experience in corporate partnerships, sponsorship strategy, sports marketing, or a related commercial role.
  •  3+ years of leadership experience managing and developing high-performing teams.
  • Proven experience developing partnership strategies, sponsorship packages, and sales presentations that drive revenue.
  • Strong strategic thinking with the ability to translate research, data, and business objectives into compelling partnership solutions.
  • Exceptional communication, presentation, project management, and cross-functional collaboration skills.
  •  Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment and work non-traditional hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays as needed.

Preferred Qualifications:  
  • High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (especially PowerPoint, Excel, Word) and design tools for mockups and presentations (e.g., Adobe Creative Suite, Canva).
  • Experience working with data analysis platforms (e.g., Kore, Salesforce, YouGov, Blinkfire)
  • Understanding of the sports, entertainment, or event sponsorship industry

Compensation: 
  • This role offers a base salary of $100,000
  • This role is classified as Full-time, exempt
  •  This role is bonus eligible based on club wide goals · Additional benefits & perks: https://www.chicagofirefc.com/careers

Please note: 
  • Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. Our diversity initiatives start with the recruitment and selection process; therefore, we’d like to welcome all diverse candidates to apply to opportunities within our Club.  
  • CFFC is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and maintaining a workplace for employees and applicants for employment that is free from discrimination. The Company does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, citizenship, age, veteran or military status, disability, sexual orientation, sexual orientation or expression, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law. This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training.   
  • If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the People and Culture Department at peopleandculture@chicagofirefc.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.  
 
MLS Privacy Policy:  
  • By clicking apply, you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.

job.title: Creative Presentation Designer
job.html_description:
There has never been a more exciting time to join Chicago Fire FC.

As one of Major League Soccer's original clubs, Chicago Fire FC is in the midst of an exciting era fueled by tremendous investment, ambition, and growth. With a new $100M state-of-the-art Endeavor Health Performance Center now open, new offices in Chicago's iconic Wrigley Building, and plans underway for a privately-funded $750M+ soccer-specific stadium set to open in 2028 in the heart of Chicago, the Club is building the future of soccer in one of the world's great sports cities.

Chicago Fire FC is becoming a cultural force in Chicago by connecting communities, shaping experiences, and creating new ways for fans to engage with the game and the city. The Club's future home, McDonald’s Park, will anchor Chicago's newest neighborhood and serve as a catalyst of growth for the next chapter of professional sports and entertainment in the city.

Joining Chicago Fire FC today means joining an organization on the rise. Our growth creates once-in-a-lifetime opportunities for ambitious professionals to generate meaningful impact and help shape the future of our Club. Whether on the pitch working match day or in the front office, every team member plays a role in building something incredibly special. 

At Chicago Fire FC, our mission is to entertain, serve, and unite Chicago through world-class experiences and a relentless pursuit of excellence. We are guided by our core values: Integrity, Respect, Innovation, Accountability, and a commitment to Never Settle.

If you're looking for a place where your work will matter, your ideas will be heard, and your career can grow alongside one of the most ambitious projects in professional sports, we'd love to hear from you.

Position Overview:
Chicago Fire FC is seeking a creative, detail-oriented, and highly organized Creative Presentation Designer to support the Club's Corporate Partnerships team. This role will be responsible for developing compelling visual materials that bring partnership ideas to life and help drive new business, renewals, and upsell opportunities. The Creative Presentation Designer will create best-in-class sales presentations, sponsorship proposals, mockups, renderings, and branded marketing materials that effectively communicate the value of Chicago Fire FC partnerships. This role will play an important part in the commercialization of McDonald's Park, helping visualize new partnership opportunities and enhance the Club's storytelling.

Job Responsibilities: 
  •  Design high-quality sales presentations, partnership proposals, one-sheets, and marketing materials that support new business, renewals, and upsell opportunities.
  • Create compelling sponsorship mockups, renderings, signage concepts, and branded assets that help partners visualize opportunities.
  • Develop creative concepts and visual storytelling that elevate Chicago Fire FC's partnership presentations and commercial brand.
  • Translate partnership ideas into polished, client-facing materials under tight deadlines.
  • Maintain design consistency across all partnership materials while adhering to Chicago Fire FC brand standards.
  • Collaborate closely with the Partnership Strategy, Partnership Marketing, Partnership Sales, Marketing, and Creative teams to bring partnership ideas to life.
  • Support the commercialization of McDonald's Park by designing creative materials that showcase new sponsorship inventory and partnership opportunities.
  • Manage multiple design projects simultaneously while maintaining exceptional attention to detail and meeting project deadlines.
  • Develop templates, graphics, and creative resources that improve efficiency and consistency across the Corporate Partnerships department.
  • Translate research, fan insights, sponsorship analytics, and valuation data into visually compelling graphics, infographics, and presentations that strengthen partnership storytelling.
  • Stay current on design trends, emerging technologies, and creative best practices to continually enhance the Club's partnership materials.

Required Qualifications:  
  • Bachelor's degree in Graphic Design, Visual Communications, Marketing, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience.
  • 2+ years of graphic design experience, preferably in sports, entertainment, marketing, or agency environments.
  •  Exceptional proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop) and Microsoft PowerPoint.
  • Experience designing client-facing presentations and marketing materials.
  •  Strong understanding of layout, typography, branding, and visual storytelling.
  • Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced environment while meeting deadlines.
  •  Excellent communication, organization, and collaboration skills.

Preferred Qualifications:  
  • Experience creating sponsorship mockups, venue renderings, environmental graphics, or branded experiential concepts.
  • Experience with presentation and visualization tools.
  •  Knowledge of sports sponsorship, live events, or the sports and entertainment industry.
  • Motion graphics or video editing experience is a plus

Compensation: 
  •  This role offers a base salary of $65,000
  • This role is classified as Full-time, exempt
  • This role is bonus eligible based on club wide goals 
  • Additional benefits and perks: https://www.chicagofirefc.com/careers. 

Please note: 
  • Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. Our diversity initiatives start with the recruitment and selection process; therefore, we’d like to welcome all diverse candidates to apply to opportunities within our Club.  
  • CFFC is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and maintaining a workplace for employees and applicants for employment that is free from discrimination. The Company does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, citizenship, age, veteran or military status, disability, sexual orientation, sexual orientation or expression, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law. This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training.   
  • If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the People and Culture Department at peopleandculture@chicagofirefc.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.

  • By clicking apply, you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.

This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training. - If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the People and Culture Department at peopleandculture@chicagofirefc.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.
MLS Privacy Policy:
- By clicking apply, you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.

job.title: Fundraising and Events Manager
job.html_description:
This Fundraising and Events Manager role is an opportunity to help grow sustainable income that enables City in the Community to continue empowering healthier lives through football across Manchester. Working closely with supporters, colleagues and stakeholders, you will lead our fundraising strategy, deliver impactful events and create meaningful opportunities for people to support our work.

Who We Are

Established in 1986, City in the Community is Manchester City’s official charity. We support people across Manchester by empowering healthier lives through football. Our programmes place physical and mental wellbeing at their core, whilst also creating healthy futures and healthy communities. Within our collaborative Communications and Engagement team, we connect people to our purpose, driving visibility, sustainability and belonging for City in the Community. Through fundraising, events and supporter engagement, we create opportunities for people to champion our mission while helping to ensure the long-term growth and impact of the charity.

The Role

You will lead the development and delivery of City in the Community's fundraising strategy, creating sustainable income streams that support the charity's long-term ambitions. Working across a portfolio of fundraising products, supporter engagement, donor stewardship and events, you will identify opportunities to grow income while building lasting relationships with stakeholders.

Day to day, you will oversee fundraising products including the Matchday Lottery, subscription model and auctions, monitor fundraising performance against budgets and targets, and use insight to inform future planning. You will also lead the planning and delivery of fundraising events, ensuring they provide an outstanding supporter experience while generating vital income for the charity.

Alongside fundraising activity, you will work collaboratively across Manchester City and City in the Community to maximise fundraising opportunities, strengthen supporter engagement and develop relationships with fan groups, local businesses and high-net-worth individuals. You will also line manage the Fundraising and Events Officer, helping to create a collaborative, high-performing team while embedding safeguarding and organisational policies throughout all activities.

What You'll Bring

⚽ A relevant fundraising or events qualification, or equivalent professional experience.

⚽ Experience developing and delivering successful fundraising strategies, events or income-generating campaigns.

⚽ Strong relationship-building skills with experience engaging supporters, colleagues, donors or other key stakeholders.

⚽ Experience managing budgets, monitoring performance and using insight to drive continuous improvement.

⚽ Experience in using fundraising CRMs to enhance donor journeys and rentention.

⚽ Experience leading or supporting a team, with the ability to coach, motivate and develop colleagues.

⚽ Excellent communication, organisational and presentation skills, with confidence managing multiple projects and priorities.

⚽ A proactive, creative and collaborative approach, with a genuine passion for fundraising and making a positive impact within the charitable sector.

Why Choose Us

Guided by our values of being caring, inclusive, transformative and youth-led, we are committed to creating an environment where our team feel supported, valued and able to make a meaningful difference through the work they do every day.

As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.

You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.

How to Apply

If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by 2nd August 2026.

Equal Opportunities

City Football Group is committed to achieving workforce diversity and we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, national origin, race, age, sex, gender identity, disability - physical or hidden, sexual orientation, veteran status, religious belief, or any other characteristics protected by law. We strive to provide a fully inclusive and accessible recruitment process. We encourage all applicants to reach out if they require any support or accommodation to enable them to thrive throughout our recruitment process. Please contact citcjobs@cityfootball.com.

Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

job.title: Head of Player Accommodation - Academy
job.html_description:
Based in Manchester, this full-time permanent role will lead the Academy's player accommodation provision, ensuring a high-quality residential environment that supports player wellbeing, recruitment and retention. Working within a semi-residential model that includes regular evening and overnight duties, you will play a key part in creating a safe, supportive and well-run programme for young players.

 

Who We Are

 

Manchester City is a football club rooted in East Manchester, with a story that began in 1880 and continues to grow today. Built on resilience, belief and togetherness, success on the pitch is driven by the moments and memories shared with millions of Fans around the world. City is known for its bravery, its determination to the very end and its strong connection to where we come from. With Mancunian roots and a global outlook, we are shaped by our Fans and driven by a long-term vision, from developing future talent to supporting the communities we call home. Together, we are creating the next chapter, the City way.

 

Within our Academy, the Player Welfare team plays an important role in supporting young players as they develop both on and off the pitch. We work closely with colleagues across the Academy, safeguarding and education to provide a residential experience built around care, consistency and high standards.

 

The Role

 

As Head of Player Accommodation, you will lead the strategic development and day-to-day delivery of the Academy's accommodation provision across the City Football Academy and external residences. You will oversee the quality, safety and consistency of the full residential offer, making sure it supports player wellbeing, recruitment and retention while meeting the standards expected across the Club and wider regulatory environment.

 

You will take ownership of the systems, policies and day-to-day processes that keep the programme running smoothly, from staffing and cover arrangements through to compliance, welfare, health and safety and continuous improvement. This is a hands-on leadership role, so you will be visible within the residential environment, comfortable responding to issues in real time, and confident balancing operational detail with longer-term planning and service development.

 

You will also build trusted relationships with players, parents, Academy colleagues, accommodation partners and local stakeholders, helping to make sure the accommodation offer continues to reflect the changing needs of the Academy. By combining clear leadership, careful oversight and a genuine commitment to young people's wellbeing, you will help create the conditions for players to thrive both personally and in their football journey.

 

What You'll Bring

 

  • Experience managing a residential or accommodation setting and leading a team of staff
  • Proven ability to build, develop and influence relationships with a range of internal and external stakeholders
  • Clear leadership skills, with the ability to motivate, support and guide colleagues day to day
  • A strong commitment to safeguarding, welfare and creating a positive environment for young people
  • Good organisational and administrative skills, with confidence using IT systems to support operational delivery
  • A flexible, resilient and solution-focused approach, with a willingness to take on evening and overnight duties as part of a semi-residential model

 

Why Choose Us

 

Working at Manchester City means being supported to do your best, with the flexibility and encouragement to look after your wellbeing. We are committed to helping our people learn, grow and thrive in a culture built on collaboration, openness and hard work.

 

As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday, private healthcare and dental cover, and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.

 

Manchester-based employees also benefit from free on-site parking, complimentary breakfast and lunch, and access to a range of sports and leisure clubs.

 

You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including cycle to work, Give As You Earn, discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.

 

How to Apply

 

If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by Monday 20 July 2026. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.

 

Shortlisted candidates are expected to be interviewed on Friday 24 July 2026.

 

We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.

 

Equal Opportunities

 

City Football Group promotes equal opportunities in employment and we positively welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex and sexual orientation.

 

We will screen all applicants and select candidates whose skills and experience seem to meet our needs. We will carefully consider your application during the initial screening and will contact you only if you are selected for an interview. If you require reasonable adjustments or any support throughout the process, please contact hiring@cityfootball.com and we will be happy to help.

 

All CFG recruitment communications originate from @cityfootball.com email addresses. If you receive communications from Gmail, Outlook.com or other personal email services claiming to represent CFG, please verify with the hiring team.

 

Employment is subject to the provision of proof of eligibility to work in the UK.

 

Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK-based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.
