job.title: Product Designer
job.html_description:
Based in Manchester, this permanent role gives you the opportunity to shape new product ideas before they move into scaled technology delivery. Working across prototypes, journeys and early concept validation, you will help turn strategy into clear, testable and technically considered product experiences that are ready for build.

 

Who We Are

 

Founded in 2013, City Football Group is the world’s leading private owner and operator of football clubs, with teams across the globe connected by a shared belief in doing things differently. Alongside our clubs, we invest in football-related businesses and build partnerships that help the game, and the communities around it, thrive. Everything we do is guided by our purpose: empowering better lives through football. Rooted in more than a century of “City” football, we are proud of the role our clubs play locally and globally, creating positive impact on and off the pitch.

 

Within our Product team, we work to turn approved ideas into experiences that are clear, useful and ready for delivery. This role sits close to Product Managers, Technology Solutions and operational colleagues, helping us reduce ambiguity early, test assumptions quickly and make sure product concepts are well defined before scaled implementation begins.

 

The Role

 

The Product Designer plays an important part in shaping product concepts before they move into scaled technology delivery. You will help transform approved strategy into tangible journeys, prototypes and experience ideas that can be tested, refined and assessed for early technical feasibility.

 

A big part of the role is making ideas easier to understand and easier to build. You will create wireframes, prototypes and interaction models, map customer journeys, and work with user feedback, operational insight and performance data to improve concepts before they enter the build phase.

 

You will work closely with Product Managers, Technology Solutions, engineers and wider stakeholders across commercial, marketing and operational teams. By bringing clarity to requirements and helping identify what is possible, what needs to change and where development is required, you will support better collaboration and improved product readiness across the team.

 

What You’ll Bring

 Experience designing digital products, user journeys or customer experiences within product, digital or commerce environments.

 Proven ability in prototyping, journey mapping and concept validation.

 Understanding of user experience, interaction design and usability principles.

 Experience creating wireframes, prototypes and interface designs using relevant design tools.

 Ability to work collaboratively with Product Managers, engineers and technology teams to shape early requirements and assess feasibility.

 Clear and effective communication skills, with structured problem-solving ability and comfort working in ambiguity.

 

Why Choose Us

 

Working at City Football Group means being supported to do your best, with the flexibility and encouragement to look after your wellbeing. We are committed to helping our people learn, grow and thrive in a culture built on collaboration, openness and hard work.

 

As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday, private healthcare and dental cover, and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.

 

Manchester-based employees also benefit from free on-site parking, complimentary breakfast and lunch, and access to a range of sports and leisure clubs.

 

You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including cycle to work, Give As You Earn, discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.

 

How to Apply

 

If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by 19 July 2026. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.

We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.

 

Equal Opportunities

 

City Football Group promotes equal opportunities in employment and we positively welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex and sexual orientation.

 

We will screen all applicants and select candidates whose skills and experience seem to meet our needs. We will carefully consider your application during the initial screening and will contact you only if you are selected for an interview. If you require reasonable adjustments or any support throughout the process, please contact hiring@cityfootball.com and we will be happy to help.

 

All CFG recruitment communications originate from @cityfootball.com email addresses. If you receive communications from Gmail, Outlook.com or other personal email services claiming to represent CFG, please verify with the hiring team. Employment is subject to the provision of proof of eligibility to work in the UK.

 

Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK-based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

job.title: Head of Ticket Sales
job.html_description:
Based in Manchester, this role offers the chance to shape and deliver ticket sales across football matchdays, concerts and major stadium events at the Etihad Stadium and Joie Stadium. You will turn commercial plans into practical sales activity, helping to drive revenue, improve ticket utilisation and create a smooth, customer-focused experience for every audience we serve.

 

Who We Are

 

Manchester City is a football club rooted in East Manchester, with a story that began in 1880 and continues to grow today. Built on resilience, belief and togetherness, success on the pitch is driven by the moments and memories shared with millions of Fans around the world. City is known for its bravery, its determination to the very end and its strong connection to where we come from. With Mancunian roots and a global outlook, we are shaped by our Fans and driven by a long-term vision, from developing future talent to supporting the communities we call home. Together, we are creating the next chapter, the City way.

 

Within our Matchday and Major Stadium Events team, we turn big moments into well-run sales activity. We work closely with Sales Planning, Marketing, Product and Service Support to make sure demand is captured, inventory is used well and every customer journey is commercially effective, easy to navigate and aligned to the wider matchday and events experience.

 

The Role

 

The Head of Ticket Sales leads the operational delivery of ticket sales revenue across football matchdays, concerts and major stadium events. You will translate commercial strategy into a clear annual sales plan and shape activity across general admission, memberships, season tickets, group sales and ancillary products.

 

Using sales analytics, customer insight and market data, you will help improve online conversion, audience targeting, ticket utilisation and yield. The role combines commercial judgement with practical delivery, making sure sales activity stays responsive to customer demand and aligned to business priorities.

 

You will work closely with Sales Planning, Marketing, Product and Service Support colleagues, while also building productive relationships with corporate clients, education bodies, community organisations and other group sales partners. You will lead and develop dedicated ticket sales teams, creating clarity, accountability and a high standard of delivery.

 

What You’ll Bring

 Experience in ticket sales, commercial sales or revenue delivery roles within sport, entertainment, hospitality or live event environments.

 Proven ability to deliver commercial performance against revenue and attendance targets.

 Understanding of sales planning, customer conversion, demand management and ticketing operations.

 Experience using analytics, customer insight and sales data to shape commercial activity and sales strategy.

 Comfortable working across digital sales channels and e-commerce environments, with the ability to turn online customer behaviour into ticket sales opportunities.

 Clear and effective communication skills, with the ability to plan, report and manage operational priorities well.

 

Why Choose Us

 

Working at City Football Group means being supported to do your best, with the flexibility and encouragement to look after your wellbeing. We are committed to helping our people learn, grow and thrive in a culture built on collaboration, openness and hard work.

 

As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday, private healthcare and dental cover, and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.

 

Manchester-based employees also benefit from free on-site parking, complimentary breakfast and lunch, and access to a range of sports and leisure clubs.

 

You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including cycle to work, Give As You Earn, discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.

 

How to Apply

If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by 19 July 2026. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.

 

We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.

 

Equal Opportunities

 

City Football Group promotes equal opportunities in employment and we positively welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex and sexual orientation.

 

We will screen all applicants and select candidates whose skills and experience seem to meet our needs. We will carefully consider your application during the initial screening and will contact you only if you are selected for an interview. If you require reasonable adjustments or any support throughout the process, please contact hiring@cityfootball.com and we will be happy to help.

 

All CFG recruitment communications originate from @cityfootball.com email addresses. If you receive communications from Gmail, Outlook.com or other personal email services claiming to represent CFG, please verify with the hiring team. Employment is subject to the provision of proof of eligibility to work in the UK.

 

Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK-based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.
job.plain_text_description:
Based in Manchester, this role offers the chance to shape and deliver ticket sales across football matchdays, concerts and major stadium events at the Etihad Stadium and Joie Stadium. You will turn commercial plans into practical sales activity, helping to drive revenue, improve ticket utilisation and create a smooth, customer-focused experience for every audience we serve.
Who We Are
Manchester City is a football club rooted in East Manchester, with a story that began in 1880 and continues to grow today. Built on resilience, belief and togetherness, success on the pitch is driven by the moments and memories shared with millions of Fans around the world. City is known for its bravery, its determination to the very end and its strong connection to where we come from. With Mancunian roots and a global outlook, we are shaped by our Fans and driven by a long-term vision, from developing future talent to supporting the communities we call home. Together, we are creating the next chapter, the City way.
Within our Matchday and Major Stadium Events team, we turn big moments into well-run sales activity. We work closely with Sales Planning, Marketing, Product and Service Support to make sure demand is captured, inventory is used well and every customer journey is commercially effective, easy to navigate and aligned to the wider matchday and events experience.
The Role
The Head of Ticket Sales leads the operational delivery of ticket sales revenue across football matchdays, concerts and major stadium events. You will translate commercial strategy into a clear annual sales plan and shape activity across general admission, memberships, season tickets, group sales and ancillary products.
Using sales analytics, customer insight and market data, you will help improve online conversion, audience targeting, ticket utilisation and yield. The role combines commercial judgement with practical delivery, making sure sales activity stays responsive to customer demand and aligned to business priorities.
You will work closely with Sales Planning, Marketing, Product and Service Support colleagues, while also building productive relationships with corporate clients, education bodies, community organisations and other group sales partners. You will lead and develop dedicated ticket sales teams, creating clarity, accountability and a high standard of delivery.
What You’ll Bring
Experience in ticket sales, commercial sales or revenue delivery roles within sport, entertainment, hospitality or live event environments.
Proven ability to deliver commercial performance against revenue and attendance targets.
Understanding of sales planning, customer conversion, demand management and ticketing operations.
Experience using analytics, customer insight and sales data to shape commercial activity and sales strategy.
Comfortable working across digital sales channels and e-commerce environments, with the ability to turn online customer behaviour into ticket sales opportunities.
Clear and effective communication skills, with the ability to plan, report and manage operational priorities well.
Why Choose Us
Working at City Football Group means being supported to do your best, with the flexibility and encouragement to look after your wellbeing. We are committed to helping our people learn, grow and thrive in a culture built on collaboration, openness and hard work.
As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday, private healthcare and dental cover, and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.
Manchester-based employees also benefit from free on-site parking, complimentary breakfast and lunch, and access to a range of sports and leisure clubs.
You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including cycle to work, Give As You Earn, discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.
How to Apply
If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by 19 July 2026. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.
We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.
Equal Opportunities
City Football Group promotes equal opportunities in employment and we positively welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex and sexual orientation.
We will screen all applicants and select candidates whose skills and experience seem to meet our needs. We will carefully consider your application during the initial screening and will contact you only if you are selected for an interview. If you require reasonable adjustments or any support throughout the process, please contact hiring@cityfootball.com and we will be happy to help.
All CFG recruitment communications originate from @cityfootball.com email addresses. If you receive communications from Gmail, Outlook.com or other personal email services claiming to represent CFG, please verify with the hiring team. Employment is subject to the provision of proof of eligibility to work in the UK.
Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK-based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

job.title: Community Mentoring Officer - Targeted Interventions
job.html_description:
Community Mentoring Officer – Targeted Interventions

This Community Mentoring Officer – Targeted Interventions role is an opportunity to make a lasting difference to the lives of young people across Greater Manchester. Working with colleagues, partners and local agencies, you will lead targeted mentoring programmes that support young people to overcome barriers, build resilience and create positive futures.

Who We Are

Established in 1986, City in the Community is Manchester City’s official charity. We support people across Manchester by empowering healthier lives through football. Our programmes place physical and mental wellbeing at their core, whilst also creating healthy futures and healthy communities.

Within our Targeted Interventions team, we deliver mentoring programmes that support young people facing complex challenges, helping them build confidence, develop life skills and reduce the risk of involvement in criminality. Working alongside schools, local authorities, Greater Manchester Police and other key partners, we use the power of positive relationships to help young people reach their potential.

The Role

You will coordinate City in the Community's targeted mentoring programmes, including City Ready and City Mentoring, ensuring high-quality, safe and engaging provision for young people across Greater Manchester.

Day to day, you will oversee programme delivery, maintain strong relationships with schools, local authorities, Greater Manchester Police, Youth Offending Teams and other key partners, while monitoring outcomes and ensuring programmes achieve agreed KPIs.

Alongside programme coordination, you will support the development of mentors and coaches through training and CPD, contribute to budget management and help embed safeguarding and organisational policies across all delivery, ensuring every young person receives the highest standard of support.

What You'll Bring

⚽ Experience supporting young people at risk of criminality or youth violence through community-based programmes.

⚽ A strong understanding of youth work principles and the criminal justice system.

⚽ Experience working with schools, local authorities and external partners to deliver positive outcomes.

⚽ Excellent communication skills, with confidence delivering training or presentations.

⚽ GCSEs in English, Maths and Science, with strong Microsoft Office skills. A relevant degree would be an advantage.

⚽ A resilient, inclusive and adaptable approach. Experience of Premier League Charitable Fund programmes or secure settings would be beneficial.

Why Choose Us

Guided by our values of being caring, inclusive, transformative and youth-led, we are committed to creating an environment where our team feel supported, valued and able to make a meaningful difference through the work they do every day.

As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.

You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.

How to Apply

If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by 2nd August 2026.

Equal Opportunities

City Football Group is committed to achieving workforce diversity and we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, national origin, race, age, sex, gender identity, disability - physical or hidden, sexual orientation, veteran status, religious belief, or any other characteristics protected by law. We strive to provide a fully inclusive and accessible recruitment process. We encourage all applicants to reach out if they require any support or accommodation to enable them to thrive throughout our recruitment process. Please contact citcjobs@cityfootball.com.

Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

job.title: Community Mentoring Lead Coach - Inspires
job.html_description:
Community Mentoring Lead Coach – Inspires

This Community Mentoring Lead Coach – Inspires role is an opportunity to inspire young people across Greater Manchester to build confidence, resilience and brighter futures. Working alongside schools, learners and colleagues, you will lead the delivery of our City Inspires programme, supporting young people to develop the skills, behaviours and aspirations they need to succeed.

Who We Are

Established in 1986, City in the Community is Manchester City’s official charity. We support people across Manchester by empowering healthier lives through football. Our programmes place physical and mental wellbeing at their core, whilst also creating healthy futures and healthy communities.

Within our Community Mentoring team, we deliver programmes that use mentoring, education and sport to engage young people, helping them build confidence, resilience and positive life skills. Working closely with schools and education partners, we create opportunities that inspire young people to achieve their potential and explore future pathways.

The Role

You will lead the delivery of the City Inspires programme across secondary schools in Greater Manchester, supporting young people aged 11–16 to build confidence, improve behaviour and raise aspirations through engaging mentoring and education sessions.

Day to day, you will mentor participants, monitor individual progress and work closely with schools, delivery coaches and colleagues to ensure programmes achieve positive outcomes. You will also support the planning and development of the programme, monitor KPIs and maintain strong relationships with education partners.

Alongside programme delivery, you will act as a role model for delivery coaches, contribute to the continuous improvement of the programme and help embed safeguarding and organisational policies across all activities.

What You'll Bring

⚽ Experience delivering community or sports programmes for young people aged 11–16.

⚽ A Level 2 Coaching qualification in any sport. A Youth Work or Mentoring qualification would be an advantage.

⚽ Knowledge of youth work principles and community engagement programmes.

⚽ Experience working with schools and education partners to support young people.

⚽ Excellent communication, presentation and Microsoft Office skills.

⚽ A creative, resilient and collaborative approach, with a passion for inspiring young people to achieve their potential.

⚽ A full, clean UK driving licence and access to a vehicle, with the ability to travel across Greater Manchester.

Why Choose Us

Guided by our values of being caring, inclusive, transformative and youth-led, we are committed to creating an environment where our team feel supported, valued and able to make a meaningful difference through the work they do every day.

As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.

You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.

How to Apply

If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by 2nd August 2026.

Equal Opportunities

City Football Group is committed to achieving workforce diversity and we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, national origin, race, age, sex, gender identity, disability - physical or hidden, sexual orientation, veteran status, religious belief, or any other characteristics protected by law. We strive to provide a fully inclusive and accessible recruitment process. We encourage all applicants to reach out if they require any support or accommodation to enable them to thrive throughout our recruitment process. Please contact citcjobs@cityfootball.com.

Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

job.title: Hospitality Relations Executive
job.html_description:
Based in Manchester, we have two permanent full-time roles playing an important part in delivering exceptional experiences for premium hospitality customers across football fixtures, concerts and major stadium events at the Etihad Stadium. You will support the management and retention of hospitality accounts, helping to create a professional, engaging and well-coordinated experience that strengthens customer relationships and supports long-term account value.



Who We Are



Manchester City is a football club rooted in East Manchester, with a story that began in 1880 and continues to grow today. Built on resilience, belief and togetherness, success on the pitch is driven by the moments and memories shared with millions of Fans around the world. City is known for its bravery, its determination to the very end and its strong connection to where we come from. With Mancunian roots and a global outlook, we are shaped by our Fans and driven by a long-term vision, from developing future talent to supporting the communities we call home. Together, we are creating the next chapter, the City way.



Within our Matchday and Events team, we deliver premium experiences that bring supporters, partners and guests closer to the Club. Our Hospitality Relations team plays a key role in building strong customer relationships and ensuring every interaction reflects the standards and ambition of Manchester City.



The Role



You will support the delivery of high-quality account management and customer service across premium hospitality customers, helping to create engaging and memorable experiences throughout the season. Working across football fixtures, concerts and major stadium events, you will build positive relationships with customers while supporting retention and ongoing account engagement.



Alongside customer relationship management, you will contribute to the coordination of hospitality activity across matchdays and event days, supporting guest experiences, operational delivery and communication across multiple teams. You will help ensure customers receive timely information, professional support and a consistent experience across all touchpoints.



You will also support account administration and operational coordination, maintaining accurate records and working collaboratively with Premium Sales, Hospitality Operations and Service Support teams. Through your organisation, communication and customer-focused approach, you will help contribute to the continued success of Manchester City’s premium hospitality offering.



What You’ll Bring



⚽ Experience within account management, customer service, hospitality or sales support environments
⚽ Ability to build and maintain positive customer relationships
⚽ High attention to detail and ability to organise multiple priorities effectively
⚽ Clear and effective written and verbal communication skills
⚽ Comfortable using CRM systems, booking systems or operational tools
⚽ Collaborative approach within customer-facing and operational environments



Why Choose Us



Working at Manchester City means being supported to do your best, with the flexibility and encouragement to look after your wellbeing. We are committed to helping our people learn, grow and thrive in a culture built on collaboration, openness and hard work.



As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday, private healthcare and dental cover, and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.



You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including cycle to work, Give As You Earn, discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.



How to Apply



If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by 24th July 2026. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.



We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.



Equal Opportunities



City Football Group is committed to achieving workforce diversity and we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, national origin, race, age, sex, gender identity, disability - physical or hidden, sexual orientation, veteran status, religious belief, or any other characteristics protected by law. We strive to provide a fully inclusive and accessible recruitment process. We encourage all applicants to reach out if they require any support or accommodations to enable them to thrive throughout our recruitment process. Please contact Hiring@cityfootball.com.


Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

job.title: Campus Scheduling Lead
job.html_description:
Based in Manchester, this permanent full-time role leads the coordination and integration of scheduling activity across the campus, helping to ensure spaces, resources and operational activity are planned effectively across football, commercial and event environments. You will play a key role in balancing operational priorities, stakeholder requirements and campus readiness while supporting the safe and efficient delivery of day-to-day activity and major events.

Who We Are

Manchester City is a football club rooted in East Manchester, with a story that began in 1880 and continues to grow today. Built on resilience, belief and togetherness, success on the pitch is driven by the moments and memories shared with millions of Fans around the world. City is known for its bravery, its determination to the very end and its strong connection to where we come from. With Mancunian roots and a global outlook, we are shaped by our Fans and driven by a long-term vision, from developing future talent to supporting the communities we call home. Together, we are creating the next chapter, the City way.

We support the planning and coordination of activity across a busy and evolving campus environment. Scheduling plays an important role in ensuring operational readiness, effective collaboration and efficient use of space across football, commercial and community activity.

The Role

You will lead the coordination and management of campus scheduling activity, helping to ensure operational, sporting, commercial and community activity is planned effectively across the wider campus. Working closely with internal stakeholders and external partners, you will maintain integrated schedules that support operational readiness and efficient use of space, time and resources.

Alongside managing the master campus schedule, you will coordinate competing priorities, identify potential clashes and support the resolution of scheduling conflicts through agreed frameworks and escalation processes. You will work collaboratively with Operations, Events, Commercial, Safety and Facilities teams to ensure schedules are deliverable and aligned to operational requirements.

You will also contribute to continuous improvement and planning activity, supporting scenario planning, reporting and operational insight across campus usage and scheduling trends. Through your organisation, communication and collaborative approach, you will help support a safe, efficient and well-coordinated campus environment.

What You’ll Bring

⚽ Experience within scheduling, planning, operations coordination or venue management environments
⚽ Strong organisational and prioritisation skills within fast-moving operational settings
⚽ Ability to coordinate multiple stakeholders with competing priorities
⚽ High attention to detail with awareness of wider operational impacts
⚽ Comfortable working with scheduling systems, planning tools and operational data
⚽ Collaborative approach across operational and support functions

Why Choose Us

Working at Manchester City means being supported to do your best, with the flexibility and encouragement to look after your wellbeing. We are committed to helping our people learn, grow and thrive in a culture built on collaboration, openness and hard work.

As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday, private healthcare and dental cover, and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.

You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including cycle to work, Give As You Earn, discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.

How to Apply

If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by 26 July 2026. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.

We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.

Equal Opportunities

City Football Group is committed to achieving workforce diversity and we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, national origin, race, age, sex, gender identity, disability - physical or hidden, sexual orientation, veteran status, religious belief, or any other characteristics protected by law. We strive to provide a fully inclusive and accessible recruitment process. We encourage all applicants to reach out if they require any support or accommodations to enable them to thrive throughout our recruitment process. Please contact Hiring@cityfootball.com.

Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

Through your organisation, communication and collaborative approach, you will help support a safe, efficient and well-coordinated campus environment.What You’ll Bring⚽Experience within scheduling, planning, operations coordination or venue management environments⚽Strong organisational and prioritisation skills within fast-moving operational settings⚽Ability to coordinate multiple stakeholders with competing priorities⚽High attention to detail with awareness of wider operational impacts⚽Comfortable working with scheduling systems, planning tools and operational data⚽Collaborative approach across operational and support functionsWhy Choose UsWorking at Manchester City means being supported to do your best, with the flexibility and encouragement to look after your wellbeing. We are committed to helping our people learn, grow and thrive in a culture built on collaboration, openness and hard work.
As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday, private healthcare and dental cover, and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.
You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including cycle to work, Give As You Earn, discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.How to ApplyIf this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. job.title: People Partner
job.html_description:
Based in Manchester, this role plays a key part in shaping and delivering people priorities across the business. Acting as a trusted adviser to leaders, you will help turn people priorities into practical action, strengthen capability and support organisational performance through credible,  focused partnering.


 
Who We Are
 


As a People Team we work closely with the business to make sure our people strategy is impactful, joined up and aligned to business priorities. This role sits at the heart of that work, partnering with leaders, shaping people plans and helping to create an inclusive, high-performing environment where teams thrive. 


 
The Role


 
In this role you will partner with leaders within the business to deliver people plans aligned to business priorities. You will be a trusted adviser to senior leaders, building strong relationships, coaching managers and providing clear, pragmatic advice that helps people decisions translate into better business outcomes.


 
A central part of the role is turning people priorities into delivery. You will help shape and implement activity across organisational effectiveness, talent, succession, capability, engagement and workforce planning, while also supporting cyclical processes such as performance, pay review and succession planning. The role requires sound judgement, clear priorities and the ability to move confidently between longer-term thinking and day-to-day people matters.


 
You will work closely with the People Business Partnering Team and centres of expertise such as Reward, Employee Relations, EDI and Talent Acquisition to implement the People strategy.. The role also uses data, insight and continuous improvement to  spot opportunities and strengthen ways of working.


 
What You’ll Bring


•    Proven experience operating as a generalist HR or People Business Partner, owning relationships within a defined business area.
•    Experience partnering with Directors, Heads of Department or similarly senior operational leaders.
•    Experience delivering cyclical people activities such as performance management, talent reviews and pay reviews.
•    Experience coaching managers and influencing decision-making across a broad range of people matters.
•    Experience supporting organisational change and implementing people initiatives in a complex environment.
•    Experience in role modelling  equality, diversity and inclusion embedding through everyday business partnering.


 
Why Choose Us


 
Working at City Football Group means being supported to do your best, with the flexibility and encouragement to look after your wellbeing. We are committed to helping our people learn, grow and thrive in a culture built on collaboration, openness and hard work.


 
As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday, private healthcare and dental cover, and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.


 
Manchester-based employees also benefit from free on-site parking, complimentary breakfast and lunch, and access to a range of sports and leisure clubs.
 


You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including cycle to work, Give As You Earn, discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.


 
How to Apply
 


If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by 21 July 2026. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.


We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.


 
Equal Opportunities


 
City Football Group promotes equal opportunities in employment and we positively welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex and sexual orientation.


 
We will screen all applicants and select candidates whose skills and experience seem to meet our needs. We will carefully consider your application during the initial screening and will contact you only if you are selected for an interview. If you require reasonable adjustments or any support throughout the process, please contact hiring@cityfootball.com and we will be happy to help.


 
All CFG recruitment communications originate from @cityfootball.com email addresses. If you receive communications from Gmail, Outlook.com or other personal email services claiming to represent CFG, please verify with the hiring team.


 
Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK-based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

job.title: Head of Medical
job.html_description:
Based in Shenzhen this is a key leadership role at Shenzhen Peng City Football Club. A City Football Group Club. You will shape clinical decision-making, deliver effective injury management and return-to-play planning, and help make sure the medical department works seamlessly with coaching and performance colleagues.

 

Who We Are

 

Shenzhen Peng City Football Club is building a high-performance environment with player welfare, clear standards and collaborative working at its core.

 

The Club brings together medical, performance and football colleagues to support players with the care, consistency and expertise they need to perform at their best.

 

The Role

 

This role gives you responsibility for the club’s medical standards, clinical governance and day-to-day medical leadership. You will make informed decisions on injury risk, treatment pathways and return-to-play progress, while ensuring that care remains aligned with the realities of professional football and the expectations of the wider football operation.

 

You will lead the medical team through screenings, assessments, treatment planning and rehabilitation support, with particular focus on complex, high-risk and recurring injuries. Just as importantly, you will help create a clear and well-organised working environment, with accurate records, effective communication and robust processes that support player care every day.

 

Working closely with the Sporting Director, Head Coach, Head of Performance, external specialists and interpreters where needed, you will keep key stakeholders aligned on player availability and recovery progress. You will also help develop the people around you, building confidence, capability and a culture where concerns can be raised early and handled constructively.

 

What You’ll Bring

 

⚽ A degree in Physiotherapy / Physical Therapy or an equivalent qualification, together with registration in good standing with a recognised professional body.

⚽ Experience leading medical or physiotherapy provision in elite football or another high-performance sporting environment.

⚽ Experience managing complex, recurring or high-risk injuries, including informed return-to-play decision making.

⚽ Experience working across multidisciplinary teams and building effective relationships with coaches, performance staff, external specialists and interpreters.

⚽Experience working within a global, multicultural organisation, building trusted relationships and collaborating effectively with colleagues and stakeholders across different countries, cultures and business functions.

 

Why Choose Us

 

Working at Shenzhen Peng City Football Club means being part of an ambitious football environment that is part of City Football Group where high standards, collaboration and care for people matter every day. This is an opportunity to make a visible impact on player health, football performance and the development of a growing medical operation.

 

How to Apply

 

If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by 26 July 2026. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.

 

We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.

 

Equal Opportunities

 

City Football Group promotes equal opportunities in employment and we positively welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex and sexual orientation.

 

We will screen all applicants and select candidates whose skills and experience seem to meet our needs. We will carefully consider your application during the initial screening and will contact you only if you are selected for an interview. If you require reasonable adjustments or any support throughout the process, please contact hiring@cityfootball.com and we will be happy to help.

 

All CFG recruitment communications originate from @cityfootball.com email addresses. If you receive communications from Gmail, Outlook.com or other personal email services claiming to represent CFG, please verify with the hiring team.

job.title: Physical Performance Coach - Academy Rehab
job.html_description:
Based at City Football Academy in Manchester, this full-time permanent role offers the chance to support the rehabilitation and return-to-performance of Academy players. You will help create the right environment for injured players to recover well, progress safely and return to training with confidence, while contributing to the wider development of talented young footballers.

 

Who We Are

 

Manchester City is a football club rooted in East Manchester, with a story that began in 1880 and continues to grow today. Built on resilience, belief and togetherness, success on the pitch is driven by the moments and memories shared with millions of Fans around the world. City is known for its bravery, its determination to the very end and its strong connection to where we come from. With Mancunian roots and a global outlook, we are shaped by our Fans and driven by a long-term vision, from developing future talent to supporting the communities we call home. Together, we are creating the next chapter, the City way.

 

Within our Academy Performance Services team, we support the development of young players through evidence-led rehabilitation, physical development and collaborative performance support. Working closely with medical, physiotherapy and coaching colleagues, we help create an environment where players can recover well, build confidence and continue progressing across the Academy pathway.

 

The Role

 

This role gives you the opportunity to support the rehabilitation and return-to-performance of Academy players. You will help deliver tailored programmes that respond to individual needs, enabling injured players to progress safely, rebuild physical qualities and return to training with confidence.

 

Working across monitoring, testing and player management, you will use objective data and day-to-day observation to inform loading, progression and programme adjustments. You will also help maintain accurate records and support consistent rehabilitation processes that reflect Academy physical performance principles.

 

Alongside medical, physiotherapy, coaching and physical performance colleagues, you will contribute to a joined-up interdisciplinary environment where player welfare, development and long-term outcomes remain central. You will also help model Academy standards and behaviours, supporting a culture of professionalism, accountability and continuous improvement.

 

What You’ll Bring

 

⚽ Experience delivering physical performance, rehabilitation or strength and conditioning support within youth sport or academy environments.
⚽ Understanding of adolescent athlete development, including growth, maturation and the physical factors that can influence injury risk and performance.
⚽ Ability to plan and deliver individualised rehabilitation and return-to-performance support for young players.
⚽ Experience using monitoring, testing, profiling or player management data to inform decisions about progression and loading.
⚽ Clear and effective communication skills, with the ability to build positive relationships across multidisciplinary teams.
⚽ A relevant degree, with UKSCA accreditation or work towards accreditation, plus FA safeguarding and emergency aid certificates

 

Why Choose Us

 

Working at Manchester City means being supported to do your best, with the flexibility and encouragement to look after your wellbeing. We are committed to helping our people learn, grow and thrive in a culture built on collaboration, openness and hard work.

 

As part of our team, you will receive 26 days’ annual leave plus a day off for your birthday, private healthcare and dental cover, and an annual discretionary bonus. You will also have access to a Health Cash Plan, Employee Assistance Programme with 24/7 GP support, and a company pension and life assurance.

 

Manchester-based employees also benefit from free on-site parking, complimentary breakfast and lunch, and access to a range of sports and leisure clubs.

 

You can enjoy a range of additional benefits, including cycle to work, Give As You Earn, discounted match tickets, savings in the City Store, a free City+ subscription, stadium tour discounts, and wider retail and lifestyle offers.

 

How to Apply

 

If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by 29 July 2026. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.

 

We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.

 

Equal Opportunities

 

City Football Group promotes equal opportunities in employment and we positively welcome applications from all candidates regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex and sexual orientation.

 

We will screen all applicants and select candidates whose skills and experience seem to meet our needs. We will carefully consider your application during the initial screening and will contact you only if you are selected for an interview. If you require reasonable adjustments or any support throughout the process, please contact hiring@cityfootball.com and we will be happy to help.

 

All CFG recruitment communications originate from @cityfootball.com email addresses. If you receive communications from Gmail, Outlook.com or other personal email services claiming to represent CFG, please verify with the hiring team.

 

Employment is subject to the provision of proof of eligibility to work in the UK.

 

Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK-based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

job.title: Casual Catering Assistant
job.html_description:
Based at Manchester City Academy, the casual Catering Assistant role plays an important part in delivering a professional and welcoming catering service for Academy staff and visitors. You will help keep our food service areas clean, well-presented and ready for use, while supporting high standards of service in a busy performance environment.

 

Who We Are

 

Manchester City is a football club rooted in East Manchester, with a story that began in 1880 and continues to grow today. Built on resilience, belief and togetherness, success on the pitch is driven by the moments and memories shared with millions of Fans around the world. City is known for its bravery, its determination to the very end and its strong connection to where we come from. With Mancunian roots and a global outlook, we are shaped by our Fans and driven by a long-term vision, from developing future talent to supporting the communities we call home. Together, we are creating the next chapter, the City way.

 

Within Manchester City Academy, our catering team helps create a positive and reliable experience for players, staff and visitors every day. We work in a fast-paced environment where service, presentation and attention to detail all matter, and where everyone plays a part in supporting the wider Academy experience.

 

The Role

 

As Catering Assistant (Front of House), you will help prepare the refectory and Parents Lounge for breakfast, lunch and dinner service where required, making sure each area is ready to the expected standard. You will support the smooth running of the front-of-house operation and help create a welcoming environment for everyone who uses the Academy facilities.

 

You will provide efficient and friendly service to customers, helping to meet their needs and ensuring food and drinks are served accurately and with care. The role also includes supporting first-class service at internal and external events, where consistency, presentation and professionalism are especially important.

 

You will also help maintain health and safety standards, with particular attention to food safety and cleanliness. Working closely with the wider catering team, you will contribute to an organised, dependable service that reflects the high standards of Manchester City Academy.

 

What You’ll Bring

 

⚽ Proven experience in a catering or hospitality environment.

⚽ Excellent communication skills and strong customer care skills.

⚽ Ability to work under your own initiative and as part of a team.

⚽ A professional, friendly and efficient approach to service.

⚽ Good attention to detail when preparing, presenting and maintaining food service areas.

⚽ A good understanding of best practice when handling or storing food, with relevant food safety knowledge or certificates desirable.

 

How to Apply

 

If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by Sunday 2nd August. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.

 

We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.

 

Equal Opportunities

 

City Football Group is committed to achieving workforce diversity and we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, national origin, race, age, sex, gender identity, disability - physical or hidden, sexual orientation, veteran status, religious belief, or any other characteristics protected by law. We strive to provide a fully inclusive and accessible recruitment process. We encourage all applicants to reach out if they require any support or accommodations to enable them to thrive throughout our recruitment process. Please contact Hiring@cityfootball.com.

 

Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

⚽ Good attention to detail when preparing, presenting and maintaining food service areas.
⚽ A good understanding of best practice when handling or storing food, with relevant food safety knowledge or certificates desirable.
How to Apply
If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by Sunday 2nd August. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.
We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.
Equal Opportunities
City Football Group is committed to achieving workforce diversity and we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, national origin, race, age, sex, gender identity, disability - physical or hidden, sexual orientation, veteran status, religious belief, or any other characteristics protected by law. We strive to provide a fully inclusive and accessible recruitment process. We encourage all applicants to reach out if they require any support or accommodations to enable them to thrive throughout our recruitment process. Please contact Hiring@cityfootball.com.
Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

job.title: Casual Chef
job.html_description:
Based in Manchester, this casual Chef de Partie role gives you the chance to support the day-to-day delivery of high-quality food for Manchester City Academy. You will help prepare meals that support player health, performance and recovery, while working in a professional kitchen environment that values consistency, teamwork and excellent service.

 

Who We Are

 

Manchester City is a football club rooted in East Manchester, with a story that began in 1880 and continues to grow today. Built on resilience, belief and togetherness, success on the pitch is driven by the moments and memories shared with millions of Fans around the world. City is known for its bravery, its determination to the very end and its strong connection to where we come from. With Mancunian roots and a global outlook, we are shaped by our Fans and driven by a long-term vision, from developing future talent to supporting the communities we call home. Together, we are creating the next chapter, the City way.

 

Within Manchester City Academy, our catering team plays an important part in supporting performance across the environment. We deliver nutritious, well-presented food in a fast-paced setting, working closely with colleagues to make sure players and staff receive a reliable and high-quality service every day.

 

The Role

 

As Chef de Partie, you will play a hands-on role in the preparation and presentation of food that supports the nutritional needs of the Academy environment. Working to agreed menus and standards, you will help deliver meals that are consistent, appealing and aligned to performance goals.

 

You will support menu development, kitchen organisation and day-to-day service, while helping to maintain high standards of hygiene, food safety and stock control. The role also includes completing relevant paperwork, supporting allergen logging and contributing to safe and efficient kitchen operations.

 

This is a role for someone who enjoys working collaboratively, takes pride in quality and is comfortable adapting to the needs of a busy sporting environment. You will work closely with the Head Chef, Nutritionist and wider team to help create a service that is dependable, professional and focused on the needs of the people we support.

 

What You’ll Bring

 

⚽ Experience in a hospitality or food service environment, ideally in a setting where quality and consistency matter.
⚽ Excellent communication and customer service skills, with a calm and professional approach.
⚽ Ability to prepare and present food to a high standard, including working to nutritional requirements and agreed menus.
⚽ Knowledge of food safety, hygiene and HACCP practices, with a relevant Level 3 qualification or willingness to work to that standard.
⚽ Experience supporting stock control, ordering and kitchen organisation, with attention to waste reduction and cost awareness.
⚽ Comfortable working both independently and as part of a team, with the flexibility to respond positively to changing needs.

 

How to Apply

 

If this role sounds like it is for you, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your application via our careers site by Sunday 2nd August. We encourage you to apply as soon as you can.

 

We work with a select group of agency partners and do not accept unsolicited applications. Any CVs submitted without prior agreement will be treated as a direct application.

 

Equal Opportunities

 

City Football Group is committed to achieving workforce diversity and we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, national origin, race, age, sex, gender identity, disability - physical or hidden, sexual orientation, veteran status, religious belief, or any other characteristics protected by law. We strive to provide a fully inclusive and accessible recruitment process. We encourage all applicants to reach out if they require any support or accommodations to enable them to thrive throughout our recruitment process. Please contact Hiring@cityfootball.com.

 

Due to our safeguarding promise, all UK based CFG roles will be subject to a DBS check upon commencing employment with us. This will be either a basic, standard or enhanced check depending on the job role and responsibilities.

job.title: 2025 Marketing Matchday Activation Intern
job.html_description:
Columbus Crew Job Opportunity



Description

Do you have a passion for marketing, promotions, and fan development? The Columbus Crew is looking for outgoing and engaging college students to serve as Marketing Matchday Activation interns for the 2025 MLS season. This position provides a variety of sports-marketing related learning experiences, specifically in event production and fan experience.

 

Opportunities & Responsibilities
  • Execute matchday activations including coordinating with on-site vendors, placing banners, setting up tents, tables, and running supporter engagement stations
  • Demonstrate and maintain Crew brand standards while serving as an ambassador of the brand
  • Assist in executing in-match experiences, halftime contests and pre/post-match activations
  • Provide a fun, entertaining and positive experience by interacting and engaging with Lower.com Field guests
  • Promote the Crew by creating a positive and exciting atmosphere while interacting with guests, sponsors, and supporters
  • Assist guests with various Matchday elements
  • Assist with additional Crew grassroots and marketing events as needed

 

Required skills, experience & abilities
  • Available for the entire 2025 MLS Season
  • Max of 20 hours per match week paid hourly
  • Must be a minimum of 18 years of age and an active student or recent graduate of a college or university program
  • Ability to attend ALL regular and post-season Crew home matches, including evening and weekend assignments
  • Must be able to lift and maneuver a minimum of 50 pounds
  • Must be able to climb ladders, carry, walk, sit, push, pull and move continuously during work hours
  • Must be able to work outdoors in varying weather conditions
  • Ability to communicate effectively with a diverse population, including strong verbal communication skills. Customer service experience a plus
  • Able to work independently and with a team
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment
  • Ability to work a flexible schedule including evenings, weekends, and holidays

job.title: Matchday Ticket Operations - Seasonal
job.html_description:
Overview
Sell tickets and troubleshoot ticket issues using Archtics ticketing software. Support ticket scanning and entry at both the ticket office and stadium gates. Must display the highest level of courtesy, professionalism, integrity and enthusiasm in servicing all customers at the ticket office. Must be able to communicate clearly and persuasively in positive and negative situations and remain professional with all guests of the Columbus Crew and our venues. 

Please note that this is a seasonal position which only requires working Columbus Crew home matches and other potential concerts, events and non-event days.
 
Minimum Qualifications: 
  • High school diploma
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills
  • Proven organization skills
  • Computer literate
  • Strong customer service skills preferred
  •  Ticketmaster and Archtics experience a plus, but not necessary
  • Must possess a willingness to learn and adapt to change in a fast-paced, professional setting

job.title: Academy Performance Coach
job.html_description:
Overview

Columbus Crew is seeking a Performance Coach to work with our youth academy. Within this role the individual will be directly responsible for planning and implementation of fitness and strength programming for the u15 and u16 academy teams. Furthermore, this role will require an individual who is interested and enthusiastic about overall player development. The individual needs to value strong working relationships and enjoy working with multiple stakeholders. In performing the underlined duties, the Academy Performance Coach will play a key supporting role in the successful implementation of the Academy's mission and One Club mantra. The candidate will also have a vital role in the development, and monitoring, of professional soccer players within Columbus Crew.

 

Responsibilities:

 

  • Ensure unified approach and One Club philosophy
  • Deliver group and individual warmups under the direction of Manager of Performance
  • Deliver group and individual gym sessions under the direction of the Manager of Performance
  • Collate and organize GPS data to inform decision making processes.
  • Communicate with technical staff about daily & weekly loading as part of the larger periodization framework.
  • Plan and deliver player rehabilitation process in collaboration with other key Medical and Performance staff with a particular focus on end stage RTP
  • Perform player testing, data collection/organization and analysis
  • Assist in the monitoring and contribution of player Individual Physical Developmental Plans
  • Other related tasks as assigned by the Manager of Performance and Academy Director
  • Comply with all club and league medical policies, protocol, and Game Operations Manual
 

Requirements

  • Bachelor's Degree in Exercise Science, or related fields, required
  • Strength and Conditioning certification (CSCS or equivalent), required
  • Minimum of 2 years working experience at Academy or equivalent level
  • Performance and Sports Science certification (CPSS), preferred
  • Experience in using GPS, VALD and other relevant systems, essential
  • Postgraduate degree in Exercise Science, or related fields, preferred
  • Strong organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to work in a high performing environment
  • Available to travel when necessary and work evenings, weekends and holidays with some irregular hours
  • High proficiency in Microsoft Office products, including PowerPoint, Word and Excel
 

Demands & Unique Criteria

  • Enthusiasm for continued learning is extremely important
  • Ability to think quickly and act with diligence and speed while utilizing an attention to detail
  • Understanding of the nuances surrounding Long-term Athletic Development (LTAD) and youth age groups
  • Ability to collaborate in a team environment
  • Strong communication skills and the ability to actively listen
  • Detail-oriented and critical thinking

job.title: Intern, Ticket Sales & Service - Fall
job.html_description:
Position Summary: 
The Columbus Crew is seeking college students who want the opportunity to develop valuable skills and gain firsthand experience in the professional sports industry as a Client Services/Ticket Sales Intern. Participants will be supervised by the Director Client Services and Manager of Season Ticket Sales for the Columbus Crew. The intern will learn all aspects of the coordination and administration of Ticket Sales, Fan Service, Ticket Operations, Grassroots Event Operations and Game Day Operations. This internship is designed for college students who require an internship to receive college credit. The summer internship will run from August-November.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities: 
  • Assisting with daily correspondence to Columbus Crew guests and clients
  • Delivering superior customer service and exceeding fan expectations
  • Researching customer retention strategies
  • Administrative office duties including filing, organizing, data entry and mailing
  • Assisting in planning and implementing special marketing events and promotions
  • Working closely with sales staff to fulfill orders
  • Assisting with ticket sales activities and events, including receptions and game day entertainment
  • Contribute to brainstorming; planning and implementation of ticket sales plans; providing ideas and proposals

Required Skills, Experience & Abilities: 
  • Background in sales, marketing, business, customer service or communications is encouraged
  • Strong communication skills; both verbal and written
  • Detail oriented and enthusiastic about working within a sports-related environment
  • Creative and outgoing self-starter
  • Must receive college credit for an internship with Columbus Crew

job.title: Director, Corporate Partnership Activation
job.html_description:
Overview

The Director, Corporate Partnership Activation, is responsible for overseeing the management of all the Club's corporate partnerships, as well as achieving the revenue goal tied to the retention/renewal and growth of all partnerships. The Director will oversee all strategy for setting each partnership's goals and KPIs, activation plan for the year, communication plan, measurement plan, and will work with each individual account leader to stay accountable to this strategy. The Crew have a highly engaged portfolio of partners, with a strong desire to activate outside of the venue and use the venue in creative ways. This position will lead those innovative efforts.

The Director will also work very closely with the partnership sales team throughout the new business process, helping to identify opportunities through best practices, growth/change status of categories, and identifying an account executive who will assist in the partnership development from the early stages. This individual must be very sales minded, as this position is a combination of both marketing and sales.

 

Essential Duties & Responsibilities
  • Management of all Corporate Partnership account executives, including Managers and Coordinators 
  • Thorough knowledge of all corporate partnerships, including contract details, opportunities for growth and advancement, areas of improvement and category changes
  • Develop relationships within each corporate partnership, and establish his/herself as a leader and decision maker within the company/department
  • Assist with all renewal and upsell negotiations 
  • Oversee the general planning and execution of all partnership activation plans, taking a leadership role with cross-departmental collaboration 
  • Oversee and further develop partnership communications, including regular outreach calls/meetings, recap meetings and presentations, also identifying the right opportunities to engage executive leadership with accounts
  • Further the renewal and partner growth efforts by identifying opportunities, with the staff, to present to partners, and encouraging the staff to think months ahead 
  • Set aggressive timelines and revenue goals with the staff to stay progressive
  • Work closely on a high level with other departments, most importantly Marketing & Business Intelligence, to understand their goals and plans, to drive collaboration, and provide optimal opportunities to share with prospective and current partners
  • Represent the Team at various networking events and participate in other sales and community events, as defined by the Team
  • Oversee the department's expenditure portion of the budget, and collaborate with the Vice President of Corporate Partnerships in setting revenue goals each year
  • Develop and oversee all partner networking and entertainment opportunities
  • Provide guidance, motivation, and training (where applicable) to staff, with a focus on helping them grow into their next role within or outside of the organization
 

Required Experience & Skills
  • 4-year college degree
  • Minimum 5-7 years of sales and activation and/or agency experience (sports experience preferred)
  • Minimum 1-3 years of staff management experience 
  • Strong written and oral communication skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft programs with expertise in PowerPoint 
  • Proficiency in CRM and best practices in account management tools 
  • Ability to think creatively and "outside of the box" as it pertains to developing marketing and activation campaigns for small, mid and large business partners
  • Ability to work weekends, nights and holidays as dictated by events or requirements

job.title: Event Operations Assistant - Seasonal
job.html_description:
Description 

Crew Events is seeking Assistants for Event Operations, that will play an integral part in the planning, organization, and execution of soccer and non-soccer events, hosted at Historic Crew Stadium, ScottsMiracle-Gro Field, and the OhioHealth Performance Center.


Essential Duties and Responsibilities 

  • Oversee the Credential Management process on event days:
    • Assist with printing, organizing and laminating the credentials for Match Operations.
    • Read and interpret event orders, task lists, and venue software to effectively approve, print and archive credentials
    • Distribute credentials through our Will Call Office using exemplary organization and communication.
  • Organize and manage a variety of events, both soccer and non-soccer, at our three venues:
    • Learn and utilize SharePoint, calendar management systems and other planning software to plan all events.
    • Create and distribute the documents used to drive all events including run of show, event briefs and budgeting sheets.
    • Manage smaller scale events that range from Soccer matches to corporate meetings.
  • Encouraged to prioritize and be available for 1st team Crew matches and MLS NextPro as needed
  • Work in tandem with all branches of the operations team, along with other departments in the organization
  • Effectively communicate with guests to create positive and memorable experiences
  • Oversee the execution of small scale events as the event lead.
  • Represent the Crew and MLS in all interactions
  • Other duties/tasks as assigned
 

Required Skills and Experience 

  • Must be 18 years of age or older.
  • Experience coordinating events in a stadium or arena
  • Detailed oriented and highly organized
  • Ability to troubleshoot and problem solve in high-stress, live event situations
  • Exceptional interpersonal, leadership and communication skills
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, holidays and non-traditional hours.
