Coaches will deliver engaging, age-appropriate sessions aligned to the Club’s coaching philosophy, creating an enjoyable, challenging and safe environment that supports every player's long-term development
Responsibilities:
- Plan, prepare, deliver and evaluate high-quality coaching sessions aligned to the Club’s coaching philosophy. - Support the delivery of the Pre-Academy games programme, including fixtures, festivals and tournaments. - Attend evening training sessions and weekend fixtures as required. - Contribute to Individual Development Plans (IDPs) and player review meetings. - Support the technical, tactical, physical, psychological and social development of young players. - Implement Club coaching standards and game principles consistently. - Build positive relationships with players, parents and colleagues. - Complete all mandatory CPD, safeguarding and compliance requirements. - Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager. - Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.
Experience, Skills and Qualifications:
Essential
- UEFA B Licence (or working towards). - FA Safeguarding Children qualification. - FA Emergency First Aid qualification. - Experience coaching within a Pre-Academy, Foundation Phase or grassroots environment. - Strong understanding of how young players (ages 6–8) learn and develop. - Excellent communication and organisational skills. - Reliable, enthusiastic and professional approach. - Full UK driving licence and access to transport (desirable for travel between
Desirable
- FA Advanced Youth Award (5–11). - Experience working within a Professional Game Academy. - Knowledge of small-sided games and Foundation Phase player development. - Existing understanding of the South London grassroots football landscape.
Our commitment to Equality
At Crystal Palace Football Club, Equality, Diversity & Inclusion is spirited and continues to grow due to the working environment, which makes all staff feel appreciated and welcomed. Crystal Palace Football Club do this by encouraging good relations and practices towards different groups and are committed to promoting employment opportunities that celebrate diversity.
We are committed to attracting, recruiting, and retaining the most talented candidates and the club intends to ensure that everyone who wishes to engage with the club, whether as matchday fans, staff, players, board members, participants in Foundation programmes and any other person engaged with the club's activities, has a real and equal opportunity to do so.
Reasonable Adjustment
We are proud to be a Disability Confident Employer. We guarantee an interview to all disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria for the role, ensuring equitable opportunities for everyone.
If you require disability-related adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact DLO@cpfc.co.uk (DLO inbox is for disability-related queries only, if you wish to gain further information regarding this vacancy, please contact cpfc.recruitment@cpfc.co.uk). We are here to help you through the whole recruitment process including trying to apply for a vacancy, getting in touch with us through the process, and/or helping with access in case you are required to come in for an interview.
Safeguarding
Crystal Palace Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and vulnerable people and expects all staff to share this commitment. As such, the post holder will be required to undergo any relevant security checks, including a comprehensive background check, Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) to ensure the safety and protection of children and vulnerable individuals.

job.title: Part-time Security Operator
job.html_description:
Job Summary
D.C. Soccer, LLC is seeking Security Operators (SOs) to provide part-time security coverage at Audi Field during special events. Responsibilities include ensuring high-quality security services to protect individuals and property and communicating effectively with security and operational teams.

Compensation
$22/hr

Responsibilities
  • Provide excellent security services at the venue.
  • Communicate effectively and efficiently with security and surveillance teams using radio and phone communications.
  • Monitor and operate facility cameras and computer systems.
  • Report safety concerns and security breaches.
  • Handle security issues and emergency situations appropriately.
  • Participate in security/safety training programs.
  • Perform regular patrols in order to maintain a safe, orderly, and secure venue.
  • Authorize entrance of vehicles or people on the property, when prior notice has been given.
  • Remove trespassers from premises and secure all exits, doors, and windows
  • Provide quality customer service to guests and employees
  • Write reports of daily activities and incidents
  • Act as a dispatcher in relaying information and emergencies
  • Work weekends, nights, holidays, and/or extended shifts.
  • Maintain knowledge of applicable DC SPO (Special Police Officer) and SO (Security Officer) laws and policies.
 
Qualifications & Requirements
  •  1+ year event security experience.
  •  Must be age 21 or older.
  •  Must have no felony convictions.
  •  Possess effective written and oral communication skills.
  •  Neat and professional appearance.
  •  Ability to be trained and certified in First Aid/CPR.
  •  Ability to handle crisis situations calmly.
  •  Intermediate knowledge of computer technology.
  •  Proficient in English communication, both written and oral.
  •  Must successfully complete background check and drug/alcohol test.
  •  Must maintain required SPO licensing and training standards.
     
    Physical/Environmental Requirements
    The job involves working in a high activity outdoor sports venue, sometimes at heights, and in varying weather conditions. Physical tasks include standing for long periods, walking long distances, climbing stairs, lifting/moving up to 45 pounds, and kneeling or stooping.
     
     D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.
     
     D.C. United will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform critical job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.
     
     D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE). D.C. United reaffirms its commitment to the principles of equal opportunity and diversity. D.C. United is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. D.C. United is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, pregnancy, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with the requirements of all federal, state, and local laws. All employment decisions are made on the basis of individual skills, knowledge, abilities, job performance, and other appropriate qualifications.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Event Operations Staff - Part-Time
job.html_description:
Position Summary
D.C. United is seeking hard-working, committed staff to assist with events in this part-time role for the 2026 season. The Event Operations staff are responsible for working soccer matches (MLS, NWSL, USL, International Friendlies, etc.), other sporting events (UFL, Congressional Sporting Events, etc.), as well as concerts, festivals, and corporate events hosted at Audi Field. This staff will work closely with the D.C. United Operations staff to fulfill these responsibilities and represent the club professionally on event days. This position is a part-time, event-based role and does not qualify for college credit.

Compensation
$19/hr

 Position Responsibilities
  • Set-up/breakdown for all full-stadium events at Audi Field.
  • Preparing stadium areas, including the field, gates, locker rooms, concourse, hospitality spaces, and other activation spaces on match days. 
  • Operational oversight of Fan Experience Programs and participants in D.C. United match day. 
  • Coordinate with full-time staff members to ensure successful event set-up and execution.
  • Adhere to a strict event timeline and tasks for preparing the stadium.
  • Assist with the coordination of various space changeovers between events as needed.
  • Provide operational and logistical support for D.C. United professional staff during events. 
 
Skills
  • Candidates should be very comfortable performing physical labor activities.
  • Ability to multitask, problem-solve, and be goal-oriented in a fast-paced environment.
  • Proactive work ethic, team mindset, and professional attitude are essential.
  • Strong interpersonal skills and confidence interacting with fans and other staff members.
  • Previous experience in sports or events is ideal, and a leadership background is a plus.
 
Position Requirements
  • Must be available to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays.
  • Ability to lift 40+lbs without strain and comfortable walking for long durations.
  • Comfortable working outside in all weather conditions.
  • Commitment to work at least 80% of Full Stadium Audi Field Events (D.C. United, Washington Spirit, UFL, USL, etc.)
  • Must be available to attend mandatory training session(s).
  • Selected candidates must pass a background check. 

D.C. United will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform critical job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.
   
D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE). D.C. United reaffirms its commitment to the principles of equal opportunity and diversity. D.C. United is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. D.C. United is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, pregnancy, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with the requirements of all federal, state, and local laws. Employment decisions can include hiring, termination, compensation, benefits, promotion, training selection, or other statuses or conditions of employment. All employment decisions are made on the basis of individual skills, knowledge, abilities, job performance, and other appropriate qualifications.

 We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Academy Administrator
job.html_description:
Position Summary

The D.C. United Academy Administrator manages the daily administrative and operational functions of the Club’s Academy in Major League Soccer. This role supports coaches, players, families, and technical staff by coordinating schedules, compliance, travel, communication, and league operations to ensure the academy runs efficiently and professionally.

Compensation
$45,000 - $50,000

Key Responsibilities

Academy Operations
  • Coordinate daily academy schedules, training sessions, matches, and events
  • Manage player registration, roster updates, and league documentation
  • Maintain accurate player records, medical forms, and eligibility information
  • Organize travel arrangements including flights, hotels, meals, and transportation
  • Coordinate facility usage, equipment distribution, and operational logistics
League & Compliance Administration
  • Ensure compliance with MLS NEXT rules, club policies, and federation requirements
  • Submit league reports, match documentation, and player eligibility forms
  • Track player participation, attendance, and disciplinary records
  • Assist with safeguarding, risk management, and safety protocols
Communication & Support
  • Serve as a primary contact for players, parents, coaches, and league officials
  • Distribute schedules, travel itineraries, announcements, and academy updates
  • Support parent communication and academy meetings
  • Assist coaching staff with administrative needs and match-day preparation
Financial & Administrative Duties
  • Process invoices, expense reports, and budget tracking
  • Assist with academy purchasing and inventory management
  • Maintain operational databases and filing systems
  • Support academy events, camps, showcases, and tournaments
Match & Event Coordination
  • Coordinate home and away match logistics
  • Prepare match documentation and team materials
  • Support tournament registration and event planning
  • Assist with hospitality and visiting team coordination when needed

Qualifications

Education
  • Bachelor’s degree in Sports Management, Business Administration, Communications, or related field preferred
Experience
  • 2–5 years of administrative or operations experience in sports, youth development, or professional athletics preferred
  • Experience working in soccer, college or elite youth sports environments is a plus
Skills
  • Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
  • Attention to detail and problem-solving capability
  • Ability to work evenings, weekends, and travel as required
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and athlete management systems
Preferred Certifications

  • SafeSport certification
  • CPR/AED certification
Work Environment

  • Office, training facility, and match-day environments
  • Frequent evening and weekend responsibilities
  • Occasional travel for matches and tournaments
The selected candidate must pass a background check.

D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.

D.C. United will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform critical job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE). D.C. United reaffirms its commitment to the principles of equal opportunity and diversity. D.C. United is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. D.C. United is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, pregnancy, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with the requirements of all federal, state, and local laws. Employment decisions can include hiring, termination, compensation, benefits, promotion, training selection, or other statuses or conditions of employment. All employment decisions are made based on individual skills, knowledge, abilities, job performance, and other appropriate qualifications.

job.title: Intern- Corporate Partnerships Intern (Fall 2026)
job.html_description:
Corporate Partnerships Intern D.C. United is a Major League Soccer (MLS) team based in Washington, D.C., with a rich history of success both on and off the field. We are committed to fostering a culture of excellence, innovation, and community engagement. As a member of our team, you will have the unique opportunity to contribute to the growth of one of the most storied franchises in American soccer.
Position Overview D.C. United is seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Corporate Partnerships Intern to assist our Corporate Partnerships team for the Fall semester of the 2026 season. This internship offers an excellent opportunity to gain hands-on experience in sports marketing, sponsorships, and relationship management. The ideal candidate will be passionate about sports, eager to learn, and ready to contribute to the success of D.C. United's corporate partnerships.
This internship will follow a hybrid schedule aligned with the DCU Full Time Staffing schedule. In-person work at the D.C. United Offices at Audi Field will be required on Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays, as well as on game days, which are typically Saturday afternoons and evenings, but also on other days. The other days, excluding game days or events, will be virtual. To be considered for this position, the ability to work on-site during matches is required. Key Responsibilities 
  • Help coordinate and run partnership activations and events 
  • Support the development and maintenance of partner-related materials, including presentations, proposal and recaps 
  • Research potential partners and industry trends to support partnership development 
  • Collaborate with internal teams including marketing, ticketing, and operations to ensure successful partnership fulfillment 
  • Assist in tracking and reporting partnership deliverables to ensure all obligations are met
  • Provide general administrative support to the Corporate Partnerships team including scheduling meetings, preparing agendas and maintaining records 
Requirements 
  • Eligible to receive college credit for the internship 
  • Dedicate no more than 25 hours per week to the internship from August through November 2026 
  • Available to work on match days (including weekends) 
  • Work may require occasional weekend or evening work 
  • The selected candidate will be required to pass a background check 
Qualifications 
  • Must be able to receive college credit
  • Strong interest in sports, particularly soccer.
  • Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
  • Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Knowledge of Photoshop is preferred.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple tasks simultaneously.
  • Team player with a positive attitude and a willingness to learn.
  • Previous experience in sports marketing or corporate partnerships is a plus, but not required. 
Application Process To apply, please submit your resume, cover letter, and any relevant work samples. D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

D.C. United reaffirms its commitment to the principles of equal opportunity and diversity. D.C. United is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. D.C. United is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, pregnancy, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, Veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with the requirements of all federal, state, and local laws. Employment decisions can include hiring, termination, compensation, benefits, promotion, training selection, or other statuses or conditions of employment. All employment decisions are made on the basis of individual skills, knowledge, abilities, job performance, and other appropriate qualifications.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Account Executive, Group Sales
job.html_description:
| Who We’re Looking For
Are you passionate about creating unforgettable group experiences and building lasting relationships in the community? D.C. United is seeking a driven, coachable, and team-oriented sales professional to join our Group Sales team. This is more than just a sales role—it’s an opportunity to help build the top-performing group sales team in Major League Soccer. We’re looking for someone who thrives in a collaborative, high-energy environment and is committed to excellence, innovation, and impact.
As an Account Executive, Group Sales, you’ll be the go-to expert for organizing large-scale outings— ex. non-profit organizations, colleges, alumni groups, and local associations and membership groups. You’ll represent D.C. United in the community, build strong relationships, and help fans create memorable matchday experiences at Audi Field.
 | WHAT YOU WILL DO
  • Lead group sales initiatives focused on youth organizations, nonprofits, colleges, alumni, associations, and membership groups.
  • Build and maintain relationships with group leaders to drive renewals and referrals
  • Prospect and engage new leads through outbound calls, emails, texts, and in-person meetings
  • Represent D.C. United at home matches, grassroots events, and community activations
  • Use CRM tools to manage your sales pipeline, track progress, and uncover new opportunities
  • Collaborate with teammates to foster a fun, competitive, and supportive sales culture
  • Actively participate in ongoing training and coaching to sharpen your skills and grow professionally
  • Contribute ideas, energy, and leadership to help make D.C. United’s group sales team the benchmark for success across MLS
 | WHO YOU ARE
  • A group sales expert with a passion for creating impactful events
  • Coachable, enthusiastic, and resilient—always striving to improve and support your team
  • A strong communicator who thrives in a fast-paced, goal-driven environment
  • A community-minded individual who values building connections and making a difference
  • Positive, energetic, and motivated to exceed expectations
  • Bilingual (English/Spanish) candidates are strongly encouraged to apply
  | REQUIREMENTS
  • Proven success in group sales, preferably in sports or entertainment
  • Excellent time management, organization, and communication skills
  • Willingness to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and matchdays
  • Passion for sports and a desire to grow within the industry
  • The selected candidate must pass a background check
SCHEDULE
  • 9:00am- 5:30pm EST
  • Flexible hours including evenings, weekends, and holidays based on the season schedule

WHY D.C. United
  At D.C. United, you’ll be part of one of Major League Soccer’s most storied clubs, located in the heart of the nation’s capital. We are committed to building a culture that reflects the diverse community we serve and developing talent from within. You’ll have the opportunity to make a real impact, learn from the best, and grow your career in a competitive and exciting environment.

COMPENSATION
  • Competitive base salary ($35,000) + commission/bonus opportunities. Expected annual total cash compensation is $55,000 to $65,000 in year one and $70,000 to $85,000 in year two. Third-year reps have the potential to make over six figures
  • Comprehensive benefits package including health, dental, vision, 401(k), and paid time off
  • Complimentary tickets to D.C. United home matches
  • Opportunities for career advancement within D.C. United 

D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.

D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE).  D.C. United reaffirms its commitment to the principles of equal opportunity and diversity. D.C. United is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer.

Are you passionate about creating unforgettable group experiences and building lasting relationships in the community? D.C. United is seeking a driven, coachable, and team-oriented sales professional to join our Group Sales team. This is more than just a sales role—it’s an opportunity to help build the top-performing group sales team in Major League Soccer. We’re looking for someone who thrives in a collaborative, high-energy environment and is committed to excellence, innovation, and impact. As an Account Executive, Group Sales, you’ll be the go-to expert for organizing large-scale outings— ex. non-profit organizations, colleges, alumni groups, and local associations and membership groups. You’ll represent D.C. United in the community, build strong relationships, and help fans create memorable matchday experiences at Audi Field. | WHAT YOU WILL DO
- Lead group sales initiatives focused on youth organizations, nonprofits, colleges, alumni, associations, and membership groups. - Build and maintain relationships with group leaders to drive renewals and referrals - Prospect and engage new leads through outbound calls, emails, texts, and in-person meetings - Represent D.C. United at home matches, grassroots events, and community activations - Use CRM tools to manage your sales pipeline, track progress, and uncover new opportunities - Collaborate with teammates to foster a fun, competitive, and supportive sales culture - Actively participate in ongoing training and coaching to sharpen your skills and grow professionally - Contribute ideas, energy, and leadership to help make D.C. United’s group sales team the benchmark for success across MLS

job.title: New Business Development Representative
job.html_description:
WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

Are you passionate about sports and excited to launch a career in sales? D.C. United is looking for energetic, motivated, and curious individuals to join our team as New Business Development Representatives (NBDRs). This is a great opportunity for someone eager to learn, grow, and build the foundation for a successful career in the sports industry.

As a New Business Development Rep, you’ll be on the front lines of our ticket sales team—learning from experienced leaders, connecting with fans, and selling full season, partial plan, and group tickets for D.C. United home matches at Audi Field.

Whether you’re a recent graduate or someone looking to switch careers into a fast-paced, team-oriented environment, we want to hear from you. You’ll receive top-tier sales training, mentorship, and professional development from day one.

WHAT YOU’LL DO:
Learn and apply best-in-class outbound sales techniques through personalized training and daily coaching.
  • Make a high volume of outbound phone calls, texts, emails, and social touches to engage new potential ticket buyers.
  • Sell full season, partial plan, and group ticket packages to individuals and organizations.
  • Build relationships with fans and organizations across the D.C. area and beyond.
  • Represent D.C. United at home games, networking events, and community initiatives.
  • Use CRM tools to track activity, sales progress, and manage your sales pipeline.
  • Collaborate with teammates to create a fun, competitive, and supportive sales environment.

WHO YOU ARE:
You’re excited to learn and grow in a high-performance sales culture.
  • You thrive in a fast-paced environment and are comfortable making cold calls and prospecting new leads.
  • You are coachable, enthusiastic, and resilient—eager to push through challenges and celebrate wins.
  • You bring energy, effort, and curiosity every day.
  • You value teamwork, inclusivity, and being part of something bigger than yourself.

REQUIREMENTS:
Previous experience in customer service, sales, or a competitive team environment is a plus, but not required.
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills.
  • Passion for sports and a desire to build a career in the business of sports.
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and matchdays.
  • Bilingual (English/Spanish) candidates are strongly encouraged to apply.
  • The selected candidate must pass a background check.

WHY D.C. UNITED:
At D.C. United, you’ll be part of one of Major League Soccer’s most storied clubs, located in the heart of the nation’s capital. We are committed to building a culture that reflects the diverse community we serve and developing talent from within. You’ll have the opportunity to make a real impact, learn from the best, and grow your career in a competitive and exciting environment.

COMPENSATION & BENEFITS:

  • Competitive base salary ($35,000) + commission/bonus opportunities. Expected annual total cash compensation: $50,000 - $65,000.
  • Comprehensive benefits package including health, dental, vision, and 401(k).
  • Access to ongoing professional development and sales training.
  • Complimentary tickets to D.C. United home matches.
  • Opportunities for advancement within the organization.
D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.

D.C. United will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform critical job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE). D.C. United reaffirms its commitment to the principles of equal opportunity and diversity. D.C. United is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. D.C. United is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, pregnancy, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with the requirements of all federal, state, and local laws. Employment decisions can include hiring, termination, compensation, benefits, promotion, training selection, or other statuses or conditions of employment. All employment decisions are made on the basis of individual skills, knowledge, abilities, job performance, and other appropriate qualifications.

D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds and experiences. We believe that a diverse and inclusive team leads to stronger outcomes on and off the field.

job.title: Intern- Ticket Sales & Service Intern – Fall 2026
job.html_description:
The D.C. United Ticket Sales & Service team is looking for talented undergraduate or graduate students aspiring to enter the sports industry to join us as interns for the fall semester of 2026. D.C. United Ticket Sales & Service Interns will be introduced to the sales world with the goal of helping you launch your sales career within the sales industry, while helping fulfill an internship requisite. As a Ticket Sales & Service Intern, you will establish an understanding of the sales process, be introduced to the sales industry, be part of an intern cohort that will work on interdepartmental projects and gain real-life sales experience! Tuesday to Thursday will be in person at the D.C. United offices located at Audi Field. All other days, excluding any game days, will be optional/virtual. The ability to work at least 25 hours per week in the office, work D.C. United home matches, additional opportunities provided through DC Power FC home matches and D.C. United grassroot marketing events is a requirement to be considered for the position.

Must be an active college student and receive college course credit for the internship

Essential Duties and Responsibilities (including but not limited to the following):

  • Actively selling all D.C. United ticket products
  • Attend onboarding/offboarding sessions
  • Assist Account Executives and Ticket Sales Managers and Directors with the creation of unique sales campaigns
  • Participate in sales training and make sales calls to prospective buyers
  • Administrative duties including data entry, research, and lead generation
  • Matchday responsibilities such as sales table set up, running fan experience programs, and supporting prospecting events
  • Must be available for the 2026 Fall Semester: Approx. September 2026 – November 2026
  • Willingness to work grassroots marketing events offsite
  • Represent D.C. United & DC Power FC in a positive image
  • Participate in D.C. United Internship Cohort
  • Other Duties as assigned
Key qualities include:

  • Coachable, hardworking, multi-tasking, and positive attitude
  • Ability to effectively interact with staff and clients
  • Ability to work flexible schedule inclusive of evenings and weekends
  • Must be an active college student and receive college course credit for the internship
  • Knowledge of basic computer programs such as Excel, PowerPoint, etc.
  • Proficient in Spanish is a plus, but not required
All candidates must complete a successful background check.

D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.

D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE)

D.C. United reaffirms its commitment to the principles of equal opportunity and diversity. D.C. United is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. D.C. United is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, pregnancy, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, Veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with the requirements of all federal, state, and local laws. Employment decisions can include hiring, termination, compensation, benefits, promotion, training selection, or other statuses or conditions of employment. All employment decisions are made on the basis of individual skills, knowledge, abilities, job performance, and other appropriate qualifications.

job.title: Intern- D.C. United Brand Marketing Fall 2026 Internship
job.html_description:
The D.C. United Brand Marketing team is looking for talented, hardworking undergraduate and/or graduate students aspiring to enter the professional sports industry to join us as interns for the Fall semester of the 2026 season. D.C. United Brand Marketing Interns will be introduced to the world of marketing with the goal of launching your career within the marketing industry while fulfilling the internship requisite. As a Brand Marketing Intern, you will establish a level of understanding of the different facets of marketing including event promotion, brand awareness and fan engagement, be included in an intern cohort that will work on interdepartmental projects and gain real-life marketing experience.
 
This internship will be completed under a hybrid schedule and be aligned with the DCU Full Time Staffing schedule. Tuesday, Wednesdays, and Thursdays will be in-person at the D.C. United Offices located at Audi field. The remaining days of the week, excluding any game days or events, will be virtual. The ability to work matches on site is required to be considered for this position.
 
Essential Duties and Responsibilities (including but not limited to the following):
  •  Attend onboarding/offboarding sessions 
  • Provide matchday and fan experience support 
  •  Pursue continued career growth
  • Willingness to work events both on and offsite
  • Represent D.C. United in a positive image
  • Participate in D.C. United Internship Cohort
  • Provide Administrative support such as department emails and coordinating internal meetings
  • Assist Marketing Manager and Coordinator with the creation of unique promotional campaigns
  • Maximum of twenty-five (25) office hours per week from (September – November 2026)
  • Other duties as assigned
 
Key qualities include:
  • Coachable, hardworking, multi-tasking, and positive attitude
  • Ability to effectively interact with staff and clients
  • Demonstrate attention to detail
  • Strong organizational & communication skills
  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Office 365
  • Ability to work flexible schedule inclusive of evenings and weekends
  • Proficient in Spanish is a plus, but not required
  • Must be an active college or graduate student and have the ability to receive credit for the internship
All candidates must complete a successful background check.

D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.

D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE)

job.title: Intern- Guest Experience Fall 2026 Intern
job.html_description:
The D.C. United Guest Experience team is seeking motivated undergraduate or graduate students who are passionate about live-event environments, fan engagement, and exceptional customer service. Guest Experience Interns will support the planning and execution of all Audi Field events including D.C. United, Washington Spirit, DC Power FC, UFL games, and special events, to help deliver a best-in-class experience for every guest.
Interns will gain hands-on experience in stadium operations, staffing logistics, employee engagement, and fan-focused service delivery. While primarily embedded with the Guest Experience team, interns will also be exposed to the four branches of the Audi Field Operations Department throughout the internship: Game Operations, Event/Stadium Operations, Guest Experience, and Security.
This internship is ideal for students who are energetic, detail-oriented, and eager to contribute to a world-class fan environment in professional sports. Interns will report directly to the Guest Experience Manager and collaborate cross-departmentally.
 
Internship Duration: September 1st, 2026 – November 20th, 2026
Essential Duties and Responsibilities (including but not limited to the following):
Event Support 
·       Assist with all phases of event operations, including staff check-in, pre-event setup, concourse presence, and post-event breakdown.
·        Support the delivery of exceptional guest experience by greeting fans throughout the stadium, answering questions, and helping maintain a welcoming environment in all guest-facing areas.
·        Provide proactive and professional guest assistance, escalating and resolving concerns in coordination with supervisors and leadership.
Staffing & Employee Engagement
·      Support staff scheduling logistics and management of 100+ part-time ambassadors.
·      Assist with attendance tracking, incentive programs, and employee communications.
·      Distribute, maintain, and inventory staff uniforms and gameday supplies.
·      Support onboarding and training initiatives for new team members.
Operational Preparedness
·      Assist with creation and updates of matchday guides and department resources.
·      Participate in pre-event briefings and post-event recaps with Guest Experience leadership.
·      Conduct research to identify enhancements to fan experience and stadium hospitality programs.
Collaboration & Administrative Support
·      Work cross-functionally with Operations, Facility Services, Ticketing, Sales, and Partnerships teams.
·      Maintain lost and found documentation and inventory organization.
·      Assist in collecting data, gameday reporting, and operational notes to drive continuous improvement.
Requirements:
  • Eligible to receive college credit for internship
  • Eligible to attend full-stadium events and most corporate/entertainment events
  • Ability to work event/match days
  • Experience in sports or events is a plus
  • Must be able to successfully pass a background check
Competencies (KSAs):
  • Coachable, hardworking, multi-tasking, and positive attitude
  • Ability to effectively interact with staff and clients
  • Knowledge of basic computer programs such as Excel, PowerPoint, etc.
  • Detailed Oriented
  • Represent D.C. United in a positive image 
D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.
D.C. United will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform critical job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

United is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. D.C. United is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, pregnancy, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with the requirements of all federal, state, and local laws. Interns will gain hands-on experience in stadium operations, staffing logistics, employee engagement, and fan-focused service delivery. While primarily embedded with the Guest Experience team, interns will also be exposed to the four branches of the Audi Field Operations Department throughout the internship: Game Operations, Event/Stadium Operations, Guest Experience, and Security.
This internship is ideal for students who are energetic, detail-oriented, and eager to contribute to a world-class fan environment in professional sports. Interns will report directly to the Guest Experience Manager and collaborate cross-departmentally.
Internship Duration: September 1st, 2026 – November 20th, 2026
Essential Duties and Responsibilities (including but not limited to the following):
Event Support
· Assist with all phases of event operations, including staff check-in, pre-event setup, concourse presence, and post-event breakdown.
· Support the delivery of exceptional guest experience by greeting fans throughout the stadium, answering questions, and helping maintain a welcoming environment in all guest-facing areas.
· Provide proactive and professional guest assistance, escalating and resolving concerns in coordination with supervisors and leadership.
Staffing & Employee Engagement
· Support staff scheduling logistics and management of 100+ part-time ambassadors.
· Assist with attendance tracking, incentive programs, and employee communications.
· Distribute, maintain, and inventory staff uniforms and gameday supplies.
· Support onboarding and training initiatives for new team members.
Operational Preparedness
· Assist with creation and updates of matchday guides and department resources.
· Participate in pre-event briefings and post-event recaps with Guest Experience leadership.
· Conduct research to identify enhancements to fan experience and stadium hospitality programs.
Collaboration & Administrative Support
· Work cross-functionally with Operations, Facility Services, Ticketing, Sales, and Partnerships teams.
· Maintain lost and found documentation and inventory organization.
· Assist in collecting data, gameday reporting, and operational notes to drive continuous improvement.
Requirements:
- Eligible to receive college credit for internship - Eligible to attend full-stadium events and most corporate/entertainment events - Ability to work event/match days - Experience in sports or events is a plus - Must be able to successfully pass a background check
Competencies (KSAs):
- Coachable, hardworking, multi-tasking, and positive attitude - Ability to effectively interact with staff and clients - Knowledge of basic computer programs such as Excel, PowerPoint, etc. - Detailed Oriented - Represent D.C. United in a positive image
D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.

job.title: Intern- D.C. United Videographer Internship (Fall 2026)
job.html_description:
The Videographer Intern will support D.C. United’s Creative and Content team in capturing, editing, and producing engaging video content that tells the story of the club, players, and fans during the fall 2026 semester. This role provides hands-on experience in professional sports content creation, including matchday coverage, training sessions, social media features, and branded storytelling within a fast-paced MLS environment. Interns will be included in an intern cohort that will work oninterdepartmental projects and gain real-life videography experience.
 
This internship is ideal for students pursuing careers in videography, sports media, digital content, or creative production. It will be completed under a hybrid schedule and be aligned with the DCU Full Time Staffing schedule. Tuesdays through Thursdays will be in-person at the D.C. United Offices located at Audi field. The remaining days of the week, excluding any game days or events, will be virtual. The ability to work matches on site is required to be considered for this position.
  
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
•       Film, edit, and format video content for social media (X, Instagram, YouTube), the stadium video board, and broadcast partners.
•       Quickly edit highlights, hype videos, and behind-the-scenes footage for daily distribution.
•       Assist with pre-game/in-game video shoots, setup/breakdown of equipment, and production roles.
•       Professional development through mentorship, team collaboration, and leadership sessions.
•       Capture, log, organize, and maintain video footage and media elements.
•       Work with the video team, producers, and other departments to bring creative concepts to life. 
REQUIREMENTS
•       Must be an active undergraduate or graduate student eligible to receive college credit for the internship as this is an unpaid opportunity.
•       Available to dedicate up to 25 hours per week from August – November 2026.
•       Willingness to work matchdays, weekends, and/or evenings.
•       Prior experience in sports or related fields is a plus.
•       Coursework in videography, film production, media, or related field
•       Proficiency with Adobe Premiere Pro and other creative cloud applications.
KEY QUALITIES & SKILLS
•       Coachable, hardworking, and able to multitask with a positive attitude.
•       Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to interact effectively with staff, partners, and fans.
•       Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
•       Clear and professional communication abilities.
•       Working knowledge of Microsoft Office 365.
•       Flexibility to work evenings and weekends as needed.
•       Spanish language proficiency is a plus but not required.
All candidates must complete a successful background check.
D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.

D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE)

job.title: Intern- Match Presentation Intern (Fall 2026)
job.html_description:
Match Presentation Intern 
D.C. United is a Major League Soccer (MLS) team based in Washington, D.C., with a rich history of success both on and off the field. We are committed to fostering a culture of excellence, innovation, and community engagement. As a member of our team, you will have the unique opportunity to contribute to the growth of one of the most storied franchises in American soccer.
Position Overview 
D.C. United is looking for motivated individuals who are passionate about gaining event presentation experience with a professional sports organization.  The Match Presentation Intern will be responsible for enhancing the match experience for D.C. United and DC Power FC Matches. The ideal candidate will be passionate about sports, eager to learn, and ready to contribute to the success of D.C. United's operations team.
ESSENTIAL DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES
·      Serve as Stage Manager during matches, coordinating live elements from pre-game to post-game.
·      Collaborate with in-game hosts and high-profile personalities.
·      Work closely with team members to support presentation elements.
·      Deliver excellent customer service to enhance the match-day experience
·      Assist with the setup and breakdown of interactive elements.
·      Be open to working at other full stadium events.
·      Other duties as assigned.

Requirements 
·      Must be available for the 2026 Fall Semester: Approx. September 2026 – November 2026
·      Eligible to receive college credit for the internship 
·      Available to work on match days (including weekends) 
·      The selected candidate will be required to pass a background check
·      Ability to communicate, work well with others, and thrive in dynamic environments.
·      Experience working in live events, preferred.

Competencies (KSAs):
·      Coachable, hardworking, multi-tasking, and positive attitude
·      Ability to effectively interact with staff and fans
·      Detailed Oriented
·      Represent D.C. United in a positive image

D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.

D.C. United will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform critical job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

job.title: Director, Ticket Operations & Strategy
job.html_description:
JOB SUMMARY
The D.C. United & Audi Field Business Strategy & Analytics team is looking for an enthusiastic and detailed leader to oversee our ticket operations and ticket strategy efforts as Director, Ticket Operations & Strategy.
The Director, Ticket Operations & Strategy will focus on leading and supporting all aspects of ticket operations, ticket strategy and box office operations for events at Audi Field, including owned properties: D.C. United (MLS), Audi Field Events, DC Power FC (USL-Super League) and tenants: Washington Spirit (NWSL) and DC Defenders (UFL). This position also acts as a key liaison between the Ticket Sales and Service team, venue management and other internal/external stakeholders, such as MLS, USL, concert promoters, and event promoters.

ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS, DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
·      Lead and direct all ticket operations duties for D.C. United (MLS), DC Power FC (USL-Super League) and Audi Field Events, including but not limited to: event creation, order processing, inventory management, renewals, payment plans, mobile ticket assistance, ticket exchanges, member account requests, etc.
·      Oversee box office operations for events at Audi Field 
·      Oversee access control systems for all related events
·      Support our venue tenants DC Defenders (UFL) and Washington Spirit (NWSL) ticket operations on event days
·      First point-of-contact for D.C. United Ticket Sales & Service Leadership
·      Focus on ticket product and sales strategy, ie. multi-match plans, special offers, etc.
·      Manifest management and venue scaling
·      Inventory distribution and pricing management for all owned events
·      Main point-of-contact for external ticket partners and event promoters
·      Utilize Ticketmaster suite of products, including: Archtics, Account Manager, TM One, Event Management Tool, TM Host, Virtual Venue, etc.
·      Work with Ticketmaster client support to troubleshoot system issues, event creation, reporting, etc.
·      Assist Finance team in reconciliation of all related events held at D.C. United-owned and operated properties
·      Maintain quality control on all related events ticketed products
·      Lead recurring training sessions for ticketing department
·      Form creation and data capture processes
·      Work with various D.C. United partners on operational builds, ie. Fevo, VetTix, SuitePro
·      Maneuver within various large data sets to efficiently execute operational tasks

EDUCATION: Bachelor’s degree or relevant experience required. 
WORK EXPERIENCE: 5+ years of ticket operations experience preferred but not required
COMPETENCIES (KSAs)
  • Ticketmaster Archtics experience preferred
  • Project Management Software (Asana) experience preferred 
  • Microsoft Excel skills strongly preferred
  • Experience using Microsoft Dynamics CRM preferred
  • Excellent time management, leadership, organization and customer service skills 
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends and holidays
 
LANGUAGES:   Spanish is a plus 
SCHEDULE:  8:30am - 5:30pm · Work demand is based on team and front office schedule · Ability to work flexible hours including evenings, weekends, and holidays based on the season’s schedule
 
SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITIES:      Two (2) to Three (3) Direct Reports·  Ten (10)+ PT match-day staff
TRAVEL:      Minimal, will remain in greater DC, MD, VA area

ENVIRONMENTAL AND PHYSICAL CONDITIONS:    Must be available to work events in evenings, weekends and holidays as necessary 
SALARY:      The good faith anticipated base pay range for this role is shown below, and actual base pay will depend on your skills, qualifications, experiences, location, and other business and organizational needs. Anticipated base pay: $80,000-$90,000
OTHER:    The candidate must be able to successfully pass a background check.  Other duties as assigned (see disclaimer below) 
 
DISCLAIMER:
The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by the employee(s) assigned to this title. This description should not be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required of incumbents holding the position. All personnel may be required to perform duties outside of their normal responsibilities from time to time, as needed. Further neither this description, nor completion of tasks associated with this title should be construed as a contract of employment. All D.C. United employees, unless otherwise designated by the Co-Chairs, are at-will employees.
D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup. 
D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE)
D.C. United reaffirms its commitment to the principles of equal opportunity and diversity. D.C. United is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. D.C. United is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, pregnancy, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, Veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with the requirements of all federal, state, and local laws. Employment decisions can include hiring, termination, compensation, benefits, promotion, training selection, or other statuses or conditions of employment. All employment decisions are made on the basis of individual skills, knowledge, abilities, job performance, and other appropriate qualifications.

job.title: Intern- Grassroots Marketing Fall 2026 Intern
job.html_description:
The D.C. United Ticket Sales & Service team is looking for talented, hardworking undergraduate and/or graduate students aspiring to enter the professional sports industry to join us as interns for the fall semester of the 2026 season. D.C. United Grassroots Marketing Interns will be introduced to the revenue-generating side of community-driven marketing, with the goal of launching your career within the marketing & sales industry while fulfilling your internship requisite. As a Grassroots Marketing Intern, you will learn how brands grow revenue from the ground up through authentic community engagement, local outreach, and on-the-ground activations that convert into ticket sales. You will gain hands-on experience driving revenue for D.C. United through the communities and fans that make the club what it is, be included in an intern cohort that willwork on interdepartmental projects and gain real-life marketing & sales experience.

This internship will be completed under a hybrid schedule and be aligned with the DCU Full Time Staffing schedule. Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays will be in-person at the D.C. United Offices located at Audi Field. The remaining days of the week, excluding any game days or events, will be virtual. The ability to work matches on site is required to beconsidered for this position.
 
Essential Duties and Responsibilities (including but not limited to the following):

  • Attend onboarding/offboarding sessions
  • Provide matchday and fan experience support
  • Pursue continued career growth
  • Willingness to work events both on and offsite
  • Represent D.C. United in a positive image
  • Participate in D.C. United Internship Cohort
  • Provide administrative support such as department emails and coordinating internal meetings
  • Assist Grassroots & Fan Experience Coordinator with the planning and execution of community-driven campaignsand local outreach initiatives designed to drive ticket sales and revenue
  • Support the identification and development of grassroots partnerships with local organizations, businesses, and community groups
  • Help coordinate logistics for grassroots activations, street teams, and branded events across the D.C. area
  •  Lift items up to fifty (50) pounds
  • Maximum of twenty-five (25) office hours per week from September 1 - November 20, 2026
  • Other duties as assigned

What You'll Learn:

  • How to lead and execute grassroots activations that translate community engagement into ticket sales
  • Direct-to-consumer sales skills, including generating and closing ticket sales through FEVO and other platforms
  • How to support experienced sales reps throughout the sales cycle, from prospecting to close
  • How to identify, qualify, and hand off sales leads to maximize conversion
  • How grassroots marketing efforts tie directly into the club's broader revenue and business goals

Key qualities include:

  • Coachable, hardworking, multi-tasking, and positive attitude
  • Ability to effectively interact with staff and clients
  • Demonstrate attention to detail
  • Strong organizational & communication skills
  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Office 365
  • Ability to work flexible schedule inclusive of evenings and weekends
  • Proficient in Spanish is a plus, but not required
  • Must be an active college or graduate student and have the ability to receive credit for the internship; college seniors and graduate students are preferred 

All candidates must complete a successful background check. 

D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.
D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE)
D.C. United reaffirms its commitment to the principles of equal opportunity and diversity. D.C. United is proud to be anequal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. D.C. United is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, pregnancy, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship
status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, Veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with the requirements of all federal, state, and local laws.
Employment decisions can include hiring, termination, compensation, benefits, promotion, training selection, or otherstatuses or conditions of employment. You will gain hands-on experience driving revenue for D.C. United through the communities and fans that make the club what it is, be included in an intern cohort that willwork on interdepartmental projects and gain real-life marketing & sales experience.
This internship will be completed under a hybrid schedule and be aligned with the DCU Full Time Staffing schedule. Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays will be in-person at the D.C. United Offices located at Audi Field. The remaining days of the week, excluding any game days or events, will be virtual. The ability to work matches on site is required to beconsidered for this position.
Essential Duties and Responsibilities(including but not limited to the following):
- Attend onboarding/offboarding sessions - Provide matchday and fan experience support - Pursue continued career growth - Willingness to work events both on and offsite - Represent D.C. United in a positive image - Participate in D.C. United Internship Cohort - Provide administrative support such as department emails and coordinating internal meetings - Assist Grassroots & Fan Experience Coordinator with the planning and execution of community-driven campaignsand local outreach initiatives designed to drive ticket sales and revenue - Support the identification and development of grassroots partnerships with local organizations, businesses, and community groups - Help coordinate logistics for grassroots activations, street teams, and branded events across the D.C. area - Lift items up to fifty (50) pounds - Maximum of twenty-five (25) office hours per week from September 1 - November 20, 2026 - Other duties as assigned
What You'll Learn:
- How to lead and execute grassroots activations that translate community engagement into ticket sales - Direct-to-consumer sales skills, including generating and closing ticket sales through FEVO and other platforms - How to support experienced sales reps throughout the sales cycle, from prospecting to close - How to identify, qualify, and hand off sales leads to maximize conversion - How grassroots marketing efforts tie directly into the club's broader revenue and business goals
Key qualities include:
- Coachable, hardworking, multi-tasking, and positive attitude - Ability to effectively interact with staff and clients - Demonstrate attention to detail - Strong organizational & communication skills - Working knowledge of Microsoft Office 365 - Ability to work flexible schedule inclusive of evenings and weekends - Proficient in Spanish is a plus, but not required - Must be an active college or graduate student and have the ability to receive credit for the internship; college seniors and graduate students are preferred
All candidates must complete a successful background check.
D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.
D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE)
D.C. United reaffirms its commitment to the principles of equal opportunity and diversity. D.C. United is proud to be anequal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. D.C. United is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, pregnancy, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship
status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, Veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with the requirements of all federal, state, and local laws.

job.title: Intern- Communications Fall 2026 Internship
job.html_description:
OVERVIEW
The internship offers an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience in sports media within the fast-paced world of professional sports. As an intern, you'll work closely with the communications team to help build and promote the team's brand through building out game notes with statistics, creating storylines to pitch to the media, assisting with press conferences, and other media operations.  

RESPONSIBILITIES
●      Assist on matchday and the coordination of events such as press conferences, media day, etc.
●      Support the development of departmental publications (e.g., press releases, media advisories, game notes, media guide, etc.).
●      Monitor and report on team coverage in new and traditional local and national media.
●      Perform other duties as assigned by the Communications department

WHAT YOU'LL LEARN
●      Learn how to develop and execute communication strategies through creating press releases, post-match facts, and putting together game notes by researching all aspects of the club.
●      Understanding and applying the set up for home matches/events, how home matches/events and first team training are covered by the media and how to disseminate information.
●      Media logistics for open practices, pre -match and post-match press conferences, as well as any first-team interviews
●      Gain experience in working closely with the content team, marketing team and the first team to ensure a unified communication approach, aligning messages with the broader goals of the organization

EXPERIENCE
●      Must be currently enrolled in college and seek credit for the internship.
●      Experience in sports preferred, but not required
●      Strong communication and editorial skills
  •    Proficiency in Microsoft Word & Excel is required, while the knowledge of Adobe InDesign and Photoshop is not required, but preferred.

This is an unpaid, for credit internship (proof that you will receive credit is required).
You must be authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship.
The candidate must be able to successfully pass a background check.
Because of the cyclical nature of the entertainment industry, your assigned hours may vary to reflect our needs (includes nights and weekends).

D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer and we will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of age, race, creed, color, national origin, ancestry, marital status, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, nationality or any other classification protected by law.
D.C. United are one of 29 teams in Major League Soccer (MLS). One of the founding clubs of Major League Soccer in 1996, D.C. United are among the most decorated teams in the United States with 13 domestic and international trophies. D.C. United have called the District home for the last 28 seasons and play their home games at Audi Field in the Buzzard Point neighborhood of Southwest, Washington, D.C, which opened in 2018. The Black-and-Red have won the MLS Cup four times (1996, 1997, 1999 and 2004), the Supporters’ Shield four times (1997, 1999, 2006 and 2007) and the Lamar Hunt U.S. Open Cup three times (1996, 2008 and 2013).

job.title: Regional Development School (RDS) Coach
job.html_description:
JOB SUMMARY
The Regional Development School (RDS) Coach will be responsible for the coaching, development of talent, and delivery of multiple RDS locations per season. The RDS Coach will deliver the RDS curriculum and actively scout and assist in the recruitment of talent for the Academy within the guidelines of D.C. United, US Soccer and the MLS. The RDS Coach will be responsible for delivering summer camps and other clinics. This position will report directly to the Director of Youth Programs.
 
ESSENTIAL DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Assist in planning and organization of RDS tryouts.
  • Develop training program for weekly training sessions for RDS participants.
  • Consistently plan, execute, demonstrate and deliver high energy training sessions that are competitive and engaging
  • Provide player assessment and feedback of strengths/weaknesses each season
  • Guide players in development of an “action plan” setting specific goals to reach a desired outcome. 
  • Identify and recommend, talented, potential Academy players.
  • Attend mandatory education meetings and classroom sessions.
  • Organize and assist in the running of other camps/clinics/tryouts throughout the year.
  • Ongoing assessment and mentoring of summer coaching staff. 
  • Other coaching requirements as needed

QUALIFICATIONS
-       A current US Soccer C license coach or equivalent (minimum)
-       Possess the drive to develop youth players and work as part of a team
-       Knowledge of MLS/DMV soccer landscape preferred
-       Available to work flexible hours including nights, weekends & holidays
-       Have the ability to plan, create, execute and document training sessions and programs 
-       Have a basic knowledge of the MLS Next rules and regulations
-       The selected candidate must successfully pass a background check.

Compensation
Hourly (scale depending on coaching license)

D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup. 

D.C. United will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform critical job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.  

D.C. United is an equal opportunity employer (EOE).  D.C. United reaffirms its commitment to the principles of equal opportunity and diversity. D.C. United is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. D.C. United is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, pregnancy, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with the requirements of all federal, state, and local laws. Employment decisions can include hiring, termination, compensation, benefits, promotion, training selection, or other statuses or conditions of employment. All employment decisions are made on the basis of individual skills, knowledge, abilities, job performance, and other appropriate qualifications.

job.title: Youth Futsal Coach
job.html_description:
JOB SUMMARY
The Youth Futsal Coach will be responsible for the coaching of futsal clinics around the DMV area throughout the year. The youth futsal coach will deliver from different curricula depending on the program. In addition, the coach will actively scout and recommend players for DC United’s Regional Development School program. The coach will report directly to the Director of Youth Programs.

ESSENTIAL DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES
  • A coach’s working week is comprised of on field coaching hours & administration duties
  • Required to attend mandatory paid pre-season orientation event(s)
  • Assist in Regional Development School (RDS) tryouts as needed
  • Scout/recommend talented, potential RDS players.
  • Organize and assist in the running of other camps/clinics/tryouts throughout the year.
  • Assist with Community Relations events as needed.
  • Assist with training for affiliate programs as needed.
QUALIFICATIONS
-       A current US Soccer D license coach or equivalent (minimum)
-       Possess the drive to develop youth players and work as part of a team
-       Knowledge of MLS/DMV futsal landscape preferred
-       Available to work flexible hours including nights, weekends & holidays
-       Have the ability to plan, create, execute and document training sessions and programs 
-       Valid US Driver’s License
-       The selected candidate must be able to successfully pass a background check

Compensation
-       Hourly (scale depending on coaching license)

D.C. United is committed to uplifting our community, staff, and club. We are seeking candidates to be a part of our journey in our quest for continued organizational growth and another MLS Cup.
