- University degree in Information Systems, Event Technology, or related field. - Strong experience in accreditation systems or event technology at major international events. - Strong understanding of access control, data workflows, and system integration. - Experience working with accreditation platforms (e.g., Event-Works, FAME, Accredit, or similar). - Familiarity with GDPR and data privacy best practices.
Skills & Competencies
- Strong technical and analytical skills - Excellent problem-solving and troubleshooting abilities - Effective communication and training delivery - High attention to detail and data quality - Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Requirements
- Right to work in Brazil. - Fluent in English (spoken and written) - Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written) - Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Event Logistics Manager
job.html_description:
As the organiser of some of the biggest and most iconic sport events in the world, we lead the way in the sport and event industry.

Join the team and support us on our mission.
These are your key tasks:
  • Plan, prepare, and execute international shipments for FIFA tournaments, draws, and corporate events.
  • Coordinate logistics requirements with internal stakeholders, host country teams, suppliers, venues, and hotels to ensure timely and efficient delivery of goods and services.
  • Lead and oversee key logistics workstreams including international freight, customs clearance, warehousing, distribution, venue logistics, and the movement of team equipment.
  • Prepare logistics tender documentation, support procurement processes, and evaluate supplier proposals.
  • Coordinate warehouse operations and event shipments, including physical picking, packing, unpacking, repacking, and distribution activities in Zurich and on site.
  • Manage shipment-related requests and troubleshoot operational challenges, including urgent and last-minute requirements.
  • Prepare and issue international customs documentation, including invoices, packing lists, certificates of origin, and ATA carnets.
  • Act as the primary point of contact for customs matters and support the implementation of customs procedures in collaboration with host countries and local organizing entities.
  • Manage FIFA storage facilities both in Zurich and at events, ensuring effective inventory and material management.
  • Monitor logistics service delivery, establish operational processes, and drive continuous improvements to ensure the highest standards of quality and efficiency.
  • Support cross-functional projects and operational activities
  • Travel extensively to FIFA tournaments and events worldwide, providing on-site logistics expertise throughout event delivery.
We work hard at FIFA.
We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.

And we respect our values. Always.

For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.

The specific competencies we require for this position are:
  • A completed commercial apprenticeship or equivalent qualification in international freight forwarding, customs, logistics, or a related field.
  • Additional training or certifications in international trade, customs, supply chain management, or logistics operations is an advantage.
  • At least 5 years of professional experience in international freight forwarding and customs operations, including air, road, and sea freight.
  • Proven experience managing international import and export processes and customs clearance activities across multiple regions.
  • Experience within event logistics, exhibition logistics, sports events, or international fair forwarding environments.
  • Strong knowledge of customs regulations, international trade documentation, and global logistics processes.
  • A solution-oriented, proactive, and pragmatic approach combined with the ability to remain calm under pressure and adapt to changing priorities.
  • Strong organizational skills with the ability to work independently, manage multiple tasks simultaneously, and prioritize effectively.
  • Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to collaborate across cultures and work effectively with diverse international teams.
  • A customer-focused mindset and a willingness to go the extra mile when required.
  • Fluency in written and spoken English; proficiency in another FIFA language (French, Spanish, German, or Arabic) is considered an asset.
  • Solid Microsoft Office skills, particularly in Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
  • A valid driving licence.
  • Willingness to travel extensively and spend extended periods on-site at tournaments and events.
  • Physical fitness and the ability to perform hands-on warehouse and event logistics activities, including packing, lifting, and material handling.
The biggest benefit of a career at FIFA is the privilege to work for the beautiful game and create something that matters.

Alongside that privilege, we also offer some extra perks and benefits:
  • Health and fitness: Regular football sessions for all abilities, plus on-site gym.
  • Home office: Up to two days of home office per week if work permits and home office allowance for all.
  • Language courses: Offered to all staff to aid personal and professional growth.
  • Continuous learning: We care about your development and encourage internal mobility, offering a variety of training and education to help achieve it.
  • Pension: Flexible pension benefits with generous employer contributions.
  • Relocation assistance: A dedicated team will be on hand to support your relocation.
  • Sustainability: We support all employees using public transport.
  • Vacation: Maintain a healthy work/life balance with a generous vacation allowance.
  • Volunteering: Make an impact beyond your regular role and bond with colleagues by volunteering with one of our partners.
job.plain_text_description:
As the organiser of some of the biggest and most iconic sport events in the world, we lead the way in the sport and event industry.
Join the team and support us on our mission.
These are your key tasks:
- Plan, prepare, and execute international shipments for FIFA tournaments, draws, and corporate events. - Coordinate logistics requirements with internal stakeholders, host country teams, suppliers, venues, and hotels to ensure timely and efficient delivery of goods and services. - Lead and oversee key logistics workstreams including international freight, customs clearance, warehousing, distribution, venue logistics, and the movement of team equipment. - Prepare logistics tender documentation, support procurement processes, and evaluate supplier proposals. - Coordinate warehouse operations and event shipments, including physical picking, packing, unpacking, repacking, and distribution activities in Zurich and on site. - Manage shipment-related requests and troubleshoot operational challenges, including urgent and last-minute requirements. - Prepare and issue international customs documentation, including invoices, packing lists, certificates of origin, and ATA carnets. - Act as the primary point of contact for customs matters and support the implementation of customs procedures in collaboration with host countries and local organizing entities. - Manage FIFA storage facilities both in Zurich and at events, ensuring effective inventory and material management. - Monitor logistics service delivery, establish operational processes, and drive continuous improvements to ensure the highest standards of quality and efficiency. - Support cross-functional projects and operational activities - Travel extensively to FIFA tournaments and events worldwide, providing on-site logistics expertise throughout event delivery.
We work hard at FIFA. We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.
And we respect our values. Always.
For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.
The specific competencies we require for this position are:
- A completed commercial apprenticeship or equivalent qualification in international freight forwarding, customs, logistics, or a related field. - Additional training or certifications in international trade, customs, supply chain management, or logistics operations is an advantage. - At least 5 years of professional experience in international freight forwarding and customs operations, including air, road, and sea freight. - Proven experience managing international import and export processes and customs clearance activities across multiple regions. - Experience within event logistics, exhibition logistics, sports events, or international fair forwarding environments. - Strong knowledge of customs regulations, international trade documentation, and global logistics processes. - A solution-oriented, proactive, and pragmatic approach combined with the ability to remain calm under pressure and adapt to changing priorities. - Strong organizational skills with the ability to work independently, manage multiple tasks simultaneously, and prioritize effectively. - Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to collaborate across cultures and work effectively with diverse international teams. - A customer-focused mindset and a willingness to go the extra mile when required. - Fluency in written and spoken English; proficiency in another FIFA language (French, Spanish, German, or Arabic) is considered an asset. - Solid Microsoft Office skills, particularly in Excel, Word, and PowerPoint. - A valid driving licence. - Willingness to travel extensively and spend extended periods on-site at tournaments and events. - Physical fitness and the ability to perform hands-on warehouse and event logistics activities, including packing, lifting, and material handling.
The biggest benefit of a career at FIFA is the privilege to work for the beautiful game and create something that matters.
Alongside that privilege, we also offer some extra perks and benefits:
- Health and fitness: Regular football sessions for all abilities, plus on-site gym. - Home office: Up to two days of home office per week if work permits and home office allowance for all. - Language courses: Offered to all staff to aid personal and professional growth. - Continuous learning: We care about your development and encourage internal mobility, offering a variety of training and education to help achieve it. - Pension: Flexible pension benefits with generous employer contributions. - Relocation assistance: A dedicated team will be on hand to support your relocation. - Sustainability: We support all employees using public transport. - Vacation: Maintain a healthy work/life balance with a generous vacation allowance. - Volunteering: Make an impact beyond your regular role and bond with colleagues by volunteering with one of our partners.

job.title: IT Strategy & Operations Intern- FIFA Clearing House
job.html_description:
The establishment of the FIFA Clearing House (“FCH”) is a key element of the first reform package of the  transfer system adopted by the FIFA Council in 2018, to promote solidarity, transparency, and integrity in professional football.

The FCH is established in France and operates independently from FIFA.
The FCH will act as a payment service provider and intermediary in the worldwide payments relating to Training Rewards (solidarity mechanism and training compensation), deriving from the FIFA Regulation on the Status of Transfer of Players.  

These are your key tasks:
  •  Structure and monitor Build to Run processes: incidents, releases, changelogs, alerting, SLAs. 
  • Implement governance routines: IT/Run committees, roadmaps, indicators.
  • Participate in drafting and formalising IT procedures (changes, incidents, DRP, BCP). 
  • Consolidate technical and functional documentation (Confluence, SharePoint).
  • Contribute to IT fleet management (orders, inventory, onboarding). 
  • Support budget monitoring (forecasts, accruals, monitoring tables).     
  • Contribute to the implementation of dashboards for the IT department.
  • Contribute to the definition of the Product roadmap, in coordination with different users. 
We work hard at FIFA Clearing House.
We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.

And we respect our values. Always.

For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.

The specific competencies we require for this position are:
  •  You demonstrate an ability to work in a multicultural environment and a strong desire to join a start-up team. 
  • You are ready to contribute to the expansion of the business and the improvement of IT processes and tools.
  • You are motivated by ethics, excellence and a results-oriented approach, and you want to be part of a major change in the football industry.
  •  Bachelor’s or Master’s degree (engineering school, business school) 
  •  Structured thinking, rigour, autonomy
  • Interest in IT organisation and complex environments
  • Proficiency in collaborative tools (Excel, PowerPoint, Teams, JIRA)
  • Fluent in professional English
  • Proficiency in French is an asset 
  • No work experience is needed but could be profitable

The biggest benefit of a career at FIFA is the privilege to work for the beautiful game and create something that matters.

Alongside that privilege, we also offer some extra perks and benefits:
  • Home office: Up to two days of home office per week if work permits and home office allowance for all.
  • Language courses: Offered to all staff to aid personal and professional growth.
  • Continuous learning: We care about your development and encourage internal mobility, offering a variety of training and education to help achieve it.
  • Relocation assistance: A dedicated team will be on hand to support your relocation.
  • Sustainability: We support all employees using public transport.
  • Vacation: Maintain a healthy work/life balance.
  • Volunteering: Make an impact beyond your regular role and bond with colleagues by volunteering with one of our partners.

job.title: Digital Product Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ Digital Product Manager will be the driving force behind FIFA’s digital direct-to-consumer experiences for FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™. Whilst the role will be based in Rio, the successful candidate will act as an integral member of the global Product & Design team, defining fans’ digital journey across FIFA’s owned and operated platforms, notably including FIFA.com and FIFA’s official mobile app.

Reporting to Head of Brand & Marketing, the primary responsibility will involve taking the lead in evolving FIFA’s fan-facing websites and apps specifically for FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027. This will include developing digital activations that support tournament goals and objectives, as well as delivery of commercial value for FIFA’s partners.

With the biggest, most ambitious FIFA Women’s World Cup™ on the horizon, this is your chance to help shape the next generation of digital experiences and grow the Women’s game!


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Strategic Vision: Play a leading role in defining and developing FIFA’s digital consumer-facing proposition, specifically ensuring products and experiences reflect local fan behaviours and consumption habits, as well as aligning cultural nuances and emerging trends across Brazil and South America.
  • Fan Engagement: Support FIFA’s audience growth goals by optimizing acquisition, engagement and retention journeys across owned platforms, as well as contributing to first-party data strategies.
  • Value Generation: Partner closely with Commercial and Marketing teams to develop scalable digital activations and integrations that deliver measurable value to fans and partners alike.
  • Tournament Planning & Delivery: Become an integral enabler of tournament-time digital operations, delivering high-traffic match moments, live content publishing, rapid issue resolution and cross-functional coordination during competition periods.
  • Collaboration: Operate as an integral member of Digital Delivery, working alongside Product Managers located across Europe and acting as the department’s key representative within FIFA27.
  • Governance & Communication: Providing clear, consistent updates to senior management and key stakeholders. Participation in cross-functional steering groups to maintain a balance between critical tournament milestones and product scope delivery.
  • Drive Innovation: Champion a consumer-first mindset; exploring opportunities for innovation and application of AI-driven solutions to enhance content personalization and discovery, improve operational efficiency and create new fan engagement experiences that resonate with fans globally and locally.
  • Data-driven Performance:  Establish and track key performance indicators, using a blend of behavioural insights and user research to inform product development priorities, optimise product performance and exceed tournament goals and objectives.

Education & Qualifications:
  • Bachelor’s degree in computer sciences, business management or related discipline
  • Relevant product development philosophies, e.g. Agile, are essential
  • Relevant project management qualifications, e.g. PRINCE2, highly beneficial
Work Experience:
  • Product management experience, demonstrating end-to-end experience across the digital product lifecycle; from UX/UI design and roadmap development to rigorous testing and release management.
  • Experience working in a direct-to-consumer business, ideally within sports, media or related industries.
  • Experience leading complex projects will be highly beneficial, demonstrating aptitude for balancing personal development of others with empowerment to “get the job done”.
  • Exceptional communication skills - both written and verbal – to succinctly and confidently interact with a wide range of stakeholders up to and including senior FIFA management.
  • Demonstrable experience working autonomously and/or with ambiguity; with an ability to adapt swiftly to organisational change or leverage short-term opportunities.
Competencies:
  • Confidence leading multiple projects with autonomy, often with colleagues and/or suppliers located in different territories and time zones.
  • Passion for developing consumer experiences and thinking big picture; complimented with a demonstrable understanding of digital product development and design principles.
  • Data-driven with an aptitude for harnessing data, forming KPIs, and leveraging consumer insights to make informed decisions.
  • Comfortable working with senior stakeholders and cross-functional teams across FIFA, each with varying degrees of digital maturity and technical understanding.
  • Willingness to travel to other FIFA offices and venues, typically in Zurich, as well as relocating to another location for a period of time, e.g. during FIFA Women’s World Cup in 2027.
Languages:
  • Fluent in Portuguese and English.
  • Proficiency in another official FIFA language, such as Spanish, is a benefit.
Technology:
  • Strong knowledge of digital direct-to-consumer ecosystems, including mobile and video.
  • Solid understanding and passion for digital innovation, including application of AI
  • Experience with industry-standard solutions, e.g. Figma, Asana and Confluence
Requirements:
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Event Controller
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Under the responsibility of the FIFA27 Head of Finance and in collaboration with the FIFA Finance division in Zurich and the Senior Event Controller, the Event Controller will support in Brazil the budget and forecast process throughout the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ project lifecycle providing finance business partnering and support to the various budget owners and to FIFA27 senior management.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Implementation Phase 
    • Assist with the implementation of the FIFA budgeting and reporting tools and organize hands-on training with the budget owners in Brazil. 
    • Prepare cash flow forecast requirements in connection with the Brazilian operational budget. 
  • Planning & Operational Phase 
    • Support in Brazil the financial planning and budget review processes working closely with the FIFA Senior Event Controller (based in Zurich) and with all budget owners (Zurich and Brazil based). 
    • Work closely with the FIFA27 Senior Event Controller, to implement FIFA Finance processes in Brazil and to support a smooth consolidation of the full tournament operational budgets. 
    • Assist the Presentation of the quarterly forecast exercise as well as recommended corrective actions (when necessary) to the various stakeholders. 
    • Support the accrual process at year-end supporting the FIFA Zurich and FIFA27 Accounting teams and the various budget owners. 
    • Assist the review of monthly cost allocations to various project codes and prepare re-allocation journals when required. 
    • Support the preparation monthly management reports providing insightful commentary for budget owners and senior management. 
    • Monitor and maintain monthly cash flow forecasts, reflecting the Brazilian operational budget, to ensure the liquidity needs of FIFA27 are met. 
  • Perform ad-hoc analysis and prepare additional financial reports as requested by the Head of Finance and senior management.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field.  
  • Experience in accounting and Financial Planning & Analysis. 
  • Experience with budgeting and reporting tools. 
  •  Experience in preparing financial statements, preferably in a multi-entity environment. 
  •  Experience in an international environment.  
  •  Experience in procurement of advantage. 
  •  SAP experience preferred. 
  •  Well-developed organisational skills with an ability to prioritize your workload and meet strict deadlines. 
  •  Excellent written and verbal communication skills to facilitate business partnering. 
  •  Have a proactive hands-on approach to problem solving - a 'can do' attitude. 
  •  Strong attention to detail and a focus on quality and accuracy. 
  •  Proficiency in Microsoft Office Excel, Power BI Word & PowerPoint presentation 
  •  Ability to work independently and with a team in a fast-paced and high-volume environment. 
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

- Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field. - Experience in accounting and Financial Planning & Analysis. - Experience with budgeting and reporting tools. - Experience in preparing financial statements, preferably in a multi-entity environment. - Experience in an international environment. - Experience in procurement of advantage. - SAP experience preferred. - Well-developed organisational skills with an ability to prioritize your workload and meet strict deadlines. - Excellent written and verbal communication skills to facilitate business partnering. - Have a proactive hands-on approach to problem solving - a 'can do' attitude. - Strong attention to detail and a focus on quality and accuracy. - Proficiency in Microsoft Office Excel, Power BI Word & PowerPoint presentation - Ability to work independently and with a team in a fast-paced and high-volume environment. - Right to work in Brazil. - Fluent in English (spoken and written) - Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written) - Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Uniforms Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA27 established as the Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil, to work together with FIFA HQ in Zurich and globally, to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”). 


The Uniforms Manager is responsible for the planning and operational delivery of the corporate FIFA27 Business Casual uniform as well as the FWWC2027 Tournament Dedicated and FIFA Formal uniform programmes in line with the overall FIFA Uniform Strategy and the approved usage and entitlement matrix. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Responsible for project management and implementation of the FWWC2027 uniform programme including issuance operations that support both workforce and volunteers
  • Ensure all FIFA27 workforce have uniform according to the entitlement and usage matrix
  • Effectively collaborate with key FIFA Zurich and FIFA27 stakeholders including Workforce Planning, Volunteer Management, Host City Management, Logistics, Accreditation and Partnership Rights Delivery
  • Create strategy, process and flows for the full project plan for FWWC2027 uniform programme capturing risks and mitigations throughout
  • Develop the operational plan, setting up affiliated systems, identify functional distribution spaces, engage with stakeholders, contract local suppliers to deliver the FWWC2027 uniform programme, across all the Uniforms Distribution Centers
  • Oversee FIFA27 uniform budget, including creating accurate forecasts for each FIFA27 related event e.g. FWWC2027 Draw
  • Manage relationships with external uniform suppliers that may be required, ensuring compliance in line with Framework Agreement and enforcing contractual obligations 
  • Recruit and effectively train and manage the uniforms team (both workforce and volunteers) during planning and delivery periods ensuring best practices are clearly defined and the service level provided across all Uniform Distribution Centers is consistent
  • Manage stock levels and keep up-to-date records of each uniform item during pre-tournament (e.g. Business Casual uniform/ formal items that may be required for side events) and tournament time.
  • Communicate the agreed FIFA27 Uniform Guidelines and Usage Policy at each FIFA tournament and event 
  • Ensure the uniform guidelines are upheld and enforced across all client groups including FIFA Formal Uniform eligibility
  • Plan uniform distribution to all related FWW2027 additional events, e.g. Final Draw, 
  • Work with relevant FIFA27 stakeholders to agree and provide guidelines to Suppliers who are not entitled to Tournament Dedicated Uniforms Other duties as necessary to ensure the smooth running of the FWWC2027 Uniform operations. 
  • Experience of 3 years in Uniform Management; preferably with experience in mega (sports) events and corporate organisations  
  • Excellent people manager, motivating, inspiring and seeing results through others
  • Knowledge of event planning and event project management processes 
  • Strong IT skill including MS Office (Word, Excel, Visio, PowerPoint) 
  • Fluency in Brazilian Portuguese and English. Other languages are an added benefit  
  • Strong written and oral communication 
  • Proven track record in administering uniform projects across complex quantity structures 
  • Team Player, willing to work to tight timelines 
  • Positive attitude, patience and persistence, with a pragmatic approach to solving problems
  • Excellent time management skills and ability to balance competing priorities 
  • Demonstrate initiative and a strong focus on providing a high level of service 
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

Work with relevant FIFA27 stakeholders to agree and provide guidelines to Suppliers who are not entitled to Tournament Dedicated Uniforms Other duties as necessary to ensure the smooth running of the FWWC2027 Uniform operations.
- Experience of 3 years in Uniform Management; preferably with experience in mega (sports) events and corporate organisations - Excellent people manager, motivating, inspiring and seeing results through others - Knowledge of event planning and event project management processes - Strong IT skill including MS Office (Word, Excel, Visio, PowerPoint) - Fluency in Brazilian Portuguese and English. Other languages are an added benefit - Strong written and oral communication - Proven track record in administering uniform projects across complex quantity structures - Team Player, willing to work to tight timelines - Positive attitude, patience and persistence, with a pragmatic approach to solving problems - Excellent time management skills and ability to balance competing priorities - Demonstrate initiative and a strong focus on providing a high level of service - Right to work in Brazil. - Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Wayfinding and Signage Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Wayfinding and Signage Manager reports to the Dressing and Signage Senior Manager, serving as a key member of the FIFA27 Operations team in close collaboration with the Venue Management team and other stakeholders.

The FIFA Women's World Cup Brazil 2027™ requires outstanding look and feel project to be delivered in a seamless tournament experience. The Wayfinding and Signage Manager is responsible for leading the wayfinding and signage implementation, operations and delivery strategy from planning through implementation to closure across all FIFA Official Sites.
This position is responsible for overseeing the delivery of a world class wayfinding and signage project for all target groups attending matches of the FIFA Women's World Cup Brazil 2027™, ensuring that the wayfinding and signage is up to FIFA quality standards. 
They will be initially responsible for the technical development of wayfinding signage guidelines and principles in collaboration with the contracted provider(s), department senior team and the other Cluster Managers. Subsequently, the candidate will be responsible for directly overseeing the wayfinding signage planning and implementation at stadiums and other official sites. 

In all aspects of their role, they will ensure that key project deliverables and deadlines are met and will be required to input into strategic project decisions taken by senior members of the team.  They will oversee contracted provider operations, ensuring that they are managed efficiently while monitoring budgetary spend within agreed limits.  In addition, they will take guidance from other cluster managers to ensure that precedents and strategic directives from the Head of Dressing and Signage and Senior Dressing and Signage Manager are followed as required by the members of the team.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Contributing to the delivery of a world class dressing and signage project for all target groups attending matches of the FIFA Women’s World Cup Brazil 2027™. 
  • Providing expertise in assisting in the compilation of technical wayfinding signage guidelines, principles and templates. 
  • Inputting into the development of the wayfinding signage system(s) used to assist with the planning of the project. 
  • Providing strategic input into the development and implementation of the wayfinding signage kit of parts to be utilized at all official sites. 
  • Overseeing the wayfinding signage planning and implementation at the FIFA Women’s World Cup Brazil 2027™ stadiums in Brazil. 
  • Working with other Cluster Managers ensuring that precedents are followed to guarantee consistency across all tournament stadiums. 
  • Oversight of contracted provider planning, production and implementation for all sites within the sites.
  • Constant monitoring of the budgetary impact of allocated wayfinding operations and reporting updates to senior department members.
  • Adhering to project deadlines and deliverables as instructed by senior members of the department.
  • Daily liaison with other members of the wider Dressing and Signage team to ensure efficient planning and delivery is optimized at all stages of the project. 
  • Liaising with site owners and operators as required to gain approval on implementation plans. 
  • Attending department-specific and organization site visits at both assigned sites and potentially additional official sites. 
  • Acting as the main wayfinding point of contact for Dressing and Signage Venue Managers and other Functional Area Venue Managers during the implementation phase of the project. 
  • Assisting with the recruitment of Dressing and Signage project team members in collaboration with the senior department leads if required.
  • Ensuring the needs of internal FIFA functional areas are captured and delivered against. 
  • Contribution to project management and knowledge management initiatives including event debriefs, and knowledge capture and documentation activities.
  • Ability to possibly be relocated to a Host City for up to three months in to act as a Dressing and Signage Venue Manager at an allocated stadium during the event periods.
  • Relevant experience in delivering wayfinding and signage projects for events of a similar scale to FIFA World Cups, Olympic Games etc. and experience managing major sports events in a role that included the delivery of comprehensive wayfinding and signage related activities. 
  • Experience in devising technical guidelines and wayfinding signage kit of parts for large-scale events. 
  • A working understanding of the technical aspects of delivering dressing and signage projects (i.e. production methods, installation techniques, materials etc.)
  • Strong understanding of stadium wayfinding and signage requirements and large-scale temporary installations.
  • Budget management experience on complex and multi-faceted events and projects. 
  • Experience in managing providers of services for world-class events.
  • Knowledge of event planning, requirements and event project management processes for large sporting events.
  • Significant stakeholder management experience across multiple organizations and departments. 
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to assess and implement effective solutions.
  • Bachelor’s or master’s degree in appropriate subject (or adequate training/vocational education).
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools.
  • The role will require extensive use of the appointed Dressing and Signage Supplier(s) project, content and scoping management systems so a readiness to learn new technology processes, systems and programs is essential.
  • The role will require attending site visits and being away from home for periods of time, including on-site attendance during the tournament period.
    Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Workforce Training Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Reporting to the Senior Manager Workforce Operations, the Workforce Training Manager will be a key member of the FIFA27 Workforce Management team, and work in close collaboration with operational delivery Functional Areas within FIFA27, as well as People & Culture in FIFA Zurich, and other key individuals responsible for delivering a cohesive, consistent, and operationally appropriate training programme for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
The main responsibilities and oversights of the Workforce Training Manager for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ include:  
 
Training Coordination and Project Management:   
  • Develop and implement a comprehensive training plan that aligns with tournament deliverables/objectives and Functional Area requirements.  
  • Coordinate with department heads, trainers/training providers, and other stakeholders to assess and define the training needs strategy. 
  • Establish a coordinated and integrated training journey for the tournament workforce, across multiple client groups from planning and development to execution and evaluation.  
  • In collaboration with FA Leads training delivery mechanisms for whole client group training sessions and support/provide guidance to Functional Areas on appropriate training schedules, venues, and resources to ensure timely and effective delivery of training programs.  
  • Ensure training programs are consistent, culturally appropriate, and tailored to meet the specific needs of different regions 
  • Manage the creation and distribution of template training materials, including accessibility considerations, such as multiple languages.  
  • Track and report on the progress of training initiatives, managing any changes, delays, or risks.  
  • Ensure alignment with the event's objectives, ensuring that efficient and effective training delivery supports the overall success of the Tournament being delivered. 
Stakeholder Management:   
  • Collaborate with internal teams (Workforce, Planning & Integration, Volunteer Management, HR, Operations, Security, Technology, etc.), external vendors, and consultants to facilitate consistent training delivery. 
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for all organisation-wide training-related planning, inquiries and concerns.  
  • Regularly communicate with leadership to provide updates on the progress of training initiatives and address any challenges.  
Monitoring and Evaluation:   
  • In collaboration with the Senior Manager Workforce Operations, oversee budget requirements for organizational training initiatives, ensuring efficiencies are kept at front of mind.  
  • Work in close collaboration with Planning & Integration to create tools that support the effective evaluation of training programs through feedback, assessments, and performance metrics.  
  • Adjust training plans and methods based on feedback from trainees and trainers to ensure continuous improvement from one Tournament to the next.  
  • Prepare detailed reports on training outcomes, including participant performance, satisfaction, and areas for improvement.
Education
  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent level of education.

Experience
  • Solid experience designing and delivering Training/Learning and Development programmes in a fast-paced environment. 
  • Demonstrated experience of utilising strong organizational skills and attention to detail to switch from strategic to detailed planning. 
  • Significant experience designing and delivering Training/L&D programmes within the Sporting Industry/large-scale events. 

Certifications & Requirements
  • Right to work in Brazil
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Workforce Planning & Operations Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Working alongside the FIFA Workforce Management team based in Zurich and reporting to the FIFA27 Head of Workforce, the Manager, Workforce Planning & Operations is responsible for executing all workforce planning phases and processes, ensures the organisation has the right talent in the right roles at the right time.

Workforce Strategy & Planning
• Support the implementation of workforce planning and operations for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027 (FWWC2027)
• Coordinate and execute workforce planning activities across all workforce types (paid staff, freelancers, tournament-time hires, secondees, etc.)
• Work closely with the FIFA Workforce Management team to support an integrated planning process, ensuring resources align with operational needs and service levels
• Assist in defining workforce categories and supporting the development of entitlements for each group
• Maintain and update the centralised headcount plan, ensuring data accuracy and accessibility
• Support change management activities to enable continuity in workforce planning and execution
• Contribute inputs to workforce budgets, timelines, and planning cycles

Workforce Operations & Management
• Help develop and execute operational plans for workforce delivery during tournament time, including supporting site operations
• Assist in defining requirements for physical spaces, overlay, technology, and other workforce-related needs on site
• Develop workforce policies & procedures with key operational stakeholders (transport, accommodation, catering etc.) and core central business areas such as HR, technology, finance. 
• Define and map out key workforce planning & operational processes, understanding the cross-functional collaboration required.
• Support the coordination of business processes and system requirements for workforce operations tools
• Deliver role-specific and venue-specific training for workforce operations teams
• Support the day-to-day supervision, scheduling and coordination of workforce operations teams, including workforce and volunteers
• Provide operational support to venue managers during event delivery

  • Work Experience
    • Experience in workforce planning or operations, ideally within international events and/or the sports industry
    • Good working knowledge of workforce planning systems, tools, and practices
    • Experience supporting or coordinating operational venue teams at large-scale events
    • Understanding of data-driven decision-making and workforce planning processes in complex environments
    • Demonstrated ability to manage projects, prioritise tasks effectively, and work with a level of independence
    • Experience supervising team members and contributing to team performance through positive leadership and collaboration
    • Strong communication skills, both written and verbal
    • Demonstrated adaptability, with experience supporting discussions, problem-solving, and stakeholder coordination
    • Experience working in a multicultural and/or multinational environment
    • Fluent in English, both written and spoken  
    • Fluent/Advanced Portuguese

  • Technology
    • Proficient in MS Office tools (Word, PowerPoint, Visio & advanced Excel) 
    • Proficiency in using project planning tools such as WeTrack and Monday.com
    • Experience with workforce system implementations advantageous

- Work Experience • Experience in workforce planning or operations, ideally within international events and/or the sports industry • Good working knowledge of workforce planning systems, tools, and practices • Experience supporting or coordinating operational venue teams at large-scale events • Understanding of data-driven decision-making and workforce planning processes in complex environments • Demonstrated ability to manage projects, prioritise tasks effectively, and work with a level of independence • Experience supervising team members and contributing to team performance through positive leadership and collaboration • Strong communication skills, both written and verbal • Demonstrated adaptability, with experience supporting discussions, problem-solving, and stakeholder coordination • Experience working in a multicultural and/or multinational environment • Fluent in English, both written and spoken • Fluent/Advanced Portuguese - Technology • Proficient in MS Office tools (Word, PowerPoint, Visio & advanced Excel) • Proficiency in using project planning tools such as WeTrack and Monday.com • Experience with workforce system implementations advantageous

job.title: Dressing & Signage Cluster Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Dressing & Signage Cluster Manager reports to the Dressing and Signage Senior Manager, serving as a key member of the FIFA27 Operations team in close collaboration with the Venue Management team and other stakeholders. The FIFA Women's World Cup Brazil 2027™ requires outstanding look and feel project to be delivered in a seamless tournament experience. The Dressing & Signage Cluster Manager is responsible for leading the venue branding, look and signage operations and delivery strategy from planning through implementation to closure across all FIFA Official Sites. This position is responsible for overseeing the delivery of a world class dressing and signage project for all target groups attending matches of the FIFA Women's World Cup Brazil 2027™, ensuring that the look and feel is up to FIFA quality standards.  

The role assumes event operational proficiency, venue dressing, signage and wayfinding knowledge with project management skills to directly overseeing the dressing implementation at four FIFA Women’s World Cup Brazil 2027™ stadia. They will ensure that key project deliverables and deadlines are met within their sites and will be required to input into strategic project decisions taken by senior members of the team. They will oversee contracted provider operations within their sites, ensuring that all are managed successfully and efficiently while monitoring budgetary spending within agreed limits. They will ensure that precedents and strategic directives from the Head of Dressing and Signage and Senior Dressing and Signage Manager are followed as required by the members of the team. The role will collaborate closely with other dressing, signage, wayfinding and non-competition managers to maximize creativity and ensure consistency across all stadiums. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Overseeing the turnkey planning of dressing and signage implementation across all official sites during planning phase, and stadium dressing for 3-5 stadia closer to the tournament. 
  • Collaborating with the other Stadium Dressing Managers and ensuring that precedents are followed to guarantee consistency across all tournament stadiums.  
  • Working closely with the Non-Competition Dressing Managers and the Wayfinding and Signage Managers as well as other Functional Area Managers, to maximize efficiencies across the wider project.  
  • Being the focal point for all general stadium dressing questions or requests from the Head of Department.  
  • Oversight of contracted provider planning, production and implementation for all stadiums within the assigned sites. 
  • Constant monitoring of the budgetary impact of dressing operations and reporting updates to the Senior Manager and Head of Department regularly. 
  • Providing strategic input into the stadium dressing planning process and offering expertise when required.  
  • Adhering to project deadlines and deliverables as instructed by senior members of the department. 
  • Daily liaison with other members of the wider Dressing and Signage team to ensure efficient planning and delivery is optimized at all stages of the project. 
  • Liaising with stadium owners and operators as required to gain approval on implementation plans. 
  • Presenting to senior team members, management and other FIFA Women’s World Cup Brazil 2027™ functional areas as and when required. 
  • Attending department-specific and organization site visits at assigned stadiums and other stadiums or official sites. 
  • Inputting into the site-specific dressing strategies utilizing expertise from previous events and directives from management. 
  • Acting as the main stadium dressing point of contact for Dressing and Signage Venue Managers and other Functional Area Venue Managers within the assigned sites during the implementation phase of the project.  
  • Participating in the creative design process for stadiums within the assigned sites. 
  •  Assisting with the recruitment of Dressing and Signage project team members in collaboration with the senior department leads if required. 
  •  Ensuring the needs of internal FIFA functional areas are captured and delivered against within the assigned sites. 
  • Contribution to project management and knowledge management initiatives including event debriefs, and knowledge capture and documentation activities. 
  • Ability to possibly be relocated to a Host City for up to three months in to act as a Dressing and Signage Venue Manager at an allocated stadium during the event periods. 
  • Experience in delivering dressing, signage, branding or look projects for events of a similar scale to FIFA World Cups, Olympic Games etc. or a minimum 2 years’ experience managing major sports events in a role that included the delivery of comprehensive dressing or signage related activities.  
  • A working understanding of the technical aspects of delivering dressing and signage projects (i.e. production methods, installation techniques, materials etc.). 
  • Strong understanding of stadium look and feel requirements and large-scale temporary installations. 
  • Budget management experience on complex and multi-faceted events and projects.  
  • Experience in managing providers of services for world-class events. 
  • Knowledge of event planning, requirements and event project management processes for large sporting events. 
  • Significant stakeholder management experience across multiple organizations and departments.  
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to assess and implement effective solutions. 
  • Bachelor’s or master’s degree in appropriate subject (or adequate training/vocational education) Design, Architecture, Project Management, or a related field. 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools. 
  • The role will require extensive use of the appointed Dressing and Signage Supplier(s) project, content and scoping management systems so a readiness to learn new technology processes, systems and programs is essential. 
  • The role will require attending site visits and being away from home for periods of time, including on-site attendance during the tournament period. 
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

Providing strategic input into the stadium dressing planning process and offering expertise when required. - Adhering to project deadlines and deliverables as instructed by senior members of the department. - Daily liaison with other members of the wider Dressing and Signage team to ensure efficient planning and delivery is optimized at all stages of the project. - Liaising with stadium owners and operators as required to gain approval on implementation plans. - Presenting to senior team members, management and other FIFA Women’s World Cup Brazil 2027™ functional areas as and when required. - Attending department-specific and organization site visits at assigned stadiums and other stadiums or official sites. - Inputting into the site-specific dressing strategies utilizing expertise from previous events and directives from management. - Acting as the main stadium dressing point of contact for Dressing and Signage Venue Managers and other Functional Area Venue Managers within the assigned sites during the implementation phase of the project. - Participating in the creative design process for stadiums within the assigned sites. - Assisting with the recruitment of Dressing and Signage project team members in collaboration with the senior department leads if required. - Ensuring the needs of internal FIFA functional areas are captured and delivered against within the assigned sites. - Contribution to project management and knowledge management initiatives including event debriefs, and knowledge capture and documentation activities. - Ability to possibly be relocated to a Host City for up to three months in to act as a Dressing and Signage Venue Manager at an allocated stadium during the event periods.
- Experience in delivering dressing, signage, branding or look projects for events of a similar scale to FIFA World Cups, Olympic Games etc. or a minimum 2 years’ experience managing major sports events in a role that included the delivery of comprehensive dressing or signage related activities. - A working understanding of the technical aspects of delivering dressing and signage projects (i.e. production methods, installation techniques, materials etc.). - Strong understanding of stadium look and feel requirements and large-scale temporary installations. - Budget management experience on complex and multi-faceted events and projects. - Experience in managing providers of services for world-class events. - Knowledge of event planning, requirements and event project management processes for large sporting events. - Significant stakeholder management experience across multiple organizations and departments. - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to assess and implement effective solutions. - Bachelor’s or master’s degree in appropriate subject (or adequate training/vocational education) Design, Architecture, Project Management, or a related field. - Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools. - The role will require extensive use of the appointed Dressing and Signage Supplier(s) project, content and scoping management systems so a readiness to learn new technology processes, systems and programs is essential. - The role will require attending site visits and being away from home for periods of time, including on-site attendance during the tournament period. - Right to work in Brazil. - Fluent in English (spoken and written) - Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written) - Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Hospitality Operations & Infrastructure Venue Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Sitting within the Ticketing & Hospitality Department, Hospitality (along with Ticketing) aims to deliver the most engaging and innovative FIFA Women's World Cup 2027™ experience for all commercial partners, customers, guests and the FIFA football family.

Reporting organizationally to the Head of FWWC27 Hospitality Operations, the Hospitality Operations & Infrastructure Venue Manager will help serve Hospitality efforts for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ that is hosting 32 teams across 8 venues.

This role will be responsible for the supervision of the implementation of all stadium Hospitality infrastructure requirements and technical operations across all stadiums of the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™, and later assuming a fixed position as Rio de Janeiro Stadium Hospitality Operations & Infrastructure Venue Manager during tournament time. She/he will work closely with the Official Hospitality Provider and FIFA’s stadium operations and infrastructure functional areas to ensure contractual and requirements compliance.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Responsible for the management and operational readiness and delivery of the Affiliates and Commercial Hospitality programmes, reporting duties, maintenance/creation of operational and infrastructure documentations, etc
  • Ensuring Infrastructure readiness and maintenance services of Hospitality areas, in close cooperation with the FIFA other functional areas, the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP) and Stadium Authorities
  • Responsible for the on-site management of the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP) and their suppliers, and Hospitality Volunteers
  • Checking and validating the Operational plans from the service-providing departments to Hospitality (Cleaning and Waste, Security, Medical, Signage, Transport, Fan Operations, Accreditation, Power, IT, TV, ticketing, etc.)
  • Ensuring excellent service to the FIFA Affiliates on site, overlooking the set-up and readiness of their facilities, monitoring service delivery from the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP) from the set-up phase through to match day delivery of services
  • Ensuring contractual deliverables to the FIFA Commercial Affiliates and to the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP)
  • Overall on-site hospitality guest management
  • Ensure service consistency throughout the different hospitality programmes across all matches in the venue
  • Validating all on-site hospitality directional signage
  • Ensuring Health and Safety regulations are strictly observed and respected by the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP)
  • Ensuring Hospitality guest numbers are updated and shared with all relevant FIFA stakeholders
  • Ensuring that all stakeholders are appropriately accredited. Manage the distribution of SADs (supplementary access devices) to all relevant functional areas, restricting access to hospitality areas as needed
  • Ensure the catering service runs as expected in the Affiliates and Commercial Hospitality programmes
  • Handling all hospitality related issues on Match Day
  • Attend the FIFA Daily Coordination Meeting at the Venue and hold daily Hospitality Team Meetings with the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP)
  • Reporting on a daily basis to the FIFA HQ, reporting issues in good time
  • Bachelor’s Degree or similar level of education in Event Management, Sports Hospitality Management, Project Management, etc.
  • Experience in stadium hospitality or venue management, managing several venues simultaneously, preferably for major sports or entertainment events.
  • Proven experience with event overlay (including provision of temporary power and water), ticketing, dressing and signage, catering, logistics and security operations
  • Proven experience managing multiple service providers, particularly for Technical services, Cleaning and Waste services, Security Services, Access control Services, Accreditation, etc.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Strong customer-service attitude, even when under pressure.
  • Ability to work independently and in a team across simultaneous work streams.
  • Exquisite organizational skills.
  • Ability to lead, manage, and motivate a team.
  • Fluent in Portuguese and English. Additional languages considered an asset.
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
  • Excellent knowledge of AutoCAD and literacy of architectural drawings

Additional languages considered an asset. - Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools - Excellent knowledge of AutoCAD and literacy of architectural drawings

job.title: Legal Counsel Commercial Legal
job.html_description:
We govern the beautiful game and ensure it's run with transparency and integrity.

Join our team in Miami and support us on our mission.
The Legal Counsel will be an active member of the Commercial Legal Team working with various Senior Legal Counsels, the Head of Content & Media and the Director of Commercial Legal to
support the delivery of legal services relating to FIFA’s global operations.

The role of Legal Counsel will support some, or all, of the business functions within FIFA’s global operations, such as the following:

  • Assisting the commercial teams by advising on their everyday operations
  • Assisting in drafting, negotiating and advising on a broad range of commercial and media partnerships matters connected with FIFA’s revenue generating activities
  • Providing support regarding the interpretation of a range of media, rights, marketing, IP, retail and licensing, agency appointments, consumer promotions and technology agreements
  • Assisting in providing legal support related to Web3 activities, social media, digital content, gaming and e-sports related matters
  • Providing legal and contractual support to FIFA’s strategic and corporate development activities
  • Providing legal and contractual support on the exploitation of commercial opportunities on FIFA’s digital platforms
  • Supporting FIFA’s subsidiaries, including the FIFA Museum and the FIFA Foundation regarding the exploitation of commercial opportunities within FIFA’s commercial framework
  • Providing advice, guidance and support for all legal, regulatory and governance matters relating to FIFA’s commercial media activities
  • Providing legal support and counsel on development and production deal structures and documentation for internal and third-party projects, including advising on pre-production and coproduction deals
  • Assisting in managing, developing and maintaining FIFA's portfolio of non-live content related templates
  • Assisting and providing support for the FIFA Technology team in support of the upcoming major FIFA tournaments worldwide
  • Assisting and providing support for the FIFA Innovation team in support of the upcoming major FIFA tournaments worldwide
  • Providing legal support for music related matters (both live and recorded music)
  • Instructing and liaising with external counsel in connection with local law matters in various jurisdictions
  • Developing and maintaining FIFA’s template agreements
We work hard at FIFA.
We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.

And we respect our values. Always.

For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.

This role would suit someone with great communication skills who is a dedicated team player and excels in being professional with excellent legal skills.

The specific competencies we require for this position are:
  • Ability to participate and be part of a high-performing team, fostering collaboration, driving results, and ensuring the successful achievement of organizational goals
  • First class written and spoken English language skills, consistent with drafting and negotiating complex, high value and nuanced English language agreements
  • Ability to provide and communicate effectively practical and solutions-based legal advice
  • Excellent understanding of the sports marketing, sports merchandising, licensing, retail, digital marketing, sponsorship, licensing, e-commerce and business intelligence industry landscapes
  • Excellent negotiation, drafting, problem solving and analytical skills
  • Structured, reliable, with a strong eye for detail
  • Ability to perform consistently within a high-performing team, fostering collaboration, driving results, and ensuring the successful achievement of organizational goals
  • Strong commercial acumen with an ability to transfer legal knowledge into commercially and operationally oriented solutions
  • Proactive, with a strong sense of personal responsibility and an ability to work autonomously on a wide variety of projects
  • Resilient with an ability to competently handle a large workload amid rapidly shifting priorities
  • Open-minded team player with an affinity for working within a variety of cultures and excellent communication skills
  • High degree of professionalism and confidence
  • Ability to be a good ambassador for FIFA in all manner of external facing situations, including during contractual negotiations, external workshops and during FIFA competition-related assignments
  • Ability to create, develop and maintain strong and reliable relationships with FIFA senior management as well as external legal and commercial counterparts
  • Willingness to operate outside of comfort zone
  • Takes initiative to reflect on how to improve and develop working styles and methods to assist internal clients
  • Juris Doctor (JD) degree from an accredited law school
  • Active bar membership is required
  • 4-6 years expertise in an in-house legal department or a top-tier law firm focusing on technology, media, sports or IP matters
  • Native level English (or equivalent), consistent with requirement to draft and negotiate complex and nuanced English language agreements and advice
  • Proficiency in any other FIFA language (French/Spanish/Portuguese) is an asset but not required
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office (Outlook / Word / PowerPoint / Excel / Teams
  • Comfortable with international travel
The biggest benefit of a career at FIFA is the privilege to work for the beautiful game and create something that matters.
 
Alongside that privilege, we also offer some extra perks and benefits:
 
Health insurance: We offer generous medical, vision, and dental insurance coverage.
Continuous learning: We care about your development and encourage internal mobility, offering a variety of training and education to help achieve it.
Retirement: We offer generous 401(k) employer contributions.
Vacation: Maintain a healthy work/life balance with a generous vacation allowance.

job.title: Legal Assistant
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Legal Assistant will be under the guidance of FWWC 2027 the Head of Legal FWWC 2027 and will provide general legal support for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ legal team in Brazil. 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


  • Prepare, review and compile PowerPoint presentations and internal documents.
  • Maintain and update legal trackers and databases, including regular updates on Wetrack tool.
  • Provide translation and proof reading of legal and operational documents.
  • Assist with document execution processes, including verification of powers and coordination of signatures. 
  • Support the preparation of egal documents and templates.
  • Assist the legal team in monitoring deadlines and follow-ups related to contracts and legal matters.
  • Support internal and external audits by organizing and compiling requested documentation.
  • Assist in monitoring relevant legislative changes.
  • Demonstrate a team-based approach to achieving the best outcomes for FWWC 2027 in accordance with FIFA’s values.
  • Support interactions with internal and external stakeholders.
  • Ensure that assigned tasks are delivered with a high level of accuracy, timeliness and attention to detail.

Qualifications:
  • Bachelor of Laws
  • Qualified to practice law in Brazil 
  • Fluency in English

Experience:
  • Experience in a law firm or legal department, including experience gained through internships or trainee positions.
  • Prior experience in a reputable law firm is considered an advantage.
  • Excellent writing skills.
  • Strong analytical and organizational skills.
Other knowledge and skills:
  • Proficiency in MS Office applications (including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, MS Teams).
  • High attention to detail, accuracy, and follow-through.
  • Skilled in problem solving, solution-oriented and pragmatic mindset.
  • Team player with the ability to build effective relationships internally and externally.
  • Empathetic, collaborative and patient, with a positive and proactive attitude.
  • Ability to communicate effectively.
  • Experience with sporting or events organisations or commercial rights holders/sponsorship is considered an advantage.
  • Strong identification with the FIFA World Cup and its potential positive impact on the Host Country.
  • Passion for football is a plus.
Requiriments:
  • Right to work in Brazil.
  • Fluency in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluency in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to relocate to Rio de Janeiro.
