- Right to work in Brazil. - Fluency in English (spoken and written) - Fluency in Portuguese (spoken and written) - Ability to relocate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Senior Legal Counsel Ethics
job.html_description:
We govern the beautiful game and ensure it's run with transparency and integrity.

Join our team in Miami and support us on our mission.
These are your key tasks:
  • Conducting autonomously ethics proceedings in accordance with the FIFA Code of Ethics;
  • Investigating ethics proceedings, including the review and analysis of financial records, the use of forensic audit techniques, and the development of financial investigative strategies;
  • Drafting communications and request for information to the interested/accused parties within an ethics proceeding;
  • Supporting the preparation of witness interviews and witness statements, and, when necessary, coordinating such tasks with other departments or external experts within the context of ethics proceedings;
  • Liaising with FIFA departments and subject-matter experts to secure support for FIFA Ethics Committee matters;
  • Supporting the relevant chairperson and members of the Ethics Committee by preparing case files, legal analyses, and draft decisions to facilitate effective deliberations;
  • Assisting the relevant chairperson of the FIFA Ethics Committee in the preparation of meetings;
  • Assessment of legal aid requests, witness protection, witness/victim’s psychological support, and when necessary, coordinating such tasks with other departments or external experts within the ethics proceedings;
  • Drafting final Reports within an ethics proceeding;
  • Analyzing testimonials and other documentary evidence, including financial and audit records, to weigh its credibility and probative value within the context and standards of the FIFA Code of Ethics;
  • Conduct legal analysis of FIFA precedent and CAS jurisprudence to support the drafting of final investigation reports and Ethics Committee decisions;
  • Drafting decisions of the Adjudicatory Chamber;
  • Contributing to the ongoing development of workflows, templates, and best practices to ensure efficiency and consistency;
  • Identify and recommend measures to prevent violations of the FIFA Code of Ethics and promote the safeguarding and integrity of football worldwide;
  • Close collaboration and support to the Head of Department in creating efficient ethics proceedings (i.e. determining processes, workflow, template creation, etc.);
  • Ensuring a worldwide recognition of the activity of the Investigation Department and the Judicial Bodies Subdivision;
  • Assistance in preparing any report related to the activities of the ethics department, including newsletters, status reports, etc.
  • Holding and participating in workshops with FIFA employees, member associations or confederations on Judicial Bodies-related issues.
We work hard at FIFA.
We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.

And we respect our values. Always.

For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.

The specific competencies we require for this position are:
  • Excellent legal drafting and presentation skills in English;
  • Proficiency legal drafting in Spanish or French is an asset;
  • Good knowledge of football governance as well as of FIFA’s regulations, and proven experience in FIFA’s Disciplinary and Ethics proceedings;
  • Ability to conduct analysis and draw out conclusions;
  • Strong ability to analyze financial data, audit findings, and forensic accounting reports to detect and understand irregularities;
  • Excellent editorial skills;
  • Good planning and organisational skills;
  • Solid IT-know how;
  • Ability to work unsupervised and precisely;
  • Ability to work well under pressure and to respect deadlines;
  • Good analytical skills;
  • Conscientiousness, accuracy and quality;
  • Punctuality;
  • Proactive approach;
  • Rigorous and consistent, particularly when handling large amounts of documents;
  • Team player with good interpersonal relations;
  • Excellent communication skills;
  • Cooperative and team-oriented mindset, fostering transparent communication and knowledge sharing;
  • Motivated, autodidact, dynamic and proactive personality;
  • Ability to adapt to changing priorities;
  • Agility;
  • Ability to adapt to an international and multicultural environment;
  • Pleasant and empathetic;
  • Law University degree (Bar Exam);
  • Post-Graduate in Sport law is an asset;
  •  Training or certification in white-collar investigations (including forensic accounting, CPA, CFE, etc.) or prior experience in an audit firm is an asset;
  • Minimum 6-8 years of experience in similar positions, dealing with disciplinary and ethics regulations matters in a sports governing body;
  •  Experience handling cases of misappropriation of funds, bribery and corruption, and working alongside compliance, finance teams, or external auditors, is a strong asset;
  • Native/C2 in English;
  • Proficiency in any other FIFA language (F / ES) is an asset;
  • Proficient in Word / PowerPoint / Excel/Jira;
  • Ability to leverage AI tools to enhance investigative efficiency while ensuring accuracy, confidentiality, and compliance with applicable legal and ethical standards.
The biggest benefit of a career at FIFA is the privilege to work for the beautiful game and create something that matters.
 
Alongside that privilege, we also offer some extra perks and benefits:
 
Health insurance: We offer generous medical, vision, and dental insurance coverage.
Continuous learning: We care about your development and encourage internal mobility, offering a variety of training and education to help achieve it.
Retirement: We offer generous 401(k) employer contributions.
Vacation: Maintain a healthy work/life balance with a generous vacation allowance.

job.title: Venue Technology Manager (Recife)
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Venue Technology Manager is responsible for supporting the Technology team with venue specific technology related deliverables for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027 TM (FWWC 2027) within Brazil. The role entails coordinating with all relevant parties the planning, implementation, readiness and operations of all Event Technology services pertinent to the tournament and / or associated events within Brazil.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Responsible to ensure all aspects of Technology, Telecommunications and Broadcast Technology Services are implemented in accordance with the pre-agreed FIFA Technology requirements at the assigned venue(s)
  • Manage a strong diverse Venue Technology team composed of paid staff, contractors and volunteers for operating the technology services at the assigned venue(s) 
  • Collaborate with stakeholders’ groups, clients, providers, and government entities in relation to ensuring the delivery of technology services across all venues, in accordance with the defined governance structure
  • Manage and contribute to the venue technology working groups in accordance with the agreed governance structure across all aspects of the tournament programme (planning, implementation, readiness and operations) 
  • Manage the technology demand / needs requirements for technology services across the tournament space (Technology Equipment Allocation Process) 
  • Plan the delivery of suitable end user technology equipment and services to all FWWC 2027 departments and FIFA constituent groups, including hardware, services and consumables 
  • Facilitate the collection and distribution of technology requirements of all client groups involved in the FWWC 2027 across all client groups, including, Media, Broadcast, Commercial, Football Technology etc. 
  • Ensure that all information pertaining to infrastructure, services and spaces needed for the delivery and support of all technology services are communicated to, and understood by, venue/site authorities and respective delivery teams 
  • Provide guidance and direction to relevant teams on matters relating to the structural build-out and technology services implementation, including the commissioning and final decommissioning of tournament venues  
  • In collaboration with assigned personnel and Stadium partners, relevant parties (private and governmental) enable / support the timely delivery of venue technology services. 
  • Identify and report on the progress of all critical dependencies in accordance with the reporting methodologies. 
  • Manage the asset tracking and management of deployed technology equipment 
  • Track the progress of structural build-out and IT implementation leading up to the timely availability of components and services and including the commissioning and final decommissioning of the venues. 
  • Develop regular progress, risk and issue management reports in accordance with the governance structure / reporting methodologies and, or PMO systems 
  •  Follow-up with various stakeholders, providers, and government entities in relation to ensuring delivery of technology services / dependencies as per agreed requirements, service levels etc. 
  • Support and ensure the team adhere to the IT Service management protocols 
  • Ensure positive attitude from the Venue Technology teams and good relationship with all the parties, including FIFA teams, Local FIFA Subsidiary (LFS) areas/projects, end-users, suppliers, Host Country/ City counterparts
  • Manage the on-site IT Technicians and support teams during tournament time operations, including end-of-day reporting and incident escalation to Technology Command Centre. 
Education
  • University degree or similar higher education, in Information Technology, Computer Science or related field
Experience
  • Overall IT experience and in a similar role
  • Experience in the Sports / major event industry is a must
  • Proven track record of successful delivery of complex IT programs and projects
Skills & Competencies
  • Manager with proven track record in similar projects
  • Good knowledge of venue technology services across major sporting events 
  • Have up to date knowledge and project management expertise in all event related technologies, including, all types of technology business solutions, community access TV (CATV), radio communications, wired communication, printing, broadcast contribution network, spectrum, audio and visual. 
  • Ability to always maintain a professional demeanor. Consistently interacts professionally and works productively with all levels of employee. 
  • Excellent cross group collaboration skills and demonstrated ability to lead virtual teams across groups, conflict management and driving consensus to deliver on program milestones  
  • Good Leadership skills and able to manage and motivate other team members 
  • Highly adept at consulting, negotiating, communicating, consensus building and facilitation
  • Excellent verbal and written communication, training, and presentation skills
  • Ability to adapt speech to audience coming from a broad spectrum of disciplines and with various levels of technical knowledge
  • Leadership and accountability across all areas of relevance  
  • Strong political acumen and adaptable to a wide range of working environments
Certifications & Requirements
  • Right to work in Brazil 
  • Fluent in English
  • Native or Fluent in Portuguese
  • Proficiency in any other FIFA language (FR / ES / GER) is an asset
Location: Recife, PE - Brazil
job.plain_text_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.
Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Venue Technology Manager is responsible for supporting the Technology team with venue specific technology related deliverables for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027 TM (FWWC 2027) within Brazil. The role entails coordinating with all relevant parties the planning, implementation, readiness and operations of all Event Technology services pertinent to the tournament and / or associated events within Brazil.
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
- Responsible to ensure all aspects of Technology, Telecommunications and Broadcast Technology Services are implemented in accordance with the pre-agreed FIFA Technology requirements at the assigned venue(s) - Manage a strong diverse Venue Technology team composed of paid staff, contractors and volunteers for operating the technology services at the assigned venue(s) - Collaborate with stakeholders’ groups, clients, providers, and government entities in relation to ensuring the delivery of technology services across all venues, in accordance with the defined governance structure - Manage and contribute to the venue technology working groups in accordance with the agreed governance structure across all aspects of the tournament programme (planning, implementation, readiness and operations) - Manage the technology demand / needs requirements for technology services across the tournament space (Technology Equipment Allocation Process) - Plan the delivery of suitable end user technology equipment and services to all FWWC 2027 departments and FIFA constituent groups, including hardware, services and consumables - Facilitate the collection and distribution of technology requirements of all client groups involved in the FWWC 2027 across all client groups, including, Media, Broadcast, Commercial, Football Technology etc. - Ensure that all information pertaining to infrastructure, services and spaces needed for the delivery and support of all technology services are communicated to, and understood by, venue/site authorities and respective delivery teams - Provide guidance and direction to relevant teams on matters relating to the structural build-out and technology services implementation, including the commissioning and final decommissioning of tournament venues - In collaboration with assigned personnel and Stadium partners, relevant parties (private and governmental) enable / support the timely delivery of venue technology services. - Identify and report on the progress of all critical dependencies in accordance with the reporting methodologies. - Manage the asset tracking and management of deployed technology equipment - Track the progress of structural build-out and IT implementation leading up to the timely availability of components and services and including the commissioning and final decommissioning of the venues. - Develop regular progress, risk and issue management reports in accordance with the governance structure / reporting methodologies and, or PMO systems - Follow-up with various stakeholders, providers, and government entities in relation to ensuring delivery of technology services / dependencies as per agreed requirements, service levels etc. - Support and ensure the team adhere to the IT Service management protocols - Ensure positive attitude from the Venue Technology teams and good relationship with all the parties, including FIFA teams, Local FIFA Subsidiary (LFS) areas/projects, end-users, suppliers, Host Country/ City counterparts - Manage the on-site IT Technicians and support teams during tournament time operations, including end-of-day reporting and incident escalation to Technology Command Centre.
Education
- University degree or similar higher education, in Information Technology, Computer Science or related field
Experience
- Overall IT experience and in a similar role - Experience in the Sports / major event industry is a must - Proven track record of successful delivery of complex IT programs and projects
Skills & Competencies
- Manager with proven track record in similar projects - Good knowledge of venue technology services across major sporting events - Have up to date knowledge and project management expertise in all event related technologies, including, all types of technology business solutions, community access TV (CATV), radio communications, wired communication, printing, broadcast contribution network, spectrum, audio and visual. - Ability to always maintain a professional demeanor. Consistently interacts professionally and works productively with all levels of employee. - Excellent cross group collaboration skills and demonstrated ability to lead virtual teams across groups, conflict management and driving consensus to deliver on program milestones - Good Leadership skills and able to manage and motivate other team members - Highly adept at consulting, negotiating, communicating, consensus building and facilitation - Excellent verbal and written communication, training, and presentation skills - Ability to adapt speech to audience coming from a broad spectrum of disciplines and with various levels of technical knowledge - Leadership and accountability across all areas of relevance - Strong political acumen and adaptable to a wide range of working environments
Certifications & Requirements
- Right to work in Brazil - Fluent in English - Native or Fluent in Portuguese - Proficiency in any other FIFA language (FR / ES / GER) is an asset
Location: Recife, PE - Brazil

job.title: Uniforms Coordinator
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Uniforms Coordinator is responsible for assisting with the planning and operational delivery of the corporate FIFA27 Business Casual uniform as well as the FWWC2027 Tournament Dedicated and FIFA Formal uniform programmes in line with the overall FIFA Uniform Strategy and the approved usage and entitlement matrix. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Provide hands-on support to execute the delivery of the corporate and tournament time uniform programme 
  • Ensure all FIFA27 workforce have corporate uniform according to the entitlement and usage matrix 
  • Effectively collaborate with key FIFA Zurich and FIFA27 stakeholders including Workforce Planning, Volunteer Management, Host City Management, Logistics, Accreditation and Partnership Rights Delivery to deliver the uniform programme in line with the agreed service levels 
  • Put into action, the strategy, process and flows for the full project plan for FWWC2027 uniform programme capturing risks and mitigations throughout 
  • Assist in the creation of the operation plan for tournament time including any adaptations required for each Uniform Distribution Centers 
  • Support relationships with external uniform suppliers as required, including taking minutes, following up with reminders and ensuring next steps and actions are clear and adhered to 
  • Contribute to defining best practices within the Uniform Team and prepare training material and documentation that will be used as the team grows to support consistency across the wider team and all Uniform Distribution Centers 
  • Manage stock levels and keep up-to-date records of each uniform item during pre-tournament (e.g. Business Casual uniform/ formal items that may be required for side events) and tournament time. 
  • Communicate and uphold the agreed FIFA27 Uniform Guidelines and Usage Policy at each FIFA tournament and event across all client groups including FIFA Formal Uniform eligibility 
  • Plan uniform distribution to all related FWW2027 events, e.g. FWWC2027 Final Draw  
  • Work with relevant FIFA27 stakeholders to agree and provide guidelines to Suppliers who are not entitled to Tournament Dedicated Uniforms  
  • Other duties as necessary to ensure the smooth running of the FWWC2027 Uniform operations.  
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.
  • Experience in retail or operations; preferably with experience in mega (sports) events and corporate organisations  
  • Excellent customer service, motivating, inspiring and seeing results through others 
  • Experience as a volunteer or working with volunteers 
  • Knowledge of event planning and event project management processes  
  • Strong IT skill including MS Office (Word, Excel, Visio, PowerPoint)  
  • Strong written and oral communication  
  • Proven track record in administering uniform projects across complex quantity structures  
  • Team Player, willing to work to tight timelines  
  • Positive attitude, patience and persistence, with a pragmatic approach to solving problems 
  • Excellent time management skills and ability to balance competing priorities  
  • Demonstrate initiative and a strong focus on providing a high level of service

job.title: Ticketing System Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Reporting to Senior Ticketing Operations Manager, and working alongside the FIFA Ticketing team in Zurich, the Ticketing System Manager configures the ticketing system to ensure best in class solutions are available to sell and manage tickets for the FIFA Women's World Cup 2027. The role offers support to ticketing system users, promotes the use of information management systems and carries out quality assurance measures.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
· Managing the configuration of the ticketing system to offer frontend and backend solutions for ticketing system users, B2C and B2B clients
· Coordinate backend operations in the ticketing system such as batch processes
· Coordinate 1st level support and liaise with the FIFA Ticketing team and the service provider for 2nd and 3rd level support
· Consult ticketing system users, review and prepare user manuals
· Conduct functional and user acceptance tests of frontend and backend solutions including for ticketing system improvements
· Manage regular meetings, trainings and other forms of learning/collaboration opportunities to support the local ticketing team in using the ticketing solution
· Carry out quality assurance measurers to ensure error-free delivery of ticketing system solutions
· Promote the use and continuous improvement of information and knowledge management systems
· Support general ticketing operations for FIFA events
Education
• Bachelor’s degree or equivalent level of education.

Experience
• Strong experience in operating / managing ticketing systems
• Proven experience working in similar roles at big scale sporting events
• Experience working in international and multicultural environments

Skills & Competencies
• Strong ability to work effectively within cross-functional teams.
• Detail-oriented, with focus on delivering high-quality projects.
• Excellent communication skills, both within and across multiple functional areas.

Certifications & Requirements
· Languages: Fluent in English and Portuguese
· Right to work in Brazil
· Fluent in English (spoken and written)
· Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
· Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Project Coordinator Administration
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Administration Project Coordinator will support the Executive Director of Administration in the planning, coordination, and day-to-day management of operational activities related to the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027.

This role is critical to ensure effective internal organization, structured follow-up of strategic priorities, and seamless coordination across functional areas under the Administration Division. The position requires strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple workstreams in a dynamic and high-performance environment.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Provide direct operational and administrative support to the Executive Director of Administration.
  • Manage and coordinate the Director’s agenda, including scheduling meetings and prioritizing commitments.
  • Prepare meeting materials, including agendas, presentations, and briefing documents.
  • Draft and distribute meeting minutes, ensuring accurate documentation of decisions, action items, and next steps.
  • Track follow-up actions and ensure timely progress with functional area leaders.
  • Monitor ongoing projects and support coordination across areas under the Administration Division.
  • Facilitate communication and alignment between departments to ensure operational efficiency.
  • Support the development of reports, dashboards, and internal documentation.
  • Assist in organizing workshops, coordinating meetings, and planning sessions.
  • Ensure proper document management and information flow within the Administration Division.
  • Effective Communication and Trust Building: Skilled in fostering open communication and creating an environment of trust. Capable of understanding and addressing the needs and expectations of others, identifying and resolving conflicts, and delivering impactful presentations in a matrix organization.
  • Client-Centric Approach: Consistently demonstrates respect and professionalism toward clients (colleagues, superiors, other stakeholders). Clearly defines and aligns on expectations, ensuring client needs are met and exceeded. Receives positive client feedback, builds strong, trusting relationships, and anticipates client needs. Maintains a flexible and innovative mindset in service delivery.
  • Proven project management skills, with experience coordinating multi-stakeholder initiatives.
  • Planning and Resource Management: Skilled in developing detailed, chronological plans, setting priorities, and allocating resources effectively. Implement systems for tracking progress and control, and adapt plans as needed to achieve objectives. Maintains a strong focus on reporting and accountability.
  • Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English (additional languages are a plus).
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint), understanding of Power BI would be appreciated.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced, international environment with high levels of responsibility.
  • Proactive, detail-oriented, and solution-driven mindset.
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Ticketing Stadium Configuration Coordinator
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Reporting organizationally to the Ticketing Stadium Configuration Manager the Ticketing Stadium Configuration Coordinator will help building and maintaining stadium seating maps for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™. Working in close collaboration with the Configuration and Inventory team in Zurich and other key individuals responsible for the management of ticketing inventory, seating plans and its maintenance in the Ticketing System, ensuring data accuracy throughout the planning phase and during the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
· Assist the team in building complete and accurate seating plans in the ticketing system for selected FIFA tournament venues in the host country
· Assist in the implementation of all necessary seating information and configuration of spectator flows, price categories and seat allocations and carries out required updates
· Maintain on day-to-day basis seating maps and configurations within the ticketing system throughout all stages of FIFA tournaments and applies changes as required
· Ensures data integrity and accuracy of all seating information and configuration
· Carry out seat assignment processes in the ticketing system
· Support team to liaise with other functional areas in the planning of seating configuration and spectator flow for selected venues
· Assess and analyze maps and plans of FIFA tournament venues to identify gaps and changes to be applied in the seating plans and configurations
· Carry out onsite seat inspection visits to fully validate seating plans as built in the ticketing system with the actual venue seating
· Evaluate outcomes of quality inspections and proposes corrective measures
· Support the overall management of ticketing inventory by conducting quality assurance measure for both ticket quotas and seat allocations
· Support administrating allocations and additional ticket requests during the tournaments
· Support general ticketing operations for FIFA events systems
· Support general ticketing operations for FIFA events
Education
• Bachelor’s degree or equivalent level of education.

Experience
• Strong professional experience
· Experience working with ticketing systems (preferably Secutix)
· Experience in working with CAD technical drawings considered as a plus
· Highly accurate and detail-oriented working style, technology minded, efficient and accountable team player

Skills & Competencies
· Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Sharepoint, Teams, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
· Strong analytical skills, detail-oriented, technology minded, efficient and accountable team player
· Ability to work independently, managing multiple priorities
· Skilled in facilitating collaboration between various business areas
· Ability to adapt to working with different environment and time zones

Certifications & Requirements
· Willingness to travel to host cities
· Availability to work extended hours during test events and tournament time
· Experience working in international, multicultural environments preferred.
· Languages: Fluent in English and Portuguese. Proficiency in additional languages is valued
· Right to work in Brazil
· Fluent in English (spoken and written)
· Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
· Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Vehicle Access and Parking Permit (VAPP) Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Vehicle Access and Parking Permit (VAPP) Programme Manager shall be responsible for the planning, development, implementation and overall management of the VAPP Programme for the FWWC2027 Tournament, ensuring that all vehicle accreditation and parking operations are delivered in accordance with FIFA27 operational requirements. The role shall coordinate the design, production, allocation, distribution and control of VAPPs, ensuring effective integration with all FIFA27 Functional Areas, Host Cities, stadiums, airports, training sites and other relevant stakeholders. The VAPP Programme Manager shall also oversee the planning, allocation and distribution of workforce public transport cards, ensuring compliance with established eligibility criteria, operational timelines and service levels, while maintaining accurate records, reporting and effective stakeholder coordination throughout the Tournament lifecycle.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Act as the primary liaison between the Transport FA and FIFA27 departments for all VAPP related planning and operational matters for FWWC2027 and any ancillary event.
  • Actively lead and contribute on the development and implementation of the Host Cities Vehicles Access and/or Parking Permits (VAPPs) and venue/stadium transport access operational plans.
  • Deliver FWWC2027 VAPP scheme taking into consideration all factors surrounding the guests and Workforce’ journey.
  • Lead the procurement, contract implementation and performance management of the service provider(s) responsible for the production, printing, packaging and distribution of all VAPPs, ensuring compliance with contractual requirements, production schedules, quality standards, security protocols, inventory control, distribution plans and agreed service levels throughout the Tournament lifecycle.
  • Manage VAPP system and the supporting tools and systems (in coordination with FIFA27 Client Services) and ensure integration with other systems/ platforms within Operations department and other FIFA27 departments.
  • Engage with all stakeholders effectively; collect requirements, update and maintain VAPPs database, and assist with the distribution of VAPPs to all guest groups in coordination with guest group owners (departments/ functional areas).
  • Create and maintain comprehensive processes to obtain necessary documents from all functional areas/ departments to facilitate the permit application.
  • Work closely with key partners and service providers to monitor and ensure VAPPs activities are being executed in alignment with the contractual requirements.
  • Develop FWWC2027 VAPP Guide(s), policies and related procedures.
  • Work closely with the relevant local authorities and FWWC2027 Security Department in the implementation of the Vehicle Permit Checkpoints (e.g. at vehicle screening areas).
  • Prepare and submit forms/ applications and letters to various departments/ functional areas having jurisdiction over required permits and/or approvals.
  • Manage the onsite VAPP helpdesk for all incoming enquiries during operational period
  • The specific duties of this role may evolve and change through the operational planning and delivery phases.

Qualifications:
Education – bachelor’s degree in Transport/Logistics/ Engineering/Project management/Event Management or a combination of education and experience relating to position. Master’s Degree is an additional asset.

Experience:
  • Experience managing service providers, including procurement support, contract administration, supplier performance management and service level monitoring.
  • Experience coordinating cross-functional teams and engaging with public authorities, venue operators, suppliers and internal stakeholders. 
  • Experience managing operational databases, permit or accreditation systems, or other information management platforms is highly desirable. 
  • Experience developing operational procedures, policies, user guides and standard operating procedures (SOPs). 
  • Experience working in an international, multicultural and fast-paced environment is desirable.
  • Previous experience in large events would be an advantage, but is not mandatory.
Skills:
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including data validation, pivot tables, advanced formulas, and large database management.
  • Strong ability to work with operational systems, online platforms, databases and reporting tools. 
  • Strong analytical skills, with the ability to identify inconsistencies, gaps, duplications and risks in data and operational records. 
  • Ability to produce clear reports, trackers, dashboards and status updates for management and stakeholders.
  • Strong attention to detail, especially when dealing with permits, vehicle data, stakeholder requests and controlled documents.
  • Ability to translate operational requirements into system requirements, user guides and practical procedures.
  • Ability to work under pressure to immoveable deadlines.
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.
job.plain_text_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.
Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Vehicle Access and Parking Permit (VAPP) Programme Manager shall be responsible for the planning, development, implementation and overall management of the VAPP Programme for the FWWC2027 Tournament, ensuring that all vehicle accreditation and parking operations are delivered in accordance with FIFA27 operational requirements. The role shall coordinate the design, production, allocation, distribution and control of VAPPs, ensuring effective integration with all FIFA27 Functional Areas, Host Cities, stadiums, airports, training sites and other relevant stakeholders. The VAPP Programme Manager shall also oversee the planning, allocation and distribution of workforce public transport cards, ensuring compliance with established eligibility criteria, operational timelines and service levels, while maintaining accurate records, reporting and effective stakeholder coordination throughout the Tournament lifecycle.
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
- Act as the primary liaison between the Transport FA and FIFA27 departments for all VAPP related planning and operational matters for FWWC2027 and any ancillary event. - Actively lead and contribute on the development and implementation of the Host Cities Vehicles Access and/or Parking Permits (VAPPs) and venue/stadium transport access operational plans. - Deliver FWWC2027 VAPP scheme taking into consideration all factors surrounding the guests and Workforce’ journey. - Lead the procurement, contract implementation and performance management of the service provider(s) responsible for the production, printing, packaging and distribution of all VAPPs, ensuring compliance with contractual requirements, production schedules, quality standards, security protocols, inventory control, distribution plans and agreed service levels throughout the Tournament lifecycle. - Manage VAPP system and the supporting tools and systems (in coordination with FIFA27 Client Services) and ensure integration with other systems/ platforms within Operations department and other FIFA27 departments. - Engage with all stakeholders effectively; Skills:
- Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including data validation, pivot tables, advanced formulas, and large database management. - Strong ability to work with operational systems, online platforms, databases and reporting tools. - Strong analytical skills, with the ability to identify inconsistencies, gaps, duplications and risks in data and operational records. - Ability to produce clear reports, trackers, dashboards and status updates for management and stakeholders. - Strong attention to detail, especially when dealing with permits, vehicle data, stakeholder requests and controlled documents. - Ability to translate operational requirements into system requirements, user guides and practical procedures. - Ability to work under pressure to immoveable deadlines. - Right to work in Brazil. - Fluent in English (spoken and written) - Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written) - Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Social Media Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Reporting organisationally to the Head of Marketing, Brand and Promotion (FIFA27, Rio de Janeiro) and the Senior Social Media Manager (FIFA HQ, Zurich), the Social Media Manager will be a key member of the FIFA27 Marketing, Brand and Promotion team, and will work in close collaboration with the Digital Marketing team in Zurich and other key individuals responsible for managing, planning and delivering social media campaigns for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Social Media Manager for the FIFA Women's World Cup 2027™ include:

• Develop and implement a comprehensive social media strategy aligned with tournament goals and target audiences based on country and consumer audience. 
• Set specific objectives for social media campaigns, such as increasing brand awareness, driving traffic, and boosting engagement through a fan-first approach. 
• Ensure all social media content aligns with the brand's voice, tone, and messaging guidelines. 
• Create, curate, and publish engaging and relevant content across social platforms, including text, images, videos, and other multimedia. 
• Develop content calendars and schedule posts across platforms including Facebook, Instagram, X (Twitter), LinkedIn, YouTube, TikTok, and more. 
• Monitor social media platforms for trends, mentions, and relevant conversations. 
• Use social listening tools to gather insights and trends for stakeholders across marketing, creative, and digital content teams. 
• Track and analyse social media performance using tools such as Google Analytics, Sprinklr, native platform analytics, and third-party tools. 
• Prepare and present reports on engagement, reach, follower growth, and ROI, using insights to optimise strategies. 
• Plan, create, and manage paid social media campaigns to achieve marketing objectives such as lead generation or sales. 
• Monitor and optimise advertising performance, adjusting targeting, creative, and budgets based on analytics. 
• Identify, manage, and collaborate with influencers, brand ambassadors, broadcasters, and partners to amplify reach and engagement. 
• Stay up to date on social media trends, tools, and best practices. 
• Execute social campaigns including contests, giveaways, and product launches. 
• Coordinate cross-platform campaigns to ensure consistent messaging and maximum impact. 
• Work closely with marketing, PR, creative services, and other departments to support overall business goals. 
• Collaborate with content creators, designers, and copywriters to produce high-quality content. 
• Act as the primary social media liaison between the Zurich-based global team and the US-based event teams. 
• Adjust strategies based on data to improve reach, engagement, and conversion rates. 
• Ensure collaboration and content optimisation with other stakeholders in the Digital Marketing group, including colleagues working on owned-and-operated platforms

  • Education & Qualifications

    • Related bachelor’s degree or a similar level of higher education.


  • Work Experience

    • Experience developing social media campaigns for sports teams, paid social campaigns, creating social content, and leadership responsibilities (preferably in the South American market). 
    • Ability to understand and execute brand guidelines. 
    • Proficiency across Instagram, Facebook, X (Twitter), TikTok, Snapchat, YouTube, LinkedIn, and WhatsApp. 
    • Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. 
    • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

  • Languages

    • Fluent in English and Portuguese 

  • Technology

    • Experience/Proficiency with; Google Analytics, Keyhole, Canva, PowerPoint, Hootsuite, Buffer, Sprout Social, Sprinklr, Adobe Creative Cloud.

job.title: Event Experiences Marketing Manager - FIFA Trophy Tour
job.html_description:
FIFA27 established as the Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil, to work together with FIFA HQ in Zurich and globally, to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”). 


The FIFA Women’s World Cup™ Trophy Tour exists to build anticipation and engagement ahead of the tournament, serving as a promotional platform to drive ticket sales, tourism, and global visibility. By bringing the Trophy to communities across Brazil and the world, it demonstrates FIFA’s truly global mission while positioning Brazilian host cities as vibrant, capable, and welcoming destinations at the heart of the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.

The FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ Trophy Tour Manager will lead the planning, marketing and delivery of the Host Country phase of the FIFA Women’s World Cup2027™ Trophy Tour across Brazil, ensuring a cohesive, high-impact and locally relevant execution across all Host Cities. They will also support in moments on the Global Tor that require local stakeholders in particular Brazilian Embassies in the 32 participating Member Associations.

Acting as the central point of coordination in-market, this role will drive alignment across FIFA’s global project team in Zurich, local stakeholders and all relevant internal functions. Working in close collaboration with the Experiential Marketing Manager in Zurich, the Manager will ensure that all FIFA Trophy Tour activities are strategically aligned, culturally relevant, and delivered to FIFA standards.

The role will also support the global leg of the FIFA Trophy Tour where required, ensuring continuity and consistency between international and Host Country phases. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Strategy, planning & alignment
  • Lead the development and execution of the Host Country FIFA Trophy Tour strategy and marketing approach across Brazil including all Host Cities in Brazil 
  • Ensure alignment with the overall FIFA Trophy Tour strategy, adapting where needed to maximize local relevance and impact 
  • Act as the primary point of contact for the eight Host Cities, coordinating planning, timelines, and delivery expectations 
  • Ensure integration with the wider FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ project, identifying opportunities to amplify impact across other initiatives 

Stakeholder management & governance
  • Manage relationships with Host Cities, local authorities, agencies and key stakeholders, acting as FIFA’s representative in-market 
  • Serve as the source of truth on local context, sensitivities and opportunities, proactively informing the global team 
  • Lead cross-functional coordination across FIFA internal teams (including Operations, Legends, Digital, Communications, Brand, Partnerships and Media), ensuring all workstreams are aligned, integrated and delivered against a unified plan 
  • Ensure consistent alignment and information flow with the Zurich-based FIFA team, proactively managing dependencies, timelines and decision-making across global and local stakeholders 
  • Oversee and approve local plans, including venues, run of show, schedules and promotional activities 
  • Ensure total compliance with FIFA rights, brand guidelines and partner commitments 
Marketing, communications & content
  • Oversee the development and execution of local promotional plans across channels (digital, media, partners, on-the-ground) in alignment with the broader global marketing and communications plan
  • Ensure Host City activations are culturally relevant, locally resonant and aligned with the overall campaign narrative 
  • Inspire Host Cities and hosting stakeholders at a local level to invest as many resources as possible to deliver the best FIFA Trophy Tour events possible. 
  • Lead coordination of media activities in-market, approved by the project team and FIFA Communications
  • Oversee content capture, approvals and distribution across FIFA channels and stakeholders (including .com, CRM, VNRs and social platforms.
On-site delivery & operations
  • Represent FIFA on-site across selected FIFA Trophy Tour stops in Brazil and support on global stops if needed
  • Oversee delivery of FIFA communications, promotional and content activities, ensuring high-quality execution and stakeholder experience 
  • Ensure proper representation, handling and protection of FIFA assets, including the FIFA Women’s World Cup™ Trophy and FIFA Legend 
  • Coordinate on-site stakeholders to ensure smooth operations, including media, partners and local teams 
Reporting & performance tracking
  • Monitor progress of the FIFA Trophy Tour across Host Cities and ensure timely escalation of risks or issues 
  • Provide regular and comprehensive reporting on activities, performance and key learnings 
  • Contribute to post-event evaluation and recommendations for future FIFA Trophy Tours 
Global coordination
  • Support the Zurich-based project team on the global leg where required.
  • Coordinate with FIFA27 Government relations to pitch the FIFA Trophy Tour opportunities to Brazilian Government representatives and embassies for inclusion both phases of the FIFA Trophy Tour to facilitate their involvement in official events and support local engagement opportunities 
  • Ensure continuity and consistency between the global and Host Country phases, capturing and sharing key learnings to inform delivery across the tour
  • Bachelor’s Degree or similar level of education in Marketing. 
  • Experience in Marketing and Events or Experiential projects, ideally within sport or major international events 
  • Preferred previous experience in football and/or professional sports. 
  • Strong understanding of sports marketing, including sponsorship, brand communication and fan engagement
  • Proven ability to deliver integrated marketing communications and promotional campaigns across online and offline channels
  • Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple workstreams in a fast-paced and evolving environment 
  • Experience managing complex internal and external stakeholder relationships, including at senior level
  • Demonstrated ability to work cross-functionally, building strong relationships and aligning diverse teams towards a shared objective
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to navigate discussions, resolve issues and drive decisions in a constructive and controlled manner
  • High level of adaptability and resilience, with the ability to respond to changing priorities, evolving plans and a dynamic operating environment
  • Comfortable working across time zones and flexible working hours, with willingness to travel extensively as required
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools e.g. Monday.com

Requirements
  • Fluent in English and Portuguese
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Senior Legal Counsel, Disputes & Litigation
job.html_description:
We govern the beautiful game and ensure it's run with transparency and integrity.

Join our team in Miami and support us on our mission.
These are your key tasks:
  • Responsible for the conduct of all external disputes and contentious matters involving FIFA, including civil and criminal matters and arbitration cases (note: this does not include the conduct of any sports-related matters in the CAS); 
  • Providing legal advice and guidance across all functional areas at FIFA related to contentious matters, including advising on litigation risks and outcomes; 
  • Conduct of settlement negotiations in the context of disputes;
  • Managing multiple cases in Switzerland and internationally; 
  • Engagement and instruction of external counsel, and responsibility for budgets for external legal spend; and
  • Attending court hearings, reviewing, drafting and commenting on documents.  
We work hard at FIFA.
We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.

And we respect our values. Always.

For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.

The specific competencies we require for this position are:
  • Fully qualified to practise law (admitted to the Florida bar or eligible to register as Florida Authorized House Counsel) having completed academic and practical training
  • 8+ years of legal practice PQE
  • Substantial professional experience of working with and drafting legal documents in English
  • Excellent negotiation, drafting, problem solving and analytical skills
  • Enhanced expertise: 8 years expertise "on the job" in a recognised firm or in a similar role in an in-house legal department
  • Demonstrable interest in or experience of sports, media, sponsorship and regulatory matters, including and interest in the interplay between sports and competition law
  • First class written and spoken English language skills, consistent with drafting and negotiating complex, high value and nuanced English language agreements
  • Comfortable providing advice and recommendations to senior leadership
  • Ability to provide and communicate effectively practical and solutions-based legal advice 
  • Excellent negotiation, drafting, problem solving and analytical skills
  • Structured, reliable, highly organised with a strong eye for detail 
  • Ability to work as part of a high-performing team, fostering collaboration, driving results, and ensuring the successful achievement of organizational goals.
  • Proactive, with a strong sense of personal responsibility with an ability to work autonomously on a wide variety of projects
  • Resilient, with an ability to competently handle a large workload amid rapidly shifting priorities
  • Open-minded team player with an affinity for working within a variety of cultures and excellent communication skills
  • High degree of professionalism and confident demeanour
  • Ability to be a good ambassador for FIFA in all manner of external facing situations, including during court hearings, mediations and negotiations, external workshops and during FIFA competition-related assignments
  • Ability to create, develop and maintain strong and reliable relationships with FIFA senior management as well as external legal and commercial counterparts
  • Willingness to operate outside of comfort zone
  • Takes initiative to reflect on how to improve and develop working styles and methods to assist the organisation and internal clients
  • Native level English (or equivalent), consistent with requirement to draft and negotiate complex and nuanced English language agreements and advice. 
  • Proficiency in any other FIFA language (French/Spanish/German) is an asset but not necessary
  • Proficient in Word / PowerPoint / Excel
  • Willingness to travel
The biggest benefit of a career at FIFA is the privilege to work for the beautiful game and create something that matters.
 
Alongside that privilege, we also offer some extra perks and benefits:
 
Health insurance: We offer generous medical, vision, and dental insurance coverage.
Continuous learning: We care about your development and encourage internal mobility, offering a variety of training and education to help achieve it.
Retirement: We offer generous 401(k) employer contributions.
Vacation: Maintain a healthy work/life balance with a generous vacation allowance.

job.title: Access Management Cluster Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Reporting to the Access Management Senior Manager, you will be a key member of the FIFA WOMEN´S WORLD CUP 2027 Access Management team.  You will be responsible for developing and implementing the Access Management operations for all the official venues across the tournament within Brasil, in line with Access Management policies and procedures.

Through close coordination with other Functional Area (FA) and venue managers, you will ensure that the operations relating to the key movements of persons holding a FIFA access device into and within their official venues meets the requirements and provide efficient, safe, and well-planned ingress, circulation & egress. 

The Access Management Cluster Manager will assist the Access Management Senior Manager by supporting the Access Management Venue Managers in the preparation stages of the tournament with the possibility of then assuming the position of Access Management Venue Manager of the Rio de Janeiro Stadium during the tournament.
 
You will ensure security personnel and other responsible staff are provided with the necessary information, training and tools to operate access control effectively at each required location.   

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Oversight, training and support to the Access Management Venue Managers across all official venues including Stadia, Broadcast Centre, Venue Specific Hotels, and Training Sites. 
  • Implementation and operation of Access Management policies and procedures for accredited individuals in line with the accreditation and safety and security concepts for the tournament, including access control point setup and entitlement resolution.
  • Planning and mapping of key client group flows, vertical transportation and ingress points onto venue maps and communication of flows and ingress points to all client groups via the provision of access tours, maps and briefings. 
  • Ensuring all client group flows, paths of travel, entrances and exits planned for people with disabilities and/or limited mobility meet FIFA and related local Accessibility standards and requirements.
  • Provision of key wayfinding information as required to FIFA Dressing & Signage to ensure wayfinding on site is correct and as per all client group flows. Ensure that all wayfinding meets FIFA Accessibility standards and requirements.
  • Facilitate additional and exceptional access requirements onsite and assist all client groups with any access related issues.
  • Ensuring all Functional Areas (FAs) at the venues can locate their working areas and know how to reach them, within the constraints of privilege zoning and restrictions.
  • Ensuring all access staff and volunteers are trained and fully understand their roles and function and can perform the duties expected of them.

Education:
  • Bachelor’s degree or similar level of higher education in IT & Systems, Sport Venue Management, Safety and Security or similar.  
  • If experience is compensating, lower degree is welcome to apply.
  • Football access management knowledge.
  • FIFA access management knowledge is a plus. 
  • Effective interpersonal communication, influencing and negotiation skills.
  • Strong problem solving and analytical skills.
  • Stature and professional experience necessary to interact as tough partner with senior colleagues.
  • Good judgement, superb integrity, maturity, and fairness in dealing with people.
Experience:
  • Experience with the sport event industry, preferably within access management, accreditation, safety and security or venue management. Experience within FIFA is an advantage.
  • Operation and setup of complex access control systems within ticketing and/or accreditation.
  • Wayfinding and route planning in a stadium environment. 
  • Single-sport, multi-venue event experience.
  • Management and leadership of a team in high pressure tournament operations. 
  • Demonstratable ability to manage multiple projects concurrently and under pressure
Requirements
  • Fluent in Portuguese and English
  • Right to work in Brazil
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Kulturvermittler/in für Führungen (Auf Buchung, im Stundenlohn)
job.html_description:
Das FIFA Museum zelebriert und bewahrt das Erbe und die Kultur des Fussballs, um die Faszination des Spiels mit der ganzen Welt zu teilen. Mit aussergewöhnlichen Ausstellungen, einzigartigen Originalobjekten, faszinierenden Geschichten, multimedialen und interaktiven Erlebnissen sowie anregenden Kultur- und Bildungsprogrammen ist das Haus der Fussballgeschichte eine lebendige und leidenschaftliche Plattform für Fans und Besucher, um sich zu treffen, miteinander in Kontakt zu treten und sich auszutauschen.
 
 
Wir suchen per sofort oder nach Vereinbarung eine/n Kulturvermittler/in für Führungen (Auf Buchung, im Stundenlohn)
  • Leitung von Führungen in der Dauerausstellung (Gruppen, Firmen, öffentliche Führungen, Schulklassen, Familien, etc.)
  • Leitung von Führungen in Sonderausstellungen
  • Einarbeitung in die Themen und Schwerpunkte der Dauerausstellung
  • Einarbeitung in Themen und Schwerpunkte von Sonderausstellungen
  • Teilnahme an Weiterbildungen und Quartalstreffen
  • Mitarbeit und Unterstützung des Museumsteams bei Spezialanlässen
  • Persönlichen Bezug und Leidenschaft für den Fussball als Sport wie auch als Kulturgut und Gesellschaftsphänomen
  • Sehr gute Sprachkenntnisse in Französisch, Deutsch und/oder Englisch
  • Bereitschaft ein modernes Vermittlungsverständnis aufzubauen
  • Flexibilität für Einsätze während den Öffnungszeiten des Museums (10.00 bis 18.00)
  • Einsätze am Wochenende
  • Erfahrungen in der Vermittlungsarbeit in anderen Kulturinstitutionen

job.title: Ticketing Stadium Operations Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Reporting organizationally to the Senior Ticketing Operations Manager, the Ticketing Stadium Operations Manager will be a key member of the FIFA27 Ticketing team, and work in close collaboration with the Senior Ticketing Operations Manager in Zurich as well as other key individuals responsible for delivering the Ticketing operation at the stadium for the FIFA Women’s World Cup Brazil 2027.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Assist in the definition of overarching and stadium specific ticketing operations concepts
  • Liaise with other Ticketing and non-Ticketing functions to plan and deliver required stadium ticketing infrastructure, technology, equipment and security
  • Liaise with other Ticketing and non-Ticketing functions to ensure all plans are refined and integrated into stadium ticket operations planning, including; stadium seating configuration, spectator flows, Electronic Access Control procedures, customer journeys and wider venue operational plans
  • Define and develop stadium ticketing operational policies and procedures
  • Define and develop stadium ticketing issue resolution processes
  • Liaise with providers and subject matter experts to configure all ticketing systems for use during tournament operations
  • Assist in the development and execution of stadium ticketing workforce planning, including; role definition, recruitment, rostering, uniform and catering
  • Design and deliver a world class training programme for stadium ticketing staff, contractors and volunteers, including; training needs analysis, session plans, learning aids, e-learning and training logistics
  • Ensure two-way communication and engagement with FIFA departments and Host City venue teams to ensure all ticketing requirements and operational plans are clearly communicated and understood
  • Plan and deliver Ticketing HQ operations support for venue teams during tournament time
  • Support general ticketing operations for other FIFA events as required
Education
  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent level of education.

Experience
  • Customer oriented, strong communication skills, flexible, efficient and accountable team player
  • Solid Ticketing Operations experience – ideally in a stadium or large venue and international sporting events
Skills & Competencies
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Sharepoint, Teams, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
  • Experience in designing and delivering face to face and online training
  • Experience in managing large projects with multiple workstreams and stakeholders 
  • Experience in delivering world class customer service
  • Knowledge of Ticketing and Electronic Access Control systems
  • Knowledge of e-learning design systems is preferred
  • Ability to work independently, managing multiple priorities
  • Ability to coordinate across internal functional areas as well as external stakeholders
  • Ability to adapt to working with different environment and time zones
Certifications & Requirements
  • Willingness to travel to host cities
  • Availability to work extended hours during test events and tournament time
  • Experience working in international, multicultural environments preferred.
  • Languages: Fluent in English and Portuguese. Proficiency in additional languages is valued
  • Right to work in Brazil
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

Certifications & Requirements

Proficiency in additional languages is valued - Right to work in Brazil - Fluent in English (spoken and written) - Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written) - Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Radio & Spectrum Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Reporting organizationally to the senior Telecom manager, the radio and spectrum manager will be a key member of the FIFA27 Technology team, and work in close collaboration with the FIFA Technology team and other key individuals responsible ensuring all the aspects of Private Mobile Radio (PMR) and Spectrum Management Services are implemented according to FIFA requirements for the associated Tournaments/Events. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Spectrum:
  • Identify the Spectrum service needs and manage collection of the Spectrum requests from all Stakeholders
  • Work closely with ANATEL to identify and plan all frequencies to be reserved or use by all the client groups during the Tournaments/Events.
  • Manage spectrum governance with the key partners, the main stakeholders and with ANATEL
  • Coordinate the implementation of the frequency plan for all these services requiring temporary frequency allocation, define the frequency provision procedure and the management of requests with ANATEL and structure the organization and management control of radio equipment and monitoring on all tournament sites.
  • Develop with ANATEL a spectrum control process to ensure spectrum is managed during operations
  • Ensure that all necessary infrastructure, services and spaces required for the delivery and support of spectrum services as per FIFA requirements, are understood and effectively communicated into the venue planning process.
  • Plan and coordinate the implementation of the Test & Tag and Incident Management processes.
Private Mobile Radio:
  • Ensure services are delivered at agreed service levels in line with Tournaments/Events operational support model. 
  • Identify needs for PMRs, work closely with Tournaments/Events Venue Management staff to plan the Fleet Map and validate it with all the Functional Areas.
  • Ensure that all necessary infrastructure, services and spaces required for the delivery and support of radio services as per FIFA requirements, are understood and effectively integrated into the venue planning process.
  • Define with all partners the Roles & Responsibilities, and any other operational process.
  • Put in place the means of controlling commitments in terms of service levels, so as to organize problem-solving operations but also to facilitate any discussion with stakeholders.
  • Lead the Request for Purchase process to procure any necessary devices and operational services
Education:
  • University degree or similar higher education, in Telecommunications, Radio Communications, Spectrum management or related field
Experience:
  • Experience with Radio and Spectrum Services.
  • Experience coordinating with diverse teams and managing vendor relationships.
  • Strong project management and organizational skills.
  • Detail-oriented and process-driven with the ability to manage competing priorities.
  • Effective communicator with strong interpersonal skills.
  • Prior experience in the Sports / major event industry is highly preferred
Skills & Competencies:
  • Manager with experience in similar projects
  • Good knowledge of TETRA systems, PoC (Push to Talk over Cellular), Fleet Map planning and Spectrum management.
  • Strong Project Management skills, with experience in leading large scale complex projects.
  • Ability to always maintain a professional demeanour. Consistently interacts professionally and works productively with all levels of employee. 
  • Excellent cross group collaboration skills and demonstrated ability to lead virtual teams across groups, conflict management and driving consensus to deliver on program milestones  
  • Good Leadership skills and able to manage and motivate other team members 
  • Highly adept at consulting, negotiating, communicating, consensus building and facilitation
  • Excellent verbal and written communication, training, and presentation skills
  • Ability to adapt speech to audience coming from a broad spectrum of disciplines and with various levels of technical knowledge
  • Leadership and accountability across all areas of relevance  
  • Strong political acumen and adaptable to a wide range of working environments
Certifications & Requirements:
  • Fluent in English
  • Native or Fluent in Portuguese
  • Proficiency in any other FIFA language (FR / ES / GER) is an asset
  • Willing to travel within Brazil (up to 30%), with some trips with a long duration (15 days or more)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

Certifications & Requirements:
- Fluent in English - Native or Fluent in Portuguese - Proficiency in any other FIFA language (FR / ES / GER) is an asset - Willing to travel within Brazil (up to 30%), with some trips with a long duration (15 days or more) - Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Team Security Coordinator
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Team Security Coordinator is responsible for the establishment, coordination and management of the Team/Referee Security Liaison Officer (TSLO/RSLO) programme for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.
Reporting to the Manager, Non-Competition Sites, the role ensures that secure movement, team protection and liaison arrangements are delivered consistently across all host cities and clusters.
The position provides structured oversight of all TSLO activities, ensuring effective coordination with public security authorities, policing partners and relevant FIFA functional areas. The role safeguards the integrity of “bubble-to-bubble” operations, secure transport arrangements and team-related security protocols, ensuring alignment with FIFA regulations, statutory requirements and tournament operational plans.

• Establish, integrate and manage the full TSLO/RSLO programme across all host cities and clusters
• Coordinate and supervise all TSLOs/RSLO, ensuring consistent operational standards and structured reporting
• Serve as the central coordination point for team-related security matters within the Safety & Security function
• Establish and maintain strong working relationships with government agencies, policing partners and relevant domestic institutions
• Ensure integration of public security services supporting team movements external to official sites
• Oversee the implementation of secure movement protocols, including “bubble-to-bubble” operations and police escort arrangements
• Ensure structured coordination of close protection and monitored transport services for teams, referees and designated FIFA officials
• Oversee daily reporting rhythms from TSLOs/RSLO and ensure timely escalation of incidents or risks
• Monitor threat and risk developments impacting teams and coordinate mitigation measures in conjunction with relevant authorities
• Facilitate team security briefings and ensure itinerary coordination with domestic security agencies
• Ensure structured liaison between TSLOs, Team Liaison Officers (TLOs), Team Security Officers (TSOs) and venue-based safety and security managers
• Gather, manage and maintain required TSLO programme documentation in compliance with host country regulations and FIFA requirements
• Support and advise on background check protocols and accreditation-related security requirements
• Liaise with competent authorities regarding qualification and licensing criteria applicable to TSLO deployment
• Participate in planning meetings, exercises and readiness activities relevant to team security operations
• Provide 24/7 programme oversight during tournament operations and ensure continuity of support to teams
• Update daily team/referee movement information for the relevant government security authorities and other delivery partners as required

Education
• University degree or higher qualification in security management, public safety, law enforcement, risk management or a related field

Experience
• Experience in public security, law enforcement, protective security or major event security operations
• Demonstrated experience coordinating multi-agency security operations
• Experience managing secure movements, close protection operations or team/delegation security
• Proven experience liaising with public security authorities at federal, state or municipal level
• Experience managing operational teams or structured security programmes

Skills & Competencies
• Strong stakeholder management and inter-agency coordination capability
• Ability to operate credibly at senior level with public security authorities
• Structured planning and programme management skills
• Strong situational awareness and crisis response coordination capability
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills
• High integrity and professional discretion
• Advanced IT proficiency including MS Office suite

Certifications & Requirements:
• Right to work in Brazil. 
• Fluent in English (spoken and written)
• Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
• Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.
