Education • University degree or higher qualification in security management, public safety, law enforcement, risk management or a related field
Experience • Experience in public security, law enforcement, protective security or major event security operations • Demonstrated experience coordinating multi-agency security operations • Experience managing secure movements, close protection operations or team/delegation security • Proven experience liaising with public security authorities at federal, state or municipal level • Experience managing operational teams or structured security programmes
Skills & Competencies • Strong stakeholder management and inter-agency coordination capability • Ability to operate credibly at senior level with public security authorities • Structured planning and programme management skills • Strong situational awareness and crisis response coordination capability • Excellent written and verbal communication skills • High integrity and professional discretion • Advanced IT proficiency including MS Office suite
Certifications & Requirements: • Right to work in Brazil. • Fluent in English (spoken and written) • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written) • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Senior Digital Newsroom Manager (Miami)
job.html_description:
We govern the beautiful game and ensure it's run with transparency and integrity.

Join our team in Miami and support us on our mission.

The Senior Digital Newsroom Manager oversees the strategic and operational management of FIFA’s global digital newsroom, ensuring continuous, coordinated, and high-quality content delivery across FIFA’s owned, earned, and shared platforms.

Reporting to the Senior Editorial & Amplification Manager, this role ensures that the newsroom generates and executes stories in alignment with FIFA’s overall communications objectives. The position supports a multimedia-first approach to storytelling, maximising reach and impact through effective editorial planning, digital innovation, and streamlined workflows.

The Senior Digital Newsroom Manager works in close collaboration with various units across the Communications Division, supporting FIFA’s global messaging, major initiatives, and institutional events with engaging and timely content.

Main duties
  • Find and deliver stories across platforms which support FIFA’s global messaging, major initiatives, and institutional events
  • Implement and champion a multimedia-first approach to editorial content creation, ensuring consistency, scalability, and platform optimization
  • Script, shoot and edit all your own video pieces, be they social assets or VNRs
  • Write first drafts of articles for Inside FIFA and keep the FIFA blogs updated with content
  • Collaborate across divisions to align editorial strategies, integrate digital tools and AI-based innovations, and support key corporate and social campaigns
  • Manage the Miami studio and take responsibility for tagging, archival, and library systems for all relevant multimedia content
  • Ensure operational excellence through effective training, multilingual content workflows, and governance of newsroom processes and administrative functions.


Newsroom management and coordination
  • Lead daily operations of FIFA’s global digital newsroom to guarantee timely, accurate, and impactful publication of content across platforms
  • Maintain a 24/7 editorial capability spanning multiple time zones, enabling continuous coverage and immediate response to global developments
  • Ensure efficient coordination between newsroom, social and multimedia teams to deliver cohesive content packages that reflect FIFA’s brand and strategic priorities
  • Implement newsroom processes that maximise agility, quality control, and cross-divisional collaboration
Editorial planning and execution
  • Contribute to editorial briefs and plans that integrate visual, textual, and video elements for maximum storytelling effectiveness
  • Promote and oversee a strong multimedia-first approach to content curation, ensuring all newsroom activity supports multi-platform delivery
  • Work with relevant colleagues in the Communications Division on strategies for content gathering and prioritisation
  • Align operational planning and budgeting with strategic business objectives under the guidance of the Head of Editorial & Amplification
Digital innovation and workflow optimisation
  • Collaborate with the relevant departments in the Communications Division to develop and integrate AI tools that enhance both content production and presentation
  • Drive innovation in newsroom systems and technologies, identifying opportunities to improve efficiency, insight, and editorial performance
  • Oversee and maintain efficient workflows for multilingual content production, ensuring consistent use and adoption across all contributors
  • Ensure all newsroom resources and contributors are trained in digital tools, including the FIFA Content Management System, the FIFA Digital Hub and editorial planning services
Content governance and collaboration
  • Oversee content for FIFA’s institutional social media channels, working closely with the responsible agency to ensure effective cross-promotion and visibility
  • Ensure all materials meet editorial standards and adhere to FIFA’s tone of voice, style guides, and communication principles
  • Collaborate with relevant colleagues in the Communications Division to create and distribute multimedia assets for FIFA’s corporate and social initiatives
  • Maintain close coordination with leaders across the Communications Division to ensure editorial outputs align with organisational priorities
Support to management and events
  • Support senior management in organising, briefing, preparing and delivering speeches, key messages, and digital assets for major FIFA events
  • Manage timelines and ensure quality control across event-related multilingual and multimedia-focused production and publication
Operational oversight
  • Maintain an efficient editorial back-office operation to support content production across all formats and languages
  • Oversee administrative processes related to newsroom logistics, including travel coordination, budgets, and monthly team deliverables
  • Foster a culture of continuous improvement within the newsroom through evaluation, data analysis, and process refinement

We work hard at FIFA.
We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.

And we respect our values. Always.

For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.

The specific competencies we require for this position are:
  • Proven experience (10+ years) working in an international sports and/or media organisation – with a specific background in football and/or journalism.
  • Media communications or public relations expertise and the ability to build and maintain an audience and maintain interest over time.
  • Excellent English skills, including writing (for print and broadcast), communication, and presentation.
  • An outstanding team spirit with an entrepreneurial mindset that thrives when being challenged.
  • Ability to foster long-term relationships with key professionals.
  • Solid experience coordinating successful communications campaigns.
  • Understand and be conscious of the political and commercial nature of a global governing body’s activities in addition to organising tournaments and events.
  • Bachelor's in languages/communications/journalism/digital media.
  • Masters in languages/communications/journalism/digital media.
  • Excellent written and spoken communication skills and management of messaging and media – as well as working under tight deadlines.
  • Perfect understanding of global football activities.
  • Excellent command of communications platforms and media relations.
  • Expertise in video production and social media as well as standard messaging and content creation.
  • Fluent in English; Proficiency in any other FIFA language (F / ES / GER) is an asset.
  • Willingness to travel at least one week per month.
  • Readiness for stand-by duties.
  • MS Office / Advanced.
  • SAP knowledge would be an asset (Finance/Budgeting, Procurement, Governance and RFP process management).
  • Ability to, beyond the department, think for the division and the organisation.
  • Ability to liaise and communicate with internal and external stakeholders.
  • Comfortable presenting to and communicating with top management and key stakeholders (internal and external).
  • Ability to handle conflictual situations.
  • Ability to develop and motivate a team.
  • Excellent communicator and listener.
  • Willing to learn new skills and take on challenge.
  • Discreet.
  • Analytical.
  • Pragmatic.
  • Flexible and open.
  • Loyal and trustworthy.
  • Proven ability to motivate, inspire and manage a team to deliver at the top of their potential – with plenty of examples and case studies.


The biggest benefit of a career at FIFA is the privilege to work for the beautiful game and create something that matters.
 
Alongside that privilege, we also offer some extra perks and benefits:
 
Health insurance: We offer generous medical, vision, and dental insurance coverage.
Continuous learning: We care about your development and encourage internal mobility, offering a variety of training and education to help achieve it.
Retirement: We offer generous 401(k) employer contributions.
Vacation: Maintain a healthy work/life balance with a generous vacation allowance.

job.title: Host City Volunteer Manager – 8 Host Cities (Brazil)
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience. 

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Host City Volunteer Manager will be responsible for the delivery of the FIFA Volunteer Programme in-line with the FIFA Volunteer Framework and FIFA’s service excellence standards supported by the local operational concept. This will include full alignment with the Senior Volunteer Manager – FIFA27 and FIFA Zurich on the programme strategy, mission, and vision, safeguarding the FIFA Volunteer brand and its reputation across all the host cities and assigned venues.

Reporting to the Volunteer Management Central Team – FIFA27 and working in close collaboration with the Lead of the project on the city level, the Host City Volunteer Manager will be the face of the FIFA Volunteer Programme for their respective Host City. The role will be responsible for supporting the delivery of the volunteer journey through the FIFA Volunteer Platform and overseeing volunteer operations within their assigned Host City.

The ideal candidate will join a dynamic, multicultural environment and bring both strategic insight and firsthand expertise to one of the most exciting sporting events in the world.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

The following points will apply to all host cities and venues where the competition takes place.
Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

Programme Delivery and Coordination at the Host City level
  • Collaborate with the Volunteer Management Central Team (FIFA27) and FIFA Zurich to support the delivery of the Volunteer Programme and reporting on the project status.
  • Act as the primary point of contact between FIFA27 Volunteer Management Central Team and Host City counterparts on all volunteer related matters, ensuring clear two-way coordination and information flow.
  • Act as the primary point of contact for Host City queries and requests related to volunteer communications and engagement activities and projects, including volunteer-focused events.
  • Establish and maintain strong working relationships with Functional Area managers who engage volunteers or support volunteer management operations.
  • Act as a Point of contact for volunteer related topics with Venue Management when necessary.
  • Work closely with Host City counterparts for pre implementation of promotion and recruitment initiatives within local communities to tournament delivery including but not exclusive to training and uniform distribution
  • Support the promotion initiatives and events of the Volunteer team by:
    - Identifying volunteer profiles for media and/or social media features.
    - Maintaining an archive of photos and videos of Host City volunteers for use at the discretion of the central volunteer management team. Oversee the organization of volunteer-focused events led by the Host City.
    - Ensuring the Host City adheres to brand and communications guidelines at all times.
  • Actively represent the FIFA Volunteer Programme in public settings, including speaking at community events, hosting volunteer information sessions, and supporting recruitment activations.
  • Implement a coordinated approach with all stakeholders across all phases of the volunteer journey, including promotion, recruitment, role offers, training, and the tournament period.
  • Attend daily HQ meetings, Venue meetings and reporting on the status of the Volunteer Programme.
  • Participate in site visits to inspect potential pre-tournament and tournament-time required spaces.
  • Oversee the set-up and daily operations - act as a venue manager - dismantling and handover of the Volunteer Centres and spaces.
  • Align and coordinate with Host City and venue teams on logistics, storage, inventory, and distribution of volunteer recognition items as part of the Recognition Programme. Ensure venues adhere to the gift distribution calendar outlined in the Recognition Plan
  • Communicate with candidates and volunteers via Volunteer Support Channel and the FIFA Volunteer Platform.
Volunteer Management
  •  Recruit, train, manage, and lead Volunteer Coordinators within the assigned Host City, ensuring alignment, clear communication, and effective team collaboration.
  • Manage volunteers’ day-to-day to ensure efficient deployment and the well-being of volunteers on-site during each volunteer journey phase.
  • Ensure the volunteer services and recognition are provided as planned and liaise with local authorities when necessary.
  • Lead the recruitment, training and oversee the operation of the journey volunteers
  • Support with planning and delivery for the Official Draw, Playoffs and other FWWC27 official events, as required.
Volunteer Platform
  • Work extensively on the Volunteer Platform to manage all stages of the volunteer journey in the host city, including but not limited to interview scheduling, role assignment, uploading shifts, shift allocation, trainings, communication, Tournament-time operations and overall communications.
  • Identify and report system-related issues and diligently follow up to ensure their resolution or escalate as necessary to mitigate potential impacts.
Reporting and Feedback
  • Raise risks and concern to the Volunteer Management Central team in a timely manner
  • Prepare and submit daily reports during tournament time
  • Support the knowledge transfer programme to ensure planning and operational learnings are carried to the future events
  • Prepare and deliver the post-tournament report for the assigned Host City.
Education
  • Master's in Sports Management or Event Management or Project Management preferred.
Experience
  • Experience with events, and strong understanding of volunteer management
  • Proven experience in leading and managing volunteer programmes supporting the volunteer journey, including fit for purpose attraction, promotion, recruitment, training, engagement, and tournament operations.
  • Knowledge of project management principles and methodologies, with the ability to plan, coordinate, and deliver multiple workstreams simultaneously.
  • High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Experience in Volunteer Management System is an advantage. (Rosterfy knowledge is preferrable)
  • Demonstrated ability to develop beneficial working relationships with internal and external stakeholders, as well as with local networks within the Host City, especially among the football community.
  • Experience operating in complex, multi-stakeholder environments, with the flexibility and resilience to adapt to change and remain solution oriented.
  • A collaborative and inclusive team player, capable of working effectively with colleagues both on-site and remotely, while fostering a positive and respectful working environment.
  • Strong public speaking and presentation skills
  • Enthusiasm, and the ability to inspire and motivate volunteers
  • Ability to facilitate workshops, information sessions, and volunteer briefings
  • Community outreach and recruitment engagement skills.
  • Knowledge of the local landscape, community networks, and key stakeholders within the respective Host City is an advantage.
 Requirements
  • Right to work in Brazil
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Intermediate English (spoken and written)
  • Based in one of the listed cities (Belo Horizonte, Brasília, Fortaleza, Porto Alegre, Recife, Rio de Janeiro, Salvador & São Paulo) or able to relocate there independently

    Expected start date: June 2, 2026

job.title: Training & Readiness Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Manager, Training & Readiness (Safety & Security) is responsible for designing, implementing and overseeing the tournament-wide capability-building, training and readiness programme for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™. The role ensures that all safety and security personnel — including direct hires and supplier staff — meet defined competence, licensing and readiness standards prior to deployment.

The position establishes structured training frameworks, certification processes and exercise programmes to ensure operational preparedness across stadiums and non-competition sites. The role supports the creation of a sustainable safety and security legacy in Brazil through structured upskilling and professionalisation of the workforce.

• Conduct a comprehensive market capability and capacity analysis to identify gaps and risks related to the provision of staffed safety and security services
• Identify applicable qualification, certification and licensing criteria within Brazil and its federal states relevant to guarding, stewarding, close protection and related disciplines
• Design and implement a bespoke capability-building strategy for internal staff and external security suppliers
• Design and deliver security training programmes tailored to specific roles (e.g. security guards, stewards, supervisors, managers, TSLOs/RSLOs and direct hires)
• Develop and implement a structured training curriculum and training materials aligned with the Safety & Security Strategy and Operational Plan
• Design, develop and implement analogue and digital training solutions, including instructor-led programmes, workshops and e-learning packages, ensuring scalable, accessible and role-specific learning delivery across all safety and security personnel
• Define readiness criteria and certification processes for safety and security personnel prior to deployment
• Establish systems to track workforce competence, training completion and deployment readiness across all official tournament sites
• Coordinate and oversee joint exercises, table-top simulations, venue rehearsals and scenario-based drills to test operational response and inter-agency coordination
• Design and implement programmes to ensure compliance with statutory qualification and licensing standards on site, including the development of quality control audit processes
• Engage with safety and security suppliers to ensure that capability-building programmes are delivered effectively and that all personnel are trained before commencing work
• Coordinate with other FWWC2027 functional areas to ensure alignment on policies, procedures and response protocols
• Oversee the delivery and evaluation of drills, simulations and readiness exercises to ensure operational effectiveness
• Identify training gaps and implement corrective actions through continuous improvement processes
• Conduct on-site quality control audits to verify training completion, certification and operational competence
• Support senior management and site management in verifying training-related supplier invoices and compliance documentation

Education
• University degree in security management, education and training, public safety, risk management or a related field
• Professional training qualification or certification (e.g. instructional design, adult learning, security training accreditation) is desirable

Experience
• Experience in safety and security training, workforce readiness or operational preparedness roles
• Demonstrated experience designing and delivering structured training programmes in large-scale operational environments
• Experience coordinating exercises, simulations and multi-agency readiness activities
• Familiarity with Brazilian licensing and qualification requirements in the security sector
• Experience working with public security authorities and private security suppliers

Skills & Competencies
• Strong instructional design and training delivery capability
• Structured and analytical approach to readiness measurement and workforce tracking
• Excellent organisational and project management skills
• Ability to coordinate complex multi-stakeholder exercises and rehearsals
• Strong leadership and stakeholder engagement capability
• Clear written and verbal communication skills
• Advanced IT proficiency including MS Office suite, particularly Excel, PowerPoint and Word

Certifications & Requirements:
• Right to work in Brazil. 
• Fluent in English (spoken and written)
• Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
• Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

development of quality control audit processes • Engage with safety and security suppliers to ensure that capability-building programmes are delivered effectively and that all personnel are trained before commencing work • Coordinate with other FWWC2027 functional areas to ensure alignment on policies, procedures and response protocols • Oversee the delivery and evaluation of drills, simulations and readiness exercises to ensure operational effectiveness • Identify training gaps and implement corrective actions through continuous improvement processes • Conduct on-site quality control audits to verify training completion, certification and operational competence • Support senior management and site management in verifying training-related supplier invoices and compliance documentation
Education • University degree in security management, education and training, public safety, risk management or a related field • Professional training qualification or certification (e.g. instructional design, adult learning, security training accreditation) is desirable
Experience • Experience in safety and security training, workforce readiness or operational preparedness roles • Demonstrated experience designing and delivering structured training programmes in large-scale operational environments • Experience coordinating exercises, simulations and multi-agency readiness activities • Familiarity with Brazilian licensing and qualification requirements in the security sector • Experience working with public security authorities and private security suppliers
Skills & Competencies • Strong instructional design and training delivery capability • Structured and analytical approach to readiness measurement and workforce tracking • Excellent organisational and project management skills • Ability to coordinate complex multi-stakeholder exercises and rehearsals • Strong leadership and stakeholder engagement capability • Clear written and verbal communication skills • Advanced IT proficiency including MS Office suite, particularly Excel, PowerPoint and Word
Certifications & Requirements: • Right to work in Brazil. • Fluent in English (spoken and written) • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written) • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Event Marketing Coordinator
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Reporting organisationally to the Head of Marketing, Brand and Promotion (FIFA27, Rio de Janeiro) and the Team Lead, Event Marketing (FIFA HQ, Zurich), the Event Marketing Coordinator will be a key member of the FIFA27 Marketing, Brand and Promotion team, and will work in close collaboration with the Event Marketing in Zurich and other key individuals responsible for all marketing campaigns for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Support the creation and implementation of strategic, high impact, end-to-end marketing campaigns to meet and exceed the objectives of the Tournament 
  • In line with the wider FIFA27 and FIFA Zurich teams, contribute to the GTM multi-channel marketing plans (in close liaison with the digital marketing, ticketing, comms, BI, and brand teams) to support strategic goals to drive awareness, attendance, engagement and retainment 
  • Coordinate between internal and external tournament stakeholders as directed including Host Cities and Stadiums, Commercial Partners, and Official Media Partners and Licensees  
  • Support the development of cross cultural and sector tactical and long-term  
    strategical promotional partnerships with partners and third parties for mutual benefit 
  • Assist with the creation of relevant briefing, publishing plans, planning, valuation, tracking and final reporting documents related to marketing stakeholder’s needs as required 
  • Support the team to deliver launches of major brand assets, such as tournament logos, mascots, posters, and songs, as well as oversee multi-stage global and localised awareness and tournament related sales campaigns  
  • Ad-hoc support of additional workstreams in relation to event marketing needs which may include contact/planning/liaising and coordination with stakeholders with football clubs or participating member associations (PMA’s) 
  • Become an expert in FIFA event marketing milestone planning and FIFA processes and organise and maintain an updated library of marketing-related assets ensuring all files are easy to locate, properly labelled, and accessible to the team 
  • Assist in providing stakeholders with timely and accurate information on promotional and marketing events, activations and opportunities and ensure stakeholders to drive optimisation of the opportunities. 
  • Provide administrative and project support across the Event Marketing team and collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders as needed 
Education 
  • Related bachelor’s degree or a similar level of higher education. 
Skills & Competencies 
  • Adaptable, effective and efficient under pressure, immovable deadlines, and in stressful situations  
  • Effective at working proactively and independently whilst providing regular updates, being able to communicate clearly and effectively via different channels with key stakeholders 
  • Ability to convey ideas clearly and collaborate effectively with team members and stakeholders 
  • Willingness to work non-traditional hours including evenings and weekends as needed 
  • Demonstrates strong attention to detail in all tasks and deliverables 
Work Experience 
  • Experience in a marketing function favourable to hold experience with sport’s governing bodies, LOCs and, or sports and entertainment rightsholders 
  • Understanding of the dynamics of football 
  • Experience in Brand Marketing and Marketing Operations, Communications, Events 
  • A passion for football 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint), planning software and online collaboration tools (Monday.com)  
Requirements
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Senior Legal Counsel Commercial Legal
job.html_description:
We govern the beautiful game and ensure it's run with transparency and integrity.

Join our team in Miami and support us on our mission.
The Senior Counsel will be an active member of the Commercial Legal Team working in a “team first” approach to support the delivery of legal services relating to FIFA’s global operations.

The role of Senior Legal Counsel will support as follows:
  • Reporting to the Commercial Legal team leads
  • Assist in supporting on legal matters relating to FIFA’s Innovation and Technology operations
  • Provide legal support in the following areas:
    • FIFA’s direct to consumer media offerings, (including distribution and syndication and advertising sales)
    • hardware and software procurement
    • the collection of match data
    • ensuring appropriate ownership, protection and exploitation of FIFA-proprietary technology developments and collaborations
    • website and other consumer-facing T&Cs in the digital environment
    • FIFA’s data and artificial intelligence initiatives
    • FIFA’s innovation portfolios (including VR/XR and AI related matters)
  • General commercial legal support to FIFA’s strategic and corporate development activities
  • Support as point of contact for legal matters for FIFA’s Football, Technology and Innovation Department
  • Providing strategic liaison with FIFA’s Data Protection department to ensure seamless integration into FIFA digital products
  • Providing detailed knowledge of FIFA’s rights and obligations in connection with its digital and technology programmes, including the implementation and protection thereof; including the provision of legal and contractual input on FIFA’s existing commercial commitments and future commercial possibilities
  • Providing commercial transactional support and guidance regarding the interpretation of relevant commercial agreements, including the assessment of relevant business and strategic risks, and commercial regulatory matters
  • Developing and maintaining suitable template agreements
  • Support in the delivery of ad hoc advice on issues relating to technology related activities
We work hard at FIFA.
We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.

And we respect our values. Always.

For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.

This role would suit someone with great organisational and communication skills who is a dedicated team player and excels in being professional with excellent legal skills.

The specific competencies we require for this position are:
  • Ability to participate and be part of a high-performing team, fostering collaboration, driving results, and ensuring the successful achievement of organizational goals
  • First class written and spoken English language skills, consistent with drafting and negotiating complex, high value and nuanced English language agreements
  • Ability to provide and communicate effectively practical and solutions-based legal advice
  • Excellent understanding of the digital and technology industry landscapes
  • Excellent negotiation, drafting, problem solving and analytical skills
  • Structured, reliable, well-organised with a strong eye for detail
  • Proactive, with a strong sense of personal responsibility with an ability to work autonomously on a wide variety of projects
  • Resilient with an ability to competently handle a large workload amid rapidly shifting priorities
  • Open-minded team player with an affinity for working within a variety of cultures and excellent communication skills
  • High degree of professionalism and confident demeanour
  • Ability to be a good ambassador for FIFA in all manner of external facing situations, including during contractual negotiations, external workshops and during FIFA competition-related assignments
  • Ability to create, develop and maintain strong and reliable relationships with FIFA senior management as well as external legal and commercial counterparts
  • Willingness to operate outside of comfort zone
  • Takes initiative to reflect on how to improve and develop working styles and methods to assist the organisation and internal clients
  • Juris Doctor (JD) degree from a top-tier accredited law school
  • Active bar membership is required
  • Minimum of 7 years expertise "on the job" in the industry in an in-house legal department or a top-tier law firm focusing on relevant matters
  • Native level English (or equivalent), consistent with requirement to draft and negotiate complex and nuanced English language agreements and advice
  • Proficiency in any other FIFA language (French/Spanish) is an asset but not necessary
  • Proficient in Word / PowerPoint / Excel
  • Comfortable with international travel
  • Readiness for stand-by duties and special projects
The biggest benefit of a career at FIFA is the privilege to work for the beautiful game and create something that matters.
 
Alongside that privilege, we also offer some extra perks and benefits:
 
Health insurance: We offer generous medical, vision, and dental insurance coverage.
Continuous learning: We care about your development and encourage internal mobility, offering a variety of training and education to help achieve it.
Retirement: We offer generous 401(k) employer contributions.
Vacation: Maintain a healthy work/life balance with a generous vacation allowance.

job.title: Venue Operations Assistant Manager – 8 Host Cities (Brazil)
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Venue Operations Assistant Manager supports the Venue Operations Manager in the planning, coordination and operational delivery of the stadium activities for the FIFA Women's World Cup Brazil 2027™.  

Working closely with FIFA functional areas, venue teams, stadiums and host city authorities and service providers, the role is responsible for coordinating operational activities, monitoring project progress, supporting venue readiness, and ensuring that venue plans are implemented efficiently and in compliance with FIFA Hosting Requirements.

The Venue Operations Assistant Manager plays a key role in facilitating communication across stakeholders, tracking operational deliverables, supporting reporting processes, meetings and inspections, and contributing to the successful delivery of match days and non-match day activities.

The tournament will be staged across 8 stadiums in Brazil, and we’re recruiting one Venue Operations Assistant Manager for each host city: 
  • Belo Horizonte - Estádio Mineirão
  • Brasília - Estádio Nacional
  • Fortaleza - Arena Castelão
  • Porto Alegre - Estádio Beira-Rio
  • Recife - Arena de Pernambuco
  • Rio de Janeiro - Estádio do Maracanã
  • Salvador - Arena Fonte Nova
  • São Paulo - Arena Itaquera
Please apply only if you currently live in one of these cities or can relocate there independently.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Venue Operations Assistant Manager for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ include:  
  •  Support the Venue Operations Manager in the planning and operational delivery of the venue activities.  
  • Coordinate day-to-day operational planning across FIFA functional areas and local stakeholders.  
  • Monitor the implementation of venue operational plans and follow up on action items and deadlines.  
  • Assist in integrating functional area requirements into the overall venue operational plan.  
  • Coordinate meetings, workshops and venue operational briefings with internal and external stakeholders.  
  • Support venue inspections, site visits, and stadium readiness activities.  
  • Track venue issues, risks and mitigation actions, escalating matters to the Venue Operations Manager when appropriate.  
  • Maintain operational documentation, reports, meeting minutes, and planning tools.  
  • Support to coordinate  Operational Maps review (BIM360) follow-up activities resulting from inspections and site visits.  
  • Support the planning and delivery of official test events and operational exercises.  
  • Assist in coordinating venue service providers and local authorities during planning and event-time operations.  
  • Support budget tracking, procurement follow-up and operational reporting as requested by the Venue Operations Manager.  
  • Contribute to knowledge management activities, operational debriefs and lessons learned documentation.  
  • Support match day and non-match day operations, ensuring timely communication and coordination across all stakeholders.  
  • Coordinate the preparation, maintenance and distribution of daily operational documentation, including the Daily Run Sheet, operational schedules, daily reports and other venue operational documents.  
  • Coordinate venue daily operational meetings, ensuring agendas, documentation, action tracking and follow-up with all relevant stakeholders.  
  • Coordinate the planning and delivery of venue team onboarding, inductions and operational training sessions to ensure all personnel are prepared for venue operations.  
  • Liaise with the Stadium Authority core team to ensure the timely delivery of all core stadium services and operational requirements, maintaining close coordination throughout the planning and operational phases.  
  • Act as a key operational focal point during match days, proactively resolving operational issues on the ground while liaising with and responding to the Venue Operations Centre (VOC), ensuring timely communication, issue escalation and coordinated implementation of solutions.  
  • Perform other operational duties as assigned by the Venue Operations Manager.
Education
  • University degree in an appropriate field (or equivalent professional experience).
Experience
  • Strong experience in venue operations, event management, sports venues or large-scale international events. 
  • Experience coordinating multiple stakeholders in complex operational environments. 
  • Strong organizational and project coordination skills. 
  • Experience supporting operational planning and event delivery. 
  • Ability to work under pressure while managing multiple priorities. 
  • Experience with reporting, planning tools and operational documentation. 
  • Experience working with public authorities, venue operators or service providers is considered an advantage. 
  • Skills & Competencies.
  • Excellent organizational and coordination skills.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal abilities. 
  • Proactive and solution-oriented mindset. 
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to follow through on deliverables. 
  • Ability to work collaboratively within multidisciplinary and multicultural teams. 
  • Good problem-solving and decision-support skills. 
  • Ability to prioritize tasks in a fast-paced environment. 
  • Adaptability, resilience and flexibility. 
  • Strong sense of ownership and accountability. 
  • Commitment to delivering operational excellence.  
Certifications & Requirements
  • Right to work in Brazil
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Strong administrative and IT skills, including Microsoft Office (Word, Excel and PowerPoint) and Monday.com.

job.title: Venue Technology Manager (Porto Alegre)
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Venue Technology Manager is responsible for supporting the Technology team with venue specific technology related deliverables for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027 TM (FWWC 2027) within Brazil. The role entails coordinating with all relevant parties the planning, implementation, readiness and operations of all Event Technology services pertinent to the tournament and / or associated events within Brazil.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Responsible to ensure all aspects of Technology, Telecommunications and Broadcast Technology Services are implemented in accordance with the pre-agreed FIFA Technology requirements at the assigned venue(s)
  • Manage a strong diverse Venue Technology team composed of paid staff, contractors and volunteers for operating the technology services at the assigned venue(s) 
  • Collaborate with stakeholders’ groups, clients, providers, and government entities in relation to ensuring the delivery of technology services across all venues, in accordance with the defined governance structure
  • Manage and contribute to the venue technology working groups in accordance with the agreed governance structure across all aspects of the tournament programme (planning, implementation, readiness and operations) 
  • Manage the technology demand / needs requirements for technology services across the tournament space (Technology Equipment Allocation Process) 
  • Plan the delivery of suitable end user technology equipment and services to all FWWC 2027 departments and FIFA constituent groups, including hardware, services and consumables 
  • Facilitate the collection and distribution of technology requirements of all client groups involved in the FWWC 2027 across all client groups, including, Media, Broadcast, Commercial, Football Technology etc. 
  • Ensure that all information pertaining to infrastructure, services and spaces needed for the delivery and support of all technology services are communicated to, and understood by, venue/site authorities and respective delivery teams 
  • Provide guidance and direction to relevant teams on matters relating to the structural build-out and technology services implementation, including the commissioning and final decommissioning of tournament venues  
  • In collaboration with assigned personnel and Stadium partners, relevant parties (private and governmental) enable / support the timely delivery of venue technology services. 
  • Identify and report on the progress of all critical dependencies in accordance with the reporting methodologies 
  • Manage the asset tracking and management of deployed technology equipment 
  • Track the progress of structural build-out and IT implementation leading up to the timely availability of components and services and including the commissioning and final decommissioning of the venues 
  • Develop regular progress, risk and issue management reports in accordance with the governance structure / reporting methodologies and, or PMO systems 
  •  Follow-up with various stakeholders, providers, and government entities in relation to ensuring delivery of technology services / dependencies as per agreed requirements, service levels etc. 
  • Support and ensure the team adhere to the IT Service management protocols 
  • Ensure positive attitude from the Venue Technology teams and good relationship with all the parties, including FIFA teams, Local FIFA Subsidiary (LFS) areas/projects, end-users, suppliers, Host Country/ City counterparts
  • Manage the on-site IT Technicians and support teams during tournament time operations, including end-of-day reporting and incident escalation to Technology Command Centre. 
Education
  • University degree or similar higher education, in Information Technology, Computer Science or related field
Experience
  • Strong overall IT experience
  • Experience in the Sports / major event industry is a must
  • Proven track record of successful delivery of complex IT programs and projects
Skills & Competencies
  • Manager with proven track record in similar projects
  • Good knowledge of venue technology services across major sporting events 
  • Have up to date knowledge and project management expertise in all event related technologies, including, all types of technology business solutions, community access TV (CATV), radio communications, wired communication, printing, broadcast contribution network, spectrum, audio and visual. 
  • Ability to always maintain a professional demeanor. Consistently interacts professionally and works productively with all levels of employee. 
  • Excellent cross group collaboration skills and demonstrated ability to lead virtual teams across groups, conflict management and driving consensus to deliver on program milestones  
  • Good Leadership skills and able to manage and motivate other team members 
  • Highly adept at consulting, negotiating, communicating, consensus building and facilitation
  • Excellent verbal and written communication, training, and presentation skills
  • Ability to adapt speech to audience coming from a broad spectrum of disciplines and with various levels of technical knowledge
  • Leadership and accountability across all areas of relevance  
  • Strong political acumen and adaptable to a wide range of working environments
Certifications & Requirements
  • Right to work in Brazil 
  • Fluent in English
  • Native or Fluent in Portuguese
  • Proficiency in any other FIFA language (FR / ES / GER) is an asset
Location: Porto Alegre, RS, Brazil
job.plain_text_description:

The role entails coordinating with all relevant parties the planning, implementation, readiness and operations of all Event Technology services pertinent to the tournament and / or associated events within Brazil.
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
- Responsible to ensure all aspects of Technology, Telecommunications and Broadcast Technology Services are implemented in accordance with the pre-agreed FIFA Technology requirements at the assigned venue(s) - Manage a strong diverse Venue Technology team composed of paid staff, contractors and volunteers for operating the technology services at the assigned venue(s) - Collaborate with stakeholders’ groups, clients, providers, and government entities in relation to ensuring the delivery of technology services across all venues, in accordance with the defined governance structure - Manage and contribute to the venue technology working groups in accordance with the agreed governance structure across all aspects of the tournament programme (planning, implementation, readiness and operations) - Manage the technology demand / needs requirements for technology services across the tournament space (Technology Equipment Allocation Process) - Plan the delivery of suitable end user technology equipment and services to all FWWC 2027 departments and FIFA constituent groups, including hardware, services and consumables - Facilitate the collection and distribution of technology requirements of all client groups involved in the FWWC 2027 across all client groups, including, Media, Broadcast, Commercial, Football Technology etc. - Ensure that all information pertaining to infrastructure, services and spaces needed for the delivery and support of all technology services are communicated to, and understood by, venue/site authorities and respective delivery teams - Provide guidance and direction to relevant teams on matters relating to the structural build-out and technology services implementation, including the commissioning and final decommissioning of tournament venues - In collaboration with assigned personnel and Stadium partners, relevant parties (private and governmental) enable / support the timely delivery of venue technology services. - Identify and report on the progress of all critical dependencies in accordance with the reporting methodologies - Manage the asset tracking and management of deployed technology equipment - Track the progress of structural build-out and IT implementation leading up to the timely availability of components and services and including the commissioning and final decommissioning of the venues - Develop regular progress, risk and issue management reports in accordance with the governance structure / reporting methodologies and, or PMO systems - Follow-up with various stakeholders, providers, and government entities in relation to ensuring delivery of technology services / dependencies as per agreed requirements, service levels etc. - Support and ensure the team adhere to the IT Service management protocols - Ensure positive attitude from the Venue Technology teams and good relationship with all the parties, including FIFA teams, Local FIFA Subsidiary (LFS) areas/projects, end-users, suppliers, Host Country/ City counterparts - Manage the on-site IT Technicians and support teams during tournament time operations, including end-of-day reporting and incident escalation to Technology Command Centre.
Education
- University degree or similar higher education, in Information Technology, Computer Science or related field
Experience
- Strong overall IT experience - Experience in the Sports / major event industry is a must - Proven track record of successful delivery of complex IT programs and projects
Skills & Competencies
- Manager with proven track record in similar projects - Good knowledge of venue technology services across major sporting events - Have up to date knowledge and project management expertise in all event related technologies, including, all types of technology business solutions, community access TV (CATV), radio communications, wired communication, printing, broadcast contribution network, spectrum, audio and visual. - Ability to always maintain a professional demeanor. Consistently interacts professionally and works productively with all levels of employee. - Excellent cross group collaboration skills and demonstrated ability to lead virtual teams across groups, conflict management and driving consensus to deliver on program milestones - Good Leadership skills and able to manage and motivate other team members - Highly adept at consulting, negotiating, communicating, consensus building and facilitation - Excellent verbal and written communication, training, and presentation skills - Ability to adapt speech to audience coming from a broad spectrum of disciplines and with various levels of technical knowledge - Leadership and accountability across all areas of relevance - Strong political acumen and adaptable to a wide range of working environments
Certifications & Requirements
- Right to work in Brazil - Fluent in English - Native or Fluent in Portuguese - Proficiency in any other FIFA language (FR / ES / GER) is an asset
Location: Porto Alegre, RS, Brazil

job.title: Guest Experience Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Guest Experience Manager is responsible for the development and implementation of the guest experience concept exclusively within the FIFA VIP Hotel environment, including the FIFA Club.

This role focuses on brand implementation, experiential elements, FIFA Club concept (including F&B), and curated guest touchpoints such as gifts and in-hotel experiences.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Lead the development and execution of the VIP Guest Experience concept within the FIFA VIP Hotel, including FIFA Club, brand implementation, and experiential elements (e.g. gifts, amenities, and curated guest touchpoints).
  • Define the FIFA Club concept (including F&B, design, and guest journey) and oversee its implementation.
  • Supervise the tender process, evaluation, and appointment of guest experience suppliers related to VIP Hotel and FIFA Club delivery (e.g. experiential agencies, branding, F&B concept providers).
  • Coordinate and maintain a strategic overview of all guest experience activities within the VIP Hotel environment.
  • During tournament time, act as FIFA Club Manager (or oversee FIFA Club Manager role), responsible for operational setup and delivery of the FIFA Club.
  • Collaborate with relevant functional areas to ensure alignment of VIP Hotel guest experience delivery (e.g. Accommodation, Protocol, Transport, Branding).
  • Establish clear lines of communication and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders and involved parties.
  • Develop training concepts and deliver training sessions for all relevant suppliers, their service staff, and volunteers, focusing on excellence and ‘wow’ customer service.
  • Attend site visits and inspections.
Education:
  • Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Business Management, Hospitality Management, Event Management, Marketing & Communications, or a related field. Specialization in hospitality, event management, or experience design is an advantage.
Experience:
  • Experience in developing and delivering premium VIP guest experience concepts and operations, ideally within hotel environments, hospitality settings, or large-scale events.
  • Extensive experience in concept development and implementation of guest experience elements, including branding, scenography, F&B concepts, and curated guest touchpoints.
  • Experience in designing and delivering premium hospitality environments (e.g. lounges, clubs, VIP spaces, or hotel-based experiences).
  • Proven experience in managing and coordinating external agencies and suppliers for experience delivery (e.g. production, branding, F&B, experiential agencies).
  • Hands-on experience in implementing on-site guest experience concepts, including setup, testing, and operational delivery.
  • Experience working in cross-functional environments, coordinating with hospitality, accommodation, and operational stakeholders.
Skills & Competencies:
  • Fluent Portuguese and English (spoken and written) with excellent communication skills.
  • Strong attention to detail with the ability to ensure high-quality delivery of premium guest experience standards.
  • Ability to translate conceptual ideas into operational execution, ensuring consistency between design and on-site delivery.
  • Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to anticipate, manage, and resolve complex situations in fast-paced environments.
  • Proven ability to manage and steer multiple external suppliers and agencies to deliver against defined concepts and timelines.
  • Collaborative mindset with strong stakeholder management skills across cross-functional teams.
  • Strong planning and organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple workstreams and priorities simultaneously.
  • High level of adaptability and resilience in dynamic, high-pressure event environments.
  • Solid understanding of premium hospitality environments and guest expectations.
  • Advanced skills in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint; Project or similar tools is an advantage)
Certifications & Requirements:
  • Project Management certification or equivalent experience is an advantage.
  • Additional certifications in hospitality, event management, or experience design are considered a plus.
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

Certifications & Requirements:

job.title: Competition Manager
job.html_description:
As the organiser of some of the biggest and most iconic sport events in the world, we lead the way in the sport and event industry.

Join the team and support us on our mission.
These are your key tasks:
  • Lead and deliver FIFA matches and tournaments in line with FIFA Match Operations Guidelines, IFAB Laws of the Game, and industry best practice
  • Act as CPM project lead for match operations at FIFA tournaments, overseeing preparation, delivery, and on-site execution
  • Establish and develop the Volunteer Programme for Competition Management
  • Drive and contribute to key development projects (e.g. competition operations tools, processes, equipment and draw deliveries)
  • Lead assigned CPM sub-projects, ensuring delivery on time, within budget, and to a high-quality standard
  • Collaborate closely with internal and external stakeholders and support senior management on administrative and strategic matters
We work hard at FIFA.
We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.

And we respect our values. Always.

For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.

The specific competencies we require for this position are:
  • Bachelor’s degree in Sport Management, Business Administration, or a related field
  • 4–6 years of solid experience in competition management, match operations, and event/project management (ideally football tournaments)
  • Strong project leadership skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, international environment
  • Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills; confident decision-maker and proactive problem-solver
  • Strong understanding of football regulations, competition frameworks, and the IFAB Laws of the Game
  • Fluent in English; additional FIFA languages are an asset
  • Proficient in MS Word, Excel and PowerPoint; knowledge of project management tools is a plus
  • Willingness to travel and take on on-site operational responsibilities
The biggest benefit of a career at FIFA is the privilege to work for the beautiful game and create something that matters.

Alongside that privilege, we also offer some extra perks and benefits:
  • Health and fitness: Regular football sessions for all abilities, plus on-site gym.
  • Home office: Up to two days of home office per week if work permits and home office allowance for all.
  • Language courses: Offered to all staff to aid personal and professional growth.
  • Continuous learning: We care about your development and encourage internal mobility, offering a variety of training and education to help achieve it.
  • Pension: Flexible pension benefits with generous employer contributions.
  • Relocation assistance: A dedicated team will be on hand to support your relocation.
  • Sustainability: We support all employees using public transport.
  • Vacation: Maintain a healthy work/life balance with a generous vacation allowance.
  • Volunteering: Make an impact beyond your regular role and bond with colleagues by volunteering with one of our partners.

job.title: Partner Rights Delivery Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Working alongside the FIFA/FIFA27 Partnership Rights Delivery (PRD) team in Zurich and Brazil and reporting to the FIFA Senior Partner Rights Delivery Manager in Zurich and the FIFA27 Head of Commercial Partnerships, the Partner Rights Delivery Manager supports the operational implementation and fulfillment of FIFA Commercial Partners’ contractual rights and obligations. This role includes coordination of official sites requirements, stadiums preparations, and the delivery of Commercial Partners activities and related deliverables.

This position plays an integral part in ensuring a successful tournament through the delivery of FIFA’s commercial operations in the host country. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Support the planning and execution of the operational implementation and delivery of the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ Commercial Partners agreements. 
  • Implement and coordinate all commercial operations in line with the rights granted to Commercial Partners for the competition and competition-related events and sites.
  • Support the implementation of all commercial/sponsorship activations and commercial rights programmes of FIFA’s Commercial Partners.
  • Collaborate with the Stadium Venue Managers, Stadium Authorities, and relevant Functional Areas to ensure delivery of the contractual rights and obligations of FIFA’s Commercial Partners.  
  • Assist in coordinating and delivering stadium inspections, open days, and Commercial Partner operational visits, ensuring alignment with commercial and F&B concessions requirements.
  • Manage and monitor the implementation of FIFA’s clean site obligation and Commercial Partners’ exclusivity in the official sites. 
  • Ensure the Commercial Partners’ recognition programme is correctly implemented in designated stadium areas in accordance with established guidelines, including within the F&B concessions programme.
  • Assist in the implementation of specific commercial/marketing projects at the stadiums, such as Stadium Fan Experience areas, Special Marketing Programmes, Youth Programme, Value-in-Kind (VIK) operations, LED pitch side board operations, and other Commercial Partner activities, in coordination with the FIFA/FIFA27 project leads and the entire Partnership Rights Delivery team.
  • Act as an operational point of contact for Commercial Partners in the host country for commercial delivery matters, supporting issue resolution and expectation management throughout the event delivery process.
  • Work with PRD suppliers and assist them in the delivery of their obligations.
  • Support the planning and delivery of all Partnership Rights Delivery related spaces, facilities and flows in the stadiums (and other official sites) as per defined stadium requirements under the guidance of the FIFA PRD project lead.
  • Provide operational support to the Partnership Rights Delivery Venue Team (Marketing Venue Managers) during the tournament period.
  • Take primary responsibility for the administrative preparation and ongoing maintenance of comprehensive documentation, operational guidelines, briefing materials, tracking tools, and structured reports, ensuring accuracy, consistency across all Partnership Rights Delivery activities.
  • Prepare comprehensive reports, guidelines, and presentations on rights delivery progress, highlighting key achievements, risks, and recommendations.
  • Contribute to the preparation of tournament debrief documentation.
  • Assume administrative responsibility for the Partnership Rights Delivery team and provide support as required
Education
  • University degree, ideally in Sports Management, Sports Administration, Business Administration, Marketing, or a related field, with exposure to commercial/marketing operations or sponsorship activations.
Experience
  • Experience in a comparable role within an international, fast-paced, and project-driven environment, preferably in the events and/or sports industry.
  • Strong knowledge of commercial operations and/or sponsorship activations in major event settings and/or the sports industry
  • Experience working in international sports events or large-scale event environments is considered a strong advantage.
  • Experience working within a multicultural and/or multinational organisation.
  • Experience in the planning and delivery of fan experience areas and activations is considered an asset.
Skills & Competencies
  • Very strong administrative skills, with proven ability to produce high-quality documentation, guidelines, reports, and structured tracking tools.
  • Strong organisational skills with high attention to detail and a structured working approach.
  • Demonstrated ability to coordinate and deliver projects within an event environment, manage priorities effectively, and work independently within defined responsibilities.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.
  • Service-oriented mindset with a proactive and solution-driven approach to commercial rights delivery.
  • Ability to work collaboratively as part of a team and perform effectively under tight timelines.
  • Positive attitude, patience, persistence, and resilience in dynamic environments.
Certification & Requirements
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, PowerPoint, Word, Teams; knowledge of Visio and MS Project is an advantage).
  • Experience using planning software and online collaboration tools.
  • Willingness to travel and work extended hours during peak project phases and tournament time.
Candidate Requirements:
  • Right to work in Brazil
  • Bilingual - Portuguese and English
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro for the duration of the employment (pre-tournament and operational tournament time)

Skills & Competencies

Candidate Requirements:
- Right to work in Brazil - Bilingual - Portuguese and English - Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro for the duration of the employment (pre-tournament and operational tournament time)

job.title: Site Security and Audit Coordinator
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.

The Site Security and Audit Coordinator is responsible for the structured protection, oversight and assurance of FIFA assets and safety and security delivery at stadium level for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.

The role develops and implements patrimonial security and asset protection plans, establishes 24/7 security protocols during the Exclusive Use Period (EUP), and ensures that deployed security resources and stewarding operations are transparent, verifiable and compliant with FIFA regulations and contractual agreements.

The position safeguards both operational integrity and financial accountability by managing deployment information, maintaining the stadium “Blue Book,” hosting and validating event stewarding information, and conducting audits and compliance checks throughout tournament operations.

• Develop patrimonial security and asset protection plans for FIFA assets at each stadium, including overlay, branding, broadcast and restricted operational areas
• Define and implement 24/7 security protocols during the Exclusive Use Period (EUP), ensuring continuous protection of FIFA property and sensitive areas
• Establish and monitor robust perimeter control, access control and asset safeguarding measures aligned with risk assessments and FIFA standards
• Manage deployment information for each stadium, including security staffing levels, steward allocations and shift structures
• Hold, manage and continuously update the official stadium “Blue Book,” ensuring accuracy and structured sharing with relevant functional areas (e.g. Finance, Operations)
• Host and maintain event stewarding information, ensuring that stewarding numbers, roles, qualifications and deployment models are clearly documented and validated
• Develop, maintain and regularly update detailed dot plans reflecting security and steward deployment positions, resource allocations and operational coverage at stadium level
• Prepare daily verification protocols for each day of the Exclusive Use Period to ensure that supplier invoices correspond to actual deployed security and stewarding resources
• Verify installation, attendance and deployment reports against contractual requirements and approved resource plans
• Identify discrepancies in deployment or billing and coordinate corrective actions with suppliers and internal stakeholders
• Arrange for and/or conduct structured audits, inspections and compliance checks during tournament time
• Assess compliance with FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Regulations, operational policies and contractual obligations
• Provide structured audit reports and assurance updates to the Senior Manager, Stadiums
• Support the Senior Manager, Stadiums with related administrative tasks, documentation control and reporting requirements
• Contribute to the drafting, review and maintenance of relevant policies and procedures relating to asset protection, deployment governance and stadium-level security operations
• Monitor implementation of corrective actions and support continuous improvement of asset protection and deployment governance processes
• Support operational readiness validation prior to each EUP phase and matchday
Education
• University degree or higher qualification in security management, risk management, business administration, audit, finance, data management or a related field

Experience
• Experience in physical security, asset protection, compliance, deployment governance or venue security operations
• Demonstrated experience managing large-scale operational deployment data or similar
• Proven experience conducting audits, inspections or compliance reviews in multi-site or major event settings
• Experience verifying operational resource allocation against contractual and financial documentation
• Experience working with private security suppliers, stewarding providers and venue operators

Skills & Competencies
• Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including pivot tables, complex formulas and structured data management
• Experience using macros and/or VBA to automate and manage large datasets desirable
• Strong analytical and numerical skills with high attention to detail
• Ability to design and maintain deployment tracking and verification systems
• Structured reporting capability for senior management
• High integrity when handling sensitive operational and financial information
• Strong organisational and coordination skills
• Clear written and verbal communication skills
• Advanced IT proficiency including MS Office suite, including PowerPoint and Word

Certifications & Requirements
• Right to live and work in Brazil
• Willingness to travel nationally and work extended hours during tournament periods
• Fluency in Portuguese (spoken and written); working knowledge of English is essential
