and compliance checks during tournament time • Assess compliance with FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Regulations, operational policies and contractual obligations • Provide structured audit reports and assurance updates to the Senior Manager, Stadiums • Support the Senior Manager, Stadiums with related administrative tasks, documentation control and reporting requirements • Contribute to the drafting, review and maintenance of relevant policies and procedures relating to asset protection, deployment governance and stadium-level security operations • Monitor implementation of corrective actions and support continuous improvement of asset protection and deployment governance processes • Support operational readiness validation prior to each EUP phase and matchday
Education • University degree or higher qualification in security management, risk management, business administration, audit, finance, data management or a related field
Experience • Experience in physical security, asset protection, compliance, deployment governance or venue security operations • Demonstrated experience managing large-scale operational deployment data or similar • Proven experience conducting audits, inspections or compliance reviews in multi-site or major event settings • Experience verifying operational resource allocation against contractual and financial documentation • Experience working with private security suppliers, stewarding providers and venue operators
Skills & Competencies • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including pivot tables, complex formulas and structured data management • Experience using macros and/or VBA to automate and manage large datasets desirable • Strong analytical and numerical skills with high attention to detail • Ability to design and maintain deployment tracking and verification systems • Structured reporting capability for senior management • High integrity when handling sensitive operational and financial information • Strong organisational and coordination skills • Clear written and verbal communication skills • Advanced IT proficiency including MS Office suite, including PowerPoint and Word
Certifications & Requirements • Right to live and work in Brazil • Willingness to travel nationally and work extended hours during tournament periods • Fluency in Portuguese (spoken and written); working knowledge of English is essential

job.title: Host City Volunteer Coordinator - 8 Host Cities (Brazil)
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
 
Support the delivery of the FIFA Volunteer Programme under the leadership ofthe Host City Volunteer Manager and the guidance of the FIFA27 Central Volunteer Management and FIFA Volunteer Management Zurich.

The role is supporting the coordination of volunteer recruitment, training, communications, and day-to-day operations within the assigned Host City.  This includes coordinating activities within the FIFA Volunteer Platform, supporting Stadium Volunteer Centre readiness and operations, assisting with volunteer recruitment and engagement initiatives, and helping ensure the consistent delivery of the Volunteer Programme in line with FIFA standards.

The ideal candidate will join a dynamic, multicultural environment and bring both strategic insight and firsthand expertise to one of the most exciting sporting events in the world.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

The following points will apply to all host cities and venues where the competition takes place. 
Responsibilities include but are not limited to:
· Support the Host City Volunteer Manager in delivering the FIFA Volunteer Programme across the assigned Host City
· Support the administration and day-to-day use of the FIFA Volunteer Platform: selecting volunteers, role offers, shift management, change maanagement, communication and any other related queries
· Support the recruitment process (through application screening) and ensuring that successful candidates are appropriately matched to roles which best suit their skills and experience (assignment).
· Participate in site visits, as necessary to inspect and validate potential pre-tournament and tournament-time required spaces
· Coordinating the delivery of the Volunteer Training Programme (general and role/venue-specific training) and support Functional Areas (FAs) with Role-Specific Training plan.
· Deliver Trainings for engagement volunteers 
· Ensuring volunteers have a good experience and are deployed depending on the daily activities taking place.
· Coordinating the set-up, daily operations and readiness of the stadium volunteer centre and providing inspiration, support, and motivation to the volunteers, as well as leading the reward and recognition programme and the engagement programme during tournament time.
· Communicate with applicants and volunteers via FIFA Communications tools 
· Leading the volunteers’ day-to-day activities and supporting them while on shift.
· Supporting the HC Volunteer Manager in coordination with FAs at the venue for shift planning, change management process and volunteer deployment.
· Monitoring volunteer attendance and escalating if any issue arises.
· Undertaking the day-to-day management of volunteers to ensure the well-being of volunteers on-site.
· Ensuring that shifts are fully covered, and FAs daily requirements are fulfilled.
· Collaborate closely with the Central volunteer management team and relevant on-site Functional Area (FA) teams, to ensure the timely delivery of engagement items and gifts. 
· Coordinate the execution of engagement initiatives and gift distribution, ensuring accurate inventory control, asset tracking, and efficient management of all items and equipment.
· Support with the planning and delivery of Volunteer Thank-You event
· Contribute to the development of volunteer communications, operational guidelines, and information materials across all phases of the volunteer journey.
· Provide support to applicants and volunteers through ServiceNow, responding to enquiries promptly and escalating complex issues where required
· Depending on your assign city: support the planning and delivery of FIFA Women's World Cup 2027™ events, including the Final Draw, Team Workshop, Play-Off Tournament, Volunteer Thank-You events, and other official tournament activities, as required.


  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in Portuguese (local language), basic level of English is an added advantage
  • Education:  Undergraduate Degree in Sports Management or Event Management or Project Management preferred.
  • Experience of working for events, and strong understanding of volunteer management 
  • Desirable experience in delivering volunteer programmes supporting the volunteer journey, including fit for purpose attraction, promotion, recruitment, training, engagement, and tournament operations.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Knowledge of volunteer or workforce system an advantage
  • Demonstrated ability to develop beneficial working relationships with internal and external stakeholders, as well as with local networks within the Host City, especially among the football community.
  • Experience operating in complex, multi-stakeholder environments, with the flexibility and resilience to adapt to change and remain solution oriented.
  • Strong public speaking and presentation skills
  • Experience in delivering workshops, volunteer briefings or training sessions
  • Enthusiasm, and the ability to inspire and motivate volunteers from diverse backgrounds
  • Ability to facilitate workshops, information sessions, and volunteer briefings
  • Knowledge of the local landscape, community networks, and key stakeholders within the respective Host City is an advantage.

job.title: Workforce Systems, Tools & Data Coordinator
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Workforce Data & Systems Coordinator supports the effective day‑to‑day operation of workforce systems, data administration and reporting for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™. The role helps ensure workforce data is accurate, consistent and timely, contributing to reliable planning, reporting and operational readiness. Working closely with the Workforce Data & Systems Manager and Workforce Planning team, the coordinator provides system, data and reporting support across workforce planning and operations, including preparation for test events and tournament delivery, helping to ensure systems and processes function smoothly throughout the event lifecycle.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Workforce Systems & Workforce Systems Support


·       Support the day‑to‑day operation of workforce planning and workforce operations systems used across the tournament.


·       Assist with system configuration, user access management, testing and troubleshooting under the guidance of the Workforce Data & Systems Manager.


·       Act as a first point of contact for workforce system users, logging issues, resolving basic queries and escalating more complex system issues as required.


·       Support the setup and maintenance of workforce operations systems


 


Workforce Data Administration


·       Maintain accurate and up‑to‑date workforce data across multiple systems, ensuring consistency and completeness.


·       Conduct regular data checks, validation and cleansing in line with agreed data standards.


·       Support the coordination of workforce data inputs from planning, recruitment, HR, training and operations teams.


·       Assist with data reconciliation between systems to ensure alignment across workforce functions


 


Reporting & Dashboards


·       Produce routine workforce reports and extracts to support workforce planning and operational decision‑making.


·       Support the maintenance and updating of Power BI dashboards and other standard workforce reports.


·       Assist with ad‑hoc data requests, ensuring timely and accurate delivery of information.


·       Help prepare data inputs and visuals for leadership updates, meetings and operational briefings


 


Process & Documentation Support


·       Assist in documenting workforce processes, system workflows and user guidance.


·       Support the development and maintenance of workforce process maps across planning and operations activities.


·       Help identify basic process inefficiencies or data gaps and raise improvement opportunities with the manager


 


Workforce Operations Support


·       Provide system and data support for tournament‑time operations.


·       Assist with operational readiness checks to ensure systems and data are accurate ahead of key milestones.


·       Be available during operational periods to support issue resolution and rapid data updates.


·       Support the use and optimization of Excel-based planning tools (advanced formulas, dynamic dashboards, data validation)


 


Collaboration & Stakeholder Support


·       Work closely with the Workforce Planning team to ensure data and systems align with planning assumptions and deployment needs.


·       Liaise with other workforce functional teams to support consistent system usage and data inputs.


·       Support user training sessions, contribute to help materials and provide ongoing guidance to system users.


·       Operate in line with FIFA policies, data protection standards and tournament governance requirements


 


Continuous Improvement


·       Support testing activities, system enhancements and new functionality rollouts.


·       Capture issues, lessons learned and feedback from users to inform system and process improvements.

·       Contribute to knowledge sharing and handover documentation for future tournament delivery
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.
  •  Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent experience) in Business
  • Administration, Information Systems, Data Analytics, HR or a related discipline.
  • Experience in a coordination, administration, systems support or data support role, ideally within workforce, HR, operations or event delivery environments.
  • Experience working with data, systems or reporting tools in a professional setting.
    Skills & Expertise
  • Practical experience using workforce systems, databases or operational platforms.
  • Working knowledge of reporting tools such as Power BI, Excel or similar data analysis tools.
  • Strong data accuracy, validation and attention‑to‑detail skills.

  • Ability to follow defined processes while supporting documentation and continuous improvement.
  • Strong organisational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines.
  • Clear written and verbal communication skills, with confidence supporting system users.
  • Ability to work collaboratively with planning, operations and technical teams.
  • Willingness to learn new systems and adapt in a fast‑paced, evolving environment.
  • Comfortable supporting operational delivery during test events and tournament periods.

job.title: Senior Manager, Ceremonies
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Ceremonies Senior Manager leads the end‑to‑end planning, creative development, and operational delivery of all official ceremonies for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™, including the Opening and Closing Ceremonies, Medal and Trophy Ceremonies, Pre‑Match Ceremonies (PMC), the Final Match Half‑Time Show (if applicable), and all additional ceremonial moments.

Reporting to the Head of Entertainment, this role is responsible for overseeing the full lifecycle of each ceremony—from planning and preparation through to live execution—ensuring full alignment with FIFA’s vision and brand standards. The role also manages the implementation of the operational delivery across the entire FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™, in accordance with the Ceremonies Concept developed by FIFA.

Working closely with the Entertainment team and key Functional Areas, the Ceremonies Senior Manager acts as the senior operational lead for all ceremony delivery, ensuring creative and operational approvals are met and delivering iconic, culturally resonant moments that celebrate women’s football on its biggest global stage.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Ceremonies Project Leadership 
  • Lead the operational delivery and implementation of the Ceremonies Concept provided by FIFA.
  • Manage the end-to-end planning and delivery of all official ceremonies across the FWWC27 tournament.
  • Oversee ceremonies planning and delivery from concept development through preproduction, execution, dissolution, and legacy handover.
  • Ensure full alignment with the Entertainment team in Zurich across all ceremonial workstreams.
  • Manage the approval process for all ceremony related creative and operational deliverables.
  • Provide senior decision-making support on creative direction, technical feasibility, and operational prioritisation.
  • Lead the development of ceremony operational plans, production schedules, rundowns, and show formats for all ceremonies, including PMC.
  • Coordinate with Venue Management, Broadcast, Safety & Security, and other Functional Areas to integrate ceremonies seamlessly into matchday and event day operations.
  • Act as the senior escalation authority for all ceremony related operational matters.
  • Ensure all ceremonies reflect the approved creative vision, brand standards, cultural sensitivities, and tournament wide entertainment strategy.
Team Leadership
  • Manage and mentor the Ceremonies Team, providing clear direction, performance oversight, and accountability structures.
  • Mentor and support all Ceremonies personnel, including fulltime staff, freelancers, and event time workforce, ensuring consistent standards, development pathways, and operational readiness.
  • Support internal talent development through coaching, guidance, and structured feedback.
  • Build team structures and optimize collaboration across creative, technical, and operational units.
  • Foster strong coordination between venue based ceremonies teams to maintain consistency in delivery standards and operational execution.
  • Foster strong teamwork, knowledge exchange, and operational alignment with the Stadium Entertainment Team to ensure unified delivery across all entertainment and ceremonial workstreams.
Major Ceremonies Oversight
  • Manage planning and delivery management for all major ceremonial events, including: Opening Ceremony, Closing Ceremony, Medal and Trophy Ceremonies, Final Match Half-Time Show (if applicable), Pre-Match Ceremonies throughout the tournament
  • Ensure that each major ceremony reflects the ambition, brand standards, and cultural significance of the FIFA Women's World Cup 2027™.
Creative & Concept Development / Creative Concept, Content Production & Management
  • Collaborate with the Entertainment team to develop and refine the ceremonial concept and creative direction for each ceremony.
  • Integrate host country cultural elements into ceremonial programming in a meaningful, authentic, and respectful way.
  • Oversee creative brief development, content commissioning, and pre-production workflows with internal teams and external agencies.
  • Ensure all creative content — including music, graphics, videos, scripts, translations, choreography and performances — is approved and locked in line with operational timelines.
  • Manage stakeholder approval pathways to ensure content is delivered and archived in accordance with operational requirements.
  • Manage IP and copyright compliance for all ceremony content.
Planning Scheduling & Documentation
  • Develop and maintain detailed project plans, WIP reports, and milestone trackers for all ceremony workstreams.
  • Ensure all operational and creative decisions are fully documented and version controlled.
  • Manage multiple suppliers, conduct regular update meetings, coordinate implementation processes, and supervise payments and reporting.
  • Prepare written and oral presentations, high-level briefings, and reports for senior leadership and FIFA counterparts.
  • Organize and participate in Review, Project Team, and Status Update meetings.
  • Lead the creation, management, and archiving of project plans, workflows, venue documentation, and readiness tools across all ceremony workstreams.
  • Manage the development of operational plans, production schedules, rundowns, and rehearsal programmes for all ceremonies.
  • Ensure readiness standards are met at all ceremony venues and oversee execution on the day of each event.
  • Act as the key operational decision-maker to maintain seamless delivery during live ceremonies.
  • Oversee rehearsal planning, scheduling, approvals, and stakeholder coordination for all ceremonies.
  • Direct venue-level pre-production processes including technical surveys, staging assessments, and infrastructure planning.
Supplier & Budget Management
  • Lead early stage budget modelling, scenario planning, and resourcing strategies to support creative and operational planning. 
  • Develop and manage Scopes of Work, Procurement activities, supplier evaluations, contract negotiations, in line with FWWC27 management and procurement policies. Manage procurement activities for stadium entertainment suppliers, including RFP development, evaluation, and contract negotiation. 
  • Maintain full budget oversight including forecasting, cost control, variations, and invoicing. Manage budget oversight for the stadium entertainment workstream, including forecasting, cost control and invoicing. 
  • Ensure contractual deliverables and timelines are aligned with preproduction schedules and operational readiness requirements. 
  • Conduct regular supplier update meetings and coordinate implementation processes. 
  • Supervise payments, deliverables and performance reports from entertainment suppliers. 
Stakeholder & Functional Area Liaison
  • Act as the senior liaison between the Ceremonies Team and all relevant Functional Areas, including Venue Operations, Broadcast, Safety & Security, Marketing, and FIFA SPE.
  • Deliver concise briefings, alignment updates, and escalation management across all internal and external stakeholders.
  • Build and maintain relationships with Host Country national bodies, cultural organizations, and local partners contributing to ceremonies.
  • Ensure early phase cross functional integration to support seamless operational delivery.
Venue Readiness & Execution
  • Lead preparation of Site Operational Plans (SOPs), ceremony production schedules, and PMC rundowns.
  • Direct venue level preproduction processes including technical surveys, staging assessments, performer logistics, and infrastructure planning.
  • Ensure readiness standards are met at all ceremony venues and oversee execution on the day of each event.
  • Act as the key operational decision maker to maintain seamless delivery during live ceremonies.
  • Oversee rehearsal planning, scheduling, approvals, and stakeholder coordination.
  • Ensure all creative and technical elements are validated during pre event testing and rehearsal phases.
Other Duties
  • Perform additional responsibilities as required by senior managership aligned with project delivery.
  • Represent the Ceremonies function in cross-functional planning forums and senior managership briefings.
  • Support the Head of Entertainment in strategic planning, operational readiness, and tournament wide entertainment integration.
Education:
  • Bachelor's degree or similar level of higher education or equivalent in industry experience.
Experience
  •  Experience in live event production and ceremonies management, ideally within major international sporting events or large-scale public events.
  • Prior experience in a FIFA tournament or comparable major international sports event is highly desirable.
  • Demonstrated experience managing complex, large-scale ceremonies including opening/closing ceremonies and awards presentations.
  • Proven track record in team managership, creative development, supplier management, and cross-functional coordination.
  • Significant experience managing RFP/procurement processes and overseeing multi-supplier contracts and budgets.
  • Experience integrating cultural programming and working with national cultural bodies and creative agencies.
Skills & Competencies
  • ADMINISTRATIVE SKILLS: Ability to manage office and production documentation including, RFPs, presentations, show rundowns, rehearsal schedules, scripts, and permits accurately.
  • ADAPTABILITY: Ability to adapt to evolving creative direction and operational challenges in high-pressure environments.
  • FLEXIBILITY: Willingness to work irregular hours, including evenings and weekends, across the full tournament period.
  • COMMUNICATION SKILLS: Outstanding written and verbal communication skills; able to manage complex stakeholder relationships at a senior level.
  • ORGANIZATIONAL SKILLS: Exceptional project management skills with the ability to manage multiple concurrent ceremony workstreams to tight deadlines.
  • PROBLEM-SOLVING SKILLS: Strategic and creative approach to problem-solving; ability to make clear decisions under pressure.
  • CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLABORATION: Proven ability to work collaboratively across internal teams and diverse functional areas, ensuring alignment and contributing to the successful achievement of delivery goals.
  • INTERPERSONAL SKILLS: Builds effective working relationships across internal and cross-functional teams, applying strong interpersonal skills to support smooth coordination and operational alignment
  • CULTURAL SENSITIVITY: Deep awareness and respect for diverse cultures; committed to ensuring ceremonies are inclusive, authentic, and globally resonant.
  • PASSION FOR ENTERTAINMENT: Genuine passion for live performance, ceremony production, and the power of sport to inspire.
Certifications & Requirements
  • Fluent in English and Brazilian Portuguese.
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning/collaboration tools.
  • Experience of Monday.com an advantage.
  • Knowledge of and previous event / tournament experience across the stadiums in Brazil a plus
  • The right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. - Manage and mentor the Ceremonies Team, providing clear direction, performance oversight, and accountability structures. - Mentor and support all Ceremonies personnel, including fulltime staff, freelancers, and event time workforce, ensuring consistent standards, development pathways, and operational readiness. - Support internal talent development through coaching, guidance, and structured feedback. - Build team structures and optimize collaboration across creative, technical, and operational units. - Foster strong coordination between venue based ceremonies teams to maintain consistency in delivery standards and operational execution. - Foster strong teamwork, knowledge exchange, and operational alignment with the Stadium Entertainment Team to ensure unified delivery across all entertainment and ceremonial workstreams.
Major Ceremonies Oversight
- Manage planning and delivery management for all major ceremonial events, including: Opening Ceremony, Closing Ceremony, Medal and Trophy Ceremonies, Final Match Half-Time Show (if applicable), Pre-Match Ceremonies throughout the tournament - Ensure that each major ceremony reflects the ambition, brand standards, and cultural significance of the FIFA Women's World Cup 2027™.
Creative & Concept Development / Creative Concept, Content Production & Management
- Collaborate with the Entertainment team to develop and refine the ceremonial concept and creative direction for each ceremony. - Integrate host country cultural elements into ceremonial programming in a meaningful, authentic, and respectful way. - Oversee creative brief development, content commissioning, and pre-production workflows with internal teams and external agencies. - Ensure all creative content — including music, graphics, videos, scripts, translations, choreography and performances — is approved and locked in line with operational timelines. - Manage stakeholder approval pathways to ensure content is delivered and archived in accordance with operational requirements. - Manage IP and copyright compliance for all ceremony content.
Planning Scheduling & Documentation
- Develop and maintain detailed project plans, WIP reports, and milestone trackers for all ceremony workstreams. - Ensure all operational and creative decisions are fully documented and version controlled. - Manage multiple suppliers, conduct regular update meetings, coordinate implementation processes, and supervise payments and reporting. - Prepare written and oral presentations, high-level briefings, and reports for senior leadership and FIFA counterparts. - Organize and participate in Review, Project Team, and Status Update meetings. - Lead the creation, management, and archiving of project plans, workflows, venue documentation, and readiness tools across all ceremony workstreams. - Manage the development of operational plans, production schedules, rundowns, and rehearsal programmes for all ceremonies. - Ensure readiness standards are met at all ceremony venues and oversee execution on the day of each event. - Act as the key operational decision-maker to maintain seamless delivery during live ceremonies. - Oversee rehearsal planning, scheduling, approvals, and stakeholder coordination for all ceremonies. - Direct venue-level pre-production processes including technical surveys, staging assessments, and infrastructure planning.
Supplier & Budget Management
- Lead early stage budget modelling, scenario planning, and resourcing strategies to support creative and operational planning. - Develop and manage Scopes of Work, Procurement activities, supplier evaluations, contract negotiations, in line with FWWC27 management and procurement policies. Manage procurement activities for stadium entertainment suppliers, including RFP development, evaluation, and contract negotiation. - Maintain full budget oversight including forecasting, cost control, variations, and invoicing. Manage budget oversight for the stadium entertainment workstream, including forecasting, cost control and invoicing. - Ensure contractual deliverables and timelines are aligned with preproduction schedules and operational readiness requirements. - Conduct regular supplier update meetings and coordinate implementation processes. - Supervise payments, deliverables and performance reports from entertainment suppliers.
Stakeholder & Functional Area Liaison
- Act as the senior liaison between the Ceremonies Team and all relevant Functional Areas, including Venue Operations, Broadcast, Safety & Security, Marketing, and FIFA SPE. - Deliver concise briefings, alignment updates, and escalation management across all internal and external stakeholders. - Build and maintain relationships with Host Country national bodies, cultural organizations, and local partners contributing to ceremonies. - Ensure early phase cross functional integration to support seamless operational delivery.
Venue Readiness & Execution
- Lead preparation of Site Operational Plans (SOPs), ceremony production schedules, and PMC rundowns. - Direct venue level preproduction processes including technical surveys, staging assessments, performer logistics, and infrastructure planning. - Ensure readiness standards are met at all ceremony venues and oversee execution on the day of each event. - Act as the key operational decision maker to maintain seamless delivery during live ceremonies. - Oversee rehearsal planning, scheduling, approvals, and stakeholder coordination. - Ensure all creative and technical elements are validated during pre event testing and rehearsal phases.
Other Duties
- Perform additional responsibilities as required by senior managership aligned with project delivery. - Represent the Ceremonies function in cross-functional planning forums and senior managership briefings. - Support the Head of Entertainment in strategic planning, operational readiness, and tournament wide entertainment integration.
Education:
- Bachelor's degree or similar level of higher education or equivalent in industry experience.
Experience
- Experience in live event production and ceremonies management, ideally within major international sporting events or large-scale public events. - Prior experience in a FIFA tournament or comparable major international sports event is highly desirable. - Demonstrated experience managing complex, large-scale ceremonies including opening/closing ceremonies and awards presentations. - Proven track record in team managership, creative development, supplier management, and cross-functional coordination. - Significant experience managing RFP/procurement processes and overseeing multi-supplier contracts and budgets. - Experience integrating cultural programming and working with national cultural bodies and creative agencies.
Skills & Competencies
- ADMINISTRATIVE SKILLS: Ability to manage office and production documentation including, RFPs, presentations, show rundowns, rehearsal schedules, scripts, and permits accurately. - ADAPTABILITY: Ability to adapt to evolving creative direction and operational challenges in high-pressure environments. - FLEXIBILITY: Willingness to work irregular hours, including evenings and weekends, across the full tournament period. - COMMUNICATION SKILLS: Outstanding written and verbal communication skills; able to manage complex stakeholder relationships at a senior level. - ORGANIZATIONAL SKILLS: Exceptional project management skills with the ability to manage multiple concurrent ceremony workstreams to tight deadlines. - PROBLEM-SOLVING SKILLS: Strategic and creative approach to problem-solving; ability to make clear decisions under pressure. - CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLABORATION: Proven ability to work collaboratively across internal teams and diverse functional areas, ensuring alignment and contributing to the successful achievement of delivery goals. - INTERPERSONAL SKILLS: Builds effective working relationships across internal and cross-functional teams, applying strong interpersonal skills to support smooth coordination and operational alignment - CULTURAL SENSITIVITY: Deep awareness and respect for diverse cultures; committed to ensuring ceremonies are inclusive, authentic, and globally resonant. - PASSION FOR ENTERTAINMENT: Genuine passion for live performance, ceremony production, and the power of sport to inspire.
Certifications & Requirements
- Fluent in English and Brazilian Portuguese. - Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning/collaboration tools. - Experience of Monday.com an advantage. - Knowledge of and previous event / tournament experience across the stadiums in Brazil a plus - The right to work in Brazil. - Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Fleet and Bus Service Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Fleet and Bus Service Manager will oversee the efficient and effective operation Tournament fleet and for any ancillary event. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing all aspects of bus services and operations, working close to the other Transport workstreams, providing a reliable, on-time and outstanding service for the established FIFA Client Groups, such as PMAs, Referees, Media and Broadcast, FIFA Workforce and Arrivals and Departures. The Fleet and Bus Services and Operations Manager will play a crucial role in optimizing bus performance, developing, and ensuring compliance with Service Level Agreements, Policies and Procedures, Operational Plan and Reports. This role reports to the Senior Transport Manager.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Act as the primary liaison between the Transport FA and FIFA27 departments for all bus and minibuses related planning and operational matters for FWWC2027 and any ancillary event.
  • Act as the primary operational interface between FIFA Transport and the event bus and minibus suppliers, ensuring full alignment on vehicle specifications, delivery timelines, KPIs, and operational needs.
  • Coordinate the reception, inspection, and acceptance of vehicles (e.g. buses), ensuring all units meet quality and operational readiness criteria before deployment. Manage the return, decommissioning, and handback process of fleet and buses post-tournament.
  • Act as the primary operational interface with cars, buses and minibuses and drivers’ suppliers ensuring service has been delivered as per contract. Monitor Fleet services performance against agreed KPIs, escalating issues and implementing corrective actions when service levels are not met.
  • Manage all ancillary contracts, if any, and operational processes related to the bus and minibus services, including vehicle washing and cleaning, refueling services, toll management, and the handling of traffic fines.
  • Develop the fleet and bus services Operational plan and well as the Depot Concept of Operations (including the design) and ensure its implementation across all Host Cities. Coordinate with all Host Cities depot locations, space, utilities, and facility requirements to confirm requirements are fully met.
  • Overall accountability for appropriate staffing levels and quality employees relating to all vehicle Operations.
  • Act as the central coordinator for the development of service-related Policies & Procedures, reports, presentations and Guidelines.
  • Manages day-to-day operations pre and during event and subordinate staff including supervisors, coordinators, dispatchers, depot planners, administrative assistants and drivers.
  • Setup of the bus timetables and any required information into Tournament Management System (TMM). Daily management and updates.
  • Ensure all FIFA and Supplier staff are properly trained by developing and maintaining training materials and coordinating the delivery of FIFA-required modules (e.g TMM, fleet management system), operational workshops and briefings, safety guidelines, and role-specific instructions. The manager maintains consistent training standards across all Host Cities.
  • It’s also responsible, in coordination with the bus and minibus service providers, for developing and delivering the full training program for all drivers and fleet operational team, including designing training content, defining formats (classroom, online, practical sessions), and establishing clear timelines and schedules. This includes planning how and when training will be delivered across all Host Cities, determining training duration and required number of days, organizing group sessions to cover the entire driver workforce, and ensuring consistent standards and materials. 
  • During tournament ensure all job requests are correctly entered, validated, and assigned with the appropriate vehicle and driver. Oversee dispatching procedures, monitor vehicles while in operation, and ensure real-time coordination with Host Cities for seamless service delivery. Particular attention should be given to PMA services, ensuring precise planning, communication, and execution in alignment with other Host City transport teams.
  • Provide regular reporting to the Transport Senior Manager, FIFA counterparts, and other key stakeholders on coordination progress, issues, and decisions.
  • Facilitate cross-functional collaboration with other event areas (Security, FIFA Travel, Guest Operations, Media, Broadcast, TSV, etc.) to ensure coherent and efficient engagement with fleet service matters.
Qualifications:
Education – Bachelor’s Degree in Transport/Logistics/ Engineering/Project management/Event Management or a combination of education and experience relating to position. Master’s Degree is an additional asset.

Experience:
  • Strong transport and fleet or bus services experience in international major events in at least two (2) world-class sport/football events. E.g. FIFA World Cup/Olympic Games. Experience should include solid experience in multiple aspects of event transport operations and project management. 
  • Strong management experience in a similar role within an organising committee leading large teams to deliver a time critical project.
  • Proven experience of successfully managing and delivering transport projects of a similar size and scale.
  • Proven experience of managing fleet services and operations projects of a similar size and scale.
  • Experience working with public and private sector partners in a collaborative environment and ability to build strong partnerships.
Skills:
  • Advanced knowledge of administration and management principles.
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including data validation, pivot tables, advanced formulas, and large database management. 
  • Advanced communication, interpersonal and negotiation skills, the ability to influence others and manage relationships.
  • Ability to design, implement and operate a transport system in the Brazilian event environment.
  • Advanced knowledge of transportation technical operating processes and activities.
  • Ability to motivate others toward a common goal and manage a multidisciplinary team through conflicting priorities and continuous change requests.
  • Ability to work and communicate in a corporate and project focused organisation.  
  • Proven project management skills within a complex project.
  • Strong analytical and organisation skills.
  • Proven solution orientated decision-making skills.
  • Ability to work under pressure to immoveable deadlines.
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Safety & Security Planning Coordinator
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Safety & Security Planning Coordinator supports the Senior Manager, Safety & Security Planning and Operations in the delivery of integrated planning, coordination and operational readiness activities for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.
The role provides structured project coordination, administrative and communication support across the Safety & Security department, acting as a central point for task tracking, documentation management and inter-functional communication. The position ensures that planning processes are organised, documented and delivered to a high professional standard in both Portuguese and English.
The Coordinator contributes to departmental efficiency, consistency and quality by preparing high-standard materials, tracking central deliverables and supporting cross-functional collaboration throughout all phases of the tournament.

• Support the Senior Manager in coordinating the integrated operational planning process across stadiums, non-competition sites and ancillary events
• Act as a central coordination point for planning-related tasks within the Safety & Security department
• Track central task completion, deadlines and deliverables across workstreams, ensuring visibility and timely follow-up
• Support communication between the Safety & Security function and other FIFA functional areas
• Prepare, coordinate and document planning meetings, including agenda preparation, minute-taking and follow-up action tracking
• Draft and format planning documents, reports, presentations and briefing materials in both Portuguese and English
• Provide small translation tasks as required to ensure clarity and consistency across bilingual documentation and/or in meetings
• Prepare high-quality materials in Word, Excel and PowerPoint to support senior management and departmental reporting
• Maintain structured documentation control, version management and file organisation across planning workstreams
• Provide general administrative support to the Senior Manager and the wider Safety & Security department
• Support preparation of operational frameworks, planning matrices and readiness tracking tools
• Assist in the preparation of procurement documentation, scopes of work and planning summaries where required
• Contribute to the consolidation of information for reporting to senior leadership and external stakeholders
• Provide flexible support to other Safety & Security workstreams as required during peak planning and operational phases

Education
• Higher education in project management, business administration, security management or a related field
• Certification or training in project coordination, administration or a security-relevant field is desirable

Experience
• Experience in project coordination, planning support or administrative roles within complex operational environments; alternatively comparable experience in a security-relevant field
• Experience supporting multi-stakeholder planning processes
• Experience preparing professional documentation and presentations for senior management
• Experience working in bilingual (Portuguese–English) environments is highly desirable

Certifications & Requirements:

• Right to work in Brazil. 
• Fluent in English (spoken and written)
• Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
• Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Guest Relations Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Guest Relations Manager is responsible for the planning, coordination, and delivery of Guest Relations activities for VVIP/VIP and other FIFA guest groups at the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.

The role focuses on guest account management (including invitation, registration, and communication), as well as the coordination of end-to-end guest journeys and liaison with key stakeholders.

Under the guidance of the FIFA Guest Relations Lead, the role ensures the implementation of the Guest Relations strategy, maintaining high service standards, efficient processes, and seamless communication across all guest touchpoints.

The Guest Relations Manager acts as a key point of reference for guest management operations and supports the training and coordination of service providers and workforce in the respective areas.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Lead and coordinate Guest Relations activities across all guest groups, ensuring alignment with service levels and event objectives.
  • Define and manage VVIP/VIP account management processes, including invitation, registration, communication, and quota management.
  • Liaise with stakeholders on guest invitation concepts, guest lists, and quota definitions, including coordination with offices and embassies of international and local authorities.
  • Map and oversee end-to-end guest journeys, ensuring high service standards across all touchpoints and efficient resolution of guest issues.
  • Oversee guest requests and bookings (e.g. program attendance, ticketing, travel, accommodation, transport), including structured change management processes.
  • Oversee ticketing-related guest processes and ensure consistency and efficiency in guest request handling.
  • Define and implement guest communication and liaison strategies, ensuring streamlined communication flows across all channels.
  • Collaborate with FIFA Zurich and relevant departments to ensure an integrated Guest Relations approach and consistent service delivery.
  • Establish and maintain clear communication lines with internal and external stakeholders.
  • Develop policies, guidelines, and protocol requirements for VVIP/VIP guests and other dignitaries.
  • Coordinate the management of guest-related IT systems and applications in alignment with FIFA Guest Relations.
  • Manage resources and support workforce planning for Guest Relations activities.
  • Ensure project delivery within defined timelines, milestones, and quality standards, including reporting and issue resolution.
  • Develop training concepts and deliver training sessions for service providers, workforce, and volunteers.
Education:
  • Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Communications & Marketing, International Relations, Business Management, Hospitality Management, or a related field. Specialization in protocol, event management, or international relations is an advantage.
Experience:
  • Extensive experience in guest relations and guest management, including exposure to protocol environments and structured guest handling processes.
  • Experience in planning, coordinating, and delivering guest services in a professional or event-based environment.
  • On-site event experience in guest relations, protocol, or VVIP/VIP service delivery.
  • Experience in managing guest accounts, invitations, registrations, and communication processes is an advantage.
  • Experience in coordinating with high-level stakeholders (e.g. government representatives, embassies, international organizations) is an asset.
  • Ideally experience within a sport/event organisation or large-scale international project, preferably in a world-class event environment.
Skills & Competencies:
  • Fluent Portuguese and English (spoken and written) with excellent communication skills.
  • Strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills.
  • High level of diplomacy and ability to handle sensitive VVIP/VIP interactions.
  • Strong organizational and planning skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities.
  • Proven problem-solving skills and ability to operate in dynamic environments.
  • Strong project management skills and ability to deliver under pressure.
  • Solid understanding of guest management processes and operational coordination.
  • Collaborative mindset with the ability to work across cross-functional teams.
  • Advanced MS Office skills (Excel, Word, PowerPoint); experience with planning tools is an asset.
Certifications & Requirements:
  • Project Management certification (e.g. PMI) or equivalent experience is an advantage.
  • Relevant certifications in hospitality, event management, or protocol are a plus.
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Senior Partnership Development Manager will be responsible for driving sponsorship sales and strategic partnerships for the FIFA Women’s World Cup Brazil 2027™, with a strong focus on the Brazilian and LATAM markets. The role requires deep market knowledge, and a robust network in the region.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Work closely with the Head of Partnership Sales, FIFA Women’s World Cup Sales Lead and the broader commercial team to deliver on sponsorship sales objectives for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™, with a strong emphasis on the Brazilian and LATAM markets.
  • Identify and pursue high-value sponsorship leads aligned with the commercial strategy of the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.
  • Source, qualify, and engage potential sponsors, securing meetings and presenting tailored partnership proposals to major regional and international brands.
  • Develop bespoke sponsorship packages that address the unique needs and objectives of prospective partners.
  • Lead negotiations through to successful acquisition, ensuring alignment with FIFA’s commercial guidelines and values.
  • Collaborate with the Strategy and Planning team to identify market opportunities and build compelling sales arguments.
  • Establish and nurture long-term relationships with key sponsor prospects, ensuring continuity and value beyond the tournament.
  • Serve as a key point of contact for the FIFA27 team in Brazil on sales-related matters, supporting or managing the sales process as needed.
  • Stay informed on emerging sponsorship categories, regional industry trends, and relevant market research, including pricing and inventory benchmarks.
  • Build strong external visibility for FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ commercial opportunities, fostering inbound interest and leads through proactive industry engagement.
  • Lead and support the organisation of commercial events, conferences, hosting programmes, dedicated to cultivating business relationships and building into the sales pipeline.
  • Highly professional, articulate and results-driven. 
  • Strong presenter and excellent relationship building skills.
  • Approximately 10 years of proven experience in sponsorship sales, business development, or commercial partnerships, preferably in sports or entertainment.
  • Experience leading commercial sponsorship teams for at least 5 years.  
  • Strong network and understanding of the Brazilian and LATAM commercial landscape.
  • Experience in dealing with and negotiating within different cultures.
  • Ability to work independently in a multi-task position within a dynamic, team-based environment. 
  • Solutions orientated.
  • Solid time management and organizational skills.
  • Client oriented, efficient and effective.
  • Adept at handling and protecting confidential information. 
  • Excellent team player and relationship builder. 
  • Attention to detail, reliable and responsible.
  • An appreciation of the international sports federation environment, passion for women’s sports and global events.
  • University degree in business, sports marketing or related area
  • Excellent in Word / PowerPoint / Excel / PDF / MS Teams / Sales CRM tools
  • Ability to travel often and when required. The position may be based in either Rio de Janeiro or São Paulo, with frequent travel between the two locations required.
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Any other FIFA language (Spanish especially) is an advantage

job.title: Senior Partnership Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience. 

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
The Senior Partnership Manager will be responsible for managing and delivering FIFA’s commercial partner rights at the local level, ensuring seamless coordination with the global FIFA Partnership Management team in Zurich. This includes day-to-day relationship management, operational delivery at venues and events where required, and strategic alignment with partner objectives within the host country and region.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Proactively lead and foster relationships with FIFA Commercial Partners operating in Brazil, ensuring consistent, high-quality servicing in line with global standards.
  • Act as the key local point of contact for assigned Commercial Partner, ensuring continuous alignment and close collaboration with the Partnership Management team at FIFA Zurich to maintain consistent servicing and delivery across all touchpoints.
  • Lead assigned partnership projects and deliverables related to the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ in Brazil, ensuring on-time, in-scope, and on-budget execution while identifying meaningful, locally relevant business and marketing opportunities that enhance partner impact and remain aligned with FIFA’s global partnership and brand strategy.
  • Project lead for assigned local partner activations and FIFA-related events in Brazil (e.g. Local Commercial Partner Workshops).
  • Ensure local partner activations and brand visibility adhere to FIFA’s third-party regulations and brand protection standards, working in close coordination with the Commercial Legal and Brand teams.
  • Track and manage local partner budgets and support reconciliation processes in close coordination with the Zurich Partnerships team.
  • Prepare and lead meetings and updates with internal and external stakeholders, including regular reporting to the Partnership Management team at FIFA Zurich.
  • Provide solution-oriented, proactive support to the Head of Commercial Partnerships in Brazil and contribute to a unified servicing approach across global and local workstreams.
  • Support continuous improvement by feeding local market insights and operational learnings into FIFA’s global partnership tools and client servicing frameworks.
  • University degree required, preferably in Business, Marketing, Sports Management, or a related field
  • A solid understanding of the business of sports, marketing, and sponsorship rights delivery
  • Project and event management experience, ideally in an international environment
  • Working knowledge of digital and social media is an advantage
  • Experience in brand marketing, sponsorship, or client servicing in the sports or entertainment industry
  • Proven track record of managing complex projects with multiple stakeholders
  • Experience working in or with international organizations and global brands
  • Familiarity with the Brazilian market and local executional landscape is a strong asset
  • Client-oriented with strong interpersonal and written communication skills
  • Efficient and effective time management and organizational skills
  • Ability to define priorities, lead projects independently, and provide direction to junior team members when required
  • Team-oriented, service-minded, and collaborative across functions
  • Strategic and creative problem-solver with a hands-on mindset
  • Comfortable working under pressure, managing multiple priorities and deadlines
  • Strong sense of ownership, initiative, and accountability
  • High level of professionalism, empathy, and commercial awareness
  • Strong representation skills, both internally and externally
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Event Experiences Marketing Manager - FIFA Fan Festival
job.html_description:
FIFA27 established as the Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil, to work together with FIFA HQ in Zurich and globally, to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”). 


The Event Experiences Marketing Manager (FFF) supports and implements all strategic deliverables prescribed by the Experiential Marketing Manager (FFF) based in Zurich. This includes developing, supporting, and delivering innovative and best-in-class FFF deliverables and other experiential marketing activations that might be developed. The core deliverable is to lead the strategic and commercial development and positioning of the FIFA Fan Festival™ across Host Cities of the FIFA Women’s World Cup Brazil 2027, focusing on the commercial, promotional, brand, entertainment, and partner and stakeholder integrations. Working closely with internal and external stakeholders, the manager will help shape the narrative, drive partner and government integration, and develop innovative fan engagement in line with FIFA’s strategic objectives from a local perspective.

This role and team will work in tandem with the Special Events team at FIFA27, who hold accountabilities for all operational elements of the FFF. However, on a day‑to‑day basis, the Strategy and Operations teams will operate and be perceived as one unified team.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

FIFA Fan Festival™ FWWC2027 - Commercial & Strategy
  • Support the development and implementation of the FIFA Fan Festival™ commercial and strategic framework. 
  • Support communications and promotional planning across Host Cities. 
  • Liaise with commercial and media partners (alongside the local Commercial and Media partnership teams) to support project alignment and approvals. 
  • Prepare workshop planning, presentations, and strategic alignment sessions.
  • Maintain all SharePoint documentation and ensure excellent level of project document management 
  • Lead on local conversations and meetings while delivering detailed meetings and project updates including meeting minutes and communications for reporting back to the team in Zurich.
  • Support budget tracking, procurement processes, and relevant strategic documentation. 
  • Support local issue resolution and escalation processes, ensuring key decisions, risks and dependencies are clearly communicated to the Zurich team.
  • Support the review of plans and materials to ensure alignment with FIFA commercial rights, partner obligations, and clean site principles.
  • Provide local market insights and stakeholder contexts to support strategic decision making, planning, and delivery when needed.
FIFA Fan Festival™ FWWC2027 - Host City Coordination
  • Support Host Cities throughout all strategy and commercial processes. 
  • Act as a key point of contact for Host Cities at local level and any other local stakeholders. 
  • Coordinate requests, tracking, review and adaptation of Host City deliverables, including promotional copy, brand assets, content inputs and local information. 
  • Review and adapt Host City content, copy and materials to ensure consistency with FIFA tone, messaging, objectives and standards. 
  • Develop a strong understanding of the FWWC27 brand manual and ensure Host City and stakeholder materials are reviewed in line with FIFA brand requirements. 
  • Coordinate feedback, brand approvals, and final sign-off with relevant internal FIFA teams. Or communicate with FIFA Zurich where necessary.
  • Support the local Special Events team to ensure FIFA Fan Festival™ strategic, commercial, promotional and brand requirements are understood and integrated into Host City planning and delivery.
  • Support the coordination of content capture 
  • Delivery of local reporting inputs and post event documentation for FIFA27 and Host Cities and relevant stakeholders.  Experiential Projects
  • Act as project manager for selected experiential marketing projects as required. 
  • Coordinate project planning, timelines, deliverables, stakeholder alignment, and execution. 
  • Support RFP processes, agency evaluation, supplier onboarding, and procurement processes when required. 
  • Manage day-to-day coordination with agencies and suppliers, ensuring clear briefs, timelines, feedback rounds, and delivery expectations. 
  • Maintain all project documentation, trackers, meeting notes, action lists, and approval records. 
  • Ensure projects are delivered on time, within scope and in line with FIFA standards and identify, track and communicate key project risks, dependencies, decisions and escalation points.
  • Support post-project reporting, learnings and evaluation, including documentation of outcomes, performance and recommendations.
  • Experience in marketing, event management, or relevant client account management
  • Proven relevant experience in similar position or another relevant role 
  • Experience working in a multi-national, multi-cultural, and multi-stakeholder environment 
  • Appreciation and understanding or sport and the role it plays in marketing, fan engagement and popular culture
  • University degree in a marketing discipline, or business administration with major in a relevant field (desired)
  • Proficient in Office 365 
  • Excellent knowledge of MS Teams, SharePoint/One Drive, Monday.com 
  • Flexible, adaptable, proactive, attention to detail, and constantly striving for excellence 
  • Multi-tasker with excellent organizational skills and time/priority management 
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills 
  • Stress resilient and able to work to immovable deadlines 
  • Excellent stakeholder and network management skills, including understanding and awareness of cultural nuances and communication needs for different stakeholders 
  • Comprehensive understanding of entertainment, major events and fan entertainment understanding 
  • Diplomatic and comfortable handling conflicts in a positive and objective manner 
  • Team Player and Adaptability with strong teamwork abilities and willing to go the extra mile
Requirements
  • Fluent in English and Portuguese
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.
