job.title: Entertainment Technical Integration Manager
job.html_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Reporting to the Head of Entertainment, this role ensures the seamless technical implementation of all entertainment elements, including in-stadium entertainment activations, audio-visual systems, show control, and content playback, IBCCC integration, venue technology interfaces, Opening and Closing Ceremonies, Medal and Trophy Ceremonies, Pre-Match Ceremonies (PMC), Final Match Half-Time Show (if applicable), and other ceremonial moments.

The role leads the coordination and integration of technical requirements across all entertainment environments, ensuring alignment between FIFA, host venues, the IBCC, production partners, technical suppliers, broadcast stakeholders, and operational teams.

The Entertainment Technical Integration Manager is responsible for translating creative and operational concepts into executable technical solutions, ensuring that all entertainment and ceremonial elements are delivered safely, consistently, and to the highest standard across the tournament.

The role supports the development, implementation, testing, and execution of integrated entertainment systems and operational processes.
The position oversees technical planning timelines, manages supplier coordination, facilitates cross-functional technical reviews, and ensures readiness across all competition and ceremony venues.
 
The Entertainment Technical Integration Manager serves as the primary focal point for entertainment-related technical integration, providing centralized coordination and governance from the planning phase through tournament delivery.

The role requires an experienced live events professional with strong technical production and project management expertise, capable of managing complex stakeholder environments and delivering world-class entertainment experiences across stadiums, ceremonies, and IBCC operations on the global stage of the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.
 
Key Responsibilities

Technical Integration & Delivery
  • Lead the technical planning, integration, and readiness of all Stadium Entertainment, Ceremonies, and Entertainment IBCCC operations across the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™, ensuring alignment with FIFA’s operational, technical, and creative objectives. 
  • Ensure the successful implementation of the FIFA-approved Stadium Entertainment and Ceremonies Concepts from a technical and operational perspective. 
  • Coordinate the integration of audio, video, lighting, LED systems, show control, communications infrastructure, content distribution platforms, graphics systems, and venue technology interfaces required to support entertainment and ceremonial delivery.
  • Act as the primary technical integration point of contact between the Entertainment Department, Broadcast, Technology, venue teams, ceremony production partners, and technical suppliers.
  • Ensure consistency, technical quality, and operational readiness across all tournament venues, ceremony locations, and centralized Entertainment operations.
Team Support 
  • Support the Entertainment Team through technical coordination, planning, and readiness activities across Stadium Entertainment, Ceremonies, and Special Events.
  • Provide technical guidance and support to internal teams, freelancers, suppliers, and project stakeholders to ensure consistent delivery standards and best practices. Foster strong collaboration between Entertainment, Technology, Broadcast, IBCC, Venue Operations, and other Functional Areas throughout the project lifecycle. Support alignment between venue-based technical teams and central project teams to maintain consistency in delivery standards across all competition and ceremony venues.
Technical Planning & Production Coordination
  • Create and manage the overall Technical Production Schedule for Stadium Entertainment, Ceremonies, IBCC Operations, ensuring alignment with tournament milestones and delivery requirements.  
  • Develop and maintain technical integration plans, readiness trackers, installation schedules, testing plans, and operational documentation.
  • Coordinate technical surveys, venue assessments, production walkthroughs, rehearsals, testing, commissioning, and operational readiness activities across all venues and project sites.
  • Coordinate rehearsals, technical run-throughs, systems testing, and operational readiness exercises to validate delivery requirements before live operations.
  • Ensure all technical requirements are accurately captured within production schedules, venue operational plans, and technical documentation.
  • Identify technical risks, dependencies, and operational challenges, ensuring appropriate mitigation and contingency planning measures are in place.
IBCC Integration & Operations
  • Lead the planning, setup, technical integration, and readiness of all Entertainment-related facilities, systems, and services within the IBCC.
  • Create and manage the IBCC Technical Production Schedule and ensure alignment with Stadium Entertainment and Ceremonies delivery requirements.
  • Develop and manage the stadium-to-IBCC integration plan, ensuring seamless connectivity between venue systems, centralized operations, communications infrastructure, content distribution platforms, and operational workflows.
  • Manage the technical relationship with Host Broadcast Services (HBS), IBCC overlay providers, venue stakeholders, and key technical suppliers.
  • Ensure seamless distribution of entertainment content, graphics, show-control cues, and operational data from the IBCC to stadium systems, while maintaining reliable two-way communication and monitoring between the IBCC and all tournament venues.
  • Oversee the integration of content distribution networks, communications systems, graphics platforms, show-control solutions, AE Live services, and other third-party systems supporting Entertainment delivery. Coordinate IBCC FF&E requirements, TEAP requirements, BIM360 updates, technical documentation, and readiness reporting to ensure tournament-wide technical preparedness. 
Venue, Ceremonies & Special Events Readiness
  • Lead stadium technical installation and readiness activities, ensuring all Entertainment infrastructure is delivered, tested, and commissioned in accordance with FIFA requirements.
  • Lead IBCC technical installation and readiness activities, coordinating delivery partners, suppliers, and technical stakeholders.
  • Support the technical planning and readiness of all Ceremonies, including Opening Ceremonies, Closing Ceremonies, Medal Ceremonies, Pre-Match Ceremonies (PMC), and other official FIFA ceremonial events. Support the technical planning and delivery requirements of FIFA Special Events, including tournament draws and other major FIFA events, ensuring alignment with Entertainment technical standards and operational requirement.
 Content & Systems Management
  • Coordinate the collection and validation of venue technical specifications, including giant screen, LED ribbon, control room, playback, and presentation system capabilities, ensuring all requirements are available for content production and delivery planning.
  • Define and communicate technical content specifications, formats, resolutions, workflows, and delivery requirements to content producers, ensuring compatibility across all stadium and ceremony presentation systems.
  • Review and validate stadium technical overlay designs, control room infrastructure, and presentation system configurations to ensure all Stadium Entertainment and Ceremonies content can be successfully delivered and operated.
  • Coordinate the integration, testing, and readiness of content playback, graphics, show-control, and presentation systems across stadiums, ceremonies, and IBCC operations.
  • Work closely with Technology, venue operators, and technical suppliers to ensure all content delivery workflows, system interfaces, monitoring tools, and operational processes are fully implemented, tested, and operational prior to tournament delivery.
  • Ensure documentation, technical specifications, system inventories, and content delivery requirements remain accurate and up to date throughout the project lifecycle.  
Planning Scheduling & Documentation
  • Prepare match-day reports, schedules, show notes and post-event summaries for the Head of Entertainment.
  • Maintain up-to-date operational documentation.
  • Participate in regular team meetings, briefings, and review sessions.
  • Flag operational risks and escalate issues to the Head of Entertainment in a timely manner.
Supplier & Budget Management
  • Support procurement processes as directed by the Head of Entertainment, including supplier briefing and evaluation. ·        Manage day-to-day relationships with technical suppliers and contractors at assigned venue(s).
  • Assist with budget management tasks, including forecasting inputs, cost‑tracking, variation documentation, and invoice processing, ensuring accuracy and timely follow‑up.
  • Help monitor contractual deliverables and timelines, ensuring they remain aligned with pre‑production schedules and operational readiness requirements, and escalate risks to the Head of Entertainment as needed.  
Stakeholder & Functional Area Liaison
  • Coordinate closely with Technology, Broadcast, Venue Operations and other Functional Areas to ensure seamless project integration. 
  • Act as the central coordination point for technical requirements impacting Stadium Entertainment, Ceremonies, IBCC Operations, and Special Events.
  • Support early‑phase cross‑functional integration to support seamless operational delivery.  
Other Duties
  • Perform additional responsibilities as required by line management in support of tournament and event delivery objectives.
  • Support the Head of Entertainment and Senior Managers in technical planning, operational readiness, project delivery, and tournament-wide integration activities.
  • Contribute to the successful delivery of world-class entertainment, ceremony, and special event experiences across FIFA competitions and events.
Education:
  • Bachelor's degree or similar level of higher education or equivalent in industry experience.
Experience
  • 5+ years’ experience in technical production, systems integration, and live event delivery, ideally within major international sporting events, ceremonies, entertainment productions, or large-scale public events.
  • Proven experience planning and delivering stadium technical infrastructure, venue technical overlay, content presentation systems, and IBCC operations across complex event environments.
  • Demonstrated experience developing and managing technical production schedules, installation programmes, testing, commissioning, and operational readiness activities across multiple venues and stakeholders.
  • Strong track record of managing technical suppliers, system integrators, Host Broadcasters, venue operators, and production partners in high-pressure, deadline-driven environments.
  • Experience supporting the technical planning and delivery of stadium entertainment, ceremonies, special events, and multi-venue tournament operations, including content delivery systems, control room environments, and venue technology integration.
Skills & Competencies
  • ADMINISTRATIVE SKILLS: Ability to manage office and production documentation including presentations, RFPs, show rundowns, rehearsal schedules, scripts, and permits accurately.
  • ADAPTABILITY: Ability to adapt to evolving creative direction and operational challenges in high-pressure environments.
  • FLEXIBILITY: Willingness to work irregular hours, including evenings and weekends, across the full tournament period.
  • COMMUNICATION SKILLS: Outstanding written and verbal communication skills; able to manage complex stakeholder relationships at a senior level.
  • ORGANIZATIONAL SKILLS: Exceptional project management skills with the ability to manage multiple concurrent ceremony workstreams to tight deadlines.
  • PROBLEM-SOLVING SKILLS: Strategic and creative approach to problem-solving; ability to make clear decisions under pressure.
  • CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLABORATION: Proven ability to work collaboratively across internal teams and diverse functional areas, ensuring alignment and contributing to the successful achievement of delivery goals.
  • INTERPERSONAL SKILLS: Builds effective working relationships across internal and cross-functional teams, applying strong interpersonal skills to support smooth coordination and operational alignment.
  • CULTURAL SENSITIVITY: Deep awareness and respect for diverse cultures; committed to ensuring ceremonies are inclusive, authentic, and globally resonant.
  • PASSION FOR ENTERTAINMENT: Genuine passion for live performance, ceremony production, and the power of sport to inspire.
Certifications & Requirements
  • Fluent in English and Brazilian Portuguese.
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning/collaboration tools.
  • Experience of Monday.com an advantage.
  • Knowledge of and previous event / tournament experience across the stadiums in Brazil a plus.
  • The right to work in Brazil.
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.
job.plain_text_description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.
Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
Reporting to the Head of Entertainment, this role ensures the seamless technical implementation of all entertainment elements, including in-stadium entertainment activations, audio-visual systems, show control, and content playback, IBCCC integration, venue technology interfaces, Opening and Closing Ceremonies, Medal and Trophy Ceremonies, Pre-Match Ceremonies (PMC), Final Match Half-Time Show (if applicable), and other ceremonial moments.
The role leads the coordination and integration of technical requirements across all entertainment environments, ensuring alignment between FIFA, host venues, the IBCC, production partners, technical suppliers, broadcast stakeholders, and operational teams.
The Entertainment Technical Integration Manager is responsible for translating creative and operational concepts into executable technical solutions, ensuring that all entertainment and ceremonial elements are delivered safely, consistently, and to the highest standard across the tournament.
The role supports the development, implementation, testing, and execution of integrated entertainment systems and operational processes. The position oversees technical planning timelines, manages supplier coordination, facilitates cross-functional technical reviews, and ensures readiness across all competition and ceremony venues.
The Entertainment Technical Integration Manager serves as the primary focal point for entertainment-related technical integration, providing centralized coordination and governance from the planning phase through tournament delivery.
The role requires an experienced live events professional with strong technical production and project management expertise, capable of managing complex stakeholder environments and delivering world-class entertainment experiences across stadiums, ceremonies, and IBCC operations on the global stage of the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.
Key Responsibilities Technical Integration & Delivery
- Lead the technical planning, integration, and readiness of all Stadium Entertainment, Ceremonies, and Entertainment IBCCC operations across the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™, ensuring alignment with FIFA’s operational, technical, and creative objectives. - Ensure the successful implementation of the FIFA-approved Stadium Entertainment and Ceremonies Concepts from a technical and operational perspective. - Coordinate the integration of audio, video, lighting, LED systems, show control, communications infrastructure, content distribution platforms, graphics systems, and venue technology interfaces required to support entertainment and ceremonial delivery. - Act as the primary technical integration point of contact between the Entertainment Department, Broadcast, Technology, venue teams, ceremony production partners, and technical suppliers. - Ensure consistency, technical quality, and operational readiness across all tournament venues, ceremony locations, and centralized Entertainment operations.
Team Support
- Support the Entertainment Team through technical coordination, planning, and readiness activities across Stadium Entertainment, Ceremonies, and Special Events. - Provide technical guidance and support to internal teams, freelancers, suppliers, and project stakeholders to ensure consistent delivery standards and best practices. Foster strong collaboration between Entertainment, Technology, Broadcast, IBCC, Venue Operations, and other Functional Areas throughout the project lifecycle. Support alignment between venue-based technical teams and central project teams to maintain consistency in delivery standards across all competition and ceremony venues.
Technical Planning & Production Coordination
- Create and manage the overall Technical Production Schedule for Stadium Entertainment, Ceremonies, IBCC Operations, ensuring alignment with tournament milestones and delivery requirements. - Develop and maintain technical integration plans, readiness trackers, installation schedules, testing plans, and operational documentation. - Coordinate technical surveys, venue assessments, production walkthroughs, rehearsals, testing, commissioning, and operational readiness activities across all venues and project sites. - Coordinate rehearsals, technical run-throughs, systems testing, and operational readiness exercises to validate delivery requirements before live operations. - Ensure all technical requirements are accurately captured within production schedules, venue operational plans, and technical documentation. - Identify technical risks, dependencies, and operational challenges, ensuring appropriate mitigation and contingency planning measures are in place.
IBCC Integration & Operations
- Lead the planning, setup, technical integration, and readiness of all Entertainment-related facilities, systems, and services within the IBCC. - Create and manage the IBCC Technical Production Schedule and ensure alignment with Stadium Entertainment and Ceremonies delivery requirements. - Develop and manage the stadium-to-IBCC integration plan, ensuring seamless connectivity between venue systems, centralized operations, communications infrastructure, content distribution platforms, and operational workflows. - Manage the technical relationship with Host Broadcast Services (HBS), IBCC overlay providers, venue stakeholders, and key technical suppliers. - Ensure seamless distribution of entertainment content, graphics, show-control cues, and operational data from the IBCC to stadium systems, while maintaining reliable two-way communication and monitoring between the IBCC and all tournament venues. - Oversee the integration of content distribution networks, communications systems, graphics platforms, show-control solutions, AE Live services, and other third-party systems supporting Entertainment delivery. Coordinate IBCC FF&E requirements, TEAP requirements, BIM360 updates, technical documentation, and readiness reporting to ensure tournament-wide technical preparedness.
Venue, Ceremonies & Special Events Readiness
- Lead stadium technical installation and readiness activities, ensuring all Entertainment infrastructure is delivered, tested, and commissioned in accordance with FIFA requirements. - Lead IBCC technical installation and readiness activities, coordinating delivery partners, suppliers, and technical stakeholders. - Support the technical planning and readiness of all Ceremonies, including Opening Ceremonies, Closing Ceremonies, Medal Ceremonies, Pre-Match Ceremonies (PMC), and other official FIFA ceremonial events. Support the technical planning and delivery requirements of FIFA Special Events, including tournament draws and other major FIFA events, ensuring alignment with Entertainment technical standards and operational requirement.
Content & Systems Management
- Coordinate the collection and validation of venue technical specifications, including giant screen, LED ribbon, control room, playback, and presentation system capabilities, ensuring all requirements are available for content production and delivery planning. - Define and communicate technical content specifications, formats, resolutions, workflows, and delivery requirements to content producers, ensuring compatibility across all stadium and ceremony presentation systems. - Review and validate stadium technical overlay designs, control room infrastructure, and presentation system configurations to ensure all Stadium Entertainment and Ceremonies content can be successfully delivered and operated. - Coordinate the integration, testing, and readiness of content playback, graphics, show-control, and presentation systems across stadiums, ceremonies, and IBCC operations. - Work closely with Technology, venue operators, and technical suppliers to ensure all content delivery workflows, system interfaces, monitoring tools, and operational processes are fully implemented, tested, and operational prior to tournament delivery. - Ensure documentation, technical specifications, system inventories, and content delivery requirements remain accurate and up to date throughout the project lifecycle.
Planning Scheduling & Documentation
- Prepare match-day reports, schedules, show notes and post-event summaries for the Head of Entertainment. - Maintain up-to-date operational documentation. - Participate in regular team meetings, briefings, and review sessions. - Flag operational risks and escalate issues to the Head of Entertainment in a timely manner.
Supplier & Budget Management
- Support procurement processes as directed by the Head of Entertainment, including supplier briefing and evaluation. · Manage day-to-day relationships with technical suppliers and contractors at assigned venue(s). - Assist with budget management tasks, including forecasting inputs, cost‑tracking, variation documentation, and invoice processing, ensuring accuracy and timely follow‑up. - Help monitor contractual deliverables and timelines, ensuring they remain aligned with pre‑production schedules and operational readiness requirements, and escalate risks to the Head of Entertainment as needed.
Stakeholder & Functional Area Liaison
- Coordinate closely with Technology, Broadcast, Venue Operations and other Functional Areas to ensure seamless project integration. - Act as the central coordination point for technical requirements impacting Stadium Entertainment, Ceremonies, IBCC Operations, and Special Events. - Support early‑phase cross‑functional integration to support seamless operational delivery.
Other Duties
- Perform additional responsibilities as required by line management in support of tournament and event delivery objectives. - Support the Head of Entertainment and Senior Managers in technical planning, operational readiness, project delivery, and tournament-wide integration activities. - Contribute to the successful delivery of world-class entertainment, ceremony, and special event experiences across FIFA competitions and events.
Education:
- Bachelor's degree or similar level of higher education or equivalent in industry experience.
Experience
- 5+ years’ experience in technical production, systems integration, and live event delivery, ideally within major international sporting events, ceremonies, entertainment productions, or large-scale public events. - Proven experience planning and delivering stadium technical infrastructure, venue technical overlay, content presentation systems, and IBCC operations across complex event environments. - Demonstrated experience developing and managing technical production schedules, installation programmes, testing, commissioning, and operational readiness activities across multiple venues and stakeholders. - Strong track record of managing technical suppliers, system integrators, Host Broadcasters, venue operators, and production partners in high-pressure, deadline-driven environments. - Experience supporting the technical planning and delivery of stadium entertainment, ceremonies, special events, and multi-venue tournament operations, including content delivery systems, control room environments, and venue technology integration.
Skills & Competencies
- ADMINISTRATIVE SKILLS: Ability to manage office and production documentation including presentations, RFPs, show rundowns, rehearsal schedules, scripts, and permits accurately. - ADAPTABILITY: Ability to adapt to evolving creative direction and operational challenges in high-pressure environments. - FLEXIBILITY: Willingness to work irregular hours, including evenings and weekends, across the full tournament period. - COMMUNICATION SKILLS: Outstanding written and verbal communication skills; able to manage complex stakeholder relationships at a senior level. - ORGANIZATIONAL SKILLS: Exceptional project management skills with the ability to manage multiple concurrent ceremony workstreams to tight deadlines. - PROBLEM-SOLVING SKILLS: Strategic and creative approach to problem-solving; ability to make clear decisions under pressure. - CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLABORATION: Proven ability to work collaboratively across internal teams and diverse functional areas, ensuring alignment and contributing to the successful achievement of delivery goals. - INTERPERSONAL SKILLS: Builds effective working relationships across internal and cross-functional teams, applying strong interpersonal skills to support smooth coordination and operational alignment. - CULTURAL SENSITIVITY: Deep awareness and respect for diverse cultures; committed to ensuring ceremonies are inclusive, authentic, and globally resonant. - PASSION FOR ENTERTAINMENT: Genuine passion for live performance, ceremony production, and the power of sport to inspire.
Certifications & Requirements
- Fluent in English and Brazilian Portuguese. - Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning/collaboration tools. - Experience of Monday.com an advantage. - Knowledge of and previous event / tournament experience across the stadiums in Brazil a plus. - The right to work in Brazil. - Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

job.title: Academy Kit Assistant
job.html_description:
The Academy Kit Assistant supports the smooth day-to-day running of the Academy by preparing, maintaining, and distributing training and match kit for players and staff. 

This role is 20hrs per week.

Responsibilities

Essential and Desirable
  • Organised and reliable.
  • Strong attention to detail.
  • Positive team player with a flexible approach to working evenings and weekends.
  • Full Driving License
  • Able to work in a fast-paced football environment while maintaining high professional standards.

Requirements

The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role:  
 
  • Prepare and distribute training and match kit.
  • Ensure all kit and equipment is clean, organised, and ready for use.
  • Assist with stock control, kit issuing, and inventory management.
  • Support academy training sessions, fixtures, and tournaments as required.
  • Maintain high standards within the kit room and storage areas.
  • Work closely with academy staff to meet operational needs.
job.plain_text_description:
The Academy Kit Assistant supports the smooth day-to-day running of the Academy by preparing, maintaining, and distributing training and match kit for players and staff.
This role is 20hrs per week.
Responsibilities
Essential and Desirable
- Organised and reliable. - Strong attention to detail. - Positive team player with a flexible approach to working evenings and weekends. - Full Driving License - Able to work in a fast-paced football environment while maintaining high professional standards.
Requirements
The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role:
- Prepare and distribute training and match kit. - Ensure all kit and equipment is clean, organised, and ready for use. - Assist with stock control, kit issuing, and inventory management. - Support academy training sessions, fixtures, and tournaments as required. - Maintain high standards within the kit room and storage areas. - Work closely with academy staff to meet operational needs.

job.title: Security Officer - Day
job.html_description:
To be responsible and protect the Clubs assets, security of the grounds, its contents & the safety of staff and visitors across all Fulham Football Club & Fulham Pier premises.

Requirements

• To open and secure the premises at the correct times and in the correct manner.

• To be fully conversant with the intruder and fire alarm systems and to report any problems that may arise with these systems.

• To carry out regular & varied patrols of the grounds to deter theft, anti-social behaviour and to detect fire.

• To record all incidents and tasks undertaken during every shift. These entries will be made in the security Daily Occurrence Book.

• To report any matter having a bearing on the safety of the club and its staff and visitors.

• To inform the Security Manager of any incident this may involve dishonesty or when Company rules and regulations have been broken. 

• To carry out investigation as directed by the Security Manager involving cash, stock and property of the club and the personal effects of the staff.

• To conduct bag and vehicle searches on staff and contractors as directed by the Security Manager.

• To keep, the confidentiality of any matter concerning the club and Security department.

• To maintain a good working relationship with the staff and managers and encourage a high standard of Security.

• To maintain a smart appearance and to act in a courteous and professional manner when dealing with members of the public and staff.

• To issue keys from the security office and to record the issue and receiving of those keys in the appropriate register, reporting any missing keys or damaged locks.

• To record lost property in the lost property, register and to update the register when required.

• To ensure that all visitors are recorded in the Visitors Register, for the purpose of maintaining accurate records.

• To be fully conversant with the operation of CCTV, Access Control and Two way radio systems used by the Club.

• To be fully conversant with the Company rules and regulations, health and Safety at work and the Fire and Emergency procedures and ensure accurate logs of these are kept.

• To ensure accurate goods inwards records are kept.

• Other ad hoc duties as required by the business.

job.title: Female Football Coach (Casual)
job.plain_text_description:
Fulham FC Foundation are currently seeking to recruit an enthusiastic and committed person to take up the casual position of a Female Football Coach.
Reporting directly to our Operations Officers the Female Football Coach will be responsible for managing a number of female teams at one of the foundations partner schools. This role would involve both the management of these teams, as well as providing high-quality, team-based training sessions throughout the week. You will also be responsible for overseeing any fixtures these team have across the season. This is a casual role where flexibility is essential, as the delivery of training sessions and fixtures changes across the school term.
The successful candidate will be somebody who has prior experience managing a female team, and holds at least a UEFA C License qualification. Due to the requirements stipulated by the school, the successful candidate will be a female coach with relatable experience to their players allowing them to be a positive role model to them.
Although this role is casual, with the hours varying across the season, meaning that there is no set timetable. There are some key times when training sessions and fixtures are likely to happen. Therefore, the ideal candidate will have good availability during the afternoons of the working week. The ideal availability will be:
Monday – Friday from 2pm to 6pm
There will also be opportunity to work across other foundation programmes such as our ‘Player Development Centres’ and ‘Girls Development Centres’ should the successful candidate want to.
Whilst there is no obligation for Fulham FC Foundation to provide, or for the Female Football Coaches to accept, work. Female Football Coaches who do not work for an extended period of time (3 months), may no longer be offered work.
Salary: £16.50 p/h
Next steps:
The closing date for this role is the 30th of June, however applications will be reviewed consistently so any appropriate candidate may be interviewed before this date.
If successful, you will join a vibrant team who are passionate about achieving the organisation's vision of “an active healthy, inclusive and thriving community”, and you’ll have the opportunity to build on your existing skills through a variety of training and development opportunities and experiences. This role will also play a role in supporting the Foundation’s Women’s and Girls Strategy to develop and support opportunities for underrepresented groups.
*This job description should not be regarded as exclusive or exhaustive. It is intended as an outline indication of areas of activity and responsibility and will be amended in the light of the changing needs of the organisation.
Responsibilities
- To act as an Ambassador for Fulham FC Foundation (FFCF), representing the organisation professionally in line with its values.
- To design and deliver high quality, team training sessions to the teams you’ll be working with.
- To manage the teams during both home and away fixtures, including all the team admin that does along with managing the team.
- To provide high-quality, individualised feedback to each of the players in your teams to support their footballing development.
- To be a positive role model to the student athletes, helping both motivate and support them with their football, and education.
- To identify and support progression pathways for talented young people.
- To provide a safe, positive and inclusive learning environment at all times,
- To adhere to FFCF’s safeguarding policies and procedures, ensuring the timely reporting of any safeguarding concerns or incidents within sessions.
Requirements
Essential:
Experience:
- Experience of delivering high quality training sessions to female footballers. - Experience of running a 9 or 11 aside female football team. - Experience of working with others as part of a coaching team. - Has a driving license and/or is willing to travel independently.
Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:
- Excellent verbal communication skills. - Excellent knowledge of Women’s Football. - Excellent knowledge of the pathway opportunities for female players. - First Aid, Safeguarding and DBS qualification. - A UEFA C Coaching Qualification, or currently undertaking and due to complete this year. - Base knowledge of and willingness to learn & contribute towards FFCF strategic aims.
Personal Qualities:
- A positive role-model for children & young people. - Ability to travel independently across Surrey and South-West London, including transport of equipment. - Well presented, with a professional manner. - Positive, energetic persona. Able to build rapport with participants. - Excellent time-keeping & punctuality. - Willingness to learn & develop personally & professionally.
Preferred:
Experience:
- Experience of delivering football sessions in a secondary school - Experience of working with high performing footballers. - Experience of leading a local female grassroots team
Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:
- Additional football qualifications such as Goalkeeping, S&C, or Fitness. - Knowledge and links to local grassroots football teams. - UEFA B License (or willing to work towards)

job.title: Host Family
job.html_description:
Are you passionate about football and looking for a unique opportunity to make a difference in the lives of young athletes, aged 15-21?


Fulham FC is seeking warm and welcoming host families to provide a safe and supportive home environment to support our Academy players who will be required to live away from home to fulfil their education and football training at our club.


As a host parent, you will play an essential role in helping our players thrive both on and off the pitch. You'll provide a comfortable living space, home-cooked meals, emotional support and help them to adjust to life away from home as they focus on their training and academic pursuits.


What we are looking for:
  • A friendly, caring family or individual with a spare room
  • A willingness to provide support, guidance, and encouragement to our young players
  • A safe and welcoming home environment
  • Support our young people by teaching them basic independent living and self-care skills such as washing, cooking and cleaning up after themselves.
  • Providing meals in line with nutritional guidance from the Academy.
  • Accommodation has access to public and local amenities with good transport links to the Academy training ground.
  • Report any welfare concerns to the Academy Safeguarding and Player Care team should they occur.
  • Include our young people in family life as much as they feel comfortable with.
  • The club will regularly inspect and visit the placement during the season.
  • The club will provide further guidance to host families, young people and their parents explaining what would be expected of them.

In order to be considered for this position you must live within 30 minutes travel for the player from the training ground.

Fulham Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and young people and expects host families to share this commitment. As such, the host family provider will be subject to an Enhanced Disclosure & Barring Service (DBS) check and risk assessments prior to a placement commencing.


Any other person over the age of 18, who resides in the household or has regular contact with the player will be required to complete an enhanced DBS. Host Families will be required to attend safeguarding training and workshops arranged by the Academy.


We are committed to supporting an environment where all staff have a personal responsibility to uphold the Club’s Equal Opportunities Policy by treating fellow employees, prospective employees, casual workers, prospective casual workers, players, prospective players and customers fairly and impartially.

job.title: Football & Sports Coach - Development Centres
job.html_description:
To deliver high quality football coaching sessions to accomplished & aspiring grassroots players aged 6 to 16.

To deliver football & sports sessions across the wider Foundation to a range of children, young people and adults.


Responsibilities

  • To act as an Ambassador for FFCF and maintain and develop relationships to ensure high quality experiences for young people, partner organisations, other staff and customers

  • To effectively plan and deliver high quality football sessions in-line with coaching syllabus, philosophy and values

  • To effectively support the development of individual players through the creation of individual development plans throughout the season

  • To identify and support the development of progression pathways for talented young people

  • To effectively plan and deliver extra-curricular and community activities across the wider Foundation

  • To support the transition between school-based activities, wider Foundation activities and where appropriate, other community activities

  • Assist in data collection and completion of monitoring for reports on an ongoing basis, within the timescales required

  • To work closely with Football Development Officer's to understand programme standards and attendance targets and support the promotion of FFCF activities

  • To support the smooth function of the Operations Department in line with the Foundation values and targets and work across programmes, when required

Requirements

Essential:

Experience:
  • Leading football sessions to age groups U7 to U16
  • Delivery of extracurricular and community activities to all age groups
  • Supporting and developing coaches in a sports environment
  • Working with external partners to achieve successful outcome
  • Monitoring and Evaluation 
  • Marketing and promotion

Skills, knowledge & qualifications:
  • Minimum of UEFA C qualification in Coaching Football
  • FA First Aid - in date
  • FA Safeguarding - in date
  • Ability to drive, has access to a car or ability to travel independently

Personal qualities:
  • Committed and motivated by the purpose of the Foundation 
  • Demonstrates respect for colleagues, partners and customers and when possible operates above and beyond expectations
  • Well presented with a good manner
  • Excellent punctuality
  • Ability to prioritise work and consistently meet deadlines
  • Effective communicator
  • Cheerful positive attitude

Preferred:

Experience:
  • Delivery in a Club Community Organisation (CCO)
  • Delivery of activiry to young people with a Disability. 
  • Delivery of CPD events to coaches.
  • Delivery of Physical Education lessons to primary age children

Skills, knowledge ​& qualifications:
  • UEFA B qualification
  • Non-football NGB sports qualifications
  • Mentoring qualification
  • Talent Identification qualification
  • Understanding of Physical Education standards in schools (Ofsted guidance)

job.title: FA Club Placement Programme – 2026/27
job.html_description:
Fulham Football Club Academy are excited to offer one National Goalkeeping Licence qualified coach the opportunity to undertake a voluntary part time placement within the Goalkeeping Department of our Academy as part of the FA Club Placement programme.

The Academy’s mission is to support ‘every individual to fulfil their potential’.

Within this placement we have an outstanding opportunity to expose one individual to the full Academy coaching experience. Our belief is that learning, and development, happen through experiences, and we are therefore looking to design a unique placement experience that supports the successful individual to fulfil their potential as a coach.

Fulham Football Club is committed to delivering an equitable, diverse, and inclusive environment. We recognise that a diverse and inclusive workforce is crucial in creating a positive and supportive environment for all. We understand that diversity and inclusion are not only important values but also essential for the success and growth of our organisation.

Our aim is to create an environment whereby those connected to the club feel valued, respected and supported, regardless of their background, ethnicity, gender, age, sexual orientation or disability. We believe that by embracing and celebrating our differences, we can cultivate a culture of collaboration, creativity and innovation.

To achieve this, we actively promote diversity and inclusion throughout our recruitment and retention processes. The Club is committed to attracting talented individuals from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences, ensuring that our workforce reflects the diversity of our players and the communities we serve.

Responsibilities

The successful candidate will:
  • Gain an understanding of the coaching programme across the Academy pathway.
  • Support the delivery of coaching that aligns to the Academy strategy and mission, in support of the development of individual Academy players.
  • Gain an understanding of systematic planning, delivery and reviewing processes within the Academy, including Seasonal planning, Individual Player Development planning and Individual Player & Parent reviews.
  • Have the opportunity to analyse and develop player performance through video.
  • Gain invaluable experience of Talent ID and Recruitment processes within the Academy system.
  • Gain an understanding of Insights; viewing statistical reports and synthesising the information to inform training or game approaches.
  • Get first-hand experience of working within a Multi-Disciplinary Team (MDT) within a Category 1 environment.
  • Have the opportunity to attend internal MDT, Coaching Department and whole Academy meetings and CPD events.
  • Gain an understanding and knowledge of the Premier League Youth Development Rules.
  • Gain an understanding of Club policies and procedures relating to coaching and player development activities.

Role benefits & structure:
  • Candidates will attend activity on evenings and weekends at least twice per week.
  • All travel expenses will be covered by the Club.
  • Full training kit and equipment will be provided.
  • Personal development opportunities will exist across the placement, including on-going support from an internal ‘Coaching Mentor’.

Requirements

At the top levels of both the men's and women's game, coaches from Black, Asian, minority ethnic backgrounds and females, continue to be significantly underrepresented within key positions. 

The FA is committed to changing that. The is FA’s looking to develop more opportunities for coaches from Black, Asian, minority ethnic backgrounds and females to get essential experience to challenge for coaching and technical roles in football. 

As part of our Coach Inclusion and Diversity Plan, we've partnered with a group professional clubs in our ‘Club Placement Programme’ for the 2026/27 season. This initiative offers 31 exciting Academy placement opportunities for coaches from historically underrepresented backgrounds. 

The successful candidates will take on a part-time volunteer role within a club’s category 1, 2 or 3 academy, and through a carefully structured programme of learning and development opportunities they will gain invaluable experience and exposure within a professional environment.

Benefits

Application criteria
  • Male applicants of Black, Asian or Minority Ethnic origin who are aged 18 years or older
  • Female applicants (of any ethnic origin) who are aged 18 years or older
  • UEFA B Licence holders
  • FA Youth Module 1, 2 and 3 holders
  • Level 2/UEFA C (block 1 and 2)
  • FA Safeguarding and FA First Aid Qualifications
  • FA licensed coach membership

The successful applicant will be subject to an Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) check.

Be a cultural and values fit for Fulham Football Club Academy by:
  • Demonstrating an outstanding work ethic and determination in relation to care and support your provide to players, and in support of your own personal development.
  • Showcasing a level of humility that enables the development of open and trusting relationships with colleagues, players and families.
  • Demonstrating honesty within your everyday actions, interactions and reflections, to ensure that you can take the most from the placement opportunity.

Applications Close: 20th July
job.plain_text_description:
Fulham Football Club Academy are excited to offer one National Goalkeeping Licence qualified coach the opportunity to undertake a voluntary part time placement within the Goalkeeping Department of our Academy as part of the FA Club Placement programme.
The Academy’s mission is to support ‘every individual to fulfil their potential’.
Within this placement we have an outstanding opportunity to expose one individual to the full Academy coaching experience. Our belief is that learning, and development, happen through experiences, and we are therefore looking to design a unique placement experience that supports the successful individual to fulfil their potential as a coach.
Fulham Football Club is committed to delivering an equitable, diverse, and inclusive environment. We recognise that a diverse and inclusive workforce is crucial in creating a positive and supportive environment for all. We understand that diversity and inclusion are not only important values but also essential for the success and growth of our organisation.
Our aim is to create an environment whereby those connected to the club feel valued, respected and supported, regardless of their background, ethnicity, gender, age, sexual orientation or disability. We believe that by embracing and celebrating our differences, we can cultivate a culture of collaboration, creativity and innovation.
To achieve this, we actively promote diversity and inclusion throughout our recruitment and retention processes. The Club is committed to attracting talented individuals from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences, ensuring that our workforce reflects the diversity of our players and the communities we serve.
Responsibilities
The successful candidate will:
- Gain an understanding of the coaching programme across the Academy pathway. - Support the delivery of coaching that aligns to the Academy strategy and mission, in support of the development of individual Academy players. - Gain an understanding of systematic planning, delivery and reviewing processes within the Academy, including Seasonal planning, Individual Player Development planning and Individual Player & Parent reviews. - Have the opportunity to analyse and develop player performance through video. - Gain invaluable experience of Talent ID and Recruitment processes within the Academy system. - Gain an understanding of Insights; viewing statistical reports and synthesising the information to inform training or game approaches. - Get first-hand experience of working within a Multi-Disciplinary Team (MDT) within a Category 1 environment. - Have the opportunity to attend internal MDT, Coaching Department and whole Academy meetings and CPD events. - Gain an understanding and knowledge of the Premier League Youth Development Rules. - Gain an understanding of Club policies and procedures relating to coaching and player development activities.
Role benefits & structure:
- Candidates will attend activity on evenings and weekends at least twice per week. - All travel expenses will be covered by the Club. - Full training kit and equipment will be provided. - Personal development opportunities will exist across the placement, including on-going support from an internal ‘Coaching Mentor’.
Requirements
At the top levels of both the men's and women's game, coaches from Black, Asian, minority ethnic backgrounds and females, continue to be significantly underrepresented within key positions.
The FA is committed to changing that. The is FA’s looking to develop more opportunities for coaches from Black, Asian, minority ethnic backgrounds and females to get essential experience to challenge for coaching and technical roles in football.
As part of our Coach Inclusion and Diversity Plan, we've partnered with a group professional clubs in our ‘Club Placement Programme’ for the 2026/27 season. This initiative offers 31 exciting Academy placement opportunities for coaches from historically underrepresented backgrounds.
The successful candidates will take on a part-time volunteer role within a club’s category 1, 2 or 3 academy, and through a carefully structured programme of learning and development opportunities they will gain invaluable experience and exposure within a professional environment.
Benefits
Application criteria
- Male applicants of Black, Asian or Minority Ethnic origin who are aged 18 years or older - Female applicants (of any ethnic origin) who are aged 18 years or older - UEFA B Licence holders - FA Youth Module 1, 2 and 3 holders - Level 2/UEFA C (block 1 and 2) - FA Safeguarding and FA First Aid Qualifications - FA licensed coach membership
The successful applicant will be subject to an Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) check.
Be a cultural and values fit for Fulham Football Club Academy by:
- Demonstrating an outstanding work ethic and determination in relation to care and support your provide to players, and in support of your own personal development. - Showcasing a level of humility that enables the development of open and trusting relationships with colleagues, players and families. - Demonstrating honesty within your everyday actions, interactions and reflections, to ensure that you can take the most from the placement opportunity.
Applications Close: 20th July

job.title: Lewisham Advanced Centre Coach
job.html_description:
Join our team at the Lewisham Advanced Centre and play a key role in shaping the next generation of talent. Working closely alongside the Head Coach and a dedicated team of coaches, you will contribute to the development, support, and progression of players in a dynamic and professional environment.

This is an opportunity to make a real impact, delivering high-quality coaching, fostering player growth, and creating a positive, high-performance culture.

As a representative of Fulham FC, you will be expected to consistently uphold and embody the club’s core values in everything you do, both on and off the pitch.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:
  • Deliver high-quality coaching and player development across both the Lewisham Advanced Centre and Motspur Park, ensuring every session meets the highest professional standards.
  • Work collaboratively with the Head Coach, Pre-Academy Manager, and Recruitment Manager to create a safe, engaging, and enjoyable environment for all young players.
  • Provide effective support in the delivery and ongoing development of the club’s Pre-Academy programme at Motspur Park, contributing to its continued growth and success.
  • Build strong working relationships with the wider academy team, promoting high standards, sharing best practice, and creating clear, supportive pathways for players transitioning between programmes.
  • Attend and contribute to internal and external meetings as required, representing the programme with professionalism and purpose.
  • Maintain a strong focus on safeguarding at all times—ensuring all participants are safely welcomed and dismissed, and that a secure, supportive environment is consistently upheld.

Requirements

  • FA Level 1 Coaching qualification, with up-to-date FA First Aid and Safeguarding certifications.
  • IT literate, with proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Highly organised, with strong time management and planning skills.
  • Good knowledge of coaching standards and best practice.
  • Clear and effective communication skills.
  • Committed to safeguarding, equality, diversity, and inclusion in the workplace.

job.title: Performance Scientist
job.html_description:
About Us:
 
Fulham Football Club, a team rich in history and ambition, offers a unique opportunity to work within one of England’s most admired footballing institutions. With a reputation for fostering talent and embracing innovation, we pride ourselves on delivering excellence both on and off the pitch. Joining our Performance Team means becoming part of a forward-thinking environment committed to optimising player performance at the highest level.
 
Position Purpose:
 
The Performance Scientist will provide support to the First Team Performance department, assisting in the delivery of sport science and S&C services aimed at optimising player performance, reducing injury risk, and facilitating rehabilitation. This role will work under the guidance of the Head of player insights and alongside other members of the performance team to contribute to the overall success of the First Team.

Responsibilities

  • Complete general sport science tasks (Reporting, GPS recording, testing, conditioning, etc.) 
  • Conduct power & strength-based testing and deliver reports to respective leads
  • Contribute to recovery methods using evidence-based methods/techniques
  • Assist with match day sports science & S&C provision
  • Assist Research & Development run by the club
  • Assist leading staff with player profiling/ testing of all first team players
  • Assist leading staff with set up & delivery of all strength and power sessions for 1st team players
  • Assist the senior with set up & delivery of player pre-activation sessions
  • Assist with delivery of field-based warm-ups and conditioning sessions

Requirements

Essential Experience:
•  4 years’ experience of working within professional football or elite sport environments
•  Experience of working within a multi-disciplinary team.
•  Experience in reporting physical data
•  Experience of leading on pitch/off pitch delivery and rehabilitation

Essential skills, knowledge & qualifications
  • BSc in Sports Science / Strength & Conditioning
  • MSc in Sport Science/ Strength & Conditioning
  • Strong data analytical and reporting skill set (excel,      R, Tableau, Powerbi)
  • Experience assisting and leading gym programming

Personal qualities:
  • Dedicated
  • Organised
  • Enthusiastic
  • Flexible
  • Reliable
Team orientated

job.title: Academy Scout
job.html_description:
The Academy Scout (Local Recruitment) plays a vital role in identifying and monitoring talented young players within the local area, whether from grassroots clubs or other academies.


Working as part of the local Academy scouting team, you will be responsible for producing detailed player reports, sharing insights with Academy staff, and contributing to a comprehensive understanding of the player landscape within your assigned region.


We have a particular interest in recruiting scouts with strong networks and knowledge within the following areas:

  • Grassroots: South London, West London

  • Professional Academies: East London, North London

Responsibilities

• Manage your individual weekend schedule and attend fixtures within your allocated region or phase.
• Observe and report on players demonstrating potential within your area and age phase.
• Network with other local scouts to exchange information and intelligence on potential targets.
• Keep Local Recruitment Managers updated on high-potential players within your area.
• Support communication with families of players involved in Talent ID programmes and trial processes.
• Assist in the delivery of Talent ID events and scouting programmes as required.
• Contribute to scouting team meetings to review succession planning and player targets.
• Attend department meetings, CPD sessions, and all required safeguarding training.

Requirements

• Strong knowledge and network within your designated local area.
• Previous experience scouting for another Academy (Category 1, 2, or 3).
• FA Talent ID Level 1 qualification.
• FA Safeguarding certification.

Requirements
• Strong knowledge and network within your designated local area.
• Previous experience scouting for another Academy (Category 1, 2, or 3).
• FA Talent ID Level 1 qualification.
• FA Safeguarding certification.

job.title: Fire Officer
job.html_description:
Position Purpose 

 

The Fire Team will not normally be expected to be involved in the management of spectators. When deployed as a member of the Fire Team the primary is one of fire prevention and first response should an outbreak of fire occur.
This role is suitable for candidates who are or have experience of the fire industry or stewards who are willing to undergo additional fire safety training to enable them to carry out this role.

Responsibilities

  • Attend the Stadium at given dates and times as dictated by the Head of Safety.
  • Undertake pre-match and post-match fire checks as instructed by the Fire Team leader. Arrive at Steward Sign as directed.
  • Conduct Pre-Match Checks and specified documentation on Raven Controls as required.
  • Conduct patrols within the stand and under croft’s to minimise the risk of fire breaking out and assisting to prevent the spread of fire. 
  • Respond to Fire and associated incidents as directed by FFC Control, ensuring staff are supported, appropriate action is taken, and that information is passed to the Safety Management Team in FFC Control (Act – Inform – Escalate)
  • Attend during match meetings with Fire Team leader for the identification of issues to be addressed, learning points and review of specific deployments. 
  • Report any fire safety concerns to the fire team leader for appropriate action and escalation to FFC Control and the Safety Officer. 
  • Know the location of and be able to effectively operate the firefighting equipment at the Stadium. 
  • Be able to provide basic ‘First Aid’ if required. 
  • Know the location of the First Aid Rooms located within the Stadium. 
  • Be fully conversant with any methods or messages used to alert staff that an emergency has arisen. 
  • Be capable of recognising potential fire hazards and items of interest or suspect packages, reporting such matters immediately to the Fire Team leader, a Supervisor, Chief Steward, Deputy Safety Officer, or Safety Officer. 
  • Promptly comply with any instruction given by a Supervisor, Chief Steward, Deputy Safety Officer, Safety Officer, or Police Officer. 
  • Comply with the relevant instructions and guidance contained in the Stewards Handbook and standing instructions for the FFC Fire Team. 
  • Attend all required training. 
  • To understand their general responsibilities towards the health and safety and welfare of all spectators, other stewards, staff, and themselves.


Essential and Desirable 
 
Background in Fire industry (present/previous Fire Service) and willing to undergo relevant training. 

Requirements

To act in accordance with the general recommendations of the Guide to Safety at Sports Ground (Green Guide) and the specific requirements of the Safety Certificate issued by the Local Authority under the Safety of Sports Ground Act 1975. Fire Safety Officers play a key role in the event day safety team ensuring that the stadium provides a safe and secure environment for thousands of visitors. You will become a focal point for all fire safety issues, providing preventative advice and measures in an emergency.

 

  • You should be a minimum of 18 years of age, be fit, active and have the character and temperament to carry out the duties required. 
  • You should possess good communication skills.
  • You must be able to attend most first-team home fixtures and other major events held at the stadium.
  • You should be polite, courteous, and helpful to all supporters.
  • Whilst on duty you will adhere to the Club dress code and be clean and tidy.
  • You are not engaged to watch the game but are expected to concentrate on your area of responsibility. 
  • You should never show hostility or use obscene or offensive language to any supporters.
  • You should never act in any way likely to bring discredit on the Club.

job.title: Casual DisAbility Football & Sports Coach
job.plain_text_description:
Fulham FC Foundation are currently seeking to recruit an enthusiastic and committed person to take up the casual position of DisAbility Football & Sports Coach.
Reporting directly to our Senior DisAbility Development Manager, the DisAbility Football & Sports Coaches are responsible for delivering high-quality football & sports coaching to children, young people and adults with disabilities and additional needs. This role involves delivery across a wide-range of our DisAbility Programmes, including Pan DisAbility Clubs, Active Autism, Down’s Syndrome ‘Badgers’, Blind and Partially Sighted, Cerebral Palsy and Dwarfism Football Sessions. In addition there will be opportunities to work in our DisAbility Schools programme delivering PE, Enrichment Activities and After School Clubs.
While the primary focus of this role is on DisAbility Football & Sport, there may be opportunities to contribute to other FFC Foundation activities such as Skills Club, Development Centres, Soccer Schools, Primary Education, Kicks, and Health & Wellbeing. We would encourage candidates to prioritise DisAbility Programmes where possible, while remaining flexible to support other areas as needed.
This is a casual role where flexibility is essential, as the role necessitates involvement in supporting the delivery of programme activities as and when required.
The successful candidate will be somebody who has prior experience delivering football & sports coaching sessions to children & young people with disabilities or additional needs in grassroots & school settings, holds at least a FA Level 1/Introduction to Coaching Football qualification and is committed to the charitable purpose of Fulham FC Foundation.
As this role is casual, there is no set timetable. Sessions may be offered on an adhoc or regular basis, depending on the needs of Fulham FC Foundation. It is the responsibility of the DisAbility Football & Sports Coaches to maintain consistent and effective communication with our Senior DisAbility Development Manager and Operations Officers to ensure the continuation of work.
Whilst there is no obligation for Fulham FC Foundation to provide, or for the DisAbility Football & Sports Coaches to accept, work. DisAbility Football & Sports Coaches who do not work for an extended period of time, will be deactivated.
The successful candidates should be available to work across at least one of the below times, in order to receive work. We are extremely keen to hear from those who are able to work on Saturday mornings.
Weekdays – 9am to 5 pm;
Weekday Evenings – 5pm to 9pm
Weekends – Saturdays – 8am to 12noon.
Work in other timings is available, however on a far reduced frequency.
Salary Range: £12.95 p/h to £16.50 p/h
Next steps
:
There is no closing date for this role. Applications will be reviewed on a bi-weekly basis.
If successful, you will join a vibrant team who are passionate about achieving the organisation's vision of “an active, inclusive and thriving community”, and you’ll have the opportunity to build on your existing skills through a variety of training and development opportunities and experiences
*
This job description should not be regarded as exclusive or exhaustive. It is intended as an outline indication of areas of activity and responsibility and will be amended in the light of the changing needs of the organisation
.
Responsibilities
- To act as an Ambassador for Fulham FC Foundation (FFCF), representing the organisation professionally in line with its values. - To plan and deliver high-quality football and sports coaching sessions to children, young people & adults in-line with FFCF standards. - To provide a safe, positive and inclusive learning environment at all times, - To adhere to FFCF’s safeguarding policies and procedures, ensuring the timely reporting of any safeguarding concerns or incidents within sessions. - To engage, include, inspire and grow participation across FFCF activities. - To support the transition of participants between different FFCF programmes and activities. - To champion opportunities for individuals from underrepresented groups within FFCF activities. - To identify and support progression pathways for talented young people. - To support the development of FFCF programmes through positive collaboration with Department Managers / Operations Officers. - To support the promotion of FFCF programmes across the local community. - To assist in data collection and completion of monitoring & evaluation on an ongoing basis.
Requirements
Essential:
Experience:
- Delivery of football coaching sessions in a grassroots environment - Delivery of sports coaching sessions to children, young people or adults with disabilities and additional needs. - Delivery within a school/college setting. - Working in a customer-facing role.
Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:
- Introduction to Coaching Football qualification - In-date FA Emergency First Aid - In-date FA Safeguarding Children certificate - Base knowledge of and willingness to learn & contribute towards FFCF strategic aims.
Personal Qualities:
- A positive role-model for children & young people. - Ability to travel independently across South-West London, including transport of equipment. - Well presented, with a professional manner. - Positive, energetic persona. Able to build rapport with participants. - Excellent time-keeping & punctuality. - Willingness to learn & develop personally & professionally.
Preferred:
Experience:
- Delivery of football & sports coaching sessions in a Club Community Organisation (CCO). - Delivery of Physical Education within a school setting. - Delivery within a classroom environment. - Marketing & Promoting participation activities in the community. - Delivery of football & sports coaching sessions to females.
Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:
- FA Level 2/UEFA C in Coaching Football qualification (or higher). - Additional PE/HLTA qualification - Mentoring qualification - Non-football NGB sports qualifications.

job.title: Matchday Retail Assistant
job.html_description:
Position Purpose

This role involves supporting the day-to-day operations of the Club’s retail store, delivering excellent customer service, maintaining high standards of presentation, and ensuring efficient stock handling and sales processes.

Responsibilities

Essential and Desirable

  • Strong customer service and communication skills, with the ability to assist customers in a friendly and professional manner.
  • Able to work effectively in a fast-paced retail environment, both independently and as part of a team, while maintaining high standards of organisation and efficiency.
  • Available to work all home matchdays - weekends and evenings including bank holidays.
  • Adaptability to handle a variety of situations

Ideally, Matchday Retail Assistant will:
  • Previous EPOS experience
  • Retail Experience

Requirements

The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role:
• Provide excellent and helpful customer service, assisting customers with products and purchases.
• Operate the till and process card payments accurately.
• Print names and numbers onto replica shirts, ensuring customer orders are completed correctly and to a high standard.
• Replenish stock and keep stores and displays tidy and well-presented.
• Price and label products correctly across the store.
• Support the matchday running of the store and assist with additional tasks when required
• Work in various units across the stadium

job.title: FA Club Placement Programme – 2026/27
job.html_description:

The Academy’s mission is to support ‘every individual to fulfil their potential’.

Within this placement we have an outstanding opportunity to expose one individual to the full Academy coaching experience. Our belief is that learning, and development, happen through experiences, and we are therefore looking to design a unique placement experience that supports the successful individual to fulfil their potential as a coach.

Fulham Football Club is committed to delivering an equitable, diverse, and inclusive environment. We recognise that a diverse and inclusive workforce is crucial in creating a positive and supportive environment for all. We understand that diversity and inclusion are not only important values but also essential for the success and growth of our organisation.

Our aim is to create an environment whereby those connected to the club feel valued, respected and supported, regardless of their background, ethnicity, gender, age, sexual orientation or disability. We believe that by embracing and celebrating our differences, we can cultivate a culture of collaboration, creativity and innovation.

  • Gain an understanding of the coaching programme across the Academy pathway.
