  • Have the opportunity to analyse and develop player performance through video.
  • Gain invaluable experience of Talent ID and Recruitment processes within the Academy system.
  • Gain an understanding of Insights; viewing statistical reports and synthesising the information to inform training or game approaches.
  • Get first-hand experience of working within a Multi-Disciplinary Team (MDT) within a Category 1 environment.
  • Have the opportunity to attend internal MDT, Coaching Department and whole Academy meetings and CPD events.
  • Gain an understanding and knowledge of the Premier League Youth Development Rules.
  • Gain an understanding of Club policies and procedures relating to coaching and player development activities.

Role benefits & structure:
  • Candidates will attend activity on evenings and weekends at least twice per week.
  • All travel expenses will be covered by the Club.
  • Full training kit and equipment will be provided.
  • Personal development opportunities will exist across the placement, including on-going support from an internal ‘Coaching Mentor’.

Requirements

At the top levels of both the men's and women's game, coaches from Black, Asian, minority ethnic backgrounds and females, continue to be significantly underrepresented within key positions. 

The FA is committed to changing that. The is FA’s looking to develop more opportunities for coaches from Black, Asian, minority ethnic backgrounds and females to get essential experience to challenge for coaching and technical roles in football. 

As part of our Coach Inclusion and Diversity Plan, we've partnered with a group professional clubs in our ‘Club Placement Programme’ for the 2026/27 season. This initiative offers 31 exciting Academy placement opportunities for coaches from historically underrepresented backgrounds. 

The successful candidates will take on a part-time volunteer role within a club’s category 1, 2 or 3 academy, and through a carefully structured programme of learning and development opportunities they will gain invaluable experience and exposure within a professional environment.

Benefits

Application criteria
  • Male applicants of Black, Asian or Minority Ethnic origin who are aged 18 years or older
  • Female applicants (of any ethnic origin) who are aged 18 years or older
  • UEFA B Licence holders
  • FA Youth Module 1, 2 and 3 holders
  • Or holders of the current Level 2/UEFA C (block 1 and 2)
  • FA Safeguarding and FA First Aid Qualifications
  • FA licensed coach membership

The successful applicant will be subject to an Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) check.

Be a cultural and values fit for Fulham Football Club Academy by:
  • Demonstrating an outstanding work ethic and determination in relation to care and support your provide to players, and in support of your own personal development.
  • Showcasing a level of humility that enables the development of open and trusting relationships with colleagues, players and families.
  • Demonstrating honesty within your everyday actions, interactions and reflections, to ensure that you can take the most from the placement opportunity.

Applications Close: 20th July
job.plain_text_description:
Fulham Football Club Academy are excited to offer one UEFA B qualified coach the opportunity to undertake a voluntary part time placement within the Academy as part of the FA Club Placement programme.
The Academy’s mission is to support ‘every individual to fulfil their potential’.
Within this placement we have an outstanding opportunity to expose one individual to the full Academy coaching experience. Our belief is that learning, and development, happen through experiences, and we are therefore looking to design a unique placement experience that supports the successful individual to fulfil their potential as a coach.
Fulham Football Club is committed to delivering an equitable, diverse, and inclusive environment. We recognise that a diverse and inclusive workforce is crucial in creating a positive and supportive environment for all. We understand that diversity and inclusion are not only important values but also essential for the success and growth of our organisation.
Our aim is to create an environment whereby those connected to the club feel valued, respected and supported, regardless of their background, ethnicity, gender, age, sexual orientation or disability. We believe that by embracing and celebrating our differences, we can cultivate a culture of collaboration, creativity and innovation.
To achieve this, we actively promote diversity and inclusion throughout our recruitment and retention processes. The Club is committed to attracting talented individuals from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences, ensuring that our workforce reflects the diversity of our players and the communities we serve.
Responsibilities
The successful candidate will:
- Gain an understanding of the coaching programme across the Academy pathway. - Support the delivery of coaching that aligns to the Academy strategy and mission, in support of the development of individual Academy players. - Gain an understanding of systematic planning, delivery and reviewing processes within the Academy, including Seasonal planning, Individual Player Development planning and Individual Player & Parent reviews. - Have the opportunity to analyse and develop player performance through video. - Gain invaluable experience of Talent ID and Recruitment processes within the Academy system. - Gain an understanding of Insights; viewing statistical reports and synthesising the information to inform training or game approaches. - Get first-hand experience of working within a Multi-Disciplinary Team (MDT) within a Category 1 environment. - Have the opportunity to attend internal MDT, Coaching Department and whole Academy meetings and CPD events. - Gain an understanding and knowledge of the Premier League Youth Development Rules. - Gain an understanding of Club policies and procedures relating to coaching and player development activities.
Role benefits & structure:
- Candidates will attend activity on evenings and weekends at least twice per week. - All travel expenses will be covered by the Club. - Full training kit and equipment will be provided. - Personal development opportunities will exist across the placement, including on-going support from an internal ‘Coaching Mentor’.
Requirements
At the top levels of both the men's and women's game, coaches from Black, Asian, minority ethnic backgrounds and females, continue to be significantly underrepresented within key positions.
The FA is committed to changing that. The is FA’s looking to develop more opportunities for coaches from Black, Asian, minority ethnic backgrounds and females to get essential experience to challenge for coaching and technical roles in football.
As part of our Coach Inclusion and Diversity Plan, we've partnered with a group professional clubs in our ‘Club Placement Programme’ for the 2026/27 season. This initiative offers 31 exciting Academy placement opportunities for coaches from historically underrepresented backgrounds.
The successful candidates will take on a part-time volunteer role within a club’s category 1, 2 or 3 academy, and through a carefully structured programme of learning and development opportunities they will gain invaluable experience and exposure within a professional environment.
Benefits
Application criteria
- Male applicants of Black, Asian or Minority Ethnic origin who are aged 18 years or older - Female applicants (of any ethnic origin) who are aged 18 years or older - UEFA B Licence holders - FA Youth Module 1, 2 and 3 holders - Or holders of the current Level 2/UEFA C (block 1 and 2) - FA Safeguarding and FA First Aid Qualifications - FA licensed coach membership
The successful applicant will be subject to an Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) check.
Be a cultural and values fit for Fulham Football Club Academy by:
- Demonstrating an outstanding work ethic and determination in relation to care and support your provide to players, and in support of your own personal development. - Showcasing a level of humility that enables the development of open and trusting relationships with colleagues, players and families. - Demonstrating honesty within your everyday actions, interactions and reflections, to ensure that you can take the most from the placement opportunity.
Applications Close: 20th July

job.title: Chef De Partie
job.html_description:
Position Purpose 
 

  • We are looking for a chef with a background in catering for professional sports teams to join our passionate nutrition focused team.

  • You will support our Head Chef and Sous Chef in providing balanced, nutritional and high quality meals to our players and support staff at the training ground.

  • A good basis of nutrition helps our players to achieve their individual nutritional goals, ultimately enhancing health, recovery and performance.

  • To lead the kitchen team in preparing and providing meals to our youth and women teams.

  • Some travel with teams to away games may be necessary, following their weekly schedule/fixtures, ensuring all food provided to them meets the presentation, taste and performance need of the group.

Requirements

Requirements and Responsibilities
 

  • Work with the Head Chef and independently to create and develop exciting seasonal menus. 

  • Prepare meals for youth and women teams working within the nutritional guidelines and ensure junior team members are working correctly to achieve this with you.

  • Instruct junior team members accordingly to ensure youth and women teams' meals are delivered on time and presented to a high standard.

  • Support and lead junior members of the kitchen team.

  • Check stock requirements and order accordingly in the Head/Sous chef absence.

  • Travel with teams in the UK and abroad when required as the catering team representative and provide food provisions as requested.

  • Assist in the preparation of all travel food requirements whilst displaying good organisational skills.

  • Support Head/Sous chef to deliver constantly high quality food in a timely manner.

  • Cover Sous chefs' role as and when required.

  • Working closely with the Supervisor to ensure the highest level of food safety and hygiene are maintained. Compliance with COSHH.

  • Flexibility to work around the teams schedules.



Essential and Desirable
 

The Chef De Partie must have: 

  • Minimum of 1 year experience providing food to sports teams/athletes
  • Level 2 NVQ Professional Cookery or equivalent
  • Excellent communication skill to lead the junior kitchen team
  • Works well under pressure with the ability to remain calm with last minute schedule changes
 
Ideally, the Chef De Partie will: 
  • Football specific experience
  • Level 2 Food Hygiene certificate
  • Performance nutrition knowledge
job.plain_text_description:
Position Purpose
- We are looking for a chef with a background in catering for professional sports teams to join our passionate nutrition focused team. - You will support our Head Chef and Sous Chef in providing balanced, nutritional and high quality meals to our players and support staff at the training ground. - A good basis of nutrition helps our players to achieve their individual nutritional goals, ultimately enhancing health, recovery and performance. - To lead the kitchen team in preparing and providing meals to our youth and women teams. - Some travel with teams to away games may be necessary, following their weekly schedule/fixtures, ensuring all food provided to them meets the presentation, taste and performance need of the group.
Requirements
Requirements and Responsibilities
- Work with the Head Chef and independently to create and develop exciting seasonal menus. - Prepare meals for youth and women teams working within the nutritional guidelines and ensure junior team members are working correctly to achieve this with you. - Instruct junior team members accordingly to ensure youth and women teams' meals are delivered on time and presented to a high standard. - Support and lead junior members of the kitchen team. - Check stock requirements and order accordingly in the Head/Sous chef absence. - Travel with teams in the UK and abroad when required as the catering team representative and provide food provisions as requested. - Assist in the preparation of all travel food requirements whilst displaying good organisational skills. - Support Head/Sous chef to deliver constantly high quality food in a timely manner. - Cover Sous chefs' role as and when required. - Working closely with the Supervisor to ensure the highest level of food safety and hygiene are maintained. Compliance with COSHH. - Flexibility to work around the teams schedules.
Essential and Desirable
The Chef De Partie must have:
- Minimum of 1 year experience providing food to sports teams/athletes - Level 2 NVQ Professional Cookery or equivalent - Excellent communication skill to lead the junior kitchen team - Works well under pressure with the ability to remain calm with last minute schedule changes
Ideally, the Chef De Partie will:
- Football specific experience - Level 2 Food Hygiene certificate - Performance nutrition knowledge

job.title: Casual Academy Scout (Midlands)
job.html_description:
The National Academy scouting team is responsible for identifying and monitoring players either from grassroots or other Academies in national regions. They will complete reports on players and share information with other Academy staff to develop a range of player intelligence across the local area. 

There is a particular interest for us to recruit within the following teams: 
- Grassroots – Midlands 
- Academy- Midlands

Responsibilities

The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role:
• To manage your individual weekend schedule and regularly attend fixtures within your region or
across your phase.
• To complete reports on players all players with potential across your region and phase.
• Network with other national scouts to share information on potential target players.
• To keep National and department Managers informed of high potential players across their area or
age phase.
• To support with the ongoing communication to the families of players within our TID programmes
and on trial as and when required.
• To support the delivery of Talent ID programmes and events as and when required.
• Participate in scouting team meetings to further discuss succession planning and their identified
target players.
• To attend department meetings and participate in all CPD and required safeguarding training when
appropriate.

Requirements

The Casual Academy Scout (Midlands) must have:
• Knowledge and networks within the local area they scout.
• Previous experience scouting for another Academy (Cat 1, 2 or 3)
• FA Talent ID Level 2
• FA Safeguarding

Ideally, the Casual Academy Scout (Midlands) will:
• Previous experience scouting for another Cat 1 Academy.
• FA Level 2 Coaching
• Full Driver’s License

job.title: U18s Head Coach (Women)
job.html_description:
To lead, coach and support the development of the U18s team, fostering a positive and inclusive learning environment. The coach will be responsible for delivering coaching sessions that align with the club’s philosophy, and long-term player development plan.

Responsibilities

To lead, coach and support the development of the U18 Women's team, fostering a positive, inclusive and 
high-performance learning environment. The successful candidate will lead the planning and delivery of the 
U18 programme, ensuring all coaching aligns with the Club's Playing Philosophy and long-term player 
development plan. Working closely with the Head of Youth and the multidisciplinary team, the role will 
support players to maximise their potential and prepare them for progression into the First Team or 
appropriate future pathways.

The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role: 
• Plan, lead, deliver and review all U18 training sessions and matchdays alongside the U18 
multidisciplinary team. 
• Ensure all coaching sessions are aligned to the Club's Playing Philosophy and contribute towards weekly, 
monthly and seasonal objectives. 
• Create and maintain a high-quality, player-centred learning environment that supports the holistic 
development of players on and off the pitch. 
• Develop, implement and regularly review Individual Development Plans (IDPs) for all U18 players. 
• Work closely with the FFCW Head of Youth to ensure alignment across the Women's pathway and 
support player progression towards the First Team. 
• Regularly monitor, report and communicate player progress, development and potential pathway 
movement, including transition and exit routes, to the Head of Youth. 
• Lead the organisation and coordination of U18 matchdays, ensuring effective communication with 
opposition clubs, league officials and internal staff. 
• Maintain effective communication with players, parents/carers and Academy staff to support player 
development and wellbeing.
• Report and record all safeguarding, welfare and player wellbeing concerns promptly via CPOMS, 
ensuring all incidents are managed in line with the Club's safeguarding policies, procedures and reporting 
standards.
• Attend all staff, player and departmental meetings as directed by the FFCW Head of Youth. 
• Complete all planning, reporting and administrative tasks within agreed timeframes and maintain accurate 
player records. 
• Engage in Continued Professional Development (CPD) opportunities to maintain and enhance coaching 
practice and industry standards. 
• Undertake any other duties reasonably requested by the FFCW Head of Youth

Requirements

Essential and Desirable
 
The U18s Lead Pathway Coach (Women) must: 
• Have their UEFA B Licence (or currently working towards). 
• Have their FA Introduction to First Aid in Football certificate (valid and in date). 
• Have their FA Safeguarding Certificate (valid and in date). 
• Have excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build positive relationships with 
players, parents/carers, colleagues and key stakeholders. 
• Demonstrate understanding of player-centred coaching and long-term player development. 
• Have experience delivering individual player development programmes. 
• Have experience coaching within female football programmes and/or elite football environments. 
• Have experience identifying and recruiting players within the women's football pathway. 
• Show an ability to work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team, whilst also working independently 
when required. 
• Demonstrate excellent organisational skills, with strong attention to detail and consistently high 
professional standards. 
• Have the ability to appropriately challenge, support and motivate young players.

Ideally, U18s Lead Pathway Coach (Women) will:
 
• Have their UEFA A Licence (or currently working towards). 
• Demonstrate experience working with elite youth female footballers. 
• Have previous experience within an FA Regional Talent Club (RTC), Professional Game Academy 
(PGA), Women's Super League Academy or similar environment. 
• Have experience using HUDL (or similar video analysis software) to support player development. 
• Have the ability to make effective decisions under pressure. 
• Demonstrate resilience, accountability and ownership in challenging situations

job.title: Ticketing and Membership Executive - Match Days
job.plain_text_description:
In this role, you will work as part of the Ticket Office team at Craven Cottage to manage inbound enquiries across a number of different communication channels – phone, email, online and in person – and deliver on the sale of commercial products relating to Fulham Football Club through the Club’s ticketing system, Ticketmaster.
The Ticket Office team form a critical part of the sales structure of the business, and you will be required to proactively support the Club in its objective to make it easy for people to buy from Fulham FC by assisting with supporter queries on our busy matchdays.
You will form part of the team working as the first line of contact for our fans’ inquiries and as such we expect a high level of customer service and support at all times.
You will be asked to provide assistance to the Head of Ticketing and the Deputy Supporter Centre Manager with a variety of Ticket Office duties as required. A good level of communication will be required.
Responsibilities
The Ticketing and Membership Executive - Match Days must have:
- Customer Service experience - A good IT and technology systems understanding - Competent with all Microsoft Office Products - Ability to deal with the public with a focus on service excellence - Able to work under pressure - Ability to communicate with and relate to staff members at all levels - Work on own initiative / creative - Respect for confidentiality - Flexible attitude to work tasks - Genuine commitment to provide high quality customer service - Positive ‘can do’ approach - Desire to learn with enthusiasm
Ideally, The Ticketing and Membership Executive - Match Days will have:
- Previous experience with Ticketing Systems - Experience in a sales role
Requirements
The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role:
- Providing service excellence when dealing with the Clubs customers (supporters, staff etc), at all times, serving customers in a polite, courteous and professional manner - Handling any customer queries and complaints with a solution driven and positive approach - Processing all ticket sales for Fulham FC products, away games and supporter travel, with daily reconciliation of all sales through the Ticketmaster system - Updating and maintaining supporter database records to ensure data is accurately captured across the Ticketing System (Ticketmaster) and CRM System - This role will be required to work all home league matchdays – on evenings or weekends , and an other ad-hoc fixtures or events as required - You may also be required to carry out other tasks, not listed, to assist in the efficient operation of our business. At all times you will be required to act in accordance with FFC company policies, follow departmental procedures and maintain the highest level of confidentiality.

job.title: Kitchen Porter
job.html_description:
To assist the Kitchen and Front of house staff in cleaning of all kitchen items, equipment,
machinery, floors and surfaces. Maintaining high standards and safe practices in all areas.
This is generally a Monday to Friday role however some flexibility with working hours will be
required on occasions and there is opportunity to gain basic kitchen skills.

Responsibilities

  • Manning the Pot Wash area
  • Cleaning numerous kitchen items used by the kitchen and also front of house
  • To maintain a very high standard of kitchen/food hygiene at all times
  • To maintain a high standard of personal hygiene at all times
  • The cleaning of the kitchen area including machinery, storerooms
  • Putting away incoming goods in storage areas and maintaining these areas

Requirements

Essential

  • 3 months in a similar role
  • Good command of the English language
  • Basic Food Hygiene
  • Able to work at a quick pace
  • Likes to get stuck in
  • Good team Player

job.title: Part-Time Graphic Designer
job.plain_text_description:
Fulham FC Foundation is seeking a creative part-time Graphic Designer to support the communications of Fulham FC’s official charity. This role will work across a wide range of digital and print design projects, helping to bring the Foundation’s visual identity to life across all programmes and activities.
Working within established brand guidelines, you will contribute to digital campaigns and front‑end design projects, while also bringing your own creativity and ideas to each brief. Design direction will be provided, but the ability to work independently will be key to success in this role.
If you are passionate about design, looking for a new challenge, and feel you can add value to our team, please visit hhtp://careers.fulhamfc.com/
*This job description should not be regarded as exclusive or exhaustive. It is intended as an outline indication of areas of activity and responsibility and will be amended in the light of the changing needs of the organisation
.
Closing date for applications is Wednesday 5 August 2026.
Salary range: £20k-£22.5k
First stage interviews will take place across Thursday 13th & Friday 14th August 2026, and second stage interviews will take place on Thursday 20th August 2026. Please ensure you are available to interview on these dates prior to applying.
The post is subject to an enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service check.
Fulham Football Club Foundation is committed to increasing diversity and maintaining an inclusive workplace culture. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of ethnicity, race, gender, religious belief, sexual orientation, age, marital status, or disability.
Applicants must have the right to live and work in the United Kingdom and be able to provide appropriate documentation for the duration of their employment.
Responsibilities
Your responsibilities will include, but are not limited to:
• Designing and building digital assets, including social media graphics, flyers, presentations, and newsletters
• Implementing design briefs in line with the Foundation’s brand guidelines
• Supporting and contributing to the development of the Foundation’s visual identity across multiple platforms and mediums
• Managing multiple design briefs from concept through to final delivery
• Working to tight deadlines while following efficient resourcing and scheduling processes
• Supporting social media scheduling and posting across Foundation channels
• Assisting with asset management and the printing of marketing collateral
Requirements
The successful candidate will:
• Be able to manage multiple briefs and work under pressure to tight deadlines
• Demonstrate a strong portfolio showcasing a range of print and digital design work for varied audiences
• Have advanced working knowledge of Adobe Creative Suite, including Photoshop, Illustrator, and InDesign, as well as PowerPoint
• Hold a design‑related degree, or demonstrate equivalent experience through a strong portfolio
• Ideally have experience working within sport, community, or charitable organisations
Personal attributes
We are looking for a team player who is:
• Open to feedback and able to collaborate effectively
• Highly organised with strong attention to detail
• Able to problem‑solve independently
• Capable of blending creative vision with strategic thinking
• Values in-person collaboration and team culture (the role is office based)

job.title: Redhill Advanced Centre Head Coach
job.html_description:
This role involves leading the running and ongoing development of the Redhill Advanced Centre for the Pre‑Academy on Thursdays at Donyngs Leisure Centre, ensuring the programme operates to the highest standards. Responsibilities include managing all session registers, databases, and safeguarding requirements; delivering clear and consistent communication and development feedback to players and parents, including conducting retain and release conversations; and overseeing the coaching provision and day‑to‑day organisation of the centre, such as staffing, equipment management, and liaising with the facility—particularly around school holiday schedules—to ensure smooth and effective delivery.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

  • To safeguard all personel when the centre is running and correctly dismiss and welcome the children upon arrival
  • To ensure the high quality co-ordination and smooth operational running of the centre
  • To ensure the deployment of appropriate football & sports coaches onto sessions.
  • To colloborate effectively with the Pre Academy manager and Recruitment manager to ensure a safe and fun programme for our young players.
  • When required, to effectively support the running and development of the clubs Pre Academy at Motspur Park.
  • To colloborate effectively with the wider academy team, driving high standards, sharing best practice and establishing strong opportunites for participant transitions between programmes. 
  • To identify talent and organise coach delivery at the centre.
  • Attend internal and external meetings as appropriate.
  • To manage registers, databases and spectators at the venue and provide feedback to parents when appropriate. 

Requirements

Essential experience:
  • Working in a sports/participation based environment
  • Experience of managing projects
  • Coaching football to young people, specifically ages 5 to 10.
  • Experience working as an individual as well as part of a team.
  • Experience working with microsoft teams

Essential qualifications:
  • FA First Aid qualificaiton
  • FA Safeguarding qualification
  • IT Literate - proficiency in Microsoft Office; including Word, Excel and PowerPoint.
  • Excellent time management, organisational and planning skills. 
  • Knowledge of coaching standards and best practice
  • Strong communication skills
  • An understanding of and commitment to safeguarding and equality, diversity and inclusion within the workplace
  • UEFA C Licence

- Working in a sports/participation based environment - Experience of managing projects - Coaching football to young people, specifically ages 5 to 10. - Experience working as an individual as well as part of a team. - Experience working with microsoft teams
Essential qualifications:
- FA First Aid qualificaiton - FA Safeguarding qualification - IT Literate - proficiency in Microsoft Office; including Word, Excel and PowerPoint. - Excellent time management, organisational and planning skills. - Knowledge of coaching standards and best practice - Strong communication skills - An understanding of and commitment to safeguarding and equality, diversity and inclusion within the workplace - UEFA C Licence

job.title: Merchandiser
job.html_description:
Support the Head of Buying and Merchandising executing the retail strategy, with particular focus on maximising sales through range building with the Buyer, accurate record-keeping, analysis, planning, stock exit strategy and the adequate availability of product across all categories in all retail channels (physical stores, shop.fulhamfc.com and wholesale). 

Responsibilities

Enhancing sales through detailed analysis and increasing department efficiency by optimising inventory. Collaborating with internal stakeholders, suppliers, manufacturers, and stores to ensure the proper execution of sales plans. Collaborating with the Marketing team, creating and organising promotions and advertising campaigns, to enhance the customer journey and reduce inventory. Work with the Store Manager to enhance store layouts and customer experience, as well as visual merchandising of the e-commerce platforms. 

The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role: 
 
  • Work with the Buyer to build relevant ranges across all product categories, including providing administrative support as required.
  • Develop a deep understanding of the Fulham FC Buying calendar, critical path and supplier quantity deadlines. 
  • Maintain supplier relationships and support the buyer in price negotiations
  • Prepare weekly and ad hoc reports as required for circulation to relevant departments.
  • Support the Head of Buying and Merchandising with range reviews to build future product ranges.
  • Responsible for ordering and monitoring all personalisation to ensure optimum stock levels as required throughout the season.
  • Analysing sales reports and actioning plans directed by the Head of Buying and Merchandising for best sellers, worst sellers, and negative stock levels.
  • Sales reporting by store space and linear for effective space utilisation, category performance, strategic planning and decision making as well as inventory optimisation.  
  • Monitoring stock levels for online & stadium store, highlighting any imbalances and re-order requirements to the Head of Buying and Merchandising. 
  • Maintenance of the E-commerce platform and back-office stock management systems
  • Responsible for tracking delivery of all stock and communicate any changes to Retail Team.
  • Liaise with the Warehouse Manager regarding any stock queries and necessary purchase order amendments.
  • Execute the stock movement strategy as directed by the Head of Buying and Merchandising and produce the delivery schedule for the Warehouse Manager
  • Update the continuity line cards to ensure optimum stock levels for match days and conducts rebuys based on the stock requirements
  • Forecast the WSSI reports, highlighting stock gaps to the Head of Buying and Merchandising and making suggestions to enhance sales and optimise stock levels
  • Action re-pricing and markdown changes.
  • Action the implementation of kit sponsors to the kit and training wear, liaising between the embellisher, Warehouse Manager and relevant departments.
  • Maintain the Match days report for completion by the store team
  • Collate wholesale orders and work with the Warehouse Manager to fulfil in a timely manner
  • Execute kit launches in line with the Strategy as directed by the Retail and Licensing Director
  • Develop an understanding of the analytics for the online store
  • Responsible for updating and circulating the Marketing calendar and providing suggestions for offers
  • Monitor and chase the deadlines on the Marketing Calendar to ensure timely delivery of digital assets to support in-store and online sales campaigns.

Requirements

Essential and Desirable 
 
The Merchandiser mus have/be: 
  • 2-3 years’ experience in a similar role
  • Educated to degree level
  • Excellence in commercial awareness.
  • Excellent data analysis skills and      confidence working with numbers.
  • Strong      I.T. skills in Excel, Word, PowerPoint and an aptitude for learning new      systems.
  • Thrives under pressure and meets tight deadlines with quality results.
  • Strong      understanding and demonstrable experience of relationship building.
  • Excellent interpersonal, communication, negotiation and decision-making skills.
  • An ability to work well and maintain focus, accuracy, and pace under      pressure.
  • Positive, enthusiastic thinker with an ability for practicalities.
  • Pro-active, energetic and eager to learn and develop.
  • Team player but able to work alone.
 
Ideally, the Merchandiser will have: 
  • Experience building relationships with external partners and suppliers.
  • E-commerce experience and space planning.
  • An active interest in retail sports and the football industry
  • An understanding of cultural differences and how this can impact working relationships.
  • An interest in globalisation, and how events in another part of the world can affect how business is carried out.
  • Experience of working in a fast-paced retail environment.

job.title: Casual Football & Sports Coach
job.html_description:
Fulham FC Foundation are currently seeking to recruit an enthusiastic and committed person to take up the casual position of Football & Sports Coach.

Reporting directly to our Operations Officers the Football & Sports Coaches are responsible for delivering high-quality football & sports coaching to children, young people and adults. This role involves delivery across a wide-range of FFCF programmes, including Skills Club, Development Centres, Soccer Schools, Primary Education, Kicks, Health & Wellbeing and DisAbility. This is a casual role where flexibility is essential, as the role necessitates involvement in supporting the delivery of programme activities as and when required.  

The successful candidate will be somebody who has prior experience delivering football & sports coaching sessions to children & young people in grassroots & school settings, holds at least a FA Level 1/Introduction to Coaching Football qualification and is committed to the charitable purpose of Fulham FC Foundation. 

As this role is casual, there is no set timetable. Sessions may be offered on an adhoc or regular basis, depending on the needs of Fulham FC Foundation. It is the responsibility of the Football & Sports Coaches to maintain consistent and effective communication with our Operations Officers in ensure the continuation of work. 

Whilst there is no obligation for Fulham FC Foundation to provide, or for the Football & Sports Coaches to accept, work. Football & Sports Coaches who do not work for an extended period of time, will be deactivated. 

The successful candidates should be available to work across at least one of the below times, in order to receive work. 

Weekday Afternoons –3-5 pm;
Weekday Evenings - 5-9pm 
Weekends – Saturdays – 8am to 12noon. 
Work in other timings is available, however on a far reduced frequency. 
Salary Range: £12.95 p/h to £16.50 p/h

Next steps: 
There is no closing date for this role. Applications will be reviewed on a bi-weekly basis.

If successful, you will join a vibrant team who are passionate about achieving the organisation's vision of “an active, inclusive and thriving community”, and you’ll have the opportunity to build on your existing skills through a variety of training and development opportunities and experiences.

*This job description should not be regarded as exclusive or exhaustive. It is intended as an outline indication of areas of activity and responsibility and will be amended in the light of the changing needs of the organisation.


Responsibilities

  • To act as an Ambassador for Fulham FC Foundation (FFCF), representing the organisation professionally in line with its values. 
  • To plan and deliver high-quality football and sports coaching sessions to children, young people & adults in-line with FFCF standards.
  • To provide a safe, positive and inclusive learning environment at all times,
  • To adhere to FFCF’s safeguarding policies and procedures, ensuring the timely reporting of any safeguarding concerns or incidents within sessions. 
  • To engage, include, inspire and grow participation across FFCF activities.
  • To support the transition of participants between different FFCF programmes and activities.
  • To champion opportunities for individuals from underrepresented groups within FFCF activities. 
  • To identify and support progression pathways for talented young people. 
  • To support the development of FFCF programmes through positive collaboration with Operations Officers. 
  • To support the promotion of FFCF programmes across the local community. 
  • To assist in data collection and completion of monitoring & evaluation on an ongoing basis.
     
     
     
     
     

Requirements

Essential: 

Experience: 
•  Delivery of football coaching sessions in a grassroots environment
•  Delivery of sports coaching sessions to children & young people.
•  Delivery within a primary school.
•  Working in a customer-facing role.

Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:
•  Introduction to Coaching Football qualification
•  In-date FA Emergency First Aid 
•  In-date FA Safeguarding Children certificate
•  Base knowledge of and willingness to learn & contribute towards FFCF strategic aims. 

Personal Qualities:
•  A positive role-model for children & young people.
•  Ability to travel independently across South-West London, including transport of equipment.
•  Well presented, with a professional manner.
•  Positive, energetic persona. Able to build rapport with participants.
•  Excellent time-keeping & punctuality.
•  Willingness to learn & develop personally & professionally.

Experience: 
•  Delivery of football & sports coaching sessions in a Club Community Organisation (CCO).
•  Delivery of Physical Education within a primary school. 
•  Delivery within a classroom environment.
•  Marketing & Promoting participation activities in the community.
•  Delivering an activity to children & young people with a Disability.
•  Delivery of football & sports coaching sessions to females.

Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:
•  FA Level 2/UEFA C in Coaching Football qualification (or higher). 
•  Additional PE/HLTA qualification
•  Mentoring qualification
•  Non-football NGB sports qualifications.

job.title: Groundsperson
job.html_description:
Position Purpose

Work under the direction of line manager, to develop and maintain Motspur Park Football Pitch and surrounding landscapes. With the target to achieve the highest pitch parameters and maintenance standards expected by the Head Groundsperson, Squad Management and Directors of the Club.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities (below are example responsibilities but this is not intended as an exhaustive list)

  • Ensure that the Clubs Pitches, Landscapes + Surrounds are maintained to the highest standards. 

  • Follow all health and safety regulations.

  • Ensure that all machines, tools, equipment, and club properties are maintained appropriately.

  • Ensure that the Management Team are kept informed of progress with routine work and are notified of major issues as soon as they are identified. 

  • Keep line manager and staff informed of landscape & pitch works, job lists and communicate any issues to relevant parties.

  • Liaise with outside users/contractors (as required)

  • Ensure that all materials and resources are effectively and efficiently utilised to minimise waste.

  • Day to day operational duties will include but are not limited to physical activities and operating machinery including:

  • Mowing. (Hand and ride on machines) 

  • Driving tractors & utility vehicles 

  • Chemical spraying. (If qualified)

  • Hedge trimming and collection.

  • Frost covers and lighting rig deployment. 

  • Good attention to detail. 

  • Ability to work on their own.
job.plain_text_description:
Position Purpose
Work under the direction of line manager, to develop and maintain Motspur Park Football Pitch and surrounding landscapes. With the target to achieve the highest pitch parameters and maintenance standards expected by the Head Groundsperson, Squad Management and Directors of the Club.
Responsibilities
Responsibilities (below are example responsibilities but this is not intended as an exhaustive list)
- Ensure that the Clubs Pitches, Landscapes + Surrounds are maintained to the highest standards. - Follow all health and safety regulations. - Ensure that all machines, tools, equipment, and club properties are maintained appropriately. - Ensure that the Management Team are kept informed of progress with routine work and are notified of major issues as soon as they are identified. - Keep line manager and staff informed of landscape & pitch works, job lists and communicate any issues to relevant parties. - Liaise with outside users/contractors (as required) - Ensure that all materials and resources are effectively and efficiently utilised to minimise waste. - Day to day operational duties will include but are not limited to physical activities and operating machinery including: - Mowing. (Hand and ride on machines) - Driving tractors & utility vehicles - Chemical spraying. (If qualified) - Hedge trimming and collection. - Frost covers and lighting rig deployment. - Good attention to detail. - Ability to work on their own.

job.title: Groundsperson - Craven Cottage
job.html_description:
Work under the direction of line manager, to develop and maintain the Craven Cottage Football Pitch and surrounding landscapes. With the target to achieve the highest pitch parameters and maintenance standards expected by the Head of Grounds, Squad Management and Directors of the Club.
 
Responsibilities (below are example responsibilities but this is not intended as an exhaustive list)
 
  • Ensure that the Craven Cottage is maintained to the highest standard.
  • Follow all health and safety regulations.
  • Ensure that all machines, tools, equipment, and club properties are maintained appropriately.
  • Assist current team with daily procedures which are not limited to Mowing, line marking, aeration, fertilising, top dressing, seeding, pitch light movements.
  • As a Grounds Person you will, in the main, work alongside our experienced staff who will assist you with wide range of jobs associated with maintaining The Club’s grounds.
  • Assist on matchday preparations and post-match works on the pitch.
  • Assisting in end of season pitch renovations

Matchday / Weekend work
 
  • Prepare and repair pitch before and after games. 
  • General maintenance of the pitch, landscapes & equipment.
  • Watering and pitch light movements.
  • Upload pitch testing results to software platform and inform Head of Grounds of any damage, disease or areas of concern which may affect pitch standard.
  • Due to Premier league scheduling there will be potential early morning start times and late evening finishing times.

Requirements


Essential


Experience:

  • Minimum   4 years in the sports turf industry. 
  • Full UK Driving licence.
  • PA1, PA2 & PA6 spraying certificates
  • Level 2 Sports Turf Management or equivalent. 

Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:

  • Excellent communication skills   with the ability to communicate at all levels.  
  • Ability   to provide written and/or verbal progress reports to management. 
  • Machinery maintenance and setup of machinery.

Personal Qualities:

  • Physically fit
  • Ability to work unsupervised. 
  • Ability to think on your feet and use own initiative.
  • Flexible attitude to work tasks and hours.
  • Respect for confidentiality.
  • Genuine commitment to high quality   standards
  • Team worker 
  • Can do attitude. 

 
Desirable
 

Experience:

  • Football pitch maintenance.
  • Ability to identify and resolve problems.
  • Take responsibility of tasks.
  

Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:

  • Understanding of health and safety.
  • Tractor driving along with towing.
  • Safe use of pitch grow lighting units.

 

Personal Qualities:

  • Hard working
  • Works to a high standard 
  • Enjoys working as part of a team. 
  • Friendly 
  • Good problem solver
  • Keen eye for presentation

job.title: Women's u18s Assistant Coach
job.html_description:
To assist on all aspects of the U18's operational and coaching programme, supporting the Lead Pathway Coach. 

Responsibilities

  • To lead on the creation and delivery of a high-quality
  • Individual Development Programme for all WU18 players.  
  • To assist the planning and delivery of all training sessions and matches.  
  • To work closely with the FFCW Head Coach and MDT to ensure alignment to 1st team.  
  • To support the completion of all reports, monitoring and evaluation requirements to FFCW players and parents.
  • Frequently report and share progress of player development and potential pathway movement with the FFCW Head Coach.  
  • Liaise regularly with the Lead Pathway Coach on each individual plan and progress made towards targets.  
  • To support the high-quality coordination, operational running and delivery of the FFCW U18s age group, including all training session and  match day operations.  
  • Ensure each session is planned, delivered and reflected on in order to achieve weekly, monthly and season objectives.
  • Support the recruitment of new players into the U18s and support 1st Team recruitment to further improve the playing squad.  
  • Attend all staff and player meetings as directed by the FFCW Head Coach. 

Requirements

Essential
Experience: 
  • Experience of individual development coaching   
  • Experience of successful project management  
  • Experience of coaching with specific female football programmes, and/or  elite football programmes  
  • Experience of recruiting players within Women’s football pathway 

Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:  
  • Minimum FA Level 2 / UEFA C Licence  FA Level 1
  •  Introduction to First Aid in Football certificate (valid – within 3  years)  
  • FA Safeguarding Certificate (valid – within 3 years)   
  • Able to effectively vary communication styles/methods dependent on  the environment and audience  
  • Ability to manage time and tasks effectively within the working week  
  • Strong organisational skills in respect of planning and evaluation  
  • Strong presentation skills  
  • Knowledge of coaching syllabus’ and how to create one 
  • Able to lead by example and manage others  Knowledge of the Women’s Football Pathway

Personal Qualities: 
  • Able to work as part of team Flexible  
  • Enthusiastic   
  • Reliable   
  • Organised

Preferred
Experience
Worked in a Pro Game Academy environment

Skills, Knowledge & Qualifications:  
  • UEFA B Licence  
  • Knowledge of pathways to excellence
  • Knowledge and experience of managing amateur footballers

Personal Qualities: 
  • Be able to make decisions under pressure  
  • Deal with adversity  
  • Accept responsibility and accountability

job.title: Academy Education Post-16 Teacher (Maternity Cover)
job.html_description:
We are seeking a highly motivated and organised Teacher to join our team on a fixed-term basis covering maternity leave. This role is expected to last for approximately 8 months from November 2026.

The Post-16 Teacher will lead on the delivery of the Academy’s scholarship education programme, primarily teaching the BTEC Level 3 in Sporting Performance and Excellence, as well as the Sporting Excellence Professional (SEP) qualification. You will consistently deliver outstanding teaching and learning in your own practice and play a key role in driving high standards of teaching and learning across the department. You will be responsible for ensuring players make good progress towards their target grades in each qualification.
 
The successful candidate will play a key role within the pastoral team, supporting the holistic, off-pitch development of our players. Operating across phases, the role’s primary purpose is to add value to all aspects of our players’ educational programmes and to contribute towards maintaining the Academy’s Category 1 status.

Responsibilities

The Post-16 Teacher plays a central role in delivering and developing the Academy’s education programme, with a particular focus on the BTEC Level 3 in Sporting Performance and Excellence and the Sporting Excellence Professional (SEP) qualification. This role involves delivering consistently high-quality teaching and learning, tracking and supporting academic progress, and contributing to the holistic development of players through mentoring, pastoral support, and leadership of key initiatives. The successful candidate will also support wider departmental responsibilities, including destination tracking, school liaison, and compliance with EPPP and Premier League education standards, ensuring that the Academy continues to provide bespoke education pathways for its players.
 
The following is a non-exhaustive list of the responsibilities of the role: 
 
  • To help ensure the Academy Mission is achieved.
  • Deliver the BTEC Level 3 in Sporting Performance and Excellence and the Sporting Excellence Professional (SEP) qualification.
  • Plan and deliver high-quality, engaging lessons that drive outstanding teaching and learning, ensuring all learners make sustained progress.
  • Develop and maintain high-quality departmental resources, including schemes of work and assessment materials.
  • Monitor, track, and support the academic progress of all scholars, using data to inform timely interventions.
  • Create a positive, inclusive learning environment that promotes the highest standards of behaviour for learning amongst learners.
  • Take ownership of professional development and actively engage in relevant training and CPD opportunities.
  • Coordinate and conduct regular academic and pastoral progress reviews with players and their schools across phases.
  • Support the delivery of the Academy Life Plan, ensuring alignment with player development objectives.
  • To support the Youth Development Phase Compensatory Education Programme.
  • Attend relevant department, phase-specific, and league-led education meetings.
  • Support post-scholarship destination tracking and transitions.
  • Act as a liaison for school engagement, particularly with YDP players.
  • Coordinate or support YDP education projects and events.
  • Ensure compliance with EPPP, Premier League, and external quality assurance frameworks, including preparation for audits and visits.

Requirements

The Academy Education Post-16 Teacher must have: 
 
  • Educated to degree level.
  • PGCE with QTS or equivalent.
  • Experience of delivering a BTEC at KS5.
  • Experience of delivering KS3 and KS4 curriculum.
  • A commitment to plan and deliver high-quality, engaging lessons that drive outstanding teaching and learning, ensuring all learners make sustained progress.
  • Working knowledge of BTEC qualifications.
  • A commitment and experience of supporting young people holistically.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage effectively with players, parents, and colleagues.
 
Ideally Academy Education Post-16 Teacher will have: 
 
  • Experience working within an elite sporting or performance environment.
  • Knowledge of Academy football and the Elite Player Performance Plan (EPPP).
  • Experience of delivering an A-Level.
  • Experience delivering the Sporting Excellence Professional (SEP) apprenticeship.
  • PE based teaching experience. 
  • Experience in coordinating or leading education programmes.
  • Experience of working with external partners, including schools and awarding bodies.
job.plain_text_description:
We are seeking a highly motivated and organised Teacher to join our team on a fixed-term basis covering maternity leave. This role is expected to last for approximately 8 months from November 2026.
The Post-16 Teacher will lead on the delivery of the Academy’s scholarship education programme, primarily teaching the BTEC Level 3 in Sporting Performance and Excellence, as well as the Sporting Excellence Professional (SEP) qualification. You will consistently deliver outstanding teaching and learning in your own practice and play a key role in driving high standards of teaching and learning across the department. You will be responsible for ensuring players make good progress towards their target grades in each qualification.
The successful candidate will play a key role within the pastoral team, supporting the holistic, off-pitch development of our players. Operating across phases, the role’s primary purpose is to add value to all aspects of our players’ educational programmes and to contribute towards maintaining the Academy’s Category 1 status.

job.title: Sessional Coach (Goalkeeping specific)
job.html_description:
To deliver high quality football coaching sessions to accomplished & aspiring grassroots players aged 6 to 16.

To deliver football & sports sessions across the wider Foundation to a range of children, young people and adults.

The successful candidate will have availability on one or more of the following days:
Mondays 7.30pm to 9pm 
Wednesdays - 7.30pm to 9pm
Fridays - 5pm to 9pm


Responsibilities

To act as an Ambassador for FFCF and maintain and develop relationships to ensure high quality experiences for young people, partner organisations, other staff and customers

To effectively plan and deliver high quality football sessions in-line with coaching syllabus, philosophy and values.

To effectively support the development of individual players through the creation of individual development plans throughout the season.

To identify and support the development of progression pathways for talented young people

To effectively plan and deliver extra-curricular and community activities across the wider Foundation.

To support the transition between school-based activities, wider Foundation activities and where appropriate, other community activities

Assist in data collection and completion of monitoring for reports on an ongoing basis, within the timescales required

To work closely with Football Development Officer's to understand programme standards and attendance targets and support the promotion of FFCF activities

To support the smooth function of the Operations Department in line with the Foundation values and targets and work across programmes, when required

Requirements

Essential:

Experience
Leading goalkeeper specific football sessions to age groups U7 to U16
Delivery of extracurricular and community activities to all age groups


Skills and Qualifications:
Minimum of FA Level 1 in Coaching Goalkeepers, National Goalkeeping Course or equivalent experience
FA First Aid - in date
FA Safeguarding - in date
Ability to drive, has access to a car or ability to travel independently. 

Personal Qualities
Committed and motivated by the purpose of the Foundation 
Demonstrates respect for colleagues, partners and customers and when possible operates above and beyond expectations
Well presented with a good manner
Excellent punctuality
Ability to prioritise work and consistently meet deadlines
Effective communicator

Preferable:
Experience
Delivery in a Club Community Organisation (CCO)
Delivery of activity to young people with a Disability. 
Delivery of CPD events to coaches.
Delivery of Physical Education lessons to primary age children

Skills and Qualifications
FA Level 2 in Coaching Goalkeepers or UEFA Goalkeeping B Diploma
Non-football NGB sports qualifications
Mentoring qualification
Talent Identification qualification
Understanding of Physical Education standards in schools (Ofsted guidance)

job.title: Night Security Officer
job.html_description:
• To open and secure the premises at the correct times and in the correct manner.

• To be fully conversant with the intruder and fire alarm systems and to report any problems that may arise with these systems.

• To carry out regular & varied patrols of the grounds to deter theft, anti-social behaviour and to detect fire.

• To record all incidents and tasks undertaken during every shift. These entries will be made in the security Daily Occurrence Book.

• To report any matter having a bearing on the safety of the club and its staff and visitors.

• To inform the Security Manager of any incident this may involve dishonesty or when Company rules and regulations have been broken. 

• To carry out investigation as directed by the Security Manager involving cash, stock and property of the club and the personal effects of the staff.

• To conduct bag and vehicle searches on staff and contractors as directed by the Security Manager.

• To keep, the confidentiality of any matter concerning the club and Security department.

• To maintain a good working relationship with the staff and managers and encourage a high standard of Security.

• To maintain a smart appearance and to act in a courteous and professional manner when dealing with members of the public and staff.

• To issue keys from the security office and to record the issue and receiving of those keys in the appropriate register, reporting any missing keys or damaged locks.

• To record lost property in the lost property, register and to update the register when required.

• To ensure that all visitors are recorded in the Visitors Register, for the purpose of maintaining accurate records.

• To be fully conversant with the operation of CCTV, Access Control and Two way radio systems used by the Club.

• To be fully conversant with the Company rules and regulations, health and Safety at work and the Fire and Emergency procedures and ensure accurate logs of these are kept.

• To ensure accurate goods inwards records are kept.

• Other ad hoc duties as required by the business.


Requirements

Essential
  • Experience of working independently
  • Excellent attention to detail
  • One year’s previous security experience
  • SIA Door Supervisor Licence  
  • Full and clean drivers licence
  • Competent PC skills, word and excel
  • Flexible attitude to work tasks
  • Respect for confidentiality at all times
  • Genuine commitment to high quality client service
  • Honest and Reliable

Preferred
​​​​​​​​​• Working knowledge of Genetec Security Centre or Similar VMS & Security Software.
• Previous experience of working in an environment offering 1st class customer experience. 
• SIA CCTV Licence
• SIA Door Supervisor Licence
• ACT online 
• Customer Focussed
• Strong attention to detail

job.title: Employee belonging to a protected category
job.location: Viale della Liberazione, Milano, MI, 20124, IT
job.html_description:
Are you a dynamic, precise person interested in the world of sport, media & entertainment? Would you like to join an international club and work in a young and stimulating environment? FC Internazionale is constantly looking for professionals belonging to the Protected Categories (art 1 l.68/99) with whom to get in touch in order to evaluate together future collaboration possibilities for one of our departments, such as, for example:


  • Supply Chain and Procurement
  • Technology
  • E-commerce and Product Development
  • Marketing and CRM
  • Social Media & Digital
  • Ticketing & Contact Centre

In order to best align your interests and our needs, please specify function and area of potential interest when sending your application. Would you like to join our team? Then what are you waiting for, send us your CV! We look forward to getting to know you!


Those most in line may be evaluated for future needs to strengthen company structures.

job.title: Stadium Operations Crew
job.html_description:
Description

 Inter Miami CF is looking for Part-Time Operations Crew supporting the Facilities and Operations Department with event set-up, execution, teardown, changeovers and daily operational tasks. 


Essential Duties and Responsibilities: 

  • Participate in the execution of stadium and training facility events & games, including set-up and post-event breakdown. 
  • Assist with maintenance, upkeep, and organization around the facility. 
  • Maintain a safe work environment. 
  • Perform other related duties as assigned. 
  • Must be able to lift 50+lbs and walk for extended periods of time. 

Requirements

  • Past employment or internship experience in game operations or facility management preferred. 
  • Exceptional reliability, interpersonal, communication and organizational skills required. 
  • Possess strong attention to detail. 
  • Must be able to perform manual labor duties and tasks. 
  • Ability to work non-traditional hours, in non-traditional settings, within a team atmosphere. 

 

Other Details: 

  • Hours of work are based off our events schedule and may vary. 
  • Bilingual applicants not required, but preferred. 
  • All applicants must pass a pre-employment background check. 
  • Applicants must have permanent authorization to work in the U.S. at the time of application. Please note that we are unable to provide visa sponsorship now or in the future, so candidates must already possess work authorization that does not require employer sponsorship. 


  Compensation: 

  • Inter Miami CF offers a competitive compensation package, medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance and 401k retirement plan.

 Must have work authorization to work in the U.S. on a permanent and ongoing basis and must not require work visa sponsorship from Inter Miami CF now or in the future to retain authorization to work in the United States. 


All applicants must pass a pre-employment background check. 


Inter Miami CF is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by law

job.title: Ticket Office Representative
job.html_description:
Description

Join our Ticketing Operations Team as a Ticket Office Representative at Nu Stadium and help fans access the excitement of match day.


What You'll Do: 

  • Provide exceptional customer service to all guests at Inter Miami CF home matches and other events at Miami Freedom Park. 
  • Professionally and accurately sell tickets for all IMCF matches and events. 
  • Assist guests with mobile ticketing challenges and other ticketing related matters.
  • Greet all guests in a polite, professional and a friendly manner.
  • Proactively anticipate all guests’ specific concerns regarding their purchase, directions, stadium information and schedule of all events.
  • Assist with the distribution of tickets for guests and other stadium visitors.
  • Reconcile all daily sales without any overage/shortage occurring.
  • Work with other staff members to ensure a quality match/event day experience.
  • Available to work all Inter Miami CF home matches and other stadium events.
  • All other related duties as required

Requirements

  • Highly proactive, goal-oriented, and motivated with a positive attitude.
  • Customer service experience preferred.
  • Bilingual - English and Spanish a plus.
  • Knowledge of Ticketmaster and Archtics is a plus.
  • Demonstrated understanding of new technology and mobile apps.
  • Must possess reliable transportation to and from work for all games.
  • Prior customer service experience is a plus.
  • Ability to stand for 3-4 hours per time, hours at a time in various degrees of outdoor weather conditions, including but not limited to, extreme heat or rain.
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including but not limited to evenings, weekends and holidays.


All Applicants must pass a pre-employment background check. 


Inter Miami CF is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by law. 


Must have work authorization to work in the U.S. on a permanent and ongoing basis and must not require work visa sponsorship from Inter Miami CF now or in the future to retain authorization to work in the United States.

job.title: Guest Services Representative
job.html_description:
Description

As a member of Inter Miami’s Guest Services team, you will provide unique fan experiences while promoting a welcoming environment during all events hosted at our new home, Nu Stadium. Our focus is to ensure all fans have a safe and memorable experience by offering world-class customer service. We strive to make everyone feel welcome. As part of our guest services team, you will support us in ensuring every guest experiences the highest level of service and engagement at our venue. 


This is your opportunity to make a mark on matchdays, celebrate culture, and inspire fans across the globe. We are looking for highly enthusiastic and dedicated individuals to provide an exceptional service experience for all guests that visit our venues. This position will encompass the following roles: Ticket Taker, Usher, Premium Area Hosts, Gate Host, & Response Team. 


Essential Duties & Responsibilities 

  •  Maintain a positive mindset and strong people skills. 
  • Work well with others and function in a team atmosphere. 
  • Be customer-focused, proactive, and an empathetic problem solver 
  • Resolve guest complaints and conflicts in a timely and effective manner. 
  • Demonstrate a guest-service-oriented disposition and professional appearance. 
  • Attention to detail, adaptability, and the ability to resolve high-pressure situations.  
  • Ability to perform tasks in a stationary position for extended periods of time. 
  • Maintaining a presentable, clean, and safe environment for all guest-facing areas. 
  • Exhibit excellent communication, customer service, and conflict resolution skills 
  • Retain an extensive knowledge of Miami Freedom Park, policies, ticketing procedures, and emergency procedures. 
  • Additional related duties as assigned 

Position Summary 

This is a PART – TIME position with work hours based on the event scheduled at Miami Freedom Park and Chase Stadium. 

  • Location - Primarily at Miami Freedom Park (Miami), with occasional availability at IMCF Stadium (Fort Lauderdale)  
  • Average hours per shift: 5 to 6 hours per shift 
  • Average events per month – 2 to 4 (Subject to event schedule) 
  • Event Season - The event season typically runs from February through November.  

 

Applicants must have work authorization to work in the U.S. on a permanent and ongoing basis and must not require work visa sponsorship from Inter Miami CF now or in the future to retain authorization to work in the United States.


 Pre-Employment Requirements  

All applicants must pass a pre-employment background check. 


 Equal Opportunity Statement  

Inter Miami CF is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by law

Requirements

  • Must be able to work evenings and required to work weekends 
  • Must work ALL Inter Miami CF Home Matches 
  • Minimum high school diploma or equivalent. 
  •  Bilingual proficiency in English and Spanish. 
  •  Previous sports experience preferred but not required. 
  • Positive attitude and strong customer service and interpersonal skills. 
  • All applicants must pass a pre-employment background check.

job.title: Account Executive, Membership Sales
job.html_description:
Description

  

The Account Executive is responsible for generating new business by selling Inter Miami ticket inventory. It includes the selling of all specified seating categories through cold-calling, face-to-face appointments, and networking with a focus on business-to-business relationships.


Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Prospect, establish, research, and close sales leads through cold-calling, appointments, and networking.
  • Meet or exceed weekly/monthly sales and prospecting goals.
  • Work closely with Ticket Operations and Member Services departments to achieve sales/prospecting goals.
  • Maintain a high level of customer service.
  • Participate in various sales, team, and community events as assigned.
  • Represent the organization at various networking events.
  • Active role in the Inter Miami game day experience.
  • Maintain accurate records in support of sales efforts as defined by the organization.
  • All other responsibilities as required.

Requirements

  • Minimum 1-2 years of sales experience in professional or collegiate sports (or related industry) is preferred.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills required. Bilingual skills a plus.
  • Exceptional reliability, interpersonal, communication, and organizational skills required.
  • Exhibit good judgment and maintain a positive attitude.
  • Ability to work non-traditional hours, in non-traditional settings, within a team atmosphere.
  • Strong interest in soccer and sports is a plus.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) is preferred.
  • Knowledge of ticketing CRM (Salesforce and Archtics is preferred).
  • Experience in the Florida marketplace preferred but not necessary.

 

Compensation: Inter Miami CF offers a competitive compensation package, medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance and 401k retirement plan.


Applicants must have work authorization to work in the U.S. on a permanent and ongoing basis and must not require work visa sponsorship from Inter Miami CF now or in the future to retain authorization to work in the United States.


All applicants must pass a pre-employment background check. 


Inter Miami CF is an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by law

job.title: Groundskeeper (Inter Miami Training Facility)
job.location: 1350 NW 55 Street, Fort Lauderdale, FL, 33309, US
job.html_description:
Description

Inter Miami CF is looking for a passionate and dedicated Groundskeeper to join our Turf & Grounds team. In this full-time role, you’ll play a vital part in maintaining world-class playing surfaces and facilities that meet the highest professional standards. Working alongside the Director of Turf & Grounds, you’ll help ensure our players, fans, and guests experience excellence—right from the ground up. 


Essential Duties & Responsibilities: 

  • Assist with all turf management aspects including mowing, edging, chemical/fertilizer application, aerification, irrigation, pest management, etc.
  • Assist in equipment and irrigation repair and maintenance.
  • Assist with administrative duties such as correspondence, filing and research.
  • Execute other projects as assigned.


Requirements

  • Bachelor's or associate's Degree in a turf management-related field is preferred.
  • Ability or willingness to learn how to operate necessary equipment to complete required tasks.
  • Availability for home matches and other events throughout the season (primarily evenings and weekends).
  • Interest and experience in competitive sports is preferred.
  • Must possess strong organizational skills and high attention to detail.
  • Must possess a strong work ethic and good demeanor.

Physical Demands:

  • Ability to lift 50 pounds of weight frequently throughout the assigned workday.
  • Ability to work in various degrees of outdoor weather conditions, including but not limited to extreme heat or rain.
  • Ability to work in various positions, including but not limited to, stooping, standing, bending over, sitting, kneeling, and squatting for extended periods of time.

Compensation: 

  • Inter Miami CF offers a competitive compensation package, medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance and 401k retirement plan.

Applicants must have work authorization to work in the U.S. on a permanent and ongoing basis and must not require work visa sponsorship from Inter Miami CF now or in the future to retain authorization to work in the United States.

job.title: Coordinator, Stadium Operations
job.html_description:
Description

The Coordinator, Stadium Operations supports the execution of all stadium operations functions at Nu Stadium in Miami, ensuring events are delivered efficiently and to a high standard. This role plays a key part in coordinating event setups and breakdowns, maintaining operational readiness, and supporting a seamless experience for all events.

The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, hands-on, and able to thrive in a fast-paced environment, with the ability to support and guide part-time staff while maintaining strong operational standards.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Assist with the preparation, setup, and breakdown of equipment and materials for all events at Nu Stadium 
  • Maintain and track assigned inventory of event and operational equipment 
  • Support and help lead part-time stadium staff on both event and non-event days, including event conversions and operational tasks 
  • Serve as a setup lead for select events, ensuring timely and accurate execution 
  • Assist with general facility upkeep to maintain a clean, safe, and operational environment 
  • Support cross-functional teams as needed to ensure successful event delivery 
  • Perform additional duties and special projects as assigned

Requirements

  • Work flexible/varied hours as scheduled, including nights, weekends, overnights, and holidays. 
  • Bachelor's Degree in Sport Management/Administration or similar field.
  • Stadium/Event Operations experience is a plus.
  • Ability to interact with stakeholders professionally and confidently at all levels in the organization.
  • Must be a self-starter who is able to multi-task, be a quick learner, and be able to work in stressful environments independently and within the group.
  • Must possess strong organizational skills and high attention to detail.
  • Bilingual preferred (Spanish)

Physical Demands:

  • Must be able to move and/or lift to 50 pounds.
  • This position requires work inside and outside of the building and some exposure to adverse conditions.
  • Ability to work in various positions, including but not limited to, stooping, standing, bending over, sitting, kneeling, and squatting for extended periods of time. 

Applicants must have work authorization to work in the U.S. on a permanent and ongoing basis and must not require work visa sponsorship from Inter Miami CF now or in the future to retain authorization to work in the United States.
