job.title: Team Operations - Performance Scientist
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
NO PHONE CALLS OR EMAILS, PLEASE.  Thanks for your interest! Due to the volume of applications, we’re unable to respond to individual status inquiries. If selected to move forward, you’ll hear from our HR team at Nashville Soccer Club.

Position Title: Performance Scientist

Department: Team Operations- High Performance

Reports to: Director of High Performance

 

POSITION OVERVIEW

The Performance Scientist supports player availability and performance by leading sport science data operations, monitoring, reporting, and applied insights that inform training, recovery, and return-to-performance decisions. This role reports directly to the Director of High Performance and works in close collaboration to define the path forward for key initiatives. The Performance Scientist will be provided autonomy to determine the best execution plan, while ensuring alignment with coaching, performance, and medical priorities.

RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Own end-to-end sports science data operations (collection, QA/QC, documentation, and governance) across performance data streams.
  • Lead training and match monitoring systems (e.g., STS) and related technologies (GPS, heart rate, VALD, 1080 and others), ensuring reliable workflows and clean outputs.
  • Build and maintain dashboards, models, metrics, and reports that track workload, readiness, and physical performance across the season.
  • Deliver clear, actionable insights to coaches, performance/medical staff, and players (including real-time training feedback when needed).
  • Plan and execute performance testing and assessment protocols (field and gym-based); maintain benchmarks and individualized targets.
  • Collaborate with Athletic Development and Medical on activation, strength/regeneration, and end-stage return-to-performance support as needed.
  • Partner with analytics and scouting on performance questions tied to player evaluation, transactions, and projections.
  • Lead integration of second team and academy performance data into centralized systems, standards, and reporting.
  • Ensure compliance with all MLS medical manual requirements and club policies and procedures.

KEY DELIVERABLES

  • Readiness/availability updates aligned with training plans and performance/medical direction.
  • Stakeholder dashboards and reports with clear KPI definitions and refresh cadence.
  • Testing battery execution and reporting with benchmarks, targets, and action steps.
  • Multi-squad integration outputs (first team/second team/academy) using shared standards and longitudinal tracking.

MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

  • Master’s degree in Exercise Science or related field.
  • Certified Performance and Sport Scientist (CPSS).
  • Certified Strength and Conditioning Specialist (CSCS).
  • Minimum 2 years experience in an elite sport environment.
  • Demonstrated proficiency with GPS systems, heart rate, force plates, and current performance technologies.
  • Experience in a coach-facing role explaining monitoring outputs and real-time GPS results.
  • Proficiency in R, Python, and Power BI for data analysis and reporting.
  • Current CPR/AED certification (or ability to obtain promptly).

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

  • Experience querying cloud databases and supporting automated/real-time reporting (e.g., SQL, ETL, APIs).
  • Comfortable conversing in Spanish.
  • Experience scaling sport science systems across multiple squads (first team + second team + academy).
  • Strong data QA habits and reproducible workflows (e.g., documentation, version control).

We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Ticketing - Box Office Representative PT
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
POSITION OVERVIEW
The Ticket Office Representative is a part-time, event-day position responsible for providing exceptional customer service and assisting fans with ticket purchases and inquiries at GEODIS Park. This role plays an important part in delivering a seamless matchday experience by supporting ticket sales, processing transactions, and resolving ticket-related questions. Applicants must reside in the Nashville area.

RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Process ticket sales through Ticketmaster and assist guests with ticket purchases and ticketing issues.
  • Provide clear, friendly, and professional customer service throughout all interactions.
  • Address general inquiries related to seating options, pricing, stadium policies, and event information.
  • Accurately complete credit card and cash transactions in accordance with Club procedures.
  • Reconcile and balance cash drawers at the end of each shift.
  • Support Box Office leadership with additional duties as assigned.

QUALIFICATIONS
  • Prior customer service or ticketing experience preferred, but not required.
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to remain professional in a fast-paced environment.
  • Ability to learn and navigate ticketing software quickly (experience with Ticketmaster is a plus).
  • Reliable, punctual, and able to work Nashville SC home matches, special events, nights, weekends, and holidays as needed.
  • Must be detail-oriented and comfortable handling cash and sensitive customer information.
  • Candidates without a degree are encouraged to apply.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Mascot Handler
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
Nashville SC is looking for highly motivated individuals interested in being a part of the Major League Soccer team in Nashville. The ideal candidates will demonstrate initiative, the ability to multi-task, a willingness to learn and a positive attitude. Please note: This is a non-costumed, handler position.

Employment Type: Part-Time DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES:

· Assist the Nashville SC Mascot, Tempo the Coyote, in matchday operations.

· Help with match day activities such as participation as plant in skits, capturing content, serving as Tempo the Coyote's handler and working the Tempo the Coyote's Band Kids Club table.

· Assist in planning and execution of all things Tempo’s Band (Kids Club) including events, promotions, roster, etc.

· Capture content for mascot social media.

· Attend mascot appearances as a handler.

· Help create a fun and engaging environment for Tempo the Coyote fans.

· Please note this is not a costumed/performance position.

DESIRED QUALIFICATIONS:

· Knowledge and interest in sports.

· Knowledge and interest in mascots.

· Computer proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

· Proficient in social media.

· Strong communication skills and telephone etiquette.

 

REQUIREMENTS:

· Available as needed for Tempo the Coyote appearances.

· Available to assist with all Home Matches.

· The understanding that working in sports is different than being a fan. This position does not guarantee candidates will be able to watch games. Many valuable positions within the organization are behind the scenes, and they provide little ability to watch the games.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for

employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Backup Mascot
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
Nashville SC is looking for highly motivated individuals interested in being a part of the Major League Soccer team in Nashville. The ideal candidates will demonstrate initiative, the ability to multi-task, a willingness to learn and a positive attitude. Please note: This is a part-time, as needed Backup position.

Employment Type: Part-Time

 

DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES:

· Study and learn Tempo the Coyote's mannerisms and characteristics

· Assist Tempo the Coyote at home matches

· Provide support at select appearances, including birthday parties, parades, school visits, hospital visits, charitable events, and other events coordinated by Tempo the Coyote

· Assist in maintaining the condition of costumes and props

· Other duties as assigned by Tempo the Coyote

· Please note this is a part-time, as needed Backup position.

 

DESIRED QUALIFICATIONS:

· Knowledge and interest in sports.

· Knowledge and interest in mascots.

· Computer proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

· Proficient in social media.

· Strong communication skills and telephone etiquette.

· Mascot Experience (College or Professional)

 

REQUIREMENTS:

· Available as needed for Tempo the Coyote appearances.

· Available to assist with home matches.

· The understanding that working in sports is different than being a fan. This position does not guarantee candidates will be able to watch games. Many valuable positions within the organization are behind the scenes, and they provide little ability to watch the games.

· Tempo the Coyote Height Range: 5'7 - 5'10.

 

We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Coordinator, Ticket Operations
job.html_description:
NO PHONE CALLS OR EMAILS, PLEASE.  Thanks for your interest! Due to the volume of applications, we’re unable to respond to individual status inquiries. If selected to move forward, you’ll hear from our HR team at Nashville Soccer Club.

The Ticket Operations Coordinator position will provide assistance to the Ticket Sales Department for Nashville SC. Primary responsibilities include but are not limited to event creation and financial reporting, providing a high level of customer service, possessing high knowledge of all ticketing packages and benefits as well as driving season ticket sales, renewals, and group sales for ticketed events.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Assist in the processing and establishment of season ticket, group and single match ticket accounts
  • Provide game-day support in the area of ticket resolution, customer service and will call
  • Build, maintain and develop a positive working relationship with all Ticketmaster Client Support Staff
  • Facilitate a complex network of payment plans, investigate and respond to any chargebacks related to ticket payments and work closely with the Ticket Services department to ensure account compliance
  • Assist with the building of price codes, plans, promo codes, and other Archtics programming needs
  • Maintain a positive, high performance culture in the Ticket Operations department, while building a positive rapport with all other departments daily
  • Assist in the execution of Club events, including but not limited to games, Season Ticket Member functions, Club events, etc.
  • Assist in the creation of all online sales process, including but not limited to single game, group, and season ticket sales
  • Act as a higher point of contact for all elevated customer service occurrences on match day
  • Assist with Will Call responsibilities and ticket troubleshooting needs on match day
  • Other duties assigned by the Director of Ticket Operations or other Ticketing Leadership

 

QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Sports Management, Marketing or related field
  • Minimum of 1 - 2 years of related experience
  • Ticket Operations background
  • Strong interpersonal and customer service skills, team orientation and the ability to communicate effectively at all levels
  • Ability to operate Archtics Ticketing System & Salesforce CRM
  • High level of demonstrated professionalism and integrity
  • Ability to problem solve
  • Exceptional attention to detail and strong analytical skills
  • Ability to work flexible hours including daytime, evening, weekends, and holidays
  • Strong work ethic, team oriented attitude, and enthusiastic about contributing  to the success of the department and Club in a fast paced environment
  • Bilingual (Spanish) speaker is a plus, but not required 

We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Safety & Security - Venue Security Officer PT
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
NO PHONE CALLS OR EMAILS, PLEASE.  Thanks for your interest! Due to the volume of applications, we’re unable to respond to individual status inquiries. If selected to move forward, you’ll hear from our HR team at Nashville Soccer Club.

POSITION OVERVIEW:
The Venue Security Officer will provide prevention activities and contribute to the building safety and security by monitoring and reporting physical security and safety conditions.

RESPONSIBILITIES:
  • Assist with controlling access to the Stadium including registering and directing visitors.  Visually inspect the ID card of each person entering the building and verify that the individual and the cardholder are the same.
  • Help with writing reports on all accidents or potential problems that shall occur, as well as notifying the Security management team.
  • Issue temporary access badges to authorized personnel as required by procedures.
  • Escort visitors and other personnel as required by procedures.
  • Inspect packages and other designated items as required (including assignment as a searcher).
  • Conduct foot patrol and Stadium security checks.
  • Visually inspect and monitor designated post area(s).
  • Assist with monitoring performance of security/safety equipment.
  • Investigate and report any unusual or unauthorized activity within the area.
  • Verify credentials to control access in restricted areas.
  • Assist with inspecting bags and purses at all entry points for prohibited items, as well as operate a walk-through metal detector (WTMD) or Evolv machine. 
  • Be observant for fires and suspicious packages.
  • Be observant for spills and alert to odors and unusual sounds.
  • Check and report condition of area, exit, and emergency lighting.
  • Check external access to vital areas of the facility.
  • Monitor access through designated doors as required.
  • In the event of alarm conditions, assist in the safe and orderly evacuation of facility personnel if warranted.
  • Respond to local audible alarms on HVAC and electrical equipment.  Report and silence alarms as instructed by procedures and Facility Operations personnel during after-hours operations.
  • Identify and turn-off electrical appliances such as space heaters, fans, coffee pots, etc., that might be left on after normal work hours and could pose a source of heat and ignition for fire.
  • Be observant for trespassers and unauthorized persons.
  • Inspect designated areas for unauthorized activity.
  • Operate and monitor CCTV and access control systems where required.
  • Operate computer-based security/safety-related software and applications as required.
  • Assist with controlling access to the Stadium utilizing access control system.
  • Complete required testing of security and fire systems-related equipment as required by security procedures.
  • Operate and monitor alarm systems where required.
  • Initiate appropriate response as required for various alarms.
  • Initiate and maintain 24/7 communication with the Security management team. 
  • Dispatch other Security Force personnel and/or check alarms as required.
  • Additional duties as assigned. 
 
QUALIFICATIONS:
  • Minimum of a high school education or have passed a General Educational Development (GED) equivalency test.
  • Ability to be trained to operate a golf cart, magnetometer wand, and walk-through metal detector and Evolv machine.
  • Able to obtain a State of Tennessee Security Guard License.  
  • Previous security, law enforcement and/or military experience strongly preferred.
  • Effective verbal and written communications skills.
  • Ability to exercise sound judgment and remain calm during times of stress.
  • Ability to lift, pull, push and/or carry 25-60 lbs. as necessary.
  • Ability to work in outdoor weather conditions to include extreme heat, cold and rain.
  • Ability to work a flexible schedule, including but not limited to days, nights, weekends and holidays.

We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Youth Soccer Coach - Part Time
job.html_description:
Position Summary
Nashville Soccer Club is seeking energetic, reliable, and knowledgeable coaches to support the delivery of its Youth Soccer Programs. This role focuses on camps, clinics, grassroots programming, Academy pathway programs and coach education initiatives across the Nashville community. The role is centered on player development programming within the Youth Soccer Department. Coaches will represent Nashville SC while delivering high-quality soccer experiences that are fun, inclusive, and developmentally appropriate. This position is part-time, with flexible scheduling based on program needs, including evenings, weekends, and seasonal peak periods. Strong performers may be considered for expanded responsibilities or future full-time opportunities within the club. This is not an academy coaching position.

Duties & Responsibilities
  • Deliver engaging and age-appropriate coaching sessions at camps, clinics, and grassroots programs.
  • Implement Nashville SC Youth Soccer curriculum and session plans across all youth programming.
  • Create a positive, safe, and inclusive environment for all participants.
  • Support event operations including setup, check-in, and breakdown of sessions.
  • Serve as a club ambassador, maintaining professionalism with players, parents, and partners.
  • Assist with coaching education sessions and mentorship initiatives when required.
  • Provide feedback on player development and program delivery when requested.
  • Ensure all safety, risk management, and safeguarding standards are followed.
Qualifications
  • Previous coaching experience in youth soccer (recreational, grassroots, or club level required).
  • Passion for working with children and developing players of all ability levels.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Ability to work flexible hours including evenings and weekends.
  • Reliable transportation to program locations across the Greater Nashville area.
  • US Soccer Grassroots (or equivalent) License or higher (or willingness to obtain).
  • Experience working in a youth development environment (Rec, Club, Academy or camps).
  • Background in player development models.
  • Ability to be active for extended periods (standing, running, demonstrating drills)
  • Ability to lift and carry equipment up to 30 lbs

job.title: Event Operations - Event Operations Associate PT
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
NO PHONE CALLS OR EMAILS, PLEASE.  Thanks for your interest! Due to the volume of applications, we’re unable to respond to individual status inquiries. If selected to move forward, you’ll hear from our HR team at Nashville Soccer Club.

Department:  Event Operations
Reports to:  Director, Event Operations

ABOUT NASHVILLE SC:
Nashville is America’s “It City” and one of the fastest-growing soccer hubs in the country. From hosting the CONCACAF Gold Cup to FIFA World Cup Qualifiers and the FIFA Club World Cup, Music City has quickly become a destination for some of the sport’s most prominent events. Nashville SC has the largest soccer-specific stadium in Major League Soccer and in 2025 made Tennessee state history by becoming its first professional sports team champion! 
Position Summary:
Nashville Soccer Club is currently seeking an Event Operations part-time associate. This position will support the GEODIS Park Event Operations team in the planning and execution of Major League Soccer matches, as well as other major events, while also providing day-to-day support to the club's operations.  
Duties & Responsibilities:
  • Assist the Event Operations department on the planning and execution of Major League Soccer games, third-party ancillary events, and other major events.
  • Ensure Club and Stadium standard operating procedures are compliant with Major League Soccer’s operational regulations.
  • During Major League Soccer events:
  1. Ensure locker room set-ups meet MLS standardization requirements.
  2. Assist in servicing the needs of visiting teams and league officials on game day.
  3. Provide support for the referee crew before, during and after the game.
  4. Assist with match credential production and distribution.
  5. Lead, brief, and oversee ball retrievers during the event.
  6. Assist various departments with match day responsibilities.
  • During third-party/major events:
  1. Assist in ensuring that client needs, and expectations are met.
  2. Work with the Event Management Team in planning operational details of the event.
  3. Help with the movement and storage of the department’s equipment.
  4. Setting event day spaces to meet needs of various departments.
  • Other duties as assigned.
DESIRED QUALIFICATIONS:
  • Knowledge and interest in sports and entertainment.
  • Computer proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
  • Strong communication skills and professional etiquette.
  • Strong worth ethic with a positive, team-oriented approach.
REQUIREMENTS
  • Available 20-25 hours per week, including nights and weekends and often under extreme weather conditions. A typical game day can require early hours and late nights amounting to a 12 hour event day commitment.
  • Ability to lift and carry at least 50 pounds.

We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Soccer Squad (Part-Time)
job.html_description:
Position Summary:
Nashville SC Soccer Squad will serve as community ambassadors for the Club at key community-based events and programs throughout Nashville. This group will be integral to executing community outreach through soccer programming. The Soccer Squad will also serve as an extension of the Community Engagement department by assisting with our core youth soccer programs including Grassroots Soccer, IMPACT Soccer, MNPS P.L.A.Y.S., and more.
Essential Duties & Responsibilities:
  • Facilitate soccer-specific activations at community engagement events while providing superb customer service and displaying excitement about the organization.
  • Act as an extension of the club’s community engagement department by providing information on various soccer programs.
  • Assist with program set-up and breakdown. 
  • Collect and report valuable feedback and insights after each event to provide back to the team supervisors.
Shift Information:
  • Must be able to work flexible hours including evenings, weekends, and holidays.
  • Some shifts will require picking up materials to transport to the event site, plus setting up and taking down materials.
Qualifications:
  • A general interest and working knowledge of the sport of soccer. 
  • Should be able to answer basic questions about the game, NSC’s schedule and roster, etc.
  • Experience playing the game and coaching youth is preferred but not required.
  • Strong people and communication skills. 
  • Comfortable promoting and interacting with new people at NSC events with a fun and positive attitude.
  • Collaborative personality who works well with others on their team. 
  • Enjoys engaging with team members and is responsive to messages.
  • Must have transportation, a good driving record, and valid driver’s license.
  • Reliable and punctual, being on-time is important to you.
  • Bilingual (English & Spanish) is helpful but not required.
  • Must be able to move and lift a minimum of 50 pounds.
  • Background check is required.

job.title: Academy- Goalkeeper Coach PT
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
NO PHONE CALLS OR EMAILS, PLEASE.  Thanks for your interest! Due to the volume of applications, we’re unable to respond to individual status inquiries. If selected to move forward, you’ll hear from our HR team at Nashville Soccer Club.

POSITION OVERVIEW:
The Academy Part-Time Goalkeeper Coach will be responsible for the U14 and U15 academy goalkeepers. The goalkeeper coach will be tasked with constructing and executing individualized goalkeeper sessions as well as assisting head coaches on the field where necessary. This person will work closely with the Academy Goalkeeper coach, as well as academy staff and must adhere to all club and league requirements. The ideal candidate must have previous experience in the role of goalkeeper both as a player and coach.

RESPONSIBILITIES:
• Be able to work roughly 20 hours per week, mainly afternoon sessions with our younger age groups- U14 and U15 boys.
• Construct and execute individualized goalkeeper sessions geared towards our style of play and model
• Assist with Head coach needs during a training session
• Actively participate in player individualized meetings and communicate with academy goalkeepers
• Availability to attend home matches on the weekend on a need basis
• Complete Club and League registration requirements including background checks, SafeSport, Heads up.
QUALIFICATIONS:
• Playing and coaching experience in the Goalkeeper position
• Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail.
• Strong ability to communicate and work under pressure.
• Passion for youth soccer and knowledge of MLS
• Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience preferred.
• Ability to speak Spanish is a plus.


We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Director of Safety & Security
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
POSITION OVERVIEW:

The Director of Safety & Security is a dynamic position responsible for the oversight of critical infrastructure of Nashville Soccer Club, including personnel, multiple facilities, and both domestic and international events. The Director of Safety & Security will work closely with club staff, law enforcement, and league personnel on matters of safety and security for all stakeholders. The Director of Security will have oversight of venue safety and security procedures, contract security, access control, and physical security systems.

 

The Director of Safety & Security will have oversight over security planning and operations of GEODIS Park and Vanderbilt Health Training Center.

 

Essential Duties & Responsibilities: 

  • Venue Security
    • Oversight of all digital camera systems and record retention
    • Management of 24/7 internal security staff, including Manager/Coordinator and policies/procedures.
    • Creation and oversight of venue safety plans, emergency action plans, and event action plans.
    • Conduct and oversee training and tabletop emergency exercises for building, company, and events.
    • Develop building access plans and systems.
  • Event Security
    • Establish positive and collaborative relationships with Police, Fire, EMS, and other governmental agencies and schedule services as needed.
    • Creation of Event plans, staffing plans and dot maps.
    • Contract administrator of Event Staff security personnel.
    • Oversight of special event security planning, staffing and operations for all events hosted by the club.
    • Point of contact for contract and HR functions of special duty personnel, contract security, security vendors, and other governmental agency personnel.
    • Manage match day equipment including touchless screening equipment.
    • Work with Event services staff to address concerns and for policy enforcement.
    • Fan policies enforcement for all events including registered supporter groups.
    • Execute action plans for any inclement weather during matches and major events.
  • Overall management, administration, and assessment of security operations.
  • Other security functions as assigned.

 

Required Experience and Qualifications:

  • 3-5 years working in sports & entertainment or large venues in a security function.
  • Bachelor’s degree in law enforcement, Sport/Event Management, Law, or Equivalent combination of education and experience.
  • Experience with event technology, incident reporting, and other physical security systems.
  • Strong written and oral communication, interview, and collaboration experience.

 

Preferred Experience and Qualifications: 

  • 5-10 years working in sports & entertainment venues in a security function.
  • Law enforcement contacts on the federal, state, and local level.
  • Command of Large-scale special event coordination with security and local law enforcement.
  • Advanced knowledge of event logistics, planning, budgeting, and implementation
  • Prior knowledge and experience with Safety act certification.
  • Multi-lingual or Bi-lingual English and Spanish.
  • Professional certifications (CSSP, AVSS, CPP, etc.) in Security

 

Work environment: 

  • Flexible work schedule inclusive of nights, weekends, and holidays required.
  • Bending, lifting, and repeated movements including lifting 15-20 pounds.
  • Indoor and outdoor events in all weather conditions

The Director of Safety & Security will have oversight over security planning and operations of GEODIS Park and Vanderbilt Health Training Center.
Essential Duties & Responsibilities:
- Venue Security Oversight of all digital camera systems and record retention Management of 24/7 internal security staff, including Manager/Coordinator and policies/procedures. Creation and oversight of venue safety plans, emergency action plans, and event action plans. Conduct and oversee training and tabletop emergency exercises for building, company, and events. Develop building access plans and systems. - Event Security Establish positive and collaborative relationships with Police, Fire, EMS, and other governmental agencies and schedule services as needed. Creation of Event plans, staffing plans and dot maps. Contract administrator of Event Staff security personnel. Oversight of special event security planning, staffing and operations for all events hosted by the club. Point of contact for contract and HR functions of special duty personnel, contract security, security vendors, and other governmental agency personnel. Manage match day equipment including touchless screening equipment. Work with Event services staff to address concerns and for policy enforcement. Fan policies enforcement for all events including registered supporter groups. Execute action plans for any inclement weather during matches and major events. - Overall management, administration, and assessment of security operations. - Other security functions as assigned.
Required Experience and Qualifications:
- 3-5 years working in sports & entertainment or large venues in a security function. - Bachelor’s degree in law enforcement, Sport/Event Management, Law, or Equivalent combination of education and experience. - Experience with event technology, incident reporting, and other physical security systems. - Strong written and oral communication, interview, and collaboration experience.
Preferred Experience and Qualifications:
- 5-10 years working in sports & entertainment venues in a security function. - Law enforcement contacts on the federal, state, and local level. - Command of Large-scale special event coordination with security and local law enforcement. - Advanced knowledge of event logistics, planning, budgeting, and implementation - Prior knowledge and experience with Safety act certification. - Multi-lingual or Bi-lingual English and Spanish. - Professional certifications (CSSP, AVSS, CPP, etc.) in Security
Work environment:
- Flexible work schedule inclusive of nights, weekends, and holidays required. - Bending, lifting, and repeated movements including lifting 15-20 pounds. - Indoor and outdoor events in all weather conditions

job.title: Academy- Assistant Performance Coach PT
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
NO PHONE CALLS OR EMAILS, PLEASE.  Thanks for your interest! Due to the volume of applications, we’re unable to respond to individual status inquiries. If selected to move forward, you’ll hear from our HR team at Nashville Soccer Club.

POSITION OVERVIEW
The part-time Assistant Performance Coach for the Nashville SC Academy will be responsible for supporting and assisting the club by providing an integrated High-Performance LTAD program for it’s Academy players, in line with the Club and Performance department philosophy. This position will work closely with the Academy Director, Academy Performance Coach, and Academy Medical Department to create team and player-specific programs and administer those programs on the field and in the gym for developing youth athletes within the Academy. The person in this role should have prior playing and/or coaching experience and be expected to comfortably facilitate soccer-centric programs.  In addition, they should also have a formal educational background in exercise physiology (or similar degree) and load management across a variety of metrics as it relates to the development of youth soccer athletes.  This role will report directly to the Head of Performance for the First Team and will work closely with the Academy Director, Academy Performance Coach and Academy Athletic Trainer, as well as various other key coaches/staff.

RESPONSIBILITIES
  • This position will direct and/or assist in the following areas:
  • Assisting, managing and directing on-field warm up and cool down sessions, strength training, prehabilitation, as well as individual sessions for targeted or rehabilitating players in conjunction with medical staff
  • Distribution of player devices and processing data into club systems on a daily basis, along with wellness monitoring, testing administration, RPE collection, compiling, analysis, and reporting
  • Utilizing various monitoring software, tools and technologies to report on player performance and injury prevention
  • Direct and/or assist with the delivery of scientifically based optimal recovery strategies post trining and post-match
  • Help develop and implement individual and/or team off-season programs
  • Help develop strong interpersonal relationships with players and staff to effectively administer programs
  • Help producing educational materials for players to guide towards best practices in the development and optimization of sport performance
  • Organizing and maintaining field and gym equipment
  • Surveying literature and best practices for future implementation
  • Contribute to and advance LTAD programming and curriculum, data collection, testing, analytics, and reporting to develop high-performance in youth players
  • Occasional travel with team to games/events

MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS
  • Undergraduate (Bachelor’s) Degree from a 4-year accredited University in related field of study such as Kinesiology, Exercise Science, Sport Science, Biomechanics, Strength & Conditioning, and/or similar
  • NSCA-CSCS (Certified Strength & Conditioning Specialist)
  • CPR Certification
PREFERRED
  • Graduate (Master’s) Degree from an accredited University in related field of study
  • 2-3 years experience working with youth soccer within the United States
  • XPS-EXOS Performance Specialist
  • Functional Movement Screening (FMS) – Certified Level 1/2
  • Bilingual in Spanish and English
HOURS
  • 15-20 hours per week
  • Nights and weekend availability
 

We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Facility Operations - Facility Operations Associate PT
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
NO PHONE CALLS OR EMAILS, PLEASE.  Thanks for your interest! Due to the volume of applications, we’re unable to respond to individual status inquiries. If selected to move forward, you’ll hear from our HR team at Nashville Soccer Club.

POSITION OVERVIEW:
Nashville Soccer Club is looking to hire ambitious individuals with an interest in event and facility management to join our operations team. This position is responsible for assisting with daily operations of all Geodis Park events. This is a part-time position, working on an as-needed basis only and expected to work an average of 15-20 hours of work per week.  

RESPONSIBILITIES:
  • Assist with venue setup and breakdown for Major League Soccer Events:
  • Ensure locker room and field set-ups meet MLS standardization requirements.
  • Assist in servicing the needs of the visiting teams on game day.
  • Provide support for the referee crew before, during, and after the game. 
  • Work with ball retrievers during the event.
  • Stock event spaces.
  • Create and place signage.
  • Assist with laundry duties post-event.
  • Assist with other major events and private events as needed.
  • Operate heavy machinery and maintain inventory for logistics operations.
  • Collaborate with other departments during the preparation and execution of events. 
  • Help with the movement and storage of the department’s equipment.
  • Complete other duties as assigned.
 
 SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS:
  • Available for all (most) of Nashville SC home matches
  • Must be able to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends and holidays, as necessary.
  • Must possess strong communication (verbal and written) and organizational skills.
  • Dependable and team-oriented with a service attitude
  • Ability to lift a minimum of 50 pounds.
  • Ability to stand or walk for long periods of time.
  • Willingness to work in extreme conditions of different weather elements.
  • Outgoing personality, highly energetic, and enthusiastic
  • Experience in athletics, event planning execution or leading a small team preferred. 
  • Ability to work comfortably both independently and in a team environment.
  • Must be able to interact with all levels of the organization and represent the Stadium Operations Team in a professional manner.
 
 WORK ENVIRONMENT:
  • The noise level in the work environment is sometimes loud.
  • Ability to work in all types of weather, including extreme heat and cold.
  • Fast-paced work environment with many moving parts
 
CERTIFICATIONS AND LICENSES:
  • Driver’s license required.
  • Forklift certification preferred but not required.

We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Senior Manager, Membership Services
job.html_description:
NO PHONE CALLS OR EMAILS, PLEASE.  Thanks for your interest! Due to the volume of applications, we’re unable to respond to individual status inquiries. If selected to move forward, you’ll hear from our HR team at Nashville Soccer Club.

The Senior Manager, Membership Services will be charged with driving season ticket retention through successful training and development of the team, creating accountability goals and metrics, as well as spearheading a best-in-class season ticket member engagement strategy. This position will report to the Senior Director, Premium & Membership Services.   

   

 RESPONSIBILITIES:  

  • Oversee the entirety of the Season Ticket Member platform, including annual retention and year-round service and engagement strategies for the general Season Ticket Member base.  
  • Drive the creation, planning and execution of the annual renewal and relocation campaigns, while maximizing member retention and revenue generation.  
  • Lead an elite Membership Services team by recruiting, interviewing, training, developing and mentoring a talented group of individuals.   
  • Take ownership of season ticket member events, gift planning, creating customized member programs and generate other unique experiences and benefits to enhance member experience and retention.   
  • Troubleshoot issues that may arise from Season Ticket Members and the organization to determine appropriate resolution and act as a leadership point of contact for all escalated communications.  
  • Lead the Season Ticket Member onboarding process and touchpoint strategies throughout each season to build and maintain a high level of member engagement.  
  • Responsible for ongoing training and education of team members in the areas of customer service, account management, revenue generation, cross-selling, referrals, etc.   
  • Develop and track goals for individual team members and the department.   
  • Conduct one-on-ones with the Membership Services team to identify issues, help troubleshoot, and establish a relationship with the goal of helping each team member perform their jobs at the highest level.  
  • Collaborate with Marketing, Public Relations, Operations, Finance and other internal departments to ensure synergy and strong relationships across the board.  
  • Proactively recognize and anticipate challenges and opportunities for the team and the department  
  • Assist in the creation and management of the overall Ticketing department budget.   
  • Track all season ticket member renewals, feedback, attendance, event RSVPs, and other relevant member information in CRM to create reports and recaps.   
  • Other responsibilities as assigned by leadership.   

   

DESIRED QUALIFICATIONS:  

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Sports Management, Hospitality, or related field.  
  • A minimum of three (3) years of relevant work experience, preferably in the areas of sports business sales, service, revenue generation, retention, etc.
  • A minimum of one (1) year of experience in a people leadership role.     
  • Strong leadership drive with a desire to lift, lead, and serve others.   
  • Solid work ethic, team-oriented attitude, and enthusiastic about contributing to the success of the department and Club in a fast-paced environment.  
  • A proven track record in relationship building with excellent interpersonal and communication skills.   
  • Exceptional attention to detail, problem resolving ability, and ability to meet deadlines under pressure.  
  • Ability to organize and delegate responsibilities of projects and tasks to ensure timely completion of all obligations.  
  • Ability to work flexible hours including evenings, weekends, and holidays as necessary.  
  • Working knowledge of Salesforce and Archtics preferred.  
 

We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Guest Experience - Guest Experience Host PT
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
NO PHONE CALLS OR EMAILS, PLEASE.  Thanks for your interest! Due to the volume of applications, we’re unable to respond to individual status inquiries. If selected to move forward, you’ll hear from our HR team at Nashville Soccer Club.

About Nashville SC & GEODIS Park
Nashville Soccer Club is more than a team—it’s a family. We’re passionate about our community, the sport of soccer, and creating a positive culture for associates and fans alike. Based in America’s “It City,” Nashville SC has quickly become one of the fastest-growing soccer hubs, highlighted by world-class events and the opening of GEODIS Park in 2022—the largest soccer-specific stadium in Major League Soccer.
Nashville SC is seeking part-time Guest Experience Hosts to be the frontline representatives who create memorable matchday experiences and ensure every fan feels welcome at GEODIS Park.
RESPONSIBILITIES:
  • Act as a frontline representative assisting with guests and staff at all entry points of the stadium
  • Secure gates and access points to ensure guests do not enter without an appropriate ticket/credential
  • Direct fans around facility and communicate stadium policies to guests
  • Affectively answer guest questions with regards to wayfinding and assisting with mobile ticketing
  • Understand the Stadium layout and policies and procedures
  • Respond to guest concerns in a respectful and calm manner and appropriately escalate issues
  • Assist with stadium set-up procedures; including checking assigned areas, checking elevators, delivering documents and equipment such as radios, tablets, ISS devices, etc. 
  • Supervisory positions available
QUALIFICATIONS:
  • Available for 6-8 hours, 15-20 times per year, and available to attend all necessary in-person and virtual training as a prerequisite for employment.
  • Must enjoy speaking and interacting with others in a warm, and respectful manner.
  • Able to stand, walk or walk up and down stairs throughout an event.
  • Must enjoy helping others and working within a team environment.
  • Proactive in seeking out positive interactions with both guest and coworkers and interested in maintaining those positive relationships.
 

We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Manager, Marketing and Game Presentation
job.html_description:
The Manager, Marketing & Game Presentation plays a central role in shaping how fans experience Nashville SC, both in-venue at GEODIS Park and across marketing channels. This role combines event production, campaign marketing, and fan engagement to drive attendance, strengthen community connections, and elevate the matchday experience. The role partners closely with marketing, partnerships, ticketing, and production teams to ensure a consistent, compelling brand experience before, during, and after every event.

Responsibilities

  • Develop game presentation scripts, run-of-show documents, and entertainment elements for home events, in partnership with the Director.
  • Coordinate in-match promotions, contests, sponsorship activations, and fan engagement experiences.
  • Execute live entertainment elements during events.
  • Build toward a show-calling role over time, working directly with the Director to gain hands-on production experience and eventually serve as a secondary show caller.
  • Contribute creative concepts that enhance the fan experience and align with organizational brand standards.
  • Evaluate fan feedback and engagement metrics to continuously improve matchday entertainment.
  • Develop and execute integrated marketing campaigns to drive ticket sales and support key club initiatives across the organization.
  • Collaborate with creative teams to develop promotional assets across digital, social, email, and traditional channels.
  • Analyze campaign performance and provide recommendations for optimization.
  • Maintain consistency of brand messaging across all fan touchpoints.
  • Coordinate live event content with marketing calendars and promotional priorities.
  • Lead content planning for key theme nights and special events.
  • Manage timelines, budgets, vendors, and external partners.
  • Maintain event production schedules and marketing calendars.

Qualifications

  • Minimum of three to four years full-time experience in marketing, campaign management, or event/live production, ideally within professional sports, live entertainment, or a related fast-paced consumer brand environment
  • Experience supporting marketing or fan experience functions for a professional sports team preferred; knowledge of MLS and the Nashville soccer market a plus
  • Demonstrated ability to plan and execute multi-channel marketing campaigns from concept through performance reporting, balancing several concurrent workstreams against fixed deadlines
  • Comfort operating in real-time, high-pressure environments where plans change quickly; prior experience in live production, broadcast, theater, or event operations is a strong plus, even outside of sports.
  • Self-starter who can take a rough concept and build it into an executable plan with minimal oversight, while remaining coachable on new skills
  • Establishing priorities, defining action, and setting up a workflow to achieve goals while meeting deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Ability to build collaborative working relationships across marketing, partnerships, ticketing, production, and other departments
  • Ability to evaluate campaign performance and fan engagement data to inform decisions and demonstrate results
  • Ability to work extended hours, including nights, weekends, and holidays as needed, including all Nashville SC matches

job.title: Manager, Safety & Security
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
The Security Manager will be involved in all aspects of supporting the Nashville Soccer Club (NSC) Security Department and security contractors.  This candidate must possess strong interpersonal communication skills while interfacing with numerous computer software systems.  The Security Manager will oversee the day-to-day physical security of the Nashville Soccer Club (NSC) stadium and contribute to the oversight of the public safety posture of the stadium. The Security Manager will be required to work non-traditional hours that include nights, weekends, and holidays. The Security Manager will report to the Director of Safety and Security at Nashville Soccer Club (NSC).

Roles and Responsibilities

  • Manage the 24/7 stadium security department, which includes training, scheduling, and project development
  • Hire, train, coach, motivate and provide performance evaluations for security staff personnel who report into the department
  • Assist with training and exercises, including content development and delivery
  • Maintain paperless associate files for the Security Department
  • Must be highly organized and meticulous about task management
  • Lead the Security Department as a user of numerous software systems used by the security department and other departments within Nashville Soccer Club (NSC)
  • Manage the inventory and assets of the security department
  • Maintain an effective electronic/hard key access management program
  • Manage CCTV and all security programs and technologies used at the stadium
  • Develop and implement security policies and procedures for the stadium
  • Administer security measures and provide operational support, ensuring a safe environment for visitors, fans, and employees
  • Maintain and implement the fire safety, emergency preparedness, and life safety policies and procedures
  • Liaise with Human Resources to ensure company-wide compliance with all H&S guidelines and policies
  • Maintain and review licensing for organization and personnel
  • Oversee electronic tracking of all incidents along with reporting procedures
  • Administer facility security, access, and key control systems and radio communications
  • Manage quality assurance for all policies, procedures, and technologies
  • Ensure security measures comply with State, Federal, and league requirements
  • Assist in preparing and managing the department's operating budget and contribute to any capital expense requests
  • Develop effective working relationships with local agencies, including Metro Police, Fire Departments, and EMS
  • Ensure the delivering of exceptional customer service and public relations
  • Leadership - Exhibits confidence in self and others; inspires and motivates others to perform well
  • Readily accepts feedback from others and provides vision and inspiration to peers and subordinates
  • Displays enthusiasm, passion, and optimism

Qualifications Requirements

  • Minimum 3 years of experience in Security, Law Enforcement, or the Military within the Sports & Entertainment Industry
  • Proficiency in Microsoft products including Word, Excel, Publisher, PowerPoint; Vizio; Outlook
  • Experience operating 24/7 Software or ISS 24/7, Visitor Management Systems, Avigilon Control Center, Genetec VMS (or like systems)
  • Comfortable training groups or individuals about operating systems
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays
  • Previous effective leadership experience and managerial roles
  • Proven ability to lead, understand and communicate effectively
  • Proactive approach toward day-to-day activities and effective planning
  • Ability to enhance and lead a positive and encouraging work environment
  • Skilled at building positive, professional relationships with both internal and external partners
  • Proven to perform well under pressure, including strong ability to multitasking
  • Experience with innovative optimization of leading performance standards for Security processes
  • Ability to work within a team and independently
  • Work in compliance with the provisions of the Occupational Health & Safety Act
  • Available to work evenings and weekends and on-call as required
  • Thorough knowledge of CCTV, fire alarm systems, computerized access control systems, and wireless radio communication
  • Maintain an effective employee & visitor photo ID & credential program
job.plain_text_description:
The Security Manager will be involved in all aspects of supporting the Nashville Soccer Club (NSC) Security Department and security contractors. This candidate must possess strong interpersonal communication skills while interfacing with numerous computer software systems. The Security Manager will oversee the day-to-day physical security of the Nashville Soccer Club (NSC) stadium andcontribute to the oversight of the public safety posture of the stadium. The Security Manager will be required to work non-traditional hours that include nights, weekends, and holidays. The Security Manager will report to the Director of Safety and Security at Nashville Soccer Club (NSC).
Roles and Responsibilities
- Manage the 24/7 stadium security department, which includes training, scheduling, and project development - Hire, train, coach, motivate and provide performance evaluations for security staff personnel who report into the department - Assist with training and exercises, including content development and delivery - Maintain paperless associate files for the Security Department - Must be highly organized and meticulous about task management - Lead the Security Department as a user of numerous software systems used by the security department and other departments within Nashville Soccer Club (NSC) - Manage the inventory and assets of the security department - Maintain an effective electronic/hard key access management program - Manage CCTV and all security programs and technologies used at the stadium - Develop and implement security policies and procedures for the stadium - Administer security measures and provide operational support, ensuring a safe environment for visitors, fans, and employees - Maintain and implement the fire safety, emergency preparedness, and life safety policies and procedures - Liaise with Human Resources to ensure company-wide compliance with all H&S guidelines and policies - Maintain and review licensing for organization and personnel - Oversee electronic tracking of all incidents along with reporting procedures - Administer facility security, access, and key control systems and radio communications - Manage quality assurance for all policies, procedures, and technologies - Ensure security measures comply with State, Federal, and league requirements - Assist in preparing and managing the department's operating budget and contribute to any capital expense requests - Develop effective working relationships with local agencies, including Metro Police, Fire Departments, and EMS - Ensure the delivering of exceptional customer service and public relations - Leadership - Exhibits confidence in self and others; inspires and motivates others to perform well - Readily accepts feedback from others and provides vision and inspiration to peers and subordinates - Displays enthusiasm, passion, and optimism
Qualifications Requirements
- Minimum 3 years of experience in Security, Law Enforcement, or the Military within the Sports & Entertainment Industry - Proficiency in Microsoft products including Word, Excel, Publisher, PowerPoint; Vizio; Outlook - Experience operating 24/7 Software or ISS 24/7, Visitor Management Systems,Avigilon Control Center, Genetec VMS (or like systems) - Comfortable training groups or individuals about operating systems - Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays - Previous effective leadership experience and managerial roles - Proven ability to lead, understand and communicate effectively - Proactive approach toward day-to-day activities and effective planning - Ability to enhance and lead a positive and encouraging work environment - Skilled at building positive, professional relationships with both internal and external partners - Proven to perform well under pressure, including strong ability to multitasking - Experience with innovative optimization of leading performance standards for Security processes - Ability to work within a team and independently - Work in compliance with the provisions of the Occupational Health & Safety Act - Available to work evenings and weekends and on-call as required - Thorough knowledge of CCTV, fire alarm systems, computerized access control systems, and wireless radio communication - Maintain an effective employee & visitor photo ID & credential program

job.title: Housekeeping - Housekeeping PT
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
NO PHONE CALLS OR EMAILS, PLEASE.  Thanks for your interest! Due to the volume of applications, we’re unable to respond to individual status inquiries. If selected to move forward, you’ll hear from our HR team at Nashville Soccer Club.

About Nashville SC & GEODIS Park:
 Nashville is America’s “It City” and one of the fastest-growing soccer hubs in the country. From hosting the CONCACAF Gold Cup to FIFA World Cup Qualifiers and the FIFA Club World Cup, Music City has quickly become a destination for some of the sport’s most prominent events. Locally, Nashville SC is in its sixth season and continues to thrive both on and off the field, highlighted by the opening of GEODIS Park in May 2022—the largest soccer-specific stadium in Major League Soccer. 
 
What we are looking for:
  • Individuals available for 6-8 hours, 15-20 times per year, and available to attend all necessary in-person and virtual training as a prerequisite for employment
  • Someone who enjoys speaking and interacting with others in a warm, and respectful manner
  • Must enjoy helping others and working within a team environment
  • Ability to lift, pull, push and/or carry 25-60 lbs. as necessary
  • Must be able to stand for long periods of time and be exposed to different types of weather 

Primary roles & responsibilities:
  • Maintain stadium cleanliness, emptying trash cans, replacing trash cans with a new liner, wiping down countertop areas, tops of trash cans; sweeping/mopping the concourse as necessary; squeegeeing standing water to provide a safe environment for our guests; picking up and throwing away trash left in the stadium and outside perimeter that cannot be swept.
  • Responsible for providing a clean and safe facility before, during, and after all events
  • Responsible for picking up trash and emptying trash and recycle receptacles
  • Maintain all aspects of restroom cleanliness and preparedness by stocking and wiping down dispensers; cleaning and disinfecting all fixtures (toilets, urinals, sinks, baby changing tables); keeping the floor clean, dry and free of debris; and emptying trash cans
  • Respond to and resolve guests’ questions, concerns, complaints or suggestions in a professional and polite manner. Recognize and respond effectively in emergency situations
  • Supervisory positions available

We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

job.title: Coordinator, Safety & Security
job.location: GEODIS Park, 2226 Bransford Ave, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America
job.html_description:
The Safety & Security Coordinator supports the day-to-day operations of the Nashville Soccer Club (NSC) Security Department, working closely with security contractors, event day staff, and internal stakeholders. This role assists with scheduling, credentialing, incident documentation, and the daily administration of physical security systems used throughout the stadium. The Coordinator plays a key role in gameday and non-event security operations, and helps maintain a safe environment for players, staff, fans, and guests. This position requires strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to communicate effectively across departments.

Roles and Responsibilities

  • Support the daily operations of the stadium security department, including scheduling, staffing coverage, and shift logistics
  • Assist with onboarding, training coordination, and record-keeping for security staff and contractors
  • Maintain accurate, organized associate files and department documentation, including electronic recordkeeping
  • Serve as a day-to-day user of security software systems, including access control, visitor management, and CCTV platforms
  • Assist with monitoring and administering the electronic/hard key access management program
  • Support the operation and monitoring of CCTV systems and other security technologies used at the stadium
  • Help implement security policies, procedures, and post orders, and communicate updates to staff
  • Provide operational support during events, ensuring a safe and welcoming environment for visitors, fans, and employees
  • Assist in maintaining fire safety, emergency preparedness, and life safety procedures and documentation
  • Track and maintain licensing records for security personnel and the organization
  • Log, track, and help manage incident reports through the department's electronic reporting systems
  • Support facility security, access control, key control, and radio communication systems
  • Assist with quality assurance checks on policies, procedures, and technologies used by the department
  • Assist with tracking department inventory, assets, and equipment
  • Deliver excellent customer service and represent the department professionally with staff, guests, and partners
  • Demonstrate reliability, initiative, and sound judgment; support and reinforce team morale
  • Remain receptive to feedback and coaching, and contribute ideas to improve department processes

Qualifications Requirements

  • Minimum 1-2 years of experience in Security, Law Enforcement, Military, or the Sports & Entertainment industry preferred
  • Proficiency in Microsoft products including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook
  • Familiarity with security software such as 24/7 Software or ISS 24/7, Visitor Management Systems, Avigilon Control Center, Genetec VMS (or like systems) preferred
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays
  • Strong organizational skills with keen attention to detail and task management
  • Effective verbal and written communication skills
  • Ability to perform well under pressure and manage multiple priorities
  • Work in compliance with the provisions of the Occupational Health & Safety Act
  • Available to work evenings, weekends, and on-call as required
  • Working knowledge of CCTV, fire alarm systems, computerized access control systems, and wireless radio communication preferred

job.title: Host Families
job.location: Newcastle upon Tyne Discipline: Football Operations Job type: Casual Job ref: 008802 Published: 4 months ago Expiry date: 31 Jul 2026 21:00
job.html_description:
Could you provide a welcoming home for the next generation of Newcastle United stars?

 

We’re looking for Host Families to support our young players as they move to Newcastle United from outside the North East.

 

Why Become a Host Family?

 

*        Provide a stable, safe, and welcoming home

*        Play a vital role in a player’s growth on and off the pitch

*        Support their academic and personal development

*        Receive competitive remuneration

 

Family is at the heart of Newcastle United. We know our players perform at their best when they feel happy and secure at home.

 

We offer both short-term (1–2 nights) and full-time (7 nights per week) placements for players aged 14–21.

 

If you can offer a nurturing environment and want to make a real impact, we’d love to hear from you!

 

What we’re looking for:

 

We’re seeking Host Families in Newcastle upon Tyne (postcodes NE7, NE6, NE2, NE3, NE12), located close to our education partner or the Academy. You should be able to provide high-quality accommodation, ideally in a family environment.

 

Requirements:

 

*        Provide an evening meal, breakfast (for school-age players)

*        Access to laundry facilities and Wi-Fi

*        A safe and suitable environment for the player

*        Private bedroom with a double bed

*        Full or part-time placements available year-round to align with the football season

*        Willingness to accommodate trialists and provide holiday cover when needed

 

You’ll be able to:

 

*        Create a welcoming, homely environment for young players

*        Teach essential independent living skills like cooking, cleaning, and self-care

*        Provide meals following nutritional guidance from the Academy

*        Ensure accommodation is close to local amenities with good transport links to the Academy

*        Report any welfare concerns to the Academy’s Safeguarding and Wellbeing team

*        Integrate players into family life as much as they feel comfortable

*        Accommodate regular inspections and visits during the season

*        Build trusted relationships through open communication

*        Adapt to the flexible schedule of young players throughout the football season

*        Promote and demonstrate a strong anti-discriminatory practice

 

It would be great if you have:

 

*        Experience working with young people

*        Understanding of the emotional impact and well-being challenges young athletes face

*        A child-focused approach

*        The capacity to accommodate more than one player

 

We welcome applications from all sections of the community. In particular, we encourage candidates from Black, Asian and other minority ethnic backgrounds to apply, as these groups are currently under-represented in this role. If you're ready to offer a supportive home and make a lasting impact, we'd love to hear from you! 

 

To express an interest and find out more information please email Callum.Thompson@nufc.co.uk detailing the following:

 

1)      Your full name and current address

2)      Current occupation

3)      Whether you are interested in offering a full-time placement or intermittent support for trialists

4)      Your current property situation including availability of a private bedroom / number of rooms available

5)      Why you would like to become a host family in no more than 250 words

job.title: Corporate Affairs Project Manager
job.html_description:
Corporate Affairs Project Manager 

Permanent 

Benton - Newcastle Upon Tyne

Competitive Salary

 

Be part of something bigger. 

 

We are the heartbeat of the city.  A club with bold ambitions on a global stage and deep roots in our community. If you’re driven to raise the bar and make an impact, you’ll find the platform, the people and the pride to do your best work here.

 

This is a great opportunity to develop, learn and make a real contribution in a fast-paced and exciting environment.

 

About the role

 

At Newcastle United, we are building something extraordinary on and off the pitch. As our Corporate Affairs Project Manager, you will play a central role in delivering high-profile, strategic initiatives that shape the Club’s reputation, influence, and long-term success.

 

Operating at the intersection of strategy and delivery, you’ll partner with senior stakeholders, coordinate complex projects, and turn ambition into meaningful, measurable outcomes. If you thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment and want to make a tangible impact at one of football’s most exciting organisations, we’d love to hear from you.

 

What you’ll do

 

You will support the delivery of the Club’s Corporate Affairs strategy, ensuring alignment across internal teams and external stakeholders while driving key projects forward with clarity and pace.

 

Working closely with senior leaders, you will act as a connector across departments—bringing structure, energy, and accountability to critical initiatives that underpin the Club’s ambitions.

 

You must have‿

 

*       Proven project management experience within complex, multi-stakeholder environments

*       Strong ability to manage multiple workstreams with excellent organisation and attention to detail

*       Excellent stakeholder management and relationship-building skills

*       Confident communicator with strong written and verbal skills

*       Ability to translate strategy into actionable plans and measurable outcomes

*       Proactive, solutions-focused mindset with strong problem-solving capability

*       Collaborative approach with the ability to work effectively across teams

*       Degree-level education or equivalent professional experience

 

It’s not essential but it would be great if you also have‿

 

*       Project management qualification or equivalent experience

*       Experience within professional sport, public affairs, or corporate engagement environments

*       Understanding of reputation management and stakeholder landscapes

 

The team

 

This is a great opportunity to be part of a newly established team at Newcastle United. The team will play a key role in the future direction of the Club.

 

United As One.

 

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment in which everyone can be their authentic self and is enabled to achieve their full potential.

 

Safeguarding

 

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

 

How to apply 

 

To access the full job description for this role please click here.

 

For further information on this role and about the club please visit our careers page.

 

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.

 

This position is UK based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All job offers are conditional upon background checks, including reference history, DBS checks, and verification of your eligibility to work. Please note, we do not sponsor applicants without the right to work in the UK.

 

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited candidate details. Any candidates submitted without request will be considered direct applicants.

job.title: CRM Executive
job.location: Newcastle upon Tyne Discipline: Commercial Job type: Permanent Job ref: 010482 Published: 11 days ago Expiry date: 17 Jul 2026 09:00
job.html_description:
CRM Executive 

Permanent 

Newcastle Upon Tyne 

Competitive Salary 

Closing Date: 17th July 2026

Be part of something bigger. Turning fan insight into personalised engagement.

We are the heartbeat of the city. A club with bold ambitions on a global stage and deep roots in our community. If you’re driven to raise the bar and make an impact, you’ll find the platform, the people and the pride to do your best work here.

This is a great opportunity to develop, learn and make a real contribution in a fast-paced and exciting environment.

About the role 

The CRM Executive is responsible for the end-to-end delivery of multi-channel CRM campaigns that drive fan engagement, retention and commercial performance. Reporting to the CRM Manager, this role executes targeted campaigns across email, push, in-app and web personalisation, using CRM platforms to build audiences, deploy campaigns and support automated customer journeys.

You will manage the CRM campaign calendar, coordinating activity across retail, membership, ticketing, venue and partnerships, ensuring campaigns are delivered accurately, on time and at pace. This is a hands-on role requiring strong technical capability within CRM platforms, including experience in building complex audiences, executing automated journeys and delivering data-driven, personalised campaigns.

What you’ll do 

  • Own end-to-end CRM campaign execution, from briefing and build through to QA and deployment.

  • Manage and maintain the CRM campaign calendar across multiple business areas.

  • Build, configure and deploy campaigns within CRM platforms, following best practice in campaign setup and execution.

  • Create and manage audience selections using behavioural, transactional and demographic data.

  • Deliver campaigns across email, push notifications, in-app messaging and personalised web experiences.

  • Support automated, lifecycle, behavioural and trigger-based customer journeys.

  • Build and deploy transactional messaging across ticketing, events and customer service journeys.

  • Monitor campaign performance, produce reporting and highlight actionable insights to improve future delivery.

  • Partner with teams across retail, membership, venue, partnerships and supporter services to deliver CRM activity.

  • Maintain high standards of data accuracy, database integrity and GDPR-compliant CRM activity.

The team 

You’ll be part of a friendly, supportive and dedicated team who will help you settle in and succeed. We value curiosity, teamwork and a positive approach

United As One.  

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment in which everyone can be their authentic self and is enabled to achieve their full potential.

Safeguarding

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

How to apply  

To access the full job description for this role please click here.

For further information on this role and about the club please visit our careers page.

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates. 

This position is UK based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK.
