Failure to satisfy the Club and the PL's safeguarding requirements may lead to the job offer being withdrawn or, if they have already started employment, summary termination of the employment.

job.title: Academy Head of performance Analysis
job.location: The Nigel Doughty Academy, Nottingham, NG2 7SR, GB
job.html_description:
Salary: Please state your expectations within your application

Contract: Full Time, 42 hours a week

Location: The Nigel Doughty Academy, Nottingham, NG2 7SR

Department: Academy Head of Coaching

Reporting to: Assistant Academy Manager

Closing Date For Applications: 20th July 2026

About the Role:

The successful candidate will lead, coordinate, and drive the delivery of performance analysis support across all Academy age groups at Nottingham Forest FC. They will be responsible for managing the day-to-day operations of the Academy analysis department, ensuring high-quality support is provided to players and staff throughout the development pathway.

The role will involve monitoring and tracking player development through the effective use of both objective and subjective performance data, helping to inform coaching practice and individual development plans.

In addition, the position holder will play a key role in upholding and embedding the Academy’s philosophy and methodology, supporting the wider vision of the Academy. Working closely with the Head of Player Development, Methodology and Innovation and Academy Coach Development Manager, they will contribute to the education and development of coaching staff while also actively participating in broader Academy management and strategic projects.

The Perks of the Job:

  • Opportunity to access tickets for events at The City Ground.

  • Employee Assistance Programme.

  • Access to high street and gym discounts.

  • Discount within our Club shop.

  • Free on-site parking.

Key Tasks and Responsibilities:

  • To lead the support provision and requirements of the performance analysis function within the academy.

  • Oversee the analysis related to philosophy and methodology, aiding the academy vision.

  • Manage the day-to-day academy analysis operations ensuring effective processes to deliver actionable insights across all age groups.

  • Work in partnership with MDT teams to ensure high level reporting methods are delivered to players, coaches and key stakeholders across academy and loans.

  • Oversee individual analysis of Academy players using subjective and objective data to inform the Head of Academy and Academy Management Team.

  • Lead specific analysis projects related to the long-term development of Nottingham Forest Academy Analysis department.

  • Drive the club’s analytical products and processes to ensure the delivery of high-quality, actionable insights that support player development and performance outcomes.

  • Ensure effective professional development programme is implemented for both individual staff and analysis team.

  • Recruit permanent staff when the need arises in line with club protocols.

  • Manage and oversee the academy internship programme, supporting the recruitment, development, and day-to-day guidance of interns within the department.

  • Produce when required monthly, quarterly and yearly reports in line with our Academy strategy to update on progress met and achieved in relation to our performance analysis strategic pillar.

  • To create successful and professional relationships with players and staff whilst maintaining professional distance from playing staff at all times.

  • To act, at all times, in a manner appropriate to a representative of Nottingham Forest FC

  • All staff are responsible for the wellbeing and safeguarding of children and vulnerable adults on all Club sites. If you have a concern or witness a Safeguarding matter, you must report it to the department’s Designated Safeguarding Officer (DSO) or the Club’s Lead DSO or Senior Safeguarding Manager. If It’s an emergency, you are to call the relevant emergency service such as Police or CAMHS.

What are we looking for:

  • Excellent understanding of football tactics and Talent Identification in players.

  • Excellent attention to detail and is very well organised and punctual

  • Able to communicate effectively at all levels and is fully computer literate.

  • Works effectively in a fast-paced environment; remains calm under pressure and is comfortable working to tight deadlines.

  • Prior experience managing kit and equipment for a sporting institution preferably within football (desirable)

  • Full knowledge and experience of all duties involved in a role of this nature.

  • Drive to achieve deadlines, organise and prioritise workload effectively, and continually review ways to improve efficiency and effectiveness

  • Demonstrate skills and attributes in a PA/administration capacity

  • Good verbal and written communication skills and be able to understand and explain matters arising in own area of work.

  • Excellent interpersonal skills, with people of all ages and backgrounds, able to maintain confidentiality, and act with tact and diplomacy

  • Willingness and flexibility to deal with urgent changes/ demands, whilst maintaining existing workload.

  • UEFA B license and educated in either Performance Analysis, Sports Science and/or coaching.

  • Excellent IT skills, including MS Office, intranets, web and Outlook

  • Experience in health and safety duties and responsibilities appropriate to the post (desirable)

  • Skill at handling difficult situations and confidential matters according to policy and procedures, referring to others where necessary/appropriate (desirable)

Forest For Everyone

We aim to create a culture which embraces and celebrates our differences, enables individuals to feel comfortable, maximise their potential and eliminate discrimination.

Nottingham Forest Football Club is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all suitably qualified persons, regardless of age, disability, gender, gender reassignment, marital / civil partnership status, pregnancy / maternity leave, race, religion/belief, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected characteristic.

Nottingham Forest Football Club is a signatory of the FA’s Football Leadership Diversity Code. We are committed to developing policies to promote equal opportunities and diversity within our workforce. To monitor our progress against these commitments, we Invite all applicants to provide diversity data during the application process.

Disability Confident Committed Employer

We are a Disability Confident Committed employer.  We encourage applications from people with disabilities and long-term health conditions.  If you need disability-related adjustments to the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a "disabled person" according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme - please indicate this in your application form below.  The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment selections.

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

As part of the Club’s commitment to safeguarding, the successful applicant may be required to undergo a Disclosure Barring Service (“DBS”) check or an enhanced DBS check. The results of a DBS check will be considered on an individual basis, and the Club will act in a proportionate manner when deciding whether or not to proceed with the appointment.

An FA Enhanced DBS disclosure is required for all relevant new appointments to the Club. Individuals will be asked to declare any convictions, cautions or reprimands, warnings or bind-overs that may have been incurred. The Club reserves the right to carry out risk assessments where content has been disclosed on a DBS disclosure if it feels that it is relevant to the job requirements. Failure to satisfy the Club and the PL's safeguarding requirements may lead to the job offer being withdrawn or, if they have already started employment, summary termination of the employment.

job.title: Events & Marketing Activations Manager
job.location: The City Ground, Nottingham, NG2 5FJ, GB
job.html_description:
Salary: Please state your expectations within your application

Contract: Full Time, 37.5 hours a week

Location: The City Ground, Nottingham, NG2 5FJ

Department: Marketing and Content

Reporting to: Director Of Marketing

Closing Date For Applications: 29th July 2026

About the Role:

Nottingham Forest is seeking a driven Events & Marketing Activations Manager to join our growing commercial and marketing team. This is a hands-on delivery role at the heart of the Club's fan engagement, marketing, and commercial activity.

You will be the driver behind our club events, marketing and partner’s activations - taking ownership of projects from initial planning through to execution. You will also provide support to our Content Production & Partnership teams on Men, Women and Netball matchdays, helping to ensure every fan-facing moment is seamless, professional, and on-brand.

This role is built for a natural project manager who thrives on coordination, keeps multiple stakeholders aligned, and takes genuine pride in delivering exceptional experiences.

The Perks of the Job:

  • Opportunity to access tickets for events at The City Ground.

  • Employee Assistance Programme.

  • Access to high street and gym discounts.

  • Discount within our Club shop.

  • Free on-site parking.

Key Tasks and Responsibilities:

Events

  • Own and deliver all Club events from end to end, from initial concept through planning, logistics, and on-site execution.

  • Act as the primary point of contact and project lead for fan-facing events including open days, community events, and seasonal activations.

  • Work with internal departments on any ad-hoc events or activations

Marketing Activations

  • Support the Marketing team in delivering campaigns and fan activations, ensuring activations are executed on brief, on time, and to a high standard.

  • Coordinate creative and operational requirements across internal and external teams to bring marketing campaigns to life.

Partner Activations

  • Support the Partnerships team in delivering partner activations and any contractual rights that may need event / activation support

  • Maintaining strong and professional relationships with commercial partners.

Matchday Presentation Support

  • Produce scripts and running orders for matchday presentation.

  • Book and brief talent, presenters, and on-pitch personnel.

  • Check and coordinate matchday content across all production touchpoints.

  • Liaise with floor managers and production teams to ensure smooth operational delivery.

  • Serve as a key operational link between the Content Production and Marketing teams on matchdays.

Project & Stakeholder Management

  • Coordinate suppliers, freelancers, and internal stakeholders across all assigned projects.

  • Manage timelines, budgets, and project plans for all events and activations.

  • Maintain clear communication across teams to ensure everyone is briefed, aligned, and accountable.

What are we looking for:

Essential Criteria

  • Proven experience delivering events, activations or marketing campaigns from concept through to execution.

  • Excellent project management and organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and meet tight deadlines.

  • Strong stakeholder management skills, with the confidence to work collaboratively across a variety of departments and with external suppliers and partners.

  • Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build positive working relationships at all levels.

  • Highly organised with outstanding attention to detail and a proactive approach to problem solving.

  • Ability to remain calm under pressure and make effective decisions in a fast-moving environment.

  • Experience managing project timelines, budgets and logistics.

  • Comfortable taking ownership of projects and seeing them through from planning to delivery.

  • Flexible approach to working hours, including evenings, weekends and matchdays, in line with the demands of the football calendar.

  • Competent in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel, Word and PowerPoint.

Desirable Criteria

  • Experience working within professional sport, entertainment, hospitality or live events.

  • Knowledge of football matchday operations or live event production.

  • Experience coordinating marketing activations or commercial partnership fulfilment.

  • Experience producing event schedules, scripts or running orders.

  • Understanding of fan engagement and customer experience principles.

  • Experience working with creative, production or content teams.

The successful candidate will be:

  • A natural organiser who enjoys bringing people together to deliver successful projects.

  • Passionate about creating memorable experiences for supporters, partners and guests.

  • Self-motivated, proactive and willing to take ownership of their work.

  • Adaptable and comfortable working in an ever-changing environment where priorities can shift quickly.

  • A confident communicator who can influence, collaborate and build trust with colleagues and external stakeholders.

  • Professional, approachable and committed to representing Nottingham Forest to the highest standard.

  • Creative in their thinking while remaining practical and solutions-focused.

  • Resilient, dependable and willing to go the extra mile to ensure every event and activation is delivered successfully.

Forest For Everyone

We aim to create a culture which embraces and celebrates our differences, enables individuals to feel comfortable, maximise their potential and eliminate discrimination.

Nottingham Forest Football Club is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all suitably qualified persons, regardless of age, disability, gender, gender reassignment, marital / civil partnership status, pregnancy / maternity leave, race, religion/belief, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected characteristic.

Nottingham Forest Football Club is a signatory of the FA’s Football Leadership Diversity Code. We are committed to developing policies to promote equal opportunities and diversity within our workforce. To monitor our progress against these commitments, we Invite all applicants to provide diversity data during the application process.

Disability Confident Committed Employer

We are a Disability Confident Committed employer.  We encourage applications from people with disabilities and long-term health conditions.  If you need disability-related adjustments to the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a "disabled person" according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme - please indicate this in your application form below.  The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment selections.

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

As part of the Club’s commitment to safeguarding, the successful applicant may be required to undergo a Disclosure Barring Service (“DBS”) check or an enhanced DBS check. The results of a DBS check will be considered on an individual basis, and the Club will act in a proportionate manner when deciding whether or not to proceed with the appointment.

An FA Enhanced DBS disclosure is required for all relevant new appointments to the Club. Individuals will be asked to declare any convictions, cautions or reprimands, warnings or bind-overs that may have been incurred. The Club reserves the right to carry out risk assessments where content has been disclosed on a DBS disclosure if it feels that it is relevant to the job requirements. Failure to satisfy the Club and the PL's safeguarding requirements may lead to the job offer being withdrawn or, if they have already started employment, summary termination of the employment.

job.title: Girls Academy Education Lead (Part Time)
job.location: The Nigel Doughty Academy, Nottingham, NG2 7SR, GB
job.html_description:
Salary: Please state your expectations within your application

Contract: Part Time, 22.5 hours a week

Location: The City Ground, Nottingham, NG2 5FJ And The Nigel Doughty Academy, Nottingham, NG2 7SR

Department: Girls Academy

Reporting to: Girls Academy Manager

Closing Date For Applications: 30th July 2026

About the Role:

Lead, design and quality-assure the Academy Education Programme across the PGA, ensuring every player receives a personalised, high-quality academic support offer, developed in partnership with the player and their parents/carers, and aligned effectively with the football programme.

The Perks of the Job:

  • Opportunity to access tickets for events at The City Ground.

  • Employee Assistance Programme.

  • Access to high street and gym discounts.

  • Discount within our Club shop.

  • Free on-site parking.

Key Tasks and Responsibilities:

  • Designing and periodically reviewing the overall Education Programme in partnership with learning providers.

  • Coordinating academic pathways (GCSE, A-levels, BTECs, functional skills, FE/HE programmes, DiSE).

  • Working with schools and colleges to align timetables with training/competition; arranging compensatory education where required.

  • Monitoring academic performance by tracking attendance, reviewing attainments and progress and implementing early interventions

  • Ensuring accurate documentation, reporting and data management across academic records.

  • Managing internal verification and quality assurance where the club delivers any elements directly.

  • Providing guidance to parents/carers on academic pathways, expectations, and how to support learning alongside football.

  • Support players to develop confidence and independence in managing their academic commitments.

What are we looking for:

  • QTS or QTLS (with active SET membership for QTLS).

  • Enhanced DBS.

  • FA Safeguarding Children qualification.

  • Emergency First Aid / FA IFAiF (or equivalent).

  • Teaching, tutoring, mentoring or academic coaching experience.

  • Experience supporting young people through GCSE, Post-16 and FE/HE study pathways.

  • Leading or coordinating education provision in a school, FE, HE or performance environment.

  • Working with external learning providers and navigating dual career pressures.

  • Experience coordinating DiSE or similar programmes.

  • Safeguarding practice and pastoral support from an academic perspective.

  • Multidisciplinary working in environments with demanding schedules.

  • Familiarity with qualification frameworks (GCSE, BTEC, A level, FE/HE systems).

  • Understanding of how loan moves, Academy Dual Registrations and academy contracts impact education provision and player welfare.

Forest For Everyone

We aim to create a culture which embraces and celebrates our differences, enables individuals to feel comfortable, maximise their potential and eliminate discrimination.

Lead, design and quality-assure the Academy Education Programme across the PGA, ensuring every player receives a personalised, high-quality academic support offer, developed in partnership with the player and their parents/carers, and aligned effectively with the football programme.
The Perks of the Job:
- Opportunity to access tickets for events at The City Ground. - Employee Assistance Programme. - Access to high street and gym discounts. - Discount within our Club shop. - Free on-site parking.
Key Tasks and Responsibilities:
- Designing and periodically reviewing the overall Education Programme in partnership with learning providers. - Coordinating academic pathways (GCSE, A-levels, BTECs, functional skills, FE/HE programmes, DiSE). - Working with schools and colleges to align timetables with training/competition; arranging compensatory education where required. - Monitoring academic performance by tracking attendance, reviewing attainments and progress and implementing early interventions - Ensuring accurate documentation, reporting and data management across academic records. - Managing internal verification and quality assurance where the club delivers any elements directly. - Providing guidance to parents/carers on academic pathways, expectations, and how to support learning alongside football. - Support players to develop confidence and independence in managing their academic commitments.
What are we looking for:
- QTS or QTLS (with active SET membership for QTLS). - Enhanced DBS. - FA Safeguarding Children qualification. - Emergency First Aid / FA IFAiF (or equivalent). - Teaching, tutoring, mentoring or academic coaching experience. - Experience supporting young people through GCSE, Post-16 and FE/HE study pathways. - Leading or coordinating education provision in a school, FE, HE or performance environment. - Working with external learning providers and navigating dual career pressures. - Experience coordinating DiSE or similar programmes. - Safeguarding practice and pastoral support from an academic perspective. - Multidisciplinary working in environments with demanding schedules. - Familiarity with qualification frameworks (GCSE, BTEC, A level, FE/HE systems). - Understanding of how loan moves, Academy Dual Registrations and academy contracts impact education provision and player welfare.
Forest For Everyone
We aim to create a culture which embraces and celebrates our differences, enables individuals to feel comfortable, maximise their potential and eliminate discrimination.
Nottingham Forest Football Club is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all suitably qualified persons, regardless of age, disability, gender, gender reassignment, marital / civil partnership status, pregnancy / maternity leave, race, religion/belief, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected characteristic.
Nottingham Forest Football Club is a signatory of the FA’s Football Leadership Diversity Code. We are committed to developing policies to promote equal opportunities and diversity within our workforce. To monitor our progress against these commitments, we Invite all applicants to provide diversity data during the application process.
Disability Confident Committed Employer
We are a Disability Confident Committed employer. We encourage applications from people with disabilities and long-term health conditions. If you need disability-related adjustments to the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application.
If you are likely to meet the definition of being a "disabled person" according to theEquality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via theDisability Confident Scheme- please indicate this in your application form below. The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment selections.
Our Commitment to Safeguarding
As part of the Club’s commitment to safeguarding, the successful applicant may be required to undergo a Disclosure Barring Service (“DBS”) check or an enhanced DBS check. The results of a DBS check will be considered on an individual basis, and the Club will act in a proportionate manner when deciding whether or not to proceed with the appointment.
An FA Enhanced DBS disclosure is required for all relevant new appointments to the Club. Individuals will be asked to declare any convictions, cautions or reprimands, warnings or bind-overs that may have been incurred. The Club reserves the right to carry out risk assessments where content has been disclosed on a DBS disclosure if it feels that it is relevant to the job requirements. Failure to satisfy the Club and the PL's safeguarding requirements may lead to the job offer being withdrawn or, if they have already started employment, summary termination of the employment.

job.title: Part-Time Associate, Growth Marketing
job.html_description:
JOB TITLE:  Part-time Associate, Growth Marketing  (30 hours per week)
REPORTING TO: Director, Growth Marketing
DEPARTMENT: Marketing        
LOCATION:  Manhattan
COMPENSATION: $20/hour, up to 30 hours per week 
 
POSITION OVERVIEW:  
The Part Time Associate, Growth Marketing role plays a pivotal part in expanding the club’s fan base and deepening engagement. This position supports data-driven marketing initiatives designed to attract new fans, convert interest into ticket sales, and strengthen long-term loyalty. This role will provide a unique experience to learn and assist in the creation of marketing campaigns across email, paid media, and CRM platforms. 

If you’re passionate about growing the beautiful game this is a perfect opportunity to join the Club at an exciting time with New York City’s first-ever soccer specific stadium on the horizon. The ideal candidate is proactive, curious, and thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment. They bring both strategic thinking and hands-on execution to help the club reach new audiences and turn matchday moments into lifelong fandom. 
 
WHAT YOU’LL DO: 
  • Support the planning, execution, and optimization of fan acquisition and retention campaigns across digital channels. 
  • Manage campaign setup in collaboration with marketing, ticketing, and business intelligence teams. 
  • Assist in developing creative briefs, tracking performance metrics, and providing actionable insights for optimization. 
  • Monitor and analyze key growth KPIs 
  • Contribute ideas for new growth initiatives, testing opportunities, and ways to enhance the fan journey. 
  • Manage feedback and day to day maintenance of the club’s loyalty program  
  • Follow brand guideline and ensure quality communication to new and existing fans 
 
WHO YOU ARE: 
  • 1–3 years of marketing experience, ideally within sports, entertainment, or a high-growth consumer brand. 
  • Detail-oriented and capable of managing multiple projects in a fast-paced environment. 
  • Proactive attitude, open to trying new ideas, eager to learn and highly organized 
  • Thrive in a collaborative environment 
  • Must have excellent communication skills  
  • Able to work independently, take initiative, and maintain a high level of professionalism within a professional sports environment 
  • Additional language/s is a plus. 
 
ADDITIONAL REQUIRMENTS: 
  • Applicant must be able to comply with MLS Next Safety and Well-Being policy and any other applicable child safety policies provided to applicants by NYCFC 
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, as necessary 

KEY RELATIONSHIPS 
  • Director, Growth Marketing 
  • Manager, Media Buying 
  • Manager, Lifecycle Marketing 
  • Senior Manager, Digital 
  • Cross functional departments – Ticketing, Partnership Marketing, Business Intelligence & Design 

WHAT WE OFFER: 
  • Two complimentary tickets to each home match 
  • Medical benefits through a third-party provider 
  • Team outings, happy hours, and annual staff gatherings to celebrate our co-workers  
  • A dynamic, ambitious, and fun work environment  
 
NEW YORK CITY FC OVERVIEW: 
At New York City FC, we are on a mission to build this city into one of the soccer capitals of the world. The Club was announced as Major League Soccer’s 20th franchise and is the first and only MLS team located within the five boroughs of New York City. The Club has qualified for the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs nine times over the past 10 seasons, winning MLS Cup in 2021. Games are available to stream via an Apple TV subscription, providing every match of the MLS regular season, the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs, annual Leagues Cup tournament, the MLS All-Star Game, the Campeones Cup, and more all in one place and with no blackouts. New York City FC is building New York City’s first-ever soccer-specific stadium, Etihad Park, which is set to open in 2027.

New York City FC is also one of 12 clubs comprising City Football Group and counts Manchester City FC, Melbourne City FC, Yokohama F-Marinos, Girona FC, Montevideo City Torque, Shenzhen Peng City FC, Lommel SK, ESTAC Troyes, Palermo FC, Bahia, and Club Bolívar among its sister clubs.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.
  
By clicking "Apply Now", you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.   
https://www.mlssoccer.com/legal/privacy-policy

job.title: Business Development Associate
job.html_description:
JOB TITLE: Business Development Associate
REPORTING TO: Manager, Group Sales or Manager, Membership Sales
DEPARTMENT: Ticketing
LOCATION: New York, NY / Hybrid ( 3 days in office)

POSITION OVERVIEW:
The Business Development Associate is responsible for generating maximum revenue through the sales and retention of full season, partial season, and group ticket plans. This person will establish relationships with local companies, individuals and groups within the community and provide superior levels of service with prospective clients and developed accounts.

WHAT YOU'LL DO:
  • Generate new business revenue through full season, partial season, and group tickets.
  • Build relationships with current Members through phone calls, face-to-face meetings, events, and game day interactions.
  • Prospect and close new sales leads for the organization through an outbound sales effort including cold-calling, relationship selling and in-person presentations.
  • Make a required minimum of 60 outbound sales calls per day with the goal of maximizing all ticket revenue for New York City FC related events.
  • Establish presence at all New York City FC home games and local events for the purpose of developing new prospects, servicing existing customer base, and generating referrals.
  • Attend out of office meetings (corporate meetings, networking events, youth soccer meetings, etc…)
  • Develop and constantly grow a database comprised of major corporations, medium and smaller-sized businesses, and individual prospects through various professional prospecting and networking efforts throughout the community.
  • Build strong relationships with account base through proactive communication including seat visits, touch points, phone calls, emails and other communication channels.
  • Maintain a detailed customer database of all clients and prospects in the teams CRM. This includes, but is not limited to: call sheets, referral follow-up, existing client programs, new solicitation and other programs as developed.
  • Assist season ticket holders through the seat selections process and communicate how to properly manage their account through online options.
  • Provide a dedicated effort to renewal campaign.
  • Meet or exceed sales goals assigned by the Sr. Director, Ticket Sales and Service
  • Other duties as determined by the Sr. Director, Ticket Sales and Service or Director, Ticket Sales

WHO YOU ARE:
  • Bachelor’s degree in Business or related field.
  • Minimum of 6 months - 1 year sales experience. Experience in the sports industry is a plus.
  • Track record of sales success
  • Soccer knowledge is preferred, but not required.
  • Dedicated attitude, with excellent verbal and written communications skills.
  • Motivated self-starter with entrepreneurial drive.
  • Experience in the New York marketplace preferred.
  • Excellent time management, organization, and customer service skills.
  • High comfort level making cold calls.
  • Excellent knowledge of PC systems including Word, Excel and database-related platforms.
  • Creativity and excellent interpersonal skills required.
  • Working knowledge of generally accepted standards of customer service and problem resolution.
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, as necessary.

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:
  • Applicant must be able to comply with MLS Next Safety and Well-Being policy and any other applicable child safety policies provided to applicants by NYCFC
  • Must be able to work games scheduled which may be at night and on weekends and holidays

WHAT WE OFFER:
  • Base Compensation: $45,000/annual. This is a non-exempt overtime eligible position
  • Excellent health, dental & vision insurance, with options to fit you and your family’s needs
  • Comprehensive and competitive benefit programs, including maternal & paternal leave
  • Ancillary benefits: Commuter, gym membership, and Citi Bike discounts
  • Your health is important! Our wellness strategy focuses on mental and physical wellbeing through in-office and virtual programs
  • Four complementary tickets to every NYCFC home match
  • Team outings, happy hours, and annual staff gatherings to celebrate our co-workers
  • A dynamic, ambitious, and fun work environment

 NEW YORK CITY FOOTBALL CLUB OVERVIEW:
At New York City FC, we are on a mission to build this city into one of the soccer capitals of the world. The Club was announced as Major League Soccer’s 20th franchise and is the first and only MLS team located within the five boroughs of New York City. The Club has qualified for the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs nine times over the past 10 seasons, winning MLS Cup in 2021. Games are available to stream via an Apple TV subscription, providing every match of the MLS regular season, the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs, annual Leagues Cup tournament, the MLS All-Star Game, the Campeones Cup, and more all in one place and with no blackouts. New York City FC is building New York City’s first-ever soccer-specific stadium, Etihad Park, which is set to open in 2027. For more information, please visit: www.newyorkcityfc.com.

New York City FC is also one of 12 clubs comprising City Football Group and counts Manchester City FC, Melbourne City FC, Yokohama F-Marinos, Girona FC, Montevideo City Torque, Shenzhen Peng City FC, Lommel SK, ESTAC Troyes, Palermo FC, Bahia, and Club Bolívar among its sister clubs.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

By clicking "Apply Now", you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.
https://www.mlssoccer.com/legal/privacy-policy

job.title: Senior Manager, Community Engagement
job.html_description:
JOB TITLE: Senior Manager, Community Engagement
REPORTING TO:  Director, Community Development
DEPARTMENT: Community
LOCATION:  New York, NY

POSITION OVERVIEW:
New York City FC is seeking a strategic, relationship-driven Senior Manager, Community Engagement to help lead how the Club engages with communities across New York City.
Reporting to the Director of Community Development, this role combines strategy, relationship management, and execution to strengthen the Club's community impact and visibility. The Senior Manager will lead cross-functional coordination, steward key external partnerships, oversee flagship engagement initiatives, and ensure community priorities are integrated across the organization.

The ideal candidate is a thoughtful planner, exceptional writer, and collaborative leader who enjoys building relationships, developing new ideas, and delivering high-quality work in a fast-paced environment.

WHAT YOU’LL DO:
Cross-Functional Alignment
  • Serve as a strategic partner, helping translate departmental priorities into clear plans and successful execution.
  • Lead Community Department meetings, project planning, and follow-up, ensuring initiatives remain on track and aligned with broader Club priorities.
  • Drive collaboration across Marketing, Communications, Partnerships, Human Resources, Business Intelligence, Ticketing, Stadium Operations, and other departments.
  • Coordinate stakeholders, monitor deliverables, and maintain accountability across key initiatives.
Partnerships & Engagement
  • Develop and manage relationships with nonprofit organizations, schools, community-based organizations, city agencies, civic leaders, and key stakeholders.
  • Identify opportunities to strengthen partnerships through programs, events, fundraising, volunteerism, and matchday experiences.
  • Support community access initiatives connected to Etihad Park, including stakeholder engagement and key community commitments.
  • Represent New York City FC and City in the Community at meetings, events, and community forums.
Programs, Events & Fundraising
  • Lead development and execution of key engagement initiatives, including the Player Ambassador Program, Employee Volunteer Program, MLS community initiatives, community matchday integrations, and the Club's 50/50 raffle.
  • Develop and deliver community events, fundraising initiatives, stakeholder receptions, volunteer opportunities, and supporter engagement experiences.
  • Create meaningful opportunities that connect fans, partners, players, and community organizations to City in the Community's mission.
  • Evaluate programs and recommend improvements that increase participation, visibility, and impact.
Visibility & Impact
  • Increase awareness of New York City FC and City in the Community's work across the Club, New York City, Major League Soccer, and the broader community.
  • Partner with Marketing, Communications, Human Resources, and Business Intelligence to elevate community initiatives through events, content, social media, storytelling, and internal communications.
  • Lead development of the Club's Community Impact Report, executive presentations, award submissions, newsletters, and stakeholder communications.
  • Identify opportunities to strengthen the Club's reputation as a leader in community engagement and social impact.
Department Operations
  • Support annual budget development, fiscal planning, and departmental reporting.
  • Manage full-time and part-time staff, consultants, and volunteers supporting community engagement initiatives.
  • Develop project plans, timelines, and success measures that ensure consistent, high-quality delivery.
  • Continuously improve processes, strengthen collaboration, and identify new opportunities to expand the Club's community impact.

WHAT YOU'LL BRING
  • 5 to 7 years of experience in community engagement, partnership marketing, client management, nonprofit leadership, sports, corporate social responsibility, or a related field.
  • Proven ability to build and manage relationships across diverse stakeholder groups.
  • Experience leading cross-functional initiatives and driving projects from strategy through execution.
  • Strong event planning, fundraising, and partnership management experience.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, including experience developing executive presentations, impact reports, award submissions, and strategic communications.
  • Strong project management, organizational, and budget management skills.
  • A proactive mindset with the ability to anticipate needs, solve problems, and manage multiple priorities.
  • Experience managing staff, consultants, volunteers, or project teams.
  • Passion for community engagement and a commitment to creating meaningful social impact through sport.

WHO YOU ARE:
At New York FC, we are committed to upholding our core club values and our values guide how we show up and work together every day.  If you share our values listed below, please consider NYCFC as a great place for you to work (and play):
Acts Honorably: listens and communicates respectfully, without judgement
Engages Collaboratively: provides and seeks constructive feedback to and from peers, proactively sharing appreciation for quality contributions of others
Champions Inclusion: celebrates the value of differences recognizing the value in the unique backgrounds and lived experiences of every team member
Drives Forward: embraces new opportunities with curiosity and a focus on improvement and innovation
Shows Heart: demonstrates passion for the Club and sport, operating as an NYCFC ambassador in encouraging and inspiring that passion in others

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:
  • Applicant must be able to comply with MLS Next Safety and Well-Being policy and any other applicable child safety policies provided to applicants by NYCFC
  • Must be able to matchdays, community events which may be at night and on weekends and holidays
  •  Local travel throughout New York City is required.

COMPENSATION:
This exempt position has an estimated annual base salary range of $70,000 - $80,000 and is not overtime eligible. Final compensation offer amounts are based on a wide array of factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to demonstrated skill set, years and depth of relevant experience, and some role-dependent factors such as certifications and software expertise. We welcome direct conversations with each candidate about compensation in all of our initial calls.

WHAT WE OFFER:
  • Excellent health, dental & vision insurance, with options to fit you and your family’s needs
  • Comprehensive and competitive benefit programs, including maternal & paternal leave
  • Ancillary benefits: Commuter, and Citi Bike discounts
  • Your health is important! Our wellness strategy focuses on mental and physical wellbeing through in-office and virtual programs
  • Four complementary tickets to every NYCFC home match
  • Team outings, happy hours, and annual staff gatherings to celebrate our co-workers
  • A dynamic, ambitious, and fun work environment

NEW YORK CITY FOOTBALL CLUB OVERVIEW:
At New York City FC, we are on a mission to build this city into one of the soccer capitals of the world. The Club was announced as Major League Soccer’s 20th franchise and is the first and only MLS team located within the five boroughs of New York City. The Club has qualified for the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs nine times over the past 10 seasons, winning MLS Cup in 2021. Games are available to stream via an Apple TV subscription, providing every match of the MLS regular season, the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs, annual Leagues Cup tournament, the MLS All-Star Game, the Campeones Cup, and more all in one place and with no blackouts. New York City FC is building New York City’s first-ever soccer-specific stadium, Etihad Park, which is set to open in 2027. For more information, please visit: www.newyorkcityfc.com.
 
New York City FC is also one of 12 clubs comprising City Football Group and counts Manchester City FC, Melbourne City FC, Yokohama F-Marinos, Girona FC, Montevideo City Torque, Shenzhen Peng City FC, Lommel SK, ESTAC Troyes, Palermo FC, Bahia, and Club Bolívar among its sister clubs.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

By clicking "Apply Now", you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.
https://www.mlssoccer.com/legal/privacy-policy

- 5 to 7 years of experience in community engagement, partnership marketing, client management, nonprofit leadership, sports, corporate social responsibility, or a related field. - Proven ability to build and manage relationships across diverse stakeholder groups. - Experience leading cross-functional initiatives and driving projects from strategy through execution. - Strong event planning, fundraising, and partnership management experience. - Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, including experience developing executive presentations, impact reports, award submissions, and strategic communications. - Strong project management, organizational, and budget management skills. - A proactive mindset with the ability to anticipate needs, solve problems, and manage multiple priorities. - Experience managing staff, consultants, volunteers, or project teams. - Passion for community engagement and a commitment to creating meaningful social impact through sport.
WHO YOU ARE: At New York FC, we are committed to upholding our core club values and our values guide how we show up and work together every day. If you share our values listed below, please consider NYCFC as a great place for you to work (and play): Acts Honorably: listens and communicates respectfully, without judgement
Engages Collaboratively: provides and seeks constructive feedback to and from peers, proactively sharing appreciation for quality contributions of others
Champions Inclusion: celebrates the value of differences recognizing the value in the unique backgrounds and lived experiences of every team member
Drives Forward:embraces new opportunities with curiosity and a focus on improvement and innovation
Shows Heart: demonstrates passion for the Club and sport, operating as an NYCFC ambassador in encouraging and inspiring that passion in others
ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:
- Applicant must be able to comply with MLS Next Safety and Well-Being policy and any other applicable child safety policies provided to applicants by NYCFC - Must be able to matchdays, community events which may be at night and on weekends and holidays - Local travel throughout New York City is required.
COMPENSATION: This exempt position has an estimated annual base salary range of $70,000 - $80,000 and is not overtime eligible. Final compensation offer amounts are based on a wide array of factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to demonstrated skill set, years and depth of relevant experience, and some role-dependent factors such as certifications and software expertise. We welcome direct conversations with each candidate about compensation in all of our initial calls.
WHAT WE OFFER:
- Excellent health, dental & vision insurance, with options to fit you and your family’s needs - Comprehensive and competitive benefit programs, including maternal & paternal leave - Ancillary benefits: Commuter, and Citi Bike discounts - Your health is important! Our wellness strategy focuses on mental and physical wellbeing through in-office and virtual programs - Four complementary tickets to every NYCFC home match - Team outings, happy hours, and annual staff gatherings to celebrate our co-workers - A dynamic, ambitious, and fun work environment
NEW YORK CITY FOOTBALL CLUB OVERVIEW: At New York City FC, we are on a mission to build this city into one of the soccer capitals of the world. The Club was announced as Major League Soccer’s 20th franchise and is the first and only MLS team located within the five boroughs of New York City. The Club has qualified for the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs nine times over the past 10 seasons, winning MLS Cup in 2021. Games are available to stream via an Apple TV subscription, providing every match of the MLS regular season, the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs, annual Leagues Cup tournament, the MLS All-Star Game, the Campeones Cup, and more all in one place and with no blackouts. New York City FC is building New York City’s first-ever soccer-specific stadium, Etihad Park, which is set to open in 2027. For more information, please visit: www.newyorkcityfc.com.
New York City FC is also one of 12 clubs comprising City Football Group and counts Manchester City FC, Melbourne City FC, Yokohama F-Marinos, Girona FC, Montevideo City Torque, Shenzhen Peng City FC, Lommel SK, ESTAC Troyes, Palermo FC, Bahia, and Club Bolívar among its sister clubs.
We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

job.title: Ticket Operations Intern
job.html_description:
JOB TITLE: Intern, Ticket Operations
REPORTING TO: Sr. Coordinator, Ticket Operations
DEPARTMENT: Ticket Operations / Business Intelligence
LOCATION: Hybrid, New York, NY
COMPENSATION: $17/hour, 30 hours per week from July to February

POSITION OVERVIEW:
New York City FC is seeking a seasonal paid Ticket Operations Intern to support day-to-day ticketing functions and contribute to preparations for the club’s transition to its first soccer-specific stadium, Etihad Park, opening in 2027.

This role will play a key part in matchday execution and backend ticketing operations across multiple venues, including Citi Field, Yankee Stadium, and Sports Illustrated Stadium. The intern will assist with ticket fulfillment, refunds, some reporting, and will assist as an internal contact for internal stakeholders. 

WHAT YOU’LL DO: 
  • Learn how to process ticket refunds and account adjustments using Ticketmaster Archtics
  • Gain hands-on experience supporting ticket-related requests across Membership Services, Business Development, and Group Sales. 
  • Develop an understanding of matchday ticket operations, including access control set up and troubleshooting. 
  • Work all NYCFC MLS First Team home matches and gain live event operations experience.
  • Learn how to pull reports and analyze ticketing data for various internal departments.
  • Assist in educating NYCFC fans and internal staff on ticketing processes and best practices. 
  • Collaborate cross functionally to support ticket fulfillment and improve operational efficiency.
  • Contribute to special projects tied to Etihad Park readiness and future operational planning. 
  • Gain exposure to additional areas of ticket operations through various departmental projects and assignments. 
  • Opportunity to work up to 30 hours per week in a fast-paced professional sports environment. Participate in NYCFC’s internship program activities and professional development opportunities across the business. 

WHAT YOU’LL BRING: 
  • Must be a current college student seeking a career in Sports Management/Business (undergraduate or graduate) 
  • Basic knowledge with Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Power Point) 
  • Excellent written and oral communication skills, bilingual is a plus. 
  • Detail oriented with the ability to multi-task. 
  • Customer Service experience strongly preferred 
  • Self-motivated, organized, and able to solve problems in real-time. 
  • Must be comfortable in a fast-paced environment and respond well to changes in direction. 
  • Experience with Ticketmaster Archtics is a plus but not required
  • Availability to work flexible hours including nights, weekends, and some holidays. 
WHO YOU ARE:  
At New York FC, we are committed to upholding our core club values and our values guide how we show up and work together every day.  If you share our values listed below, please consider NYCFC as a great place for you to work (and play): 
 
Acts Honorably: listens and communicates respectfully, without judgement 
Engages Collaboratively: provides and seeks constructive feedback to and from peers, proactively sharing appreciation for quality contributions of others 
Champions Inclusion: celebrates the value of differences recognizing the value in the unique backgrounds and lived experiences of every team member 
Drives Forward: embraces new opportunities with curiosity and a focus on improvement and innovation 
Shows Heart: demonstrates passion for the Club and sport, operating as an NYCFC ambassador in encouraging and inspiring that passion in others 

ADDITIONAL REQUIRMENTS:
  • Applicant must be able to comply with applicable child safety policies provided to applicants by NYCFC
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, as necessary
  • Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer 

WHAT WE OFFER:
  • Two complimentary tickets to every NYCFC home match 
  • Team outings, happy hours, and annual staff gatherings to celebrate our co-workers 
  • A dynamic, ambitious, and fun work environment 

NEW YORK CITY FOOTBALL CLUB OVERVIEW: 
At New York City FC, we are on a mission to build this city into one of the soccer capitals of the world. The Club was announced as Major League Soccer’s 20th franchise and is the first and only MLS team located within the five boroughs of New York City. The Club has qualified for the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs nine times over the past 10 seasons, winning MLS Cup in 2021. Games are available to stream via an Apple TV subscription, providing every match of the MLS regular season, the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs, annual Leagues Cup tournament, the MLS All-Star Game, the Campeones Cup, and more all in one place and with no blackouts. New York City FC is building New York City’s first-ever soccer-specific stadium, Etihad Park, which is set to open in 2027. For more information, please visit: www.newyorkcityfc.com. 
 
New York City FC is also one of 12 clubs comprising City Football Group and counts Manchester City FC, Melbourne City FC, Yokohama F-Marinos, Girona FC, Montevideo City Torque, Shenzhen Peng City FC, Lommel SK, ESTAC Troyes, Palermo FC, Bahia, and Club Bolívar among its sister clubs. 
 
We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. 
 
By clicking "Apply Now", you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy. 
https://www.mlssoccer.com/legal/privacy-policy

job.title: Associate Manager, Branded Content
job.html_description:
JOB TITLE: Associate Manager, Branded Content
REPORTING TO: Senior Account Manager, Branded Content
DEPARTMENT: Marketing
LOCATION: New York, NY

WHAT YOU’LL DO:
  • Support the planning and execution of branded content campaigns across NYCFC-owned channels.
  • Coordinate projects from brief through production, publishing, and reporting.
  • Assist in the development of creative concepts and storytelling opportunities that align with Club and partner objectives.
  • Collaborate with Marketing, Partnerships, Content, Social Media, Community and Matchday teams to execute campaigns.
  • Manage campaign timelines, approvals, deliverables, and stakeholder communication.
  • Support content productions, activations, and events as needed.
  • Work with external agencies, creators, production partners, and vendors to deliver high-quality content.
  • Track campaign performance and assist in the creation of partner recap materials and reports.
  • Stay informed on content trends, fan behavior, and emerging opportunities across sports, entertainment, and digital media.
  • Deliver high-quality marketing and branded content campaigns on schedule, ensuring creative excellence and alignment with organizational objectives.
  • Build and maintain strong commercial partner relationships by providing exceptional service and delivering measurable value through strategic activations and campaigns.
  • Collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams, stakeholders, and external partners to ensure seamless project execution and achievement of business goals.
  • Contribute innovative ideas and creative solutions that enhance fan engagement, strengthen brand presence, and support audience growth initiatives.
  • Demonstrate continuous professional development and leadership potential, building expertise in branded content and positioning for future growth opportunities within the organization.

WHO YOU ARE:
At New York FC, we are committed to upholding our core club values and our values guide how we show up and work together every day.  If you share our values listed below, please consider NYCFC as a great place for you to work (and play):
  • Acts Honorably: listens and communicates respectfully, without judgement
  • Engages Collaboratively: provides and seeks constructive feedback to and from peers, proactively sharing appreciation for quality contributions of others
  • Champions Inclusion: celebrates the value of differences recognizing the value in the unique backgrounds and lived experiences of every team member
  • Drives Forward: embraces new opportunities with curiosity and a focus on improvement and innovation
  • Shows Heart: demonstrates passion for the Club and sport, operating as an NYCFC ambassador in encouraging and inspiring that passion in others

WHAT YOU’LL BRING:
  • 2–4 years of experience in branded content, content marketing, social media, sports marketing, or related field.
  • Strong organizational and project management skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities.
  • Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
  • Experience collaborating with internal stakeholders and external partners.
  • Understanding social media platforms, digital content, and performance metrics.
  • Passion for sports, culture, storytelling, and fan engagement. 
  • Experience working for a sports team, league, agency, media company, or consumer brand is preferred 
  • Experience supporting branded content campaigns, sponsorship activations, or content productions is preferred. 
  • Familiarity with content reporting and analytics tools is preferred

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:
  • Applicant must be able to comply with MLS Next Safety and Well-Being policy and any other applicable child safety policies provided to applicants by NYCFC
  • Must be able to work games scheduled which may be at night and on weekends and holidays

COMPENSATION:
This exempt position has an estimated annual base salary range of $66,500 - $75,000 and is not overtime eligible. Final compensation offer amounts are based on a wide array of factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to demonstrated skill set, years and depth of relevant experience, and some role-dependent factors such as certifications and software expertise. We welcome direct conversations with each candidate about compensation in all of our initial calls.

WHAT WE OFFER:
  • Excellent health, dental & vision insurance, with options to fit you and your family’s needs
  • Comprehensive and competitive benefit programs, including maternal & paternal leave
  • Ancillary benefits: Commuter, gym membership, and Citi Bike discounts
  • Your health is important! Our wellness strategy focuses on mental and physical wellbeing through in-office and virtual programs
  • Four complementary tickets to every NYCFC home match
  • Team outings, happy hours, and annual staff gatherings to celebrate our co-workers
  • A dynamic, ambitious, and fun work environment

NEW YORK CITY FOOTBALL CLUB OVERVIEW:
At New York City FC, we are on a mission to build this city into one of the soccer capitals of the world. The Club was announced as Major League Soccer’s 20th franchise and is the first and only MLS team located within the five boroughs of New York City. The Club has qualified for the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs nine times over the past 10 seasons, winning MLS Cup in 2021. Games are available to stream via an Apple TV subscription, providing every match of the MLS regular season, the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs, annual Leagues Cup tournament, the MLS All-Star Game, the Campeones Cup, and more all in one place and with no blackouts. New York City FC is building New York City’s first-ever soccer-specific stadium, Etihad Park, which is set to open in 2027. For more information, please visit: www.newyorkcityfc.com.

New York City FC is also one of 12 clubs comprising City Football Group and counts Manchester City FC, Melbourne City FC, Yokohama F-Marinos, Girona FC, Montevideo City Torque, Shenzhen Peng City FC, Lommel SK, ESTAC Troyes, Palermo FC, Bahia, and Club Bolívar among its sister clubs.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

By clicking "Apply Now", you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.
https://www.mlssoccer.com/legal/privacy-policy

job.title: Performance Analysis Intern
job.html_description:
JOB TITLE: Performance Analysis Intern
REPORTING TO: Academy Technical Director
DEPARTMENT: Sporting
LOCATION:  St. John’s University (Queens, NY) & CFA New York (Orangeburg, NY)
COMPENSATION: $17/hour, must be able to commit to working for the full duration of the Academy Season (August-May)
 
POSITION OVERVIEW:
The Performance Analysis Intern is responsible for supporting the delivery of performance analysis services within NYCFC’s Academy.
 
WHAT YOU’LL DO:
  • Training: film, code and database daily training sessions as requested.
  • Produce biweekly and monthly training reports.
  • Pre & Post Match: create and organize analysis’ support assets e.g. folder structures, reports templates, etc.
  • Match day: film and basic code Academy games when required
  • Contribute to the overall wider working and development of the Academy program
  • Ensure the operational requirements, such as uploading game footage to the correct platforms, are fulfilled as per deadline requirements. 
  • Attend all Academy, and other, meetings as requested. 
  • Other duties as assigned

WHAT YOU’LL BRING:
  • Must have good knowledge of soccer – either as former player, coach or similar. 
  • Must have proficient computer literacy skills and ability to assimilate new software and processes quickly
  • Familiarity with major Performance Analysis software (Sportscode, Match Analysis, Prozone, Hudl, Taka), Microsoft software (Excel, Powerpoint) and Mac software (Keynote, iBooks Author) strongly preferred
  • Familiarity and experience filming sporting events (ideally soccer) from a coaching perspective is preferred
  • Have flexibility to work differing hours on a weekly basis (including evening and weekends) – as agreed each week – depending on the game and schedule of First and Academy Team
  • Must be able to commit to working for the full duration of the Academy Season (August-May)
  • Ability to manage workload, set deadlines and priorities and utilize time management skills
  • Have an enthusiastic approach and proactive attitude
  • Must have excellent interpersonal and communication skills to effectively convey messages to colleagues, coaches and players
 
WHO YOU ARE:  
At New York FC, we are committed to upholding our core club values and our values guide how we show up and work together every day.  If you share our values listed below, please consider NYCFC as a great place for you to work (and play): 
Acts Honorably: listens and communicates respectfully, without judgement 
Engages Collaboratively: provides and seeks constructive feedback to and from peers, proactively sharing appreciation for quality contributions of others 
Champions Inclusion: celebrates the value of differences recognizing the value in the unique backgrounds and lived experiences of every team member 
Drives Forward: embraces new opportunities with curiosity and a focus on improvement and innovation 
Shows Heart: demonstrates passion for the Club and sport, operating as an NYCFC ambassador in encouraging and inspiring that passion in others 

ADDITIONAL REQUIRMENTS:
  • Applicant must be able to comply with applicable child safety policies provided to applicants by NYCFC
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, as necessary
  • Must be able to lift 15 pounds at times   

 WHAT WE OFFER:
  • Two complimentary tickets to select New York City FC home match
  • Team outings, happy hours, and annual staff gatherings to celebrate our co-workers
  • A dynamic, ambitious, and fun work environment

NEW YORK CITY FOOTBALL CLUB OVERVIEW: 
At New York City FC, we are on a mission to build this city into one of the soccer capitals of the world. The Club was announced as Major League Soccer’s 20th franchise and is the first and only MLS team located within the five boroughs of New York City. Games are available to stream via an Apple TV subscription, providing every match of the MLS regular season, the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs, annual Leagues Cup tournament, the MLS All-Star Game, the Campeones Cup, and more all in one place and with no blackouts. New York City FC is building New York City’s first-ever soccer-specific stadium, Etihad Park, which is set to open in 2027. For more information, please visit: www.newyorkcityfc.com. 
 
New York City FC is also one of 12 clubs comprising City Football Group and counts Manchester City FC, Melbourne City FC, Yokohama F-Marinos, Girona FC, Montevideo City Torque, Shenzhen Peng City FC, Lommel SK, ESTAC Troyes, Palermo FC, Bahia, and Club Bolívar among its sister clubs. 
 
We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Drives Forward:embraces new opportunities with curiosity and a focus on improvement and innovation
Shows Heart: demonstrates passion for the Club and sport, operating as an NYCFC ambassador in encouraging and inspiring that passion in others
ADDITIONAL REQUIRMENTS:
- Applicant must be able to comply with applicable child safety policies provided to applicants by NYCFC - Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, as necessary - Must be able to lift 15 pounds at times
WHAT WE OFFER:
- Two complimentary tickets to select New York City FC home match - Team outings, happy hours, and annual staff gatherings to celebrate our co-workers - A dynamic, ambitious, and fun work environment
NEW YORK CITY FOOTBALL CLUB OVERVIEW:
At New York City FC, we are on a mission to build this city into one of the soccer capitals of the world. The Club was announced as Major League Soccer’s 20th franchise and is the first and only MLS team located within the five boroughs of New York City. The Club has qualified for the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs nine times over the past 10 seasons, winning MLS Cup in 2021. Games are available to stream via an Apple TV subscription, providing every match of the MLS regular season, the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs, annual Leagues Cup tournament, the MLS All-Star Game, the Campeones Cup, and more all in one place and with no blackouts. New York City FC is building New York City’s first-ever soccer-specific stadium, Etihad Park, which is set to open in 2027. For more information, please visit: www.newyorkcityfc.com.
New York City FC is also one of 12 clubs comprising City Football Group and counts Manchester City FC, Melbourne City FC, Yokohama F-Marinos, Girona FC, Montevideo City Torque, Shenzhen Peng City FC, Lommel SK, ESTAC Troyes, Palermo FC, Bahia, and Club Bolívar among its sister clubs.
We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

job.title: Per Diem Academy Athletic Trainer, Part-Time
job.html_description:
JOB TITLE: Per Diem Academy Athletic Trainer, Part-Time
REPORTING TO: Head of Performance Services
DEPARTMENT: Sporting, Academy
LOCATION: St John’s University, Queens College, City Football Academy-Orangeburg
COMPENSATION: $50-65 per hour, commensurate with experience and qualifications. 5-25 hours per week.

POSITION OVERVIEW:
The Part-Time Academy Athletic Trainer (AT) supports the daily sports medicine operations for NYCFC Academy teams by providing medical coverage for trainings, matches, tournaments, and club events. This role is responsible for supporting injury prevention sessions, emergency care, injury evaluation, treatment, and assisting with the delivery of high-quality medical services that support player health and long-term athlete development.

Working closely with the full-time medical and performance staff, the Part-Time Academy Athletic Trainer helps deliver a safe, professional, and player-centered environment while supporting the development of the next generation of NYCFC players.

WHAT YOU’LL DO:
  • Provide athletic training coverage for Academy training, matches, tournaments, and special events.
  • Perform emergency care, first aid and immediate injury management.
  • Deliver treatment and recovery interventions within scope of practice.
  • Collaborate with Performance staff to support player health, recovery, and testing batteries.
  • Represent NYCFC professionally and contribute to a positive, player-centered environment.

WHAT YOU’LL BRING:
  • Certified Athletic Trainer (ATC) with an active New York State License.
  • BOC Certified and member in good standing with NATA (or CATA).
  • Current CPR/AED and First Aid certification.
  • Minimum 1 year of experience in sports medicine preferred.
  • Experience working with soccer and/or youth or high-performance athletes preferred.
  • Strong communication and organizational skills.
  • Bilingual (Spanish) is a plus.
  • Professional liability insurance required.

WHO YOU ARE:  
At New York FC, we are committed to upholding our core club values and our values guide how we show up and work together every day.  If you share our values listed below, please consider NYCFC as a great place for you to work (and play): 
 
Acts Honorably: listens and communicates respectfully, without judgement 
Engages Collaboratively: provides and seeks constructive feedback to and from peers, proactively sharing appreciation for quality contributions of others 
Champions Inclusion: celebrates the value of differences recognizing the value in the unique backgrounds and lived experiences of every team member 
Drives Forward: embraces new opportunities with curiosity and a focus on improvement and innovation 
Shows Heart: demonstrates passion for the Club and sport, operating as an NYCFC ambassador in encouraging and inspiring that passion in others 

ADDITIONAL REQUIRMENTS:
  • Applicant must be able to comply with applicable child safety policies provided to applicants by NYCFC
  • Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, as necessary
  • Must be able to lift 15 pounds at times   

WHAT WE OFFER:
  • Two complimentary tickets to every NYCFC home match 
  • Team outings, happy hours, and annual staff gatherings to celebrate our co-workers 
  • A dynamic, ambitious, and fun work environment 

NEW YORK CITY FOOTBALL CLUB OVERVIEW: 
At New York City FC, we are on a mission to build this city into one of the soccer capitals of the world. The Club was announced as Major League Soccer’s 20th franchise and is the first and only MLS team located within the five boroughs of New York City. The Club has qualified for the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs nine times over the past 10 seasons, winning MLS Cup in 2021. Games are available to stream via an Apple TV subscription, providing every match of the MLS regular season, the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs, annual Leagues Cup tournament, the MLS All-Star Game, the Campeones Cup, and more all in one place and with no blackouts. New York City FC is building New York City’s first-ever soccer-specific stadium, Etihad Park, which is set to open in 2027. For more information, please visit: www.newyorkcityfc.com. 
 
New York City FC is also one of 12 clubs comprising City Football Group and counts Manchester City FC, Melbourne City FC, Yokohama F-Marinos, Girona FC, Montevideo City Torque, Shenzhen Peng City FC, Lommel SK, ESTAC Troyes, Palermo FC, Bahia, and Club Bolívar among its sister clubs. 
 
We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. 
 
By clicking "Apply Now", you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy.

job.title: Academy Athletic Trainer
job.html_description:
JOB TITLE: Academy Athletic Trainer
REPORTING TO: Head of Performance Services, Academy
DEPARTMENT: Sporting, Academy
LOCATION:  St John's University, Queens / City Football Academy, Orangeburg

POSITION OVERVIEW:
New York City FC is seeking an Academy Athletic Trainer (AT). This role plays a vital role in the daily sports medicine operations for NYCFC’s Academy teams. This individual will oversee injury prevention, emergency care, injury evaluation, player treatment, and health administration [DH2.1]for Academy players, to support holistic performance development. The Academy AT will collaborate closely with the Lead Academy AT and Head of Performance Services to deliver top-tier care and performance programming aligned with the club’s methodology and long-term athlete development goals.

This is a dynamic role that blends hands-on athletic training, data-informed performance support, and collaboration across departments to help guide and develop NYCFC’s next generation of professional players.  It is through the Academy pathway that our First Team develops Homegrown talent, ensuring a strong and vibrant future while embodying the club's identity and values.  The successful candidate will align with these values and understand that they are role models for our players on and off the pitch.

WHAT YOU’LL DO:
Medical & Athletic Training Duties
  • Serve as an Athletic Trainer for NYCFC Academy teams during trainings and/or matches (Home and Away)
  • Provide medical services including injury assessment, emergency care, and ongoing treatment
  • Maintain accurate, up-to-date medical records using our athlete management system
  • Coordinate medical referrals and collaborate with physicians as needed
  • Manage medical inventory and ensure clinical equipment is in working order
Performance & Interdisciplinary Collaboration
  • Partner with Performance staff to deliver comprehensive athlete development, including:
    • Strength and conditioning support
    • Warm-up routines and mobility programs
    • Load management and recovery strategies
    • Nutrition and hydration education
    • Data monitoring 
  • Assist with fitness assessments, training periodization, and preseason/offseason planning.
  • Actively communicate with technical staff, players, and families to align care plans and progress.
Additional Contributions
  • Participate in NYCFC’s Youth Affiliate Education initiatives.
  • Contribute to a positive and collaborative environment within the Academy and wider NYCFC organization.
  • Work closely with NYCFC II and First Team departments to ensure continuity in care and long-term player development.

WHAT YOU’LL BRING: 
  • Certified Athletic Trainer (ATC) with an active NY State License.
  • Member in good standing with NATA (or CATA); NATABOC certified.
  • Minimum 1-2 years of professional experience in sports medicine (preferably with youth or high-performance athletes).
  • Current CPR and emergency care certification.
  • Certified Strength and Conditioning Specialist (CSCS) preferred.
  • Previous experience in soccer and/or with youth athletes is highly desirable.
  • Bilingual (Spanish) a plus.
  • Must hold personal professional liability insurance.

WHO YOU ARE:  
At New York FC, we are committed to upholding our core club values and our values guide how we show up and work together every day.  If you share our values listed below, please consider NYCFC as a great place for you to work (and play): 
 
Acts Honorably: listens and communicates respectfully, without judgement 
Engages Collaboratively: provides and seeks constructive feedback to and from peers, proactively sharing appreciation for quality contributions of others 
Champions Inclusion: celebrates the value of differences recognizing the value in the unique backgrounds and lived experiences of every team member 
Drives Forward: embraces new opportunities with curiosity and a focus on improvement and innovation 
Shows Heart: demonstrates passion for the Club and sport, operating as an NYCFC ambassador in encouraging and inspiring that passion in others 

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:
  • Applicants must be able to comply with MLS Next Safety and Well-Being policy and any other applicable child safety policies provided to applicants by NYCFC
  • Willingness to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and travel for matches and camps.

COMPENSATION:
This exempt position has an estimated annual base salary of $62,355 and is not overtime eligible. Final compensation offer amounts are based on a wide array of factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to demonstrated skill set, years and depth of relevant experience, and some role-dependent factors such as certifications and software expertise. We welcome direct conversations with each candidate about compensation in all of our initial calls.

WHAT WE OFFER:
  • Two complimentary tickets to every NYCFC home match 
  • Team outings, happy hours, and annual staff gatherings to celebrate our co-workers 
  • A dynamic, ambitious, and fun work environment 

NEW YORK CITY FOOTBALL CLUB OVERVIEW: 
At New York City FC, we are on a mission to build this city into one of the soccer capitals of the world. The Club was announced as Major League Soccer’s 20th franchise and is the first and only MLS team located within the five boroughs of New York City. The Club has qualified for the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs nine times over the past 10 seasons, winning MLS Cup in 2021. Games are available to stream via an Apple TV subscription, providing every match of the MLS regular season, the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs, annual Leagues Cup tournament, the MLS All-Star Game, the Campeones Cup, and more all in one place and with no blackouts. New York City FC is building New York City’s first-ever soccer-specific stadium, Etihad Park, which is set to open in 2027. For more information, please visit: www.newyorkcityfc.com. 
 
New York City FC is also one of 12 clubs comprising City Football Group and counts Manchester City FC, Melbourne City FC, Yokohama F-Marinos, Girona FC, Montevideo City Torque, Shenzhen Peng City FC, Lommel SK, ESTAC Troyes, Palermo FC, Bahia, and Club Bolívar among its sister clubs. 
 
We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. 
 
By clicking "Apply Now", you agree to the terms of the MLS Privacy Policy. 
https://www.mlssoccer.com/legal/privacy-policy
