Support ongoing alignment and integration between the WNT and U23/WYNT pathways. - Maintain and oversee key documentation related to strategic planning, pathways, and department initiatives. - Contribute to succession planning and organizational continuity by embedding repeatable systems, documentation, and processes. - Support internal and external stakeholder mapping and coordination in collaboration with the Strategy department. - Reduce administrative burden on senior leadership, enabling greater focus on strategy, relationships, and decision-making. - Undertake a progressive and ongoing personal and professional developmental process attending and participating in house CPD sessions and external sessions. - Undertake regular performance appraisals to review your personal development plan and on and off camp progress with WNT Leadership and line manager. - Execute additional departmental and team tasks as requested by WNT Strategy Team.
Requirements
Minimum Qualifications
- Bachelor’s degree required; Master’s degree in Business, or a related field preferred. - Demonstrated ability to manage complex projects, timelines, and stakeholders, translating strategy into execution. - Strong organizational, communication, and follow-through skills in fast-paced, high-performance environments. - Experience working in elite sport or high-performance settings, preferably within soccer. - Strategic, detail-oriented, and proactive, with sound judgment and discretion. - Comfortable operating across multiple departments and senior stakeholders. - Must be able to connect “micro” details to the “macro” vision and mission. - Experience with project management and collaboration tools (e.g., Microsoft Office, Airtable, Asana, Smartsheet, Notion or similar). - Strong alignment with women’s sport and athlete-centered, female-specific performance environments. - Able and willing to travel up to 50% and work non-traditional hours as required.
Desired Qualifications
- Strong passion for and understanding of women’s soccer. - Strong understanding of elite football operations and camp environments, gained through professional experience. - International soccer experience, including exposure to major tournaments, as a player and/or staff member. - Strong soccer IQ, with understanding of competition calendars, camp demands, and international football environments. - Experience working within U.S. Soccer, NWSL, USOPC, or comparable national governing bodies preferred. - Background in sport strategy, performance operations, or high-performance program management. - Experience working in women’s high-performance environments, ideally through a female-athlete lens. - Demonstrated ability to operate as a strategic thought partner to senior leadership. - Experience building and maintaining systems and processes that scale across multi-year cycles. - Familiarity with project management and tracking systems. - Bilingual (English/Spanish or other languages) a plus.
U.S. Soccer offers a comprehensive compensation package, casual work environment, an inclusive culture, and an atmosphere for professional development.
U.S. Soccer is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion, and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind on the basis of race, color, sex, religion, national origin, citizenship, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, genetic information, military status, political belief, or any other characteristic protected under the law. This policy applies to all our employment practices within our organization. We strongly encourage women, people of color, LGBTQIA, veterans, parents, and persons with disabilities to apply.

job.title: Director, Business Ventures
job.html_description:
Description


U.S. Soccer Overview


The U.S. Soccer Federation exists in service to soccer. Our aim is to ignite a national passion for the game. Because we believe that soccer is more than a sport; it is a force for good.


We understand the importance and the power of teamwork, on and off the pitch. That is why we work closely with our Federation partners and members, to inspire, support and guide every level of the game: from the grassroots to the National Teams. We want to bring soccer into every home and every community, right across America. Because we believe that soccer can transform lives like no other sport. Soccer can represent the best of U.S.


U.S. Soccer is in a period of significant growth, with ambitious plans for U.S. Soccer in the near and far future. We are, therefore, looking for dynamic servant leaders to join us on this journey: in service to soccer. Applicants must be able to demonstrate visionary leadership, analytical decision-making, professional flexibility, and an empathic management style that builds bold teams and delivers globally significant results.


Position Description


We’re seeking a strategic thinker with dogged operational skills to help drive business initiatives in new and emerging spaces. The next decade of soccer in the U.S. presents massive opportunity but also major challenges, and U.S. Soccer will need to continue to evolve with the sport to reach its growth objectives. This position will help set high-level goals within the Commercial team – with a focus on growth opportunities – while also aligning those goals with day-to-day execution.


The Director will join a team of Analysts and Data Scientists focused on optimizing the business, nurturing data-driven decisions, and ultimately driving growth via new partnerships. They’ll also work closely with every unit within the Commercial organization, and some Sporting stakeholders as well, to understand current state and identify collaborative opportunities to unlock more value for U.S. Soccer and its stakeholders. Focus areas will include new properties/platforms and strategic partnerships within the media, technology, high performance, and scouting spaces.


The ideal candidate excels at balancing the big picture with smooth execution of major projects. They’ll have sharp analytics skills, an obsession with details, great communication, and a knack for relationship building. A deep understanding of the media, gaming, and the broader technology landscape within sports will also be critical.


Primary Responsibilities

  • Opportunity hunting: Internal audits, market analyses, business cases, and vetting to steer how we allocate time and resources. Making critical connections to nurture partnerships, with an eye toward Sporting technology needs
  • Standing up new partnerships: Shepherding new projects from ideation to planning to execution and monetization. These could be commercial-only in nature or also serve broader needs for our soccer staff/players. Organizing and mobilizing cross-functional teams based on the nature of each deal
  • Content reach and relevancy: Establishing and managing strategic partnerships with 3rd-party media platforms and creators to extend our reach among soccer fans and new audiences
  • Partner management: Managing existing partners and developing new ones. You’ll be the day-to-day contact with critical commercial partners across our new ventures (e.g., The Soccer Tournament)
  • Budget management: Working closely with each Commercial unit to track and forecast revenue and expenses. Communicate status across, up, and down the organization

Requirements


Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Business, STEM, or other analytical field
  • At least 5 years of experience in roles focused on growth strategy within the technology or media spaces
  • Technologist background – you have a clear understanding of the emerging technology trends within the sports/entertainment space and can demonstrate a history of leveraging new technologies to unlock new value for customers/fans
  • Strategic thinking – you consistently anticipate and see the big picture, where to go, and how to get there
  • Sharp analytical skills – you can design quantitative frameworks to solve business problems and have an eye for finding the signal within the noise quickly
  • Proficient with business intelligence tools – advanced BI (Tableau or Power BI) and Excel skills (IF functions, macros, array formulas)
  • Detail-oriented – you can’t stand sloppiness in execution; details matter more to you than most others because of what it says about your work
  • Polished presenter – tailoring the message to the audience; you’re concise yet thorough and combine it with impeccably-packaged visuals
  • Self-starter – you thrive when allowed to proactively manage your time toward goals rather than reacting to specific tasks
  • General knowledge of soccer
  • Capable of working in fast-paced and demanding environments
  • Must be able to connect “micro” details to the “macro” vision and mission
  • Proficient experience with Microsoft Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and Outlook)

Desired Qualifications

  • Master’s degree in Business, Analytics, STEM, or other analytical field
  • Passion for soccer and a solid understanding of the soccer landscape in the United States


U.S. Soccer offers a comprehensive compensation package, casual work environment, an inclusive culture, and an atmosphere for professional development.


U.S. Soccer is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion, and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind on the basis of race, color, sex, religion, national origin, citizenship, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, genetic information, military status, political belief, or any other characteristic protected under the law. This policy applies to all our employment practices within our organization. We strongly encourage women, people of color, LGBTQIA, veterans, parents, and persons with disabilities to apply.

This policy applies to all our employment practices within our organization. We strongly encourage women, people of color, LGBTQIA, veterans, parents, and persons with disabilities to apply.

job.title: Podcast Producer
job.html_description:
Description


U.S. Soccer Overview


The U.S. Soccer Federation exists in service to soccer. Our aim is to ignite a national passion for the game. Because we believe that soccer is more than a sport; it is a force for good.


We understand the importance and the power of teamwork, on and off the pitch. That is why we work closely with our Federation partners and members, to inspire, support and guide every level of the game: from the grassroots to the National Teams. We want to bring soccer into every home and every community, right across America. Because we believe that soccer can transform lives like no other sport. Soccer can represent the best of U.S.


U.S. Soccer is in a period of significant growth, with ambitious plans for U.S. Soccer in the near and far future. We are, therefore, looking for dynamic servant leaders to join us on this journey: in service to soccer. Applicants must be able to demonstrate visionary leadership, analytical decision-making, professional flexibility, and an empathic management style that builds bold teams and delivers globally significant results.


Position Description


U.S. Soccer is seeking an experienced producer and audio/video storyteller to lead the day-to-day editorial, production and distribution of the U.S. Soccer Podcast. The Podcast Producer will create compelling conversations and original segments that bring fans closer to the people, stories and moments shaping soccer in the United States, from the Senior National Teams to the broader Federation ecosystem.


This role will own the full podcast lifecycle, from editorial planning and guest development through recording, editing, publishing, audience growth and performance reporting. The producer will create programming and integrated segments that support U.S. Soccer’s commercial, marketing and membership priorities while maintaining the editorial quality, authenticity and credibility of the show.


The Podcast Producer will build adaptable, repeatable production playbooks for international camps, tournaments, match days and other major Federation moments. They will help create a year-round content engine that keeps soccer fans engaged through an audience-led approach and close collaboration with Editorial, Social Media, Video, Partnerships Marketing and other stakeholders across the organization.


We are seeking a relationship-driven producer with the ability to collaborate, communicate and build trust with a wide range of internal and external stakeholders. Travel to team events, camps and matches will be required as needed.


Primary Responsibilities

  • Own the editorial development of the U.S. Soccer Podcast, including episode concepts, guest strategy, run of show, interview preparation, segment development and post-production storytelling.
  • Produce each episode as a multi-platform content package, including full-length audio and video episodes, short-form social clips, promotional assets, episode titles, descriptions, thumbnails and publishing metadata.
  • Produce live and live-to-tape podcast programming around matches, camps, tournaments and major Federation moments, including technical planning, guest coordination, run of show and real-time editorial decision-making.
  • Develop scalable production workflows for studio, remote, on-location and live podcast environments.
  • Build, organize and manage the podcast content calendar, including episode planning, campaign integration and release schedules, in collaboration with stakeholders throughout the organization.
  • Work closely with internal stakeholders to develop episode topics and interview outlines that align with U.S. Soccer’s brand, editorial standards and messaging.
  • Build trusted relationships with athletes, coaches, technical staff, communications teams, external guests and other stakeholders to secure meaningful access and produce thoughtful, well-prepared conversations.
  • Exercise sound editorial judgment and discretion when working with confidential, sensitive or embargoed information.
  • Partner with Editorial, Social Media, Video, Post-Production and Marketing teams to identify and deliver high-impact clips, promotional assets and supporting content around each episode.
  • Develop and execute audience-growth and distribution strategies across podcast platforms, YouTube, social media and other relevant channels.
  • Establish and report against podcast KPIs, including audience growth, listens and views, watch time, completion rate, subscriber growth, social engagement, Insiders conversion and commercial deliverables.
  • Use audience insights and performance data to refine editorial strategy, publishing cadence, distribution and promotional tactics.
  • Collaborate with Partnerships Marketing on the ideation and development of integrated segments and branded content, ensuring all deliverables meet partner objectives while preserving the authenticity and audience value of the show.
  • Coordinate podcast production logistics, including equipment, studio needs, remote-recording workflows, vendors, freelancers and production budgets, as applicable.
  • Stay current on podcast, YouTube, social video and audience-development trends, applying new formats, platform features and distribution tactics where they can strengthen the show.

Requirements


Minimum Qualifications

  • 5+ years of experience producing podcasts, audio programming, video podcasts or comparable editorial content, with demonstrated ownership of projects from concept through distribution.
  • Demonstrated experience producing video podcasts and/or YouTube-first programming.
  • Experience producing live, remote and on-location recordings.
  • Strong command of Adobe Creative Cloud, including Adobe Audition and Premiere Pro, or comparable audio/video production tools.
  • Proficient experience with Microsoft Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and Outlook).
  • Experience with podcast hosting, publishing and analytics platforms.
  • Strong writing, scripting and interview-preparation skills, with the ability to craft compelling narratives and conversations.
  • Demonstrated ability to manage multiple projects, shifting priorities and fast-turn deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
  • Strong organizational, communication and stakeholder-management skills.
  • Ability to work effectively with a broad range of internal and external stakeholders, including talent, communications teams, partners and vendors.
  • Passion for soccer and an understanding of the U.S. sports and soccer ecosystem.
  • Ability and willingness to travel and work non-traditional hours, including evenings and weekends, as needed.
  • When not traveling, ability to work from U.S. Soccer’s headquarters in Atlanta, Georgia.
  • Demonstrated discretion and sound judgment when handling confidential information.
  • Must be able to connect “micro” details to the “macro” vision and mission.
  • Portfolio or work samples demonstrating podcast, interview, audio storytelling and/or video production experience.

Desired Qualifications

  • Experience in sports media, digital media, journalism, entertainment or another fast-paced editorial environment.
  • Experience producing live sports, event-based programming or rapid-turn content around major moments.
  • Spanish-language proficiency.
  • Familiarity with multimedia storytelling, including video production, social video and digital publishing.
  • Strong command of AP style and soccer terminology.
  • Experience managing freelancers, vendors, production budgets or external production partners.


U.S. Soccer offers a comprehensive compensation package, casual work environment, an inclusive culture, and an atmosphere for professional development.


U.S. Soccer is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion, and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind on the basis of race, color, sex, religion, national origin, citizenship, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, genetic information, military status, political belief, or any other characteristic protected under the law. This policy applies to all our employment practices within our organization. We strongly encourage women, people of color, LGBTQIA, veterans, parents, and persons with disabilities to apply.

This policy applies to all our employment practices within our organization. We strongly encourage women, people of color, LGBTQIA, veterans, parents, and persons with disabilities to apply.

job.title: Nike Performance Innovation Lead
job.html_description:
Description


Position Description


Nike is seeking an applied research and innovation specialist with a strong foundation in sport science and experience working in elite high-performance environments to consult in the newly created Performance Innovation Lab at the National Training Center in Fayetteville, Georgia. The ideal candidate will combine scientific thinking with practical delivery, contributing to applied research initiatives while supporting the development and operation of the Performance Innovation Lab at US Soccer’s Arthur M. Blank National Training Center, as well as other Nike initiatives and sponsored athletes. Funded in partnership with Nike, Inc., this initiative is designed to advance the science and practice of elite soccer performance across all 27-U.S. Soccer National Teams—from youth development through senior international competition.  


This role sits at the intersection of elite applied sport science and rigorous research, and given its location, will be embedded within U.S. Soccer’s daily operations while maintaining a strategic research agenda that generates new knowledge for the federation, its athletes, and the broader U.S.  soccer ecosystem. As a Nike position, the role will also support select non-soccer Nike initiatives and athletes who access the facility and Performance Innovation Lab, creating cross-sport research opportunities.  


Employment Notice 


This position is contracted by Nike, Inc. Although posted on U.S. Soccer's career site and based at the U.S. Soccer Arthur M. Blank National Training Center, the successful candidate will be contracted solely by Nike and will be subject to Nike's code of conduct and handbook. Collaboration with and day-to-day direction from U.S. Soccer personnel does not create an employment, joint employment, agency, or contractual relationship with U.S. Soccer, which assumes no liability for employment-related matters associated with this role. By submitting an application for this position, applicants acknowledge that this will be a one year contracted role with potential for extension and that they have read and understand this Employment Notice.


Primary Responsibilities


This role will operate across two interconnected domains: 


 Applied Research

  • Support the design and execution of applied research projects aligned with performance priorities, including data collection, analysis, and interpretation 
  • Develop and maintain a multi-year research agenda aligned with federation performance priorities and Nike’s innovation interests 
  • Build longitudinal datasets—including biomechanical profiles—spanning youth through senior levels across men’s and women’s programs 
  • Identify cross-team performance patterns, developmental benchmarks, and evidence-based recommendations for coaching and performance staff 
  • Pursue external collaborations with universities, research institutions, and sport science organizations to extend the reach and rigor of the research program 
  • Present findings to internal stakeholders, at professional conferences, and through peer reviewed publication 

Practitioner Services

  • Deliver biomechanical and performance science assessments to National Teams during training camps and residencies at the Arthur M. Blank National Training Center 
  • Develop standardized assessment protocols and normative databases that can be administered across all 27 teams, including by other qualified staff 
  • Support Integrated Performance Support and Sporting staff by providing evidence-informed insights contributing to training, loading, recovery, and performance optimization decisions 
  • Support select Nike-sponsored athletes from other sports who access the lab and campus, facilitating cross-sport data collection and innovation 
  • Female 360: Contribute expertise to the design and delivery of U.S. Soccer's Female 360 health assessments and initiatives, supporting a holistic, athlete-centered approach to women's health and performance 
  • Contribute to the development of a scalable service model delivered through the Performance Innovation Lab (e.g., testing, profiling, applied workshops, educational visits) 
  • Translate research insights into practical, actionable tools and recommendations for coaching and performance staff 

Lab & Campus Innovation 

  • Support the procurement, commissioning, and ongoing execution of projects at the Performance Innovation Lab at the US Soccer Arthur M. Blank National Training Center 
  • Drive the “campus-as-lab” vision: identify opportunities to instrument training environments, integrate technology into daily operations, and turn routine activities into data collection opportunities 
  • Evaluate emerging technologies, methodologies, and tools for potential integration into the lab and broader campus infrastructure 
  • Establish data governance, quality assurance, and athlete data privacy protocols for all research and assessment activities

Requirements


Minimum Qualifications

  • Doctoral degree (PhD or equivalent) in Sport Science, Kinesiology, Biomechanics, Human Movement Science, Sport Physiology, or a closely related discipline 
  • Minimum of 8 years of combined experience in applied sport science research and elite sport performance support, including at least 3 years in a high-performance sport environment (e.g., professional club, national federation, institute of sport) 
  • Experience working across both men’s and women’s sport programs 
  • Demonstrated expertise in biomechanical assessment methodologies, including experience with force platforms, 3D motion capture, inertial measurement units, and/or pressure mapping systems 
  • A peer-reviewed publication record that reflects both scientific rigor and applied relevance 
  •  Experience designing and managing multi-site or multi-team research projects 
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with a demonstrated ability to translate complex scientific concepts for coaches, medical staff, and organizational leadership 
  • Ability to operate independently with minimal supervision while navigating a complex, multistakeholder organizational environment

Preferred Qualifications 

  • Experience in elite football/soccer environments at the club or international level  
  • Familiarity with the demands and structures of national team programs, including athlete availability constraints and multi-team coordination 
  • Experience with longitudinal athlete monitoring and developmental tracking across age groups 
  • Experience working with advanced soccer tracking technologies, including player surface interaction technology, is considered a strong advantage 
  • Background in or exposure to sport technology innovation, product development, or industry academic partnerships 
  • Proficiency with data management platforms, statistical software, and visualization tools used in applied sport science

What This Role Offers 

  • A newly created role within a new, strategically significant initiative backed by both the U.S. Soccer Federation and Nike, Inc. 
  •  Access to a purpose-built Performance Innovation Lab at a world-class National Training Center 
  • Unparalleled population access: 27 National Teams spanning youth through senior, men’s and women’s, all operating from a centralized campus 
  • The opportunity to build longitudinal datasets and research programs that have no existing equivalent in world football 
  • A platform for high-impact publication, professional visibility, and thought leadership in sport science 
  • Cross-sport exposure through Nike’s broader athlete network and innovation ecosystem 

Key Relationships


The role maintains collaborative working relationships with National Team Integrated Performance, support staff, Nike’s Sport Research Lab (NSRL) and innovation teams, and external academic and research partners. 

 

Governance & Accountability


The role reports into the Nike’s NSRL Team in Beaverton, OR and U.S. Soccer’s Performance  Innovation Team in Fayetteville, GA. U.S. Soccer research priorities are set collaboratively between U.S. Soccer leadership and Nike, with a defined engagement cadence (e.g., quarterly research updates, annual innovation reviews). The role holder is expected to maintain scientific independence and ethical standards in all research activities. 


Day-to-day project priorities may be coordinated with designated U.S. Soccer personnel; however, Nike retains sole responsibility for all contract-related matters. Nothing in this reporting or collaborative structure shall be interpreted as creating an employment relationship between the employee and U.S. Soccer.


To Apply


Interested candidates should submit a curriculum vitae, a cover letter addressing their vision for the role, and the names of three professional references. Review of applications will begin immediately and continue until the position is filled.


Nike is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity in its workforce.
job.plain_text_description:
Description
Position Description
Nike is seeking an applied research and innovation specialist with a strong foundation in sport science and experience working in elite high-performance environments to consult in the newly created Performance Innovation Lab at the National Training Center in Fayetteville, Georgia. The ideal candidate will combine scientific thinking with practical delivery, contributing to applied research initiatives while supporting the development and operation of the Performance Innovation Lab at US Soccer’s Arthur M. Blank National Training Center, as well as other Nike initiatives and sponsored athletes. Funded in partnership with Nike, Inc., this initiative is designed to advance the science and practice of elite soccer performance across all 27-U.S. Soccer National Teams—from youth development through senior international competition.
This role sits at the intersection of elite applied sport science and rigorous research, and given its location, will be embedded within U.S. Soccer’s daily operations while maintaining a strategic research agenda that generates new knowledge for the federation, its athletes, and the broader U.S. soccer ecosystem. As a Nike position, the role will also support select non-soccer Nike initiatives and athletes who access the facility and Performance Innovation Lab, creating cross-sport research opportunities.
Employment Notice
This position is contracted by Nike, Inc. Although posted on U.S. Soccer's career site and based at the U.S. Soccer Arthur M. Blank National Training Center, the successful candidate will be contracted solely by Nike and will be subject to Nike's code of conduct and handbook. Collaboration with and day-to-day direction from U.S. Soccer personnel does not create an employment, joint employment, agency, or contractual relationship with U.S. Soccer, which assumes no liability for employment-related matters associated with this role. By submitting an application for this position, applicants acknowledge that this will be a one year contracted role with potential for extension and that they have read and understand this Employment Notice.
Primary Responsibilities
This role will operate across two interconnected domains:
Applied Research
- Support the design and execution of applied research projects aligned with performance priorities, including data collection, analysis, and interpretation - Develop and maintain a multi-year research agenda aligned with federation performance priorities and Nike’s innovation interests - Build longitudinal datasets—including biomechanical profiles—spanning youth through senior levels across men’s and women’s programs - Identify cross-team performance patterns, developmental benchmarks, and evidence-based recommendations for coaching and performance staff - Pursue external collaborations with universities, research institutions, and sport science organizations to extend the reach and rigor of the research program - Present findings to internal stakeholders, at professional conferences, and through peer reviewed publication
Practitioner Services
- Deliver biomechanical and performance science assessments to National Teams during training camps and residencies at the Arthur M. Blank National Training Center - Develop standardized assessment protocols and normative databases that can be administered across all 27 teams, including by other qualified staff - Support Integrated Performance Support and Sporting staff by providing evidence-informed insights contributing to training, loading, recovery, and performance optimization decisions - Support select Nike-sponsored athletes from other sports who access the lab and campus, facilitating cross-sport data collection and innovation - Female 360: Contribute expertise to the design and delivery of U.S. Soccer's Female 360 health assessments and initiatives, supporting a holistic, athlete-centered approach to women's health and performance - Contribute to the development of a scalable service model delivered through the Performance Innovation Lab (e.g., testing, profiling, applied workshops, educational visits) - Translate research insights into practical, actionable tools and recommendations for coaching and performance staff
Lab & Campus Innovation
- Support the procurement, commissioning, and ongoing execution of projects at the Performance Innovation Lab at the US Soccer Arthur M. Blank National Training Center - Drive the “campus-as-lab” vision: identify opportunities to instrument training environments, integrate technology into daily operations, and turn routine activities into data collection opportunities - Evaluate emerging technologies, methodologies, and tools for potential integration into the lab and broader campus infrastructure - Establish data governance, quality assurance, and athlete data privacy protocols for all research and assessment activities
Requirements
Minimum Qualifications
- Doctoral degree (PhD or equivalent) in Sport Science, Kinesiology, Biomechanics, Human Movement Science, Sport Physiology, or a closely related discipline - Minimum of 8 years of combined experience in applied sport science research and elite sport performance support, including at least 3 years in a high-performance sport environment (e.g., professional club, national federation, institute of sport) - Experience working across both men’s and women’s sport programs - Demonstrated expertise in biomechanical assessment methodologies, including experience with force platforms, 3D motion capture, inertial measurement units, and/or pressure mapping systems - A peer-reviewed publication record that reflects both scientific rigor and applied relevance - Experience designing and managing multi-site or multi-team research projects - Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with a demonstrated ability to translate complex scientific concepts for coaches, medical staff, and organizational leadership - Ability to operate independently with minimal supervision while navigating a complex, multistakeholder organizational environment
Preferred Qualifications
- Experience in elite football/soccer environments at the club or international level - Familiarity with the demands and structures of national team programs, including athlete availability constraints and multi-team coordination - Experience with longitudinal athlete monitoring and developmental tracking across age groups - Experience working with advanced soccer tracking technologies, including player surface interaction technology, is considered a strong advantage - Background in or exposure to sport technology innovation, product development, or industry academic partnerships - Proficiency with data management platforms, statistical software, and visualization tools used in applied sport science
What This Role Offers
- A newly created role within a new, strategically significant initiative backed by both the U.S. Soccer Federation and Nike, Inc. - Access to a purpose-built Performance Innovation Lab at a world-class National Training Center - Unparalleled population access: 27 National Teams spanning youth through senior, men’s and women’s, all operating from a centralized campus - The opportunity to build longitudinal datasets and research programs that have no existing equivalent in world football - A platform for high-impact publication, professional visibility, and thought leadership in sport science - Cross-sport exposure through Nike’s broader athlete network and innovation ecosystem
Key Relationships
The role maintains collaborative working relationships with National Team Integrated Performance, support staff, Nike’s Sport Research Lab (NSRL) and innovation teams, and external academic and research partners.
Governance & Accountability
The role reports into the Nike’s NSRL Team in Beaverton, OR and U.S. Soccer’s Performance Innovation Team in Fayetteville, GA. U.S. Soccer research priorities are set collaboratively between U.S. Soccer leadership and Nike, with a defined engagement cadence (e.g., quarterly research updates, annual innovation reviews). The role holder is expected to maintain scientific independence and ethical standards in all research activities.
Day-to-day project priorities may be coordinated with designated U.S. Soccer personnel; however, Nike retains sole responsibility for all contract-related matters. Nothing in this reporting or collaborative structure shall be interpreted as creating an employment relationship between the employee and U.S. Soccer.
To Apply
Interested candidates should submit a curriculum vitae, a cover letter addressing their vision for the role, and the names of three professional references. Review of applications will begin immediately and continue until the position is filled.
Nike is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity in its workforce.

job.title: Director, Performance Nutrition
job.html_description:
Description


U.S. Soccer Overview


The U.S. Soccer Federation exists in service to soccer. Our aim is to ignite a national passion for the game. Because we believe that soccer is more than a sport; it is a force for good.


We understand the importance and the power of teamwork, on and off the pitch. That is why we work closely with our Federation partners and members, to inspire, support and guide every level of the game: from the grassroots to the National Teams. We want to bring soccer into every home and every community, right across America. Because we believe that soccer can transform lives like no other sport. Soccer can represent the best of U.S.


U.S. Soccer is in a period of significant growth, with ambitious plans for U.S. Soccer in the near and far future. We are, therefore, looking for dynamic servant leaders to join us on this journey: in service to soccer. Applicants must be able to demonstrate visionary leadership, analytical decision-making, professional flexibility, and an empathic management style that builds bold teams and delivers globally significant results.


Position Description


U.S. Soccer is seeking an innovative and collaborative leader to serve as the Director of Performance Nutrition. As a senior leader within the Integrated Performance Support (IPS) Unit, this role will oversee the strategic vision, operational delivery, and continued evolution of performance nutrition and culinary services across the Federation’s National Team environments.


Working within a cross-functional team, this position will collaborate across Performance Medicine, Performance Science, Performance & Data Analytics, and Performance Innovation to support player development, health, performance, and longevity.


The Director will guide an integrated model that combines world-class performance nutrition, culinary operations, player and coach education, body composition strategy, recovery fueling, and performance dining experiences to support elite preparation and sustained high performance.


Working closely with the Executive Director of Culinary Services at the Arthur M. Blank National Training Center, National Team Performance Chefs, consulting sports dietitians, and IPS practitioners, this individual will build scalable systems and standards that elevate athlete care and performance support across Youth, Extended, and Senior National Team programs.


The ideal candidate is a progressive systems thinker and relationship-builder who thrives in collaborative, fast-paced, high-performance environments, brings a team-first mindset, and a passion for innovation.


Primary Responsibilities

  

Leadership, Culinary Operations & Network Management

  • Lead and oversee U.S. Soccer’s Performance Nutrition & Culinary Services strategy across all National Team programs and events
  • Direct and support a growing network that includes:
    • National Training Center Executive Chef
    • Full-time Performance Chef staff
    • Consulting and network sports dietitians
    • External nutrition partners (i.e.: Powerade)
  • Establish operational standards, workflows, service expectations, and performance systems across all nutrition and culinary environments
  • Collaborate with culinary leadership to deliver elite dining and fueling experiences at the National Training Center and during camps, competitions, and travel
  • Develop scalable systems for menu planning, supplementation, hydration delivery, grab-and-go fueling, recovery stations, and performance dining operations
  • Oversee vendor relationships, food sourcing strategies, inventory systems, budget planning, and quality assurance processes
  • Create alignment and continuity across Youth & Extended National Team environments through centralized standards 
  • Build and support a high-performance culture centered on collaboration, innovation, learning, mentorship, and athlete service

Athlete Profiling, Fueling & Readiness Strategy

  • Lead U.S. Soccer’s fueling optimization strategy 
  • Design and operationalize evidence-informed nutrition systems that maximize availability, adaptation, recovery, resilience, and long-term athlete health
  • Lead body composition and athlete profiling initiatives in collaboration with Performance Science, Performance Medicine, and Performance & Data Analytics staff
  • Establish federation-wide standards and best practices for body composition assessment, interpretation, reporting, and education
  • Integrate body composition insights into individualized nutrition periodization, recovery planning, readiness management, and long-term athlete development strategies
  • Support growth and maturation considerations within Youth National Team environments through integrated monitoring and education systems
  • Collaborate across IPS to support integrated performance planning and real-time readiness management processes
  • Utilize data-informed approaches to optimize fueling, hydration, recovery, and player availability strategies

Integrated Performance Collaboration

  • Operate as a senior leader within a transdisciplinary Integrated Performance Support model built around shared accountability and collaborative decision-making
  • Contribute to athlete profiling and long-term player development systems
  • Partner with Performance Innovation and Data Analytics to evolve monitoring systems, education tools, and performance nutrition technologies
  • Support integrated performance planning processes that align technical, tactical, physical, psychosocial and nutritional priorities into one unified preparation strategy
  • Collaborate with clubs, external providers, and federation stakeholders to ensure continuity and alignment of athlete support systems
  • Contribute to the continued evolution of U.S. Soccer’s high-performance environments and athlete experience standards

Requirements


Minimum Qualifications

  • Master’s degree in Nutrition, Dietetics, Exercise Science, or related field required; PhD preferred
  • Registered Dietitian (RD/RDN) in good standing with applicable state licensure
  • Board Certified Specialist in Sports Dietetics (CSSD)
  • ISAK Level 1 (or higher) accreditation
  • Minimum 8 years of experience in elite sport, professional sport, collegiate athletics, Olympic sport, or federation environments
  • Demonstrated experience leading integrated performance nutrition programs and/or culinary performance operations
  • Experience managing interdisciplinary staff, consultants, vendors, or distributed service networks
  • Strong applied expertise in body composition assessment, athlete profiling, nutrition periodization, and performance fueling strategies within elite sport environments
  • Experience collaborating within interdisciplinary performance and medical teams
  • Knowledge of WADA, USADA, FIFA, and anti-doping best practices related to supplementation and athlete education
  • Strong operational leadership, communication, organizational, and project management skills
  • Capable of working in fast-paced and demanding environments
  • Must be able to connect “micro” details to the “macro” vision and mission
  • Proficient experience with Microsoft Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and Outlook)
  • Ability to work non-traditional hours, including evenings, weekends, and extensive domestic/international travel


Desired Qualifications

  • Previous experience within professional sport, Olympic sport, and National Team environments is desired


U.S. Soccer offers a comprehensive compensation package, casual work environment, an inclusive culture, and an atmosphere for professional development.


U.S. Soccer is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion, and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind on the basis of race, color, sex, religion, national origin, citizenship, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, genetic information, military status, political belief, or any other characteristic protected under the law. This policy applies to all our employment practices within our organization. We strongly encourage women, people of color, LGBTQIA, veterans, parents, and persons with disabilities to apply.

U.S. Soccer offers a comprehensive compensation package, casual work environment, an inclusive culture, and an atmosphere for professional development.

job.title: Senior Performance Therapist
job.html_description:
Description


U.S. Soccer Overview


The U.S. Soccer Federation exists in service to soccer. Our aim is to ignite a national passion for the game. Because we believe that soccer is more than a sport; it is a force for good.


We understand the importance and the power of teamwork, on and off the pitch. That is why we work closely with our Federation partners and members, to inspire, support and guide every level of the game: from the grassroots to the National Teams. We want to bring soccer into every home and every community, right across America. Because we believe that soccer can transform lives like no other sport. Soccer can represent the best of U.S.


U.S. Soccer is in a period of significant growth, with ambitious plans for U.S. Soccer in the near and far future. We are, therefore, looking for dynamic servant leaders to join us on this journey: in service to soccer. Applicants must be able to demonstrate visionary leadership, analytical decision-making, professional flexibility, and an empathic management style that builds bold teams and delivers globally significant results.


Position Description


The Senior Performance Therapist is a leader within U.S. Soccer's Integrated Performance Support (IPS) department, supporting Youth and Extended National Teams and advancing an integrated model of development, health & wellbeing, and performance.


This role is responsible for delivering world-class performance physical therapy services while leading the design and implementation of criteria-based return-to-performance (RTP) systems, supporting longitudinal athlete health strategies, and integrating performance medicine with performance science, strength & conditioning, performance nutrition, analytics, and innovation.


The Senior Performance Therapist will play a critical role in developing scalable systems and best practices for the National Training Center, ensuring that athlete care is collaborative, data-informed, and aligned with U.S. Soccer's long-term player development philosophy.


In addition to direct athlete care, this position will mentor staff, contribute to interdisciplinary decision-making, and help establish Integrated Performance Support as a world-class model for performance preservation, and continuous innovation.


Primary Responsibilities

  

Performance Therapy & Athlete Care

  • Deliver comprehensive performance therapy services across Youth National Team and Extended National Team camps, training environments, and competitions.
  • Lead injury assessment, manual therapy interventions, rehabilitation/reconditioning planning, and progressive return-to-performance programming.
  • Develop and implement individualized injury prevention and movement optimization strategies.
  • Collaborate with Integrated Performance Support staff to optimize athlete readiness, availability, and long-term development.
  • Support continuity of care across club and national team environments.

Return-to-Performance Leadership

  • Lead the design, implementation, and continuous refinement of criteria-based return-to-performance (RTP) frameworks.
  • Establish standardized rehabilitation and RTP processes across Youth National Team environments.
  • Integrate objective testing, movement quality, strength diagnostics, load tolerance, and athlete readiness into RTP decision-making.
  • Develop decision-making frameworks that promote consistency, transparency, and athlete-centered progression.
  • Continuously evaluate and improve RTP systems using current evidence and organizational data.

Integrated Performance Support Leadership

  • Serve as a collaborative leader within the Integrated Performance Support (IPS) department.
  • Foster interdisciplinary collaboration across performance medicine, performance science, strength & conditioning, performance nutrition, analytics, coaching, and innovation.
  • Develop shared mental models, communication standards, and integrated decision-making processes that improve athlete care.
  • Contribute to the development of scalable systems and best practices that support all National Team environments.
  • Champion a culture of collaboration, curiosity, accountability, and continuous improvement.

Performance Science Integration & Innovation

  • Integrate performance science into daily clinical practice and reconditioning planning.
  • Utilize the VALD performance technology suite and other athlete monitoring systems to inform reconditioning progressions and return-to-performance decisions.
  • Translate force, movement, neuromuscular, and load data into actionable clinical interventions.
  • Partner with Performance Science staff to develop testing protocols, athlete profiling strategies, and evidence-informed practices.
  • Support innovation initiatives that improve athlete care, decision-making, and organizational capability.

Longitudinal Athlete Health & Analytics

  • Support longitudinal injury surveillance and availability tracking.
  • Analyze injury trends, recurrence rates, workload patterns, and availability metrics to identify opportunities for improvement.
  • Develop reporting and insights that inform integrated planning and athlete management.
  • Support long-term athlete development through data-informed recommendations and proactive intervention strategies.

Leadership & Staff Development

  • Mentor and develop therapists, athletic trainers, interns, and other clinical support staff.
  • Foster interdisciplinary learning and knowledge sharing across the department.
  • Support onboarding, education, and professional development initiatives.
  • Contribute to building a high-performing team culture grounded in teamwork, innovation, and continuous improvement.

Requirements


Minimum Qualifications

  • Licensed Physical Therapist (PT) with active Georgia licensure or ability to obtain licensure prior to employment.
  • Elite sport experience required, including professional, Olympic, academy, or national team environments.
  • Demonstrated expertise in:
    • Rehabilitation and soccer-specific return-to-performance programming
    • Criteria-based RTP system design and implementation
    • Evidence-informed diagnostics, injury prevention and load management strategies
    • Manual therapy and therapeutic exercise and prescription
  • Strong experience integrating performance science into clinical decision-making.
  • Working knowledge of the VALD performance technology suite (ForceDecks, ForceFrame, Dynamo, SmartSpeed, HumanTrak or similar technologies) and the ability to translate objective performance data into athlete care strategies.
  • Experience working within multidisciplinary Integrated Performance environments.
  • Capable of working in fast-paced and demanding environments.
  • Must be able to connect “micro” details to the “macro” vision and mission.
  • Proficient experience with Microsoft Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and Outlook).


Desired Qualifications

  • Experience supporting elite soccer athletes within professional clubs, academies, or national team environments.
  • Experience working with youth elite athletes and an understanding of growth, maturation, and long-term athlete development principles.
  • Experience designing systems, processes, and operating standards across multiple teams or performance environments.



U.S. Soccer offers a comprehensive compensation package, casual work environment, an inclusive culture, and an atmosphere for professional development.


U.S. Soccer is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion, and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind on the basis of race, color, sex, religion, national origin, citizenship, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, genetic information, military status, political belief, or any other characteristic protected under the law. This policy applies to all our employment practices within our organization. We strongly encourage women, people of color, LGBTQIA, veterans, parents, and persons with disabilities to apply.

job.title: Director of Learning & Development (L&D)
job.html_description:
Description


U.S. Soccer Overview


The U.S. Soccer Federation exists in service to soccer. Our aim is to ignite a national passion for the game. Because we believe that soccer is more than a sport; it is a force for good.


We understand the importance and the power of teamwork, on and off the pitch. That is why we work closely with our Federation partners and members, to inspire, support and guide every level of the game: from the grassroots to the National Teams. We want to bring soccer into every home and every community, right across America. Because we believe that soccer can transform lives like no other sport. Soccer can represent the best of U.S.


U.S. Soccer is in a period of significant growth, with ambitious plans for U.S. Soccer in the near and far future. We are, therefore, looking for dynamic servant leaders to join us on this journey: in service to soccer. Applicants must be able to demonstrate visionary leadership, analytical decision-making, professional flexibility, and an empathic management style that builds bold teams and delivers globally significant results.


Position Description

  

The Director of Learning & Development (L&D) is a strategic leader responsible for designing, building, and executing a comprehensive enterprise-wide learning strategy that drives capability development, leadership effectiveness, and organizational performance. This role oversees the full lifecycle of learning—from needs assessment and competency modeling to program design, delivery, and measurement—ensuring alignment with business priorities and talent strategy.


The Director will partner closely with senior leadership to strengthen leadership pipelines, accelerate skill development at all levels, and embed a culture of continuous learning across the organization. 


Primary Responsibilities

  

Learning & Development Strategy

  • Develop and execute a multi-year enterprise L&D strategy aligned with business objectives, workforce planning, and talent priorities
  • Establish a scalable, modern learning ecosystem (including digital, in-person, and experiential learning)
  • Lead annual learning needs analysis and translate business goals into actionable development plans
  • Define success metrics and continuously evaluate effectiveness and ROI of programs

 Leadership & Executive Development

  • Design and deliver leadership development frameworks for all levels, including front-line leaders through executive leadership
  • Partner with senior executives to build customized executive coaching, development programs, and succession readiness initiatives
  • Lead enterprise leadership curriculum, including strategic thinking, change leadership, and organizational effectiveness
  • Facilitate executive-level development experiences, workshops, and offsites

 Competency Modeling & Skills Architecture

  • Build and maintain a comprehensive competency framework aligned to business strategy and future workforce needs
  • Define role-based capabilities, leadership competencies, and skill taxonomies across the organization
  • Partner with HR and Talent functions to integrate competencies into performance management, career pathing, and succession planning
  • Drive skills-based development approaches and workforce readiness for future capabilities

 Program Design & Delivery

  • Oversee the creation of high-impact learning programs using modern instructional design principles
  • Ensure effective delivery across formats (live, virtual, digital, microlearning, and blended)
  • Implement best practices in adult learning theory and learner engagement
  • Manage external vendors, platforms, and partnerships as needed

 Talent & Organization Impact

  • Drive a culture of continuous learning and professional growth throughout the organization
  • Align learning programs with employee engagement, retention, and career mobility goals
  • Partner with HR Business Partners and business leaders to address capability gaps and accelerate performance
  • Support organizational change initiatives through targeted learning interventions

 Team Leadership & Operations

  • Establish governance, processes, and operational excellence within the L&D function
  • Manage budget, resource allocation, and vendor relationships
  • Leverage learning technologies, data analytics, and reporting to drive insights and decision-making


Requirements


Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Organizational Development, Business, or related field
  • 10+ years of progressive experience in Learning & Development, Talent Development, or Organizational Effectiveness
  • Demonstrated success building and executing enterprise L&D strategies
  • Experience designing and delivering leadership development programs, including executive-level initiatives
  • Strong experience in competency modeling and skills framework development
  • Proven ability to influence and partner with senior executives
  • General knowledge of soccer
  • Capable of working in fast-paced and demanding environments
  • Must be able to connect “micro” details to the “macro” vision and mission
  • Proficient experience with Microsoft Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and Outlook)

Desired Qualifications

  • Master’s degree (MBA, Organizational Development, or related field)
  • Experience in large, complex or high-growth organizations
  • Certifications in coaching, leadership development, or instructional design (e.g., ICF, ATD, CPTD)
  • Expertise with modern learning technologies, LMS/LXP platforms, and digital learning strategies


U.S. Soccer offers a comprehensive compensation package, casual work environment, an inclusive culture, and an atmosphere for professional development.


U.S. Soccer is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion, and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind on the basis of race, color, sex, religion, national origin, citizenship, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, genetic information, military status, political belief, or any other characteristic protected under the law. This policy applies to all our employment practices within our organization. We strongly encourage women, people of color, LGBTQIA, veterans, parents, and persons with disabilities to apply.

job.title: Junior Salesforce Developer
job.html_description:
Description


U.S. Soccer Overview


The U.S. Soccer Federation exists in service to soccer. Our aim is to ignite a national passion for the game. Because we believe that soccer is more than a sport; it is a force for good.


We understand the importance and the power of teamwork, on and off the pitch. That is why we work closely with our Federation partners and members, to inspire, support and guide every level of the game: from the grassroots to the National Teams. We want to bring soccer into every home and every community, right across America. Because we believe that soccer can transform lives like no other sport. Soccer can represent the best of U.S.


U.S. Soccer is in a period of significant growth, with ambitious plans for U.S. Soccer in the near and far future. We are, therefore, looking for dynamic servant leaders to join us on this journey: in service to soccer. Applicants must be able to demonstrate visionary leadership, analytical decision-making, professional flexibility, and an empathic management style that builds bold teams and delivers globally significant results.


Position Description


U.S. Soccer is seeking a Junior Salesforce Developer to support the growth of our CRM solutions. This individual will join U.S. Soccer’s growing product and technology team and contribute to the success of our Salesforce implementation and related technologies. This individual must have a strong mix of Salesforce declarative and Apex coding capabilities. This position reports to the Senior Salesforce Administrator. 


Primary Responsibilities

  • Build new features and enhancements in Salesforce for ticket sales, customer service, advancement, partnership teams, and more
  • Assist in testing, debugging, and deploying enhancements and new features following established change management processes
  • Support data migrations, integrations, and data quality efforts, ensuring accuracy and consistency across many departments.
  • Build and maintain Salesforce reports, dashboards, custom objects, and automations

Requirements


Minimum Qualifications

  • 2+ years of experience in Salesforce administration and development
  • Experience using project management software, such as JIRA
  • Understanding of Sales Cloud and Service Cloud functionality; exposure to nonprofit/fundraising solutions (e.g., NPSP/NPC) preferred
  • Familiarity with Apex, SOQL, Lightning components, and declarative tools (flows, validation rules, page layouts)
  • Basic knowledge of data management, system integrations (e.g., APIs, middleware), and CRM best practices
  • General knowledge of soccer
  • Capable of working in fast-paced and demanding environments
  • Must be able to connect “micro” details to the “macro” vision and mission
  • Proficient experience with Microsoft Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and Outlook)


Desired Qualifications

  • Salesforce Certified Administrator or similar certification strongly preferred
  • Demonstrated strategic hustle and work ethic with diversity of thought
  • A background in, and passion for, soccer, sports and/or entertainment is preferred
  • Ability to handle multiple projects, tasks, and priorities
  • Collaborative, team-player with a results-driven mindset
  • Strong problem-solving capabilities to address and resolve issues that face a growing company
  • Creative thinker, self-starter with strong analytical skills


U.S. Soccer offers a comprehensive compensation package, casual work environment, an inclusive culture, and an atmosphere for professional development.


U.S. Soccer is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion, and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind on the basis of race, color, sex, religion, national origin, citizenship, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, genetic information, military status, political belief, or any other characteristic protected under the law. This policy applies to all our employment practices within our organization. We strongly encourage women, people of color, LGBTQIA, veterans, parents, and persons with disabilities to apply.

job.title: EQ Operations Specialist, Equipment Management Operations
job.html_description:
Description


U.S. Soccer Overview


The U.S. Soccer Federation exists in service to soccer. Our aim is to ignite a national passion for the game. Because we believe that soccer is more than a sport; it is a force for good.


We understand the importance and the power of teamwork, on and off the pitch. That is why we work closely with our Federation partners and members, to inspire, support and guide every level of the game: from the grassroots to the National Teams. We want to bring soccer into every home and every community, right across America. Because we believe that soccer can transform lives like no other sport. Soccer can represent the best of U.S.


U.S. Soccer is in a period of significant growth, with ambitious plans for U.S. Soccer in the near and far future. We are, therefore, looking for dynamic servant leaders to join us on this journey: in service to soccer. Applicants must be able to demonstrate visionary leadership, analytical decision-making, professional flexibility, and an empathic management style that builds bold teams and delivers globally significant results.


Position Description


As an Equipment Operations - Specialist for U.S. Soccer, this position is responsible managing various aspects of team equipment operations as it relates to U.S Soccer’s Youth National Teams (16), Extended National Teams (9), and key Sporting, Commercial, and Events departments based at our National Training Center (NTC) in Fayetteville, GA. The position demands a high level of familiarity with the sport of soccer, the products and equipment necessary for facilitating training and competition, both on and off the field. This position reports to and works closely with the Equipment Operations Management Team to meet departmental goals.


As a member of the Equipment Operations department, this position travels on YNT and ENT trips as required and is an integral part of the YNT and ENT support staff. A high degree of professionalism is required, as is the ability to independently create quick, effective solutions for issues that arise. The position is heavily focused on ensuring the players and coaching staff can rely on having team equipment needs secured for all training, matches, etc. on and off the field. Additionally, this role collaborates closely with YNT Administrators to ensure the success of all on and off the field requirements. 


Primary Responsibilities

  • Handle the duties of day-to-day operations of on-site equipment headquarters, NTC warehouse, storage and locker rooms
  • Use inventory software to maintain accurate product inventory, product replenishment needs and individual product dispersal 
  • Forecast and assist with the departmental purchase orders seasonal buys for all Youth National Teams, Extended National Teams, and non-promo Federation departments 
  • Process, manage and place both futures and at-once orders against yearly product allocation via NIKE GameDay online ordering system 
  • Contribute to the movement and logistical planning for all equipment needs for YNT and ENT domestic and international camps, matches and tournaments including the coordination, packing and overseeing the shipping and receiving logistics Serve as a point of contact, if needed, for any and all critical equipment fulfillment needs that arise for traveling teams, Federation staff, departments, etc. 
  • Sustain proactive communication with Management of Equipment Operations, YNT and ENT administration and coaching personnel
  • Assist the Manager of EQ Ops Sports Med/HP with shipping and receiving duties, if necessary
  • Collaborate with the YNT and ENT coaching staff with equipment and training set up, as needed
  • Responsible for effective team laundry needs while on the road and at the facility
  • Ensure presentation of the team (team gear, attire, etc.) is of the highest quality
  • Main up-to-date with FIFA rules and equipment protocol regulations
  • Support NTC-related initiatives and events as assigned, with responsibilities and event assignments determined based on organizational needs.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for assigned teams during designated cycles; however, responsibilities may extend beyond assigned teams to support broader program and organizational objectives.

Requirements


Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree required
  • At least one year of prior related experience in sports or events  
  • General soccer knowledge 
  • Ability to maintain strong level of professionalism and effective communication 
  • Ability to anticipate needs and problem solve 
  • Ability to prioritize and manage a number of different projects simultaneously
  • Quick learner who can function well in fast-paced, team-oriented environment
  • Must be able to connect “micro” details to the “macro” vision and mission
  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Word and Excel
  • Ability to lift and handle boxes weighing upwards of 75 lbs. 
  • Able and willing to travel up to 50% of the year, as needed. Actual travel requirements may vary and are not guaranteed
  • Extensive work hours required while traveling 
  • Able and willing to work non-traditional hours including evenings, weekends, and holidays as needed 

Desired Qualifications

  • 1-3 years of prior competitive sports team equipment management experience 
  • Ability to create and enhance team centric environment 
  • History of working with inventory and ordering system(s) 
  • Passion and strong understanding of the soccer game and landscape
  • Excellent organizational, planning, and leadership skills 
  • Ability to collaborate effectively with colleagues and athletes having a range of backgrounds, interests, and      perspectives 
  • Bilingual (English and Spanish)
  • Strong independent proficiency with Microsoft Suite products such as Word and Excel 


U.S. Soccer offers a comprehensive compensation package, casual work environment, an inclusive culture, and an atmosphere for professional development.


U.S. Soccer is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion, and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind on the basis of race, color, sex, religion, national origin, citizenship, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, genetic information, military status, political belief, or any other characteristic protected under the law. This policy applies to all our employment practices within our organization. We strongly encourage women, people of color, LGBTQIA, veterans, parents, and persons with disabilities to apply.

Actual travel requirements may vary and are not guaranteed - Extensive work hours required while traveling - Able and willing to work non-traditional hours including evenings, weekends, and holidays as needed
